title: Review Message Ordering Requirements
prompt: Review Message Ordering Requirements

title: Inventory all message queues in the system
prompt: Inventory all message queues in the system. For each, identify whether a dead-letter queue is configured. Find any queue where failed messages are silently dropped. Recommend DLQ configurations with appropriate retention and alerting for each.

title: Audit queue depth monitoring across all queues
prompt: Audit queue depth monitoring across all queues. Identify any queue without a depth alarm. For each unmonitored queue, describe what goes wrong operationally when it fills silently. Recommend alert thresholds based on expected throughput.

title: Find the queue that would cause the most visible user impact if it backe
prompt: Find the queue that would cause the most visible user impact if it backed up. Trace the downstream effects of a 10-minute processing pause on that queue. Identify all services that would degrade and recommend a prioritization strategy to drain it safely.

title: Identify every queue consumer that calls an external rate-limited API
prompt: Identify every queue consumer that calls an external rate-limited API. For each, assess whether the consumer processes messages at a rate that could exceed the API rate limit under full queue load. Find consumers with no throttling mechanism between the queue and the external call. Recommend a rate-aware consumer implementation with a token bucket or scheduled batch pattern. Calculate the maximum safe processing rate per consumer given the external API's published rate limits.

title: Audit all queue consumers for correct acknowledgment timing
prompt: Audit all queue consumers for correct acknowledgment timing. Find consumers that acknowledge a message before the associated side effect such as a database write, external API call, or downstream message publish has completed and committed. For each pre-processing acknowledgment, describe the data loss scenario that occurs if the consumer crashes between the ack and the side effect. Recommend an ack-after-commit pattern for each high-risk consumer.

title: Review the message schema validation strategy across all queues
prompt: Review the message schema validation strategy across all queues. Identify queues that receive messages from external or less-trusted sources with no schema validation at the consumer. Find consumers that deserialize message bodies into typed objects without validating required fields. Recommend a schema validation layer at the consumer boundary for every external-source queue. Identify queues that would benefit from producer-side schema enforcement to catch malformed messages before they reach consumers.

title: Assess the poison message isolation strategy for all queues
prompt: Assess the poison message isolation strategy for all queues. Find queues with no maximum retry count where a single malformed or unprocessable message can block all consumer progress indefinitely. Identify queues where DLQ routing is configured but the DLQ is not monitored with an alert. Find consumers that catch all exceptions and acknowledge the message regardless, silently swallowing processing errors. Recommend a standardized poison message isolation strategy with a specific max-receive-count, DLQ routing, and DLQ depth alert for each queue.

title: Evaluate the fan-out topology of all message queues
prompt: Evaluate the fan-out topology of all message queues. Identify every queue where one producer message triggers multiple downstream queue inserts. Calculate the write amplification ratio at peak produce rate for the highest fan-out paths. Find fan-out patterns where the downstream consumer capacity is insufficient to drain the amplified volume during a burst. Recommend bounded fan-out strategies such as batching, consolidation, or selective fan-out based on message attributes for the highest amplification risk paths.

title: Analyze every message queue in the system for production readiness
prompt: Analyze every message queue in the system for production readiness. Identify each queue's purpose, its producer, and its consumers. Assess whether each queue has a dead-letter queue configured with appropriate retention. Look for queues where failed message processing results in silent drops rather than DLQ routing. Check whether queue depth is monitored with alerts that fire before saturation causes user-visible errors. Identify queues with no replay capability for incident recovery. Assess whether message visibility timeouts are set correctly for the processing time required. Summarize the top five queue configuration gaps that represent operational risk.

title: Evaluate the operational tooling available for managing message queues d
prompt: Evaluate the operational tooling available for managing message queues during incidents. Determine whether engineers on-call can inspect queue contents without deploying code changes. Assess whether safe message replay is possible without risk of double-processing. Find queues where purging requires direct access to the broker with no guardrails. Identify any queue that accumulates sensitive data that should be encrypted at rest. Look for operational runbooks covering queue emergencies — DLQ drains, consumer restarts, poison message isolation. Recommend the specific tooling additions that would reduce mean time to resolution for queue-related incidents by the largest margin.

title: You are a backend infrastructure engineer conducting a production readin
prompt: You are a backend infrastructure engineer conducting a production readiness review of the message queue layer. Begin by enumerating every queue in the system, its purpose, its producer, its consumers, and its delivery guarantee. For each queue, verify that a dead-letter queue is configured with appropriate retention and that DLQ depth triggers an alert before it exceeds the team's replay capacity. Assess whether queue depth monitoring is in place for all queues and whether alerts fire before saturation causes user-visible errors. Identify queues where a single slow consumer could stall all others and recommend whether parallel consumers or prioritization would help. Review message visibility timeouts and ensure they are set conservatively longer than the maximum expected processing time to prevent duplicate deliveries during long-running operations. Find queues with no backpressure signal to producers, allowing uncapped burst publishing. Assess whether the message broker is configured for high availability with sufficient replication to survive a node failure without data loss. Produce a readiness scorecard for each queue covering monitoring, DLQ coverage, retry configuration, HA setup, and operational tooling.

title: Audit the operational tooling available for managing queues during produ
prompt: Audit the operational tooling available for managing queues during production incidents. Determine whether engineers on-call can inspect queue contents, view message headers and bodies, and identify the first failing message without deploying new code. Assess whether message replay is a self-service operation or requires direct broker access. Find queues where purging is the only recovery option because replay is not implemented. Identify any queue that accumulates sensitive user data such as PII, payment information, or authentication tokens without encryption at rest. Review whether operational runbooks exist for the most common queue incidents including DLQ overflow, consumer crash, poison message, and broker failover, and whether the runbooks are up to date. Assess whether the queue infrastructure has ever been tested with a deliberate chaos experiment and whether the results were used to improve configuration. Recommend the specific operational improvements that would have the largest impact on reducing mean time to resolution for queue-related incidents.

title: Perform a security audit of the message queue infrastructure
prompt: Perform a security audit of the message queue infrastructure. Identify every queue that is accessible from outside the application's trusted network boundary. Find queues that accept messages from external sources without authenticating the producer. Assess whether message bodies are validated for structure and authenticity before processing. Look for queues where any authenticated user can publish any message type, allowing privilege escalation through crafted payloads. Identify queues carrying sensitive data without encryption in transit and at rest. Assess whether consumer applications have the minimum necessary permissions on the broker. Find queue configurations that would allow a compromised consumer to read messages intended for other consumers. Recommend the specific access control, encryption, and validation changes required to bring the queue layer into compliance with a minimum security standard.

title: Audit all cache layers and their TTL configuration
prompt: Map every cache in the system — CDN, application, database query, in-memory. For each, document what is cached, TTL, invalidation strategy, and what happens on a cold start.
prompt_short: Map all cache layers, their TTLs, and invalidation strategies.
prompt_filtered: Cache inventory and TTL only. No performance analysis.

title: Find stale data risks in the caching layer
prompt: Identify caches that can serve stale data in a way that causes incorrect behavior for users. For each, assess how stale is too stale, and whether the current TTL is appropriate.
prompt_short: Find all places where cache staleness could cause incorrect behavior.
prompt_filtered: Staleness risks only. Flag by user-visible impact.

title: Identify cache invalidation bugs and missing purges
prompt: Find cases where data is updated but the corresponding cache is not invalidated. Look for missing invalidation calls after writes, and race conditions where the cache is read between an update and its invalidation.
prompt_short: Find cache invalidation bugs and write paths missing cache purges.
prompt_filtered: Invalidation correctness only. Write path → cache sync analysis.

title: Find expensive queries that should be cached
prompt: Identify the top 10 most expensive database queries or external API calls that are called repeatedly with the same parameters. Estimate the cache hit rate improvement and latency reduction if caching were applied.
prompt_short: Find expensive repeated queries that would benefit from caching.
prompt_filtered: Cache candidates only. No implementation — just the list with cost estimate.

title: Review cache key design for collisions and predictability
prompt: Audit all cache keys across the application. Identify keys that are too broad (causing over-invalidation), too narrow (causing misses), or predictable enough to be exploited in a poisoning attack.
prompt_short: Audit cache key design for collisions, breadth, and security risks.
prompt_filtered: Cache key design only. Security and correctness focus.

title: Assess cache stampede protection under high concurrency
prompt: Identify caches where a simultaneous expiry of many keys — or a cold start — would cause a stampede of backend requests. Find any hot key with no probabilistic early expiry, mutex lock, or stale-while-revalidate pattern to prevent thundering herd on cache miss.
prompt_short: Find caches with no stampede protection on popular key expiry.
prompt_filtered: Cache stampede only. Thundering herd and hot key expiry patterns.

title: Find caches storing sensitive data without encryption
prompt: Identify any cache — Redis, Memcached, CDN edge, or in-memory — that stores PII, auth tokens, session data, or payment information. Verify sensitive values are encrypted at rest and access to the cache is restricted by network policy and auth.
prompt_short: Find caches storing sensitive data without encryption or access controls.
prompt_filtered: Cache security only. PII, tokens, and sensitive values in caches.

title: Evaluate cache hit rate by endpoint and data type
prompt: Identify which endpoints and data types have the lowest cache hit rates. For each low-hit cache, diagnose the root cause — key variability, short TTL, poor invalidation granularity, or data that is inherently uncacheable. Recommend changes to improve hit rate where feasible.
prompt_short: Find low cache hit rates by endpoint and diagnose the root cause.
prompt_filtered: Cache hit rate only. Miss rate analysis by endpoint and data type.

title: Audit read-through vs cache-aside implementation correctness
prompt: Identify which caching pattern is in use — read-through, write-through, write-behind, or cache-aside — for each cache store. Find inconsistencies where different parts of the codebase use different patterns for the same cache, creating stale data races.
prompt_short: Audit which caching pattern each store uses and find mixed-pattern inconsistencies.
prompt_filtered: Caching pattern consistency only. Pattern mismatch causing stale data.

title: Check CDN cache bypass for authenticated or personalized content
prompt: Identify API responses or page routes that are being cached at the CDN despite containing user-specific or auth-gated data. Find cache-control headers missing private, no-store, or vary: Authorization directives that would prevent cross-user cache contamination.
prompt_short: Find CDN-cached responses that contain user-specific or authenticated data.
prompt_filtered: CDN cache contamination only. Authenticated content served from shared cache.

title: Identify stale-while-revalidate opportunities for latency reduction
prompt: Find API responses that currently block on cache miss, forcing users to wait for a backend fetch. Identify responses where serving a slightly stale value while refreshing in the background would be acceptable, reducing perceived latency without a meaningful accuracy cost.
prompt_short: Find endpoints where stale-while-revalidate would cut user-visible latency.
prompt_filtered: Stale-while-revalidate candidates only. Latency vs freshness trade-off.

title: Assess multi-region cache consistency and replication lag
prompt: For systems deployed across multiple regions, identify caches that are replicated between regions. Assess the replication lag and whether it causes reads in one region to return data that has been invalidated in another. Recommend a consistency strategy — local cache with shorter TTL, global invalidation, or region-aware keys.
prompt_short: Find multi-region cache replication lag causing cross-region stale reads.
prompt_filtered: Multi-region cache consistency only. Replication lag and invalidation timing.

title: Find missing negative caching for expensive empty results
prompt: Identify endpoints that query the database on every request for data that does not exist — empty search results, not-found lookups. Verify that negative results are cached with an appropriate TTL to prevent repeated expensive queries for missing data, while not caching miss results too long to prevent new data from appearing.
prompt_short: Find expensive not-found queries that should be negatively cached.
prompt_filtered: Negative caching only. Repeated empty-result queries hitting the database.

title: Review cache eviction policy alignment with access patterns
prompt: Identify the eviction policy configured for each cache — LRU, LFU, TTL-only, random. Assess whether the policy matches the access pattern of the data. An LRU policy on data with uniform random access evicts as often as needed data. Recommend policy changes where the mismatch causes unnecessary evictions.
prompt_short: Audit cache eviction policies against actual data access patterns.
prompt_filtered: Eviction policy only. LRU/LFU mismatch with access distribution.

title: Identify cache dependencies that create cascading invalidation storms
prompt: Find cache entries that, when invalidated, trigger the invalidation of a large number of downstream caches. A single write to a root entity should not trigger a cascade that clears thousands of derived cache entries simultaneously. Identify the highest fan-out invalidation chains and recommend bounded invalidation strategies.
prompt_short: Find single invalidations that cascade into large cache eviction storms.
prompt_filtered: Cascading invalidation only. Fan-out invalidation chain analysis.

title: Assess cache warming strategy for cold start scenarios
prompt: Identify what happens to latency and error rates during a cold cache start — after a deployment, cache flush, or new region launch. Find critical paths with no cache warming strategy that would be fully backend-loaded until the cache warms organically. Recommend pre-warming procedures for the highest-impact caches.
prompt_short: Find caches with no warming strategy that cause latency spikes on cold start.
prompt_filtered: Cache warming only. Cold start latency and backend load on miss burst.

title: Audit cache size limits and memory pressure handling
prompt: Verify that all in-memory caches have a maximum size configured. Find caches that grow without bound — holding entries indefinitely with no eviction limit — that could cause out-of-memory errors under sustained traffic. Recommend size limits with an eviction policy appropriate for each cache's access pattern.
prompt_short: Find unbounded caches that could cause OOM errors under sustained load.
prompt_filtered: Cache size limits only. Unbounded growth and OOM risk.

title: Inventory all cache stores and their TTL configurations
prompt: Inventory all cache stores and their TTL configurations. Identify any cache with no TTL that could serve stale data indefinitely. For each cache store, verify the invalidation strategy is explicit and tested rather than left to natural expiry.

title: Find the three cache keys most likely to cause a collision between diffe
prompt: Find the three cache keys most likely to cause a collision between different users or tenants. For each, explain the data leak scenario and recommend a namespace or hash strategy that eliminates the risk.

title: Identify the highest-traffic endpoint that has no caching layer
prompt: Identify the highest-traffic endpoint that has no caching layer. Estimate the database query reduction that adding a cache would provide. Propose a cache key design and TTL that balances freshness against load reduction.

title: Identify the three most expensive database queries called repeatedly wit
prompt: Identify the three most expensive database queries called repeatedly with the same parameters. For each, estimate the query cost, the call frequency, and the acceptable staleness of the cached result. Design a cache key scheme that is specific enough to prevent cross-user contamination but broad enough to maximize hit rate. Propose a TTL for each cache entry that balances freshness against load reduction. Calculate the expected database load reduction from adding each cache.

title: Audit every CDN-cached response for user-specific or auth-gated content
prompt: Audit every CDN-cached response for user-specific or auth-gated content. Find responses missing a Vary Authorization header or lacking a private cache-control directive. For each misconfigured response, describe the worst-case scenario where one user's personalized data is served to another user. Recommend the specific Cache-Control header corrections for each misconfigured response. Verify that the corrected headers do not unnecessarily degrade cache hit rates for truly public content.

title: Find every in-memory cache in the application with no maximum size confi
prompt: Find every in-memory cache in the application with no maximum size configured. For each unbounded cache, estimate the memory growth rate under sustained traffic at 2x current peak. Identify the earliest time the cache would cause an out-of-memory error at that growth rate. Recommend a maximum size limit with an eviction policy appropriate for each cache's access pattern. Verify that the size limit and eviction policy are tested under simulated load.

title: Evaluate the cache invalidation strategy for every cache layer in the sy
prompt: Evaluate the cache invalidation strategy for every cache layer in the system. Identify caches where invalidation is triggered by a TTL only with no explicit purge on write. Find write paths that modify data but fail to invalidate the corresponding cache entries. Assess whether any cache can serve data that has been logically deleted but not cache-purged. Recommend explicit invalidation triggers for every write path that corresponds to a cached read. Verify that invalidation is atomic with the write or occurs as the first step after the write commits.

title: Assess the cache key design across all cache layers
prompt: Assess the cache key design across all cache layers. Identify keys constructed from user-supplied input without normalization or hashing, which are cache poisoning attack surfaces. Find keys that do not include a version or namespace prefix, risking collisions between environments or application versions. Identify keys too broad that cause unnecessary invalidations when unrelated data changes. Find keys too narrow that fragment the cache and reduce hit rates below their theoretical maximum. Recommend a key design standard with namespace, version, and input normalization requirements.

title: Audit every caching layer in the system — in-memory caches, distributed
prompt: Audit every caching layer in the system — in-memory caches, distributed caches, CDN edge caches, and database query caches. For each, identify what data is cached, the TTL, and the invalidation strategy. Look for caches with no TTL that will serve stale data until a deployment or manual flush. Find cache keys that are too broad, causing unrelated data to be invalidated together. Identify cache keys that could collide between users, tenants, or environments, creating a data leak or cross-contamination risk. Assess whether cache warming strategies exist for cold start scenarios. Summarize the top five cache correctness risks ordered by frequency and user impact.

title: Evaluate the security posture of all caching layers
prompt: Evaluate the security posture of all caching layers. Identify any cache key that is constructed from user-supplied input without normalization or hashing, creating a cache poisoning attack surface. Check whether cached responses include authorization context or whether cached data could be served to unauthorized users. Look for sensitive data — tokens, PII, payment information — stored in caches without appropriate encryption or short TTLs. Assess whether the cache is accessible from untrusted networks or only from the application layer. Recommend the specific key design changes and access control additions that would close the highest-risk cache security gaps.

title: You are a performance engineer tasked with auditing the caching architec
prompt: You are a performance engineer tasked with auditing the caching architecture of a production system to reduce database load and improve response latency. Begin by mapping every cache layer in the system including CDN edge caches, application-level distributed caches, in-process memory caches, and database query caches. For each layer, document what data is cached, the TTL, the maximum size limit, the eviction policy, and the invalidation strategy. Identify layers with no TTL that will serve stale data until a deployment or manual flush. Find caches with no size limit that could cause out-of-memory errors under sustained traffic. Assess the cache key design for each layer looking for keys that could collide between users, tenants, or application versions, and keys that are too broad causing over-invalidation. Evaluate the cold start strategy, what happens to latency and backend load when the cache is empty after a deployment or cache flush. Review whether negative results such as not-found responses and empty search results are cached to prevent repeated expensive backend calls. Conclude with a cache improvement roadmap that prioritizes changes by the ratio of latency improvement to implementation effort.

title: Evaluate the security posture of all caching layers with a focus on data
prompt: Evaluate the security posture of all caching layers with a focus on data leakage and cache poisoning. Identify every cache that stores data derived from user identity such as personalized content, access-controlled resources, session data, or payment information. For each, verify that the cache key uniquely identifies both the resource and the authorized user so that one user's cached data cannot be served to another. Find CDN-cached responses that include user-specific headers or body content without a Vary header that would prevent cross-user serving. Identify cache keys constructed from user-controlled input that could be manipulated to poison the cache with attacker-controlled responses. Assess whether distributed cache nodes are accessible from untrusted networks or only from the application tier. Look for caches retaining sensitive data longer than the underlying resource's authorization window. Recommend the specific cache key changes, header corrections, and access control additions that eliminate the highest-risk data leakage scenarios.

title: Analyze cache hit rate performance across all endpoints and identify the
prompt: Analyze cache hit rate performance across all endpoints and identify the largest opportunities for improvement. Start by pulling the cache miss rate per endpoint over the last 30 days or estimating it from query logs if direct metrics are unavailable. Rank endpoints by miss rate multiplied by request volume to produce a miss-cost score. For the top ten endpoints by miss-cost, diagnose the root cause of the high miss rate such as key variability from dynamic parameters, TTL shorter than the data's natural change frequency, lack of negative caching for empty results, or simply no cache layer in place. Assess whether a stale-while-revalidate pattern would be acceptable for high-miss endpoints where freshness is less critical than latency. Find endpoints where a cache warming strategy would eliminate the cold start miss storm. Recommend specific TTL changes, key redesigns, and cache tier additions for the top five miss-cost endpoints, with an estimated hit rate improvement and database load reduction for each.

title: Audit search relevance and result quality
prompt: Evaluate the search implementation for result relevance. Test with common queries and identify where results are wrong, incomplete, or poorly ranked. Assess whether the ranking algorithm is appropriate for the use case.
prompt_short: Audit search result quality and relevance for common user queries.
prompt_filtered: Relevance and ranking only. No indexing or infrastructure analysis.

title: Find missing search indexes and slow query paths
prompt: Identify which fields are frequently searched but lack an index. Review query plans for the top search queries and flag full-table scans. Estimate the performance improvement from adding targeted indexes.
prompt_short: Find missing indexes and slow queries in the search implementation.
prompt_filtered: Index and query performance only. No relevance analysis.

title: Identify search use cases not currently supported
prompt: Review the product requirements and user behavior to identify search use cases the current implementation does not handle — fuzzy matching, filters, facets, synonyms, typo tolerance, multilingual support.
prompt_short: Find search use cases the current implementation does not support.
prompt_filtered: Missing capabilities only. No implementation suggestions.

title: Review search security and injection vulnerabilities
prompt: Audit all search inputs for injection vulnerabilities. Verify that search queries are parameterized, user input is sanitized, and the search backend cannot be manipulated to return unauthorized data.
prompt_short: Check search inputs for injection risks and unauthorized data exposure.
prompt_filtered: Security only. Input validation and authorization for search.

title: Assess search infrastructure for scale and reliability
prompt: Evaluate the search backend for production readiness — redundancy, failover, index replication, and what happens if search goes down. Identify single points of failure and missing fallbacks.
prompt_short: Evaluate search infrastructure for reliability and failover readiness.
prompt_filtered: Infrastructure reliability only. No relevance or query analysis.

title: Evaluate full-text tokenization and language analyzer configuration
prompt: Review the tokenizer and analyzer pipeline for each indexed field. Identify fields using the wrong language analyzer — for example, English stemming applied to a multilingual corpus — causing missed matches. Assess whether custom synonyms, stop words, and compound word handling are configured for the primary user language.
prompt_short: Audit tokenizer and analyzer config for language and synonym coverage.
prompt_filtered: Tokenization only. Analyzer pipeline and language-specific settings.

title: Find search queries bypassing authorization filters
prompt: Verify that all search queries apply document-level authorization filters before returning results. Identify queries that search a shared index without tenant or user scoping filters, potentially leaking documents from other users. Assess whether authorization is enforced at query time or post-filtering — noting that post-filtering can return empty pages for authorized users.
prompt_short: Find search queries that could return documents from other users or tenants.
prompt_filtered: Search authorization only. Cross-tenant document exposure.

title: Assess search result ranking for business relevance signals
prompt: Review the ranking signals currently used by the search engine — BM25 text score, recency, popularity, user personalization. Identify ranking factors that matter to the business but are not currently weighted in the score — purchase rate, engagement, freshness for time-sensitive content. Recommend signal additions with estimated relevance lift.
prompt_short: Find missing business-relevant ranking signals in the current search score.
prompt_filtered: Ranking signals only. Business metric alignment with search score.

title: Identify search index drift from the primary data source
prompt: Assess how the search index is kept in sync with the primary database. Identify the maximum lag between a write and its appearance in search. Find failure modes where the index permanently misses an update — skipped events, failed sync jobs, partial re-indexes. Recommend a reconciliation strategy to detect and repair index drift.
prompt_short: Find index drift risks where writes never propagate to the search index.
prompt_filtered: Index sync correctness only. Drift detection and reconciliation.

title: Evaluate facet and filter performance on large datasets
prompt: Identify faceted search queries on high-cardinality fields — user IDs, tags, categories — that are computed on every request rather than pre-aggregated. Assess the query latency and server load of these facets under peak traffic. Recommend pre-computed facet caches or aggregation indexes for the most expensive facets.
prompt_short: Find high-cardinality facets with no pre-aggregation that are expensive to compute.
prompt_filtered: Facet performance only. High-cardinality field aggregation costs.

title: Assess fuzzy matching and typo tolerance configuration
prompt: Review the fuzzy matching configuration — edit distance tolerance, prefix matching, phonetic matching. Identify whether fuzzy matching is enabled for short queries where it causes too many irrelevant matches and disabled for long queries where a single typo causes no results. Recommend edit distance thresholds tuned to query length.
prompt_short: Audit fuzzy matching config for edit distance thresholds by query length.
prompt_filtered: Fuzzy matching only. Edit distance and typo tolerance configuration.

title: Find search endpoints with no query length or complexity limits
prompt: Identify search endpoints that accept unbounded query strings or unlimited boolean operator depth. A query with thousands of terms or deeply nested boolean clauses can exhaust search engine memory and slow all concurrent searches. Recommend query length limits, clause depth limits, and complexity-based rejection.
prompt_short: Find search endpoints vulnerable to complex query denial of service.
prompt_filtered: Search resource limits only. Query length and boolean complexity caps.

title: Evaluate search analytics for query intent and zero-result rates
prompt: Assess whether search queries and their result counts are logged for analysis. Find the most common queries returning zero results — they represent unmet user intent. Find queries returning many results with low click-through rate — they indicate poor relevance. Recommend a search analytics dashboard with actionable metrics.
prompt_short: Find zero-result queries and low click-through searches from analytics.
prompt_filtered: Search analytics only. Zero-result rate and click-through rate gaps.

title: Assess geo-search configuration and bounding box accuracy
prompt: For applications with location-based search, review the geo-index configuration. Verify geo fields are mapped correctly, bounding box and radius queries return expected results, and the precision of the geo grid is appropriate for the distance tolerance required by the product. Find geo queries using bounding boxes where they intersect unexpectedly due to coordinate system assumptions.
prompt_short: Audit geo-search field mapping and bounding box correctness.
prompt_filtered: Geo-search only. Field mapping and spatial query accuracy.

title: Check scroll and pagination behavior for deep result sets
prompt: Identify search endpoints using OFFSET-based pagination on large result sets. Deep offsets in search engines like Elasticsearch are extremely expensive and slow at page 100+. Find endpoints without cursor-based or search_after pagination that would degrade significantly at depth. Recommend search_after or keyset pagination for all deep-paginated search paths.
prompt_short: Find search endpoints using OFFSET pagination that breaks at deep pages.
prompt_filtered: Search pagination only. OFFSET-based deep pagination performance.

title: Identify search warming requirements for cold index replicas
prompt: Assess whether search replicas are warmed before receiving traffic after a restart or scale-up event. A cold replica with no OS page cache will serve high-latency queries until the working set is cached in memory. Identify the highest-read indexes and recommend a pre-warm query sequence run before a new replica is added to the load balancer.
prompt_short: Find search replicas added to rotation without pre-warming that spike latency.
prompt_filtered: Index warming only. Cold replica traffic and page cache pre-loading.

title: Review highlighting and snippet generation for performance impact
prompt: Assess whether search result highlighting and snippet extraction are enabled. Highlighting re-analyzes result documents at query time and can be significantly more expensive than the search itself on large fields. Find endpoints enabling highlighting on large text fields without field size limits. Recommend highlighting limits and pre-stored term vectors to reduce per-query cost.
prompt_short: Find search highlighting configurations that add significant query-time cost.
prompt_filtered: Highlighting performance only. Snippet generation cost on large fields.

title: Check Search Result Security Filtering
prompt: Check Search Result Security Filtering

title: Evaluate search result relevance for the five most common user queries
prompt: Evaluate search result relevance for the five most common user queries. Identify which queries return irrelevant or missing results. For each, diagnose whether the root cause is index staleness, poor tokenization, missing synonyms, or a query construction bug.

title: Audit the search index update pipeline
prompt: Audit the search index update pipeline. Identify the maximum lag between a data change and that change appearing in search results. Find any failure mode where data updates are lost from the index permanently. Recommend a reconciliation strategy to detect and repair index drift.

title: Assess search infrastructure redundancy
prompt: Assess search infrastructure redundancy. Identify whether the search backend has a standby node or replica. Describe what happens to the user experience if the primary search node goes down. Recommend the minimum redundancy configuration that achieves acceptable availability.

title: Audit the search relevance ranking algorithm for the top ten most common
prompt: Audit the search relevance ranking algorithm for the top ten most common user queries. For each query, assess whether the top result is the most relevant by any reasonable metric. Identify ranking factors that are missing such as recency, popularity, user affinity, and exact match boost. Recommend specific scoring adjustments for the highest-impact queries. Estimate the change in user engagement metrics such as click-through rate and zero-result rate from applying the recommended adjustments.

title: Review the search index design for scalability
prompt: Review the search index design for scalability. Identify the largest indexed collections and estimate their growth rate over the next 12 months. Assess whether the current index structure will support that volume without performance degradation. Find index fields with high cardinality that are used in filters but not indexed, causing full scans. Recommend index optimizations such as compound indexes, partial indexes, or sharding strategies that will maintain sub-100ms query latency at projected growth.

title: Identify every search query that could return results the requesting use
prompt: Identify every search query that could return results the requesting user is not authorized to see. For each, verify that authorization filtering is applied before results are returned and not as a client-side filter after the results are received. Find queries where the search engine returns all matching documents and the application then filters by permission, leaking the existence of restricted documents through result count or timing. Recommend pre-filtering at the query level using the user's permission set as a query constraint.

title: Evaluate the search user experience for common failure modes
prompt: Evaluate the search user experience for common failure modes. Test queries with zero results and assess whether the zero-result page provides useful recovery options such as did-you-mean, related categories, or popular searches. Find queries that return too many results with no effective way to narrow them due to missing facets, filters, or sort options. Identify queries with no typo tolerance that fail on minor misspellings. Assess whether the search ranking degrades gracefully when relevance signals are incomplete or missing.

title: Assess the operational monitoring of the search layer
prompt: Assess the operational monitoring of the search layer. Identify which search metrics are currently collected such as query latency by percentile, zero-result rate, index size, replication lag, and query error rate. Find search paths with no latency alarm. Identify queries that are slow but have no query plan analysis or index optimization in place. Assess whether the search index can be rebuilt from the primary data store without downtime. Recommend a minimum monitoring standard for the search layer with specific alert thresholds for each key metric.

title: Evaluate the search system end to end for production readiness
prompt: Evaluate the search system end to end for production readiness. Identify what data is indexed, how the index is built, and how frequently it is updated. Assess whether index updates are synchronous or asynchronous and what the maximum acceptable lag is. Look for failure modes where data mutations do not propagate to the index, causing stale search results. Identify any queries that are not covered by the current index schema and would result in full scans. Evaluate the tokenization, stemming, and synonym configuration for the primary user search flows. Assess whether the search backend can handle the current peak query load and what the throughput ceiling is. Summarize the top five search correctness and reliability risks.

title: Review the search infrastructure for reliability and operational readine
prompt: Review the search infrastructure for reliability and operational readiness. Determine whether the search backend runs as a single instance or with replication. Identify the recovery path if the primary search node becomes unavailable — whether search degrades gracefully or fails completely. Check whether index corruption is detected automatically and whether re-indexing is a documented and tested procedure. Assess whether search query latency is monitored and alerted on separately from application latency. Find any search query pattern that could be used for denial of service — unbounded wildcards, deeply nested boolean queries, or expensive fuzzy matching. Recommend the infrastructure improvements that would most increase search reliability in the next six months.

title: You are a search engineering consultant reviewing a production search im
prompt: You are a search engineering consultant reviewing a production search implementation for quality, performance, and correctness. Begin by auditing the index design including which fields are indexed, which are stored, and which are used for ranking. Identify fields that are searched frequently but not indexed, causing full collection scans. Assess whether the relevance ranking algorithm is appropriate for the use case, whether it weights recency, popularity, exact match, and semantic similarity in proportions that match user intent. Test the top 20 most common queries and evaluate whether the first result is the correct result. Find queries returning zero results that could be rescued by typo tolerance, synonym expansion, or stemming. Assess whether faceted filtering is available for the dimensions users most frequently want to filter on. Identify any gap between what users search for and what the current index supports. Evaluate the index update latency, the delay between a document being created or updated in the primary store and it becoming searchable. Conclude with a search improvement roadmap prioritizing changes by impact on user-visible relevance and query success rate.

title: Evaluate the performance and scalability of the search implementation un
prompt: Evaluate the performance and scalability of the search implementation under production load. Identify the slowest search queries by p99 latency and analyze their query plans. Find queries performing full collection scans because a required index is missing. Assess whether the search infrastructure is sized for the current query volume and the projected 12-month growth in index size and query rate. Look for inefficient query patterns such as wildcard prefix searches, unbounded regex queries, or deeply nested aggregations that are computationally expensive regardless of index coverage. Assess whether query caching is in place for popular repeated queries with stable results. Identify whether the search infrastructure has hot spots where certain shards or replicas receive disproportionate traffic. Evaluate the index replication configuration for high availability. Recommend specific query optimizations, index additions, and infrastructure changes that would improve p99 search latency by the largest margin.

title: Audit the security design of the search layer with focus on authorizatio
prompt: Audit the security design of the search layer with focus on authorization enforcement and data exposure. Identify every search endpoint and assess whether it enforces document-level access control, filtering results to documents the requesting user is authorized to see. Find any search path where authorization is applied after results are returned, allowing an attacker to infer the existence of restricted documents from result counts or response timing. Look for admin search interfaces that expose internal fields such as user IDs, internal flags, and soft-deleted records that should not be visible to regular users. Identify search endpoints accessible without authentication that could be used to enumerate user-generated content or discover private data. Assess whether the search index includes fields that contain sensitive data such as email addresses, phone numbers, and payment information that should not be stored in the search layer. Recommend the specific query-level authorization changes, field-level access controls, and index schema changes required to close the highest-risk data exposure paths.

title: Audit all Dockerfiles for security and size issues
prompt: Review every Dockerfile in the project. Flag base images that are not pinned, unnecessary packages, root user in production, and layers that inflate image size unnecessarily. Prioritize by security impact.
prompt_short: Audit all Dockerfiles for security risks, unpinned images, and size bloat.
prompt_filtered: Dockerfile analysis only. No orchestration or runtime concerns.

title: Find containers running as root unnecessarily
prompt: Identify all containers that run as the root user. For each, assess whether root is required or if a non-root user would work. Provide the specific USER directive needed to fix each one.
prompt_short: Find all containers running as root and verify if root is required.
prompt_filtered: User privilege only. Flag root containers with fix.

title: Review container resource limits and requests
prompt: Check that every container has appropriate CPU and memory limits configured. Identify containers with no limits (noisy neighbor risk) and containers with limits too low (causing OOM kills or throttling).
prompt_short: Check resource limits on all containers for noisy-neighbor and OOM risks.
prompt_filtered: Resource limits only. CPU and memory configuration.

title: Assess container startup and health check configuration
prompt: Review all container health check configurations. Identify containers with no health check, health checks that are too slow to detect real failures, or readiness probes that allow traffic before the app is ready.
prompt_short: Review health check configuration for all containers in the stack.
prompt_filtered: Health checks only. Liveness and readiness probes.

title: Identify shared mutable volumes between containers
prompt: Find all shared volumes between containers. For each, identify the write pattern, assess the risk of concurrent writes, and verify there is no sensitive data written to volumes that persist beyond the container lifecycle.
prompt_short: Find shared volumes between containers and assess concurrent write risks.
prompt_filtered: Volume sharing only. Concurrency and data security focus.

title: Audit container image multi-stage build usage
prompt: Find Dockerfiles that do not use multi-stage builds. Identify images that copy build tools, test dependencies, or intermediate build artifacts into the final production image, inflating its size and attack surface. Recommend multi-stage patterns that produce the smallest possible final image.
prompt_short: Find Dockerfiles missing multi-stage builds that bloat production images.
prompt_filtered: Multi-stage build only. Build dependency leakage into final image.

title: Review cgroup resource limits for CPU throttling and OOM risk
prompt: Verify that CPU and memory limits are set via cgroup-enforced container limits rather than application-level configuration alone. Find containers where the memory limit is set below peak observed usage, causing periodic OOM kills. Identify containers with no CPU limit contributing to noisy-neighbor interference on shared nodes.
prompt_short: Find containers with cgroup limits causing OOM kills or noisy-neighbor CPU.
prompt_filtered: cgroup resource limits only. OOM kill and CPU throttle risk.

title: Identify containers missing graceful shutdown handling
prompt: Find containers that do not handle SIGTERM before receiving SIGKILL. A container ignoring SIGTERM will be forcibly killed after the grace period, dropping in-flight requests and leaving locks or transactions open. Verify each container catches SIGTERM, drains in-flight work, and exits cleanly within the configured grace period.
prompt_short: Find containers that do not handle SIGTERM and are forcibly killed.
prompt_filtered: Graceful shutdown only. SIGTERM handling and drain period.

title: Assess container image provenance and supply chain security
prompt: Identify all base images used across Dockerfiles. For each, verify the image is pulled from a trusted registry, the tag is pinned by digest rather than mutable tag, and the image publisher is a recognized vendor. Find any base image that is community-published or unpinned, creating supply chain risk.
prompt_short: Find unpinned or untrusted base images that create supply chain risk.
prompt_filtered: Image provenance only. Registry trust and digest pinning.

title: Review container network mode and host network exposure
prompt: Identify any container using host network mode or host PID/IPC namespaces. Host network mode removes container network isolation, allowing the container to bind ports on the host and access all host network interfaces. Find containers in host network mode that do not have a documented security justification.
prompt_short: Find containers using host network mode that removes network isolation.
prompt_filtered: Network mode only. Host namespace sharing and exposure risk.

title: Find containers with privileged mode or excessive capabilities
prompt: Identify containers running with privileged: true or with Linux capabilities beyond the minimum required. Privileged containers can bypass all kernel security boundaries. Find containers requesting SYS_ADMIN, NET_ADMIN, or other powerful capabilities they do not need. Recommend dropping all capabilities and adding only the specific ones required.
prompt_short: Find privileged containers and unnecessary capability grants.
prompt_filtered: Privilege escalation only. Privileged mode and capability grants.

title: Audit container secret injection methods for security gaps
prompt: Identify how secrets are injected into containers — environment variables, mounted secret volumes, runtime secret managers. Find any container with secrets baked into the image layer or passed as build args. Assess whether secrets in environment variables are accessible via process inspection or debug endpoints.
prompt_short: Find secrets in image layers or build args that violate secret hygiene.
prompt_filtered: Secret injection only. Build-time secret leakage into images.

title: Evaluate container restart policy and crash loop detection
prompt: Review the restart policy configured for each container — always, on-failure, unless-stopped. Identify containers in a crash-loop with no circuit breaker to pause restarts and alert. Find containers where a startup failure triggers infinite restart attempts that mask the root cause in logs.
prompt_short: Find containers in crash loops with no alerting or restart backoff.
prompt_filtered: Restart policy only. Crash loop detection and alerting.

title: Check container init process handling for zombie processes
prompt: Identify containers without a proper init process — like tini or dumb-init — that is responsible for reaping zombie processes. When a container runs a shell or spawns child processes that exit, an init process is needed to harvest them. Find containers where zombie processes accumulate over time, consuming PID table entries.
prompt_short: Find containers missing an init process that causes zombie process accumulation.
prompt_filtered: Init process only. Zombie reaping and PID table exhaustion.

title: Identify containers with read-write root filesystems unnecessarily
prompt: Find containers whose root filesystem is mounted read-write when the application only writes to specific paths. A read-only root filesystem with explicit writable mounts reduces the attack surface — a compromised container cannot modify application binaries or system files. Identify containers where adding readOnlyRootFilesystem: true would be safe.
prompt_short: Find containers with writable root filesystems that should be read-only.
prompt_filtered: Root filesystem mode only. Read-only root filesystem candidates.

title: Assess container image scanning integration in the CI pipeline
prompt: Verify that container image scanning for known CVEs is integrated into the CI/CD pipeline and blocks deployment on critical findings. Find pipelines that build and push images without scanning, or that scan but do not gate on severity. Recommend a scanning threshold — block on CRITICAL, warn on HIGH — with a documented exception process.
prompt_short: Find CI pipelines building containers without CVE scanning or severity gates.
prompt_filtered: Image scanning integration only. CVE gate in CI pipeline.

title: Review container log aggregation and stdout/stderr handling
prompt: Verify that all container logs are written to stdout and stderr rather than files, making them accessible to the container runtime's log driver. Find containers writing logs to files inside the container with no log rotation, risking disk exhaustion. Assess whether logs are aggregated to a centralized store and retained for the required period.
prompt_short: Find containers writing logs to files instead of stdout, risking disk exhaustion.
prompt_filtered: Log output only. stdout vs file logging and log rotation.

prompt: Audit Image Layer Caching

title: Check Base Image Pinning
prompt: Check Base Image Pinning

title: Audit all container images for base image age and known CVEs
prompt: Audit all container images for base image age and known CVEs. Identify containers running images older than 90 days. For each, assess CVE exposure and prioritize by internet-facing risk level.

title: Find all shared volumes between containers
prompt: Find all shared volumes between containers. For each, assess whether the sharing is intentional and documented. Identify any shared volume allowing one container to read or corrupt data belonging to another service.

title: Review CPU and memory resource limits across all containers
prompt: Review CPU and memory resource limits across all containers. Identify containers with no limits. Find containers whose limits are below actual peak usage, causing OOM kills. Recommend limits matching observed utilization with safe headroom.

title: Audit all container images for security hardening
prompt: Audit all container images for security hardening. Identify images running as root. Find images using a mutable base tag instead of a pinned digest. Identify packages installed in the final image that are only needed for the build stage. Recommend a multi-stage build structure that separates build dependencies from runtime dependencies. Calculate the reduction in final image size and attack surface from applying each recommendation.

title: Review all container resource configurations
prompt: Review all container resource configurations. Identify pods with no CPU or memory limits set. Find pods with no liveness or readiness probes configured. Identify pods running with privileged security context or host network access that is not explicitly required. Recommend minimum resource limits based on observed consumption and propose probe configurations that accurately reflect each container's startup and health behavior.

title: Evaluate the secrets injection strategy for all containerized services
prompt: Evaluate the secrets injection strategy for all containerized services. Identify services that receive secrets as environment variables baked into the container image or set in a ConfigMap rather than a Secret resource. Find secrets logged by entrypoint scripts or startup commands. Assess whether secrets are rotated without requiring a container rebuild or redeployment. Recommend a secrets injection approach using the platform's native secret store for each service that currently uses an insecure injection method.

title: Assess the image vulnerability posture across all container images in pr
prompt: Assess the image vulnerability posture across all container images in production. Identify images that have not been rebuilt from a fresh base in more than 30 days. Find images with known critical or high CVEs in their package layer. Assess whether a vulnerability scanner runs in CI before images are promoted to production. Recommend a rebuild cadence and CI gate that ensures no critical CVEs reach the production registry. Identify the five most vulnerable images by CVE count and severity.

title: Review the container networking configuration for isolation and attack s
prompt: Review the container networking configuration for isolation and attack surface reduction. Identify containers with ports exposed that are not required by any consumer. Find container-to-container communication that does not traverse a service mesh or network policy, allowing unrestricted lateral movement. Identify containers on the default bridge network without network isolation. Recommend network policies that apply a default-deny posture with explicit allow rules per service pair. Assess whether the external-facing attack surface is limited to intentionally exposed ingress points.

title: Audit the containerization strategy for the entire system
prompt: Audit the containerization strategy for the entire system. Inventory every container image, its base image, its pinned version, and when it was last updated. Identify any image running with a known critical CVE. Check whether multi-stage builds minimize image size and attack surface. Find containers running as root with no user override. Evaluate whether CPU and memory limits are set and whether they reflect actual production utilization. Assess health check configuration — liveness and readiness probes — for every container, noting any without a probe. Summarize the top five container security and resource management gaps ordered by risk.

title: Evaluate container isolation and data separation across the system
prompt: Evaluate container isolation and data separation across the system. Identify all shared volumes and assess whether cross-container access is intentional and least-privilege. Look for volumes exposing secrets, application code, or database files to more containers than necessary. Check whether network policies restrict inter-container communication to only required connections. Review whether each container follows the single-responsibility principle. Recommend the isolation and health check changes with the highest security and reliability impact.

title: You are a container platform engineer reviewing the containerization str
prompt: You are a container platform engineer reviewing the containerization strategy of a production system for security, efficiency, and operational readiness. Begin by auditing every container image in use including the base image, the build process, the final image size, and the installed packages. Identify images using mutable base image tags that could change between builds, creating non-reproducible deployments. Find images not built with multi-stage builds that include build toolchains in the production image, increasing attack surface and image size. Assess whether all containers run as a non-root user and flag any running as root or with a UID of zero. Review the Dockerfile for secrets baked into image layers such as build arguments that contain credentials or COPY instructions that include credential files. Identify unnecessary packages installed at the OS layer that add CVE exposure without adding functionality. Evaluate the image scanning process, whether vulnerabilities are detected in CI before promotion to production and whether critical CVEs block deployment. Conclude with an image hardening roadmap ranked by security impact, with specific Dockerfile changes for each recommendation.

title: Evaluate the runtime security posture of all containerized workloads
prompt: Evaluate the runtime security posture of all containerized workloads. Assess whether each container runs with the minimum capabilities required, using cap_drop: ALL and selectively adding only the specific Linux capabilities the process needs. Find containers with privileged set to true without a documented technical justification. Identify containers with host network or host PID namespace access that bypasses container isolation. Review whether containers mount host filesystem paths unnecessarily, particularly sensitive paths. Assess whether read-only root filesystem is enforced for containers that do not need to write to disk. Review the seccomp and AppArmor or SELinux profile configuration, whether the default profile is enforced or whether containers run without a syscall restriction profile. Find containers that have been granted write access to cloud provider credentials through mounted service account tokens or environment variables. Recommend the specific security context changes that would eliminate the highest-risk privilege escalation paths.

title: Audit the operational readiness of the container infrastructure from the
prompt: Audit the operational readiness of the container infrastructure from the perspective of an on-call engineer responding to an incident. Assess whether every container has a correctly configured liveness probe that causes the orchestrator to restart it on hang, and a readiness probe that removes it from load balancer rotation before it is ready to serve traffic. Find containers with startup times longer than the readiness probe initial delay, causing premature probe failures and restart loops during normal deployments. Evaluate the resource limit and request configuration, whether limits are set to prevent one container starving others and whether requests are calibrated to actual consumption. Identify pods with no pod disruption budget, making rolling updates unsafe during high-traffic periods. Review the logging configuration, whether container stdout and stderr are captured, structured, and shipped to a centralized log aggregation system. Assess whether container restart reasons are visible in the monitoring dashboard or require direct cluster access to diagnose. Recommend the specific probe, resource, and logging configuration changes that would reduce mean time to detect and resolve container-related incidents.

title: Audit serverless functions for cold start performance
prompt: Identify serverless functions with slow cold starts. Assess bundle size, initialization logic, and imported dependencies. Recommend specific optimizations to reduce cold start time for latency-sensitive paths.
prompt_short: Find serverless functions with slow cold starts and how to fix them.
prompt_filtered: Cold start analysis only. Bundle size and init logic.

title: Find functions violating edge runtime constraints
prompt: Identify serverless functions that import Node.js-only APIs (fs, crypto, net) that are incompatible with edge runtimes like Cloudflare Workers or Vercel Edge Functions. List each violation with the correct Web API replacement.
prompt_short: Find Node.js API usage that breaks in edge runtime environments.
prompt_filtered: Runtime compatibility only. Flag Node-only APIs with Web API alternatives.

title: Identify functions with missing timeout configuration
prompt: Review all serverless functions for timeout configuration. Identify functions with no timeout (vulnerable to indefinite hangs) and functions whose timeout is shorter than their slowest valid operation.
prompt_short: Find serverless functions with missing or misconfigured timeouts.
prompt_filtered: Timeout configuration only. Flag missing and inappropriate values.

title: Find stateful patterns that break serverless execution
prompt: Identify code patterns that assume persistent state across invocations — in-memory caches, module-level singletons, file system writes. For each, explain why it's unreliable in serverless and recommend a stateless alternative.
prompt_short: Find stateful patterns that are unreliable across serverless invocations.
prompt_filtered: Stateless compliance only. Module-level and in-memory state risks.

title: Assess serverless cost and invocation efficiency
prompt: Analyze invocation frequency, execution duration, and memory allocation across all functions. Identify the highest-cost functions and opportunities to reduce cost through right-sizing, batching, or caching upstream.
prompt_short: Find the highest-cost functions and opportunities to reduce invocations.
prompt_filtered: Cost efficiency only. Duration, memory, and invocation count.

title: Identify functions that should be provisioned for concurrency
prompt: Find latency-sensitive serverless functions that serve user-facing requests and are vulnerable to cold starts. For each, assess whether provisioned concurrency — keeping warm instances allocated — is justified by the latency SLO. Estimate the cost increase versus the latency improvement for the top candidates.
prompt_short: Find user-facing functions where provisioned concurrency would meet latency SLOs.
prompt_filtered: Provisioned concurrency candidates only. Cost vs latency trade-off.

title: Review function IAM permissions for least-privilege compliance
prompt: Audit the IAM role or execution role attached to every serverless function. Identify functions with over-broad permissions — actions like s3:* or dynamodb:* on * resources — rather than the minimum permissions needed. Find functions with AdministratorAccess or PowerUser that represent an escalation risk if exploited.
prompt_short: Find serverless functions with over-broad IAM permissions.
prompt_filtered: IAM permissions only. Least-privilege violations for function execution roles.

title: Find functions with synchronous fan-out creating latency amplification
prompt: Identify serverless functions that make multiple synchronous downstream calls in sequence or in parallel before returning a response. Sequential fan-out accumulates latency of all calls. Parallel fan-out consumes concurrent execution capacity. Recommend event-driven fan-out for non-blocking downstream calls.
prompt_short: Find synchronous fan-out patterns in functions that amplify latency.
prompt_filtered: Fan-out pattern only. Synchronous multi-call latency accumulation.

title: Assess function deployment package size and tree-shaking
prompt: Identify functions with large deployment packages — over 10MB unzipped — caused by importing entire libraries when only a subset is used. Find imports of large SDKs, lodash without tree-shaking, or bundling unused dependencies. Recommend tree-shaking, selective imports, and Lambda Layers for shared large dependencies.
prompt_short: Find functions with oversized deployment packages due to unused imports.
prompt_filtered: Bundle size only. Unused dependency and tree-shaking opportunities.

title: Evaluate retry behavior on event-triggered functions
prompt: Find all event-triggered serverless functions — from SQS, SNS, S3, EventBridge. Verify each is idempotent, because event sources typically retry on failure. Identify functions where a retry would cause duplicate side effects — double inserts, duplicate notifications, double charges. Flag each with the idempotency key strategy needed.
prompt_short: Verify event-triggered functions handle retries without duplicate side effects.
prompt_filtered: Event-trigger idempotency only. Duplicate side effects on function retry.

title: Identify functions making VPC calls that increase cold start time
prompt: Find serverless functions deployed inside a VPC. VPC functions have significantly higher cold start latency due to ENI attachment. Identify functions using VPC placement only for internet access or for services that could be accessed via a VPC endpoint instead. Recommend removing VPC placement where it is not strictly necessary.
prompt_short: Find functions with unnecessary VPC placement that inflates cold start time.
prompt_filtered: VPC placement only. Functions with VPC for non-VPC-required resources.

title: Review environment variable size and secret manager integration
prompt: Audit all serverless function environment variables. Identify functions with hardcoded secret values in environment variables visible in the deployment console. Verify that secrets are retrieved from a secrets manager at runtime rather than baked into the function configuration. Find functions approaching provider limits on total environment variable size.
prompt_short: Find secrets in function environment variables that should use a secrets manager.
prompt_filtered: Function secrets only. Hardcoded values vs secrets manager integration.

title: Assess concurrent execution limits and throttling behavior
prompt: Identify the concurrent execution limit for each function and for the account overall. Find functions without a reserved concurrency limit that could consume the full account concurrency pool during a traffic spike, throttling all other functions. Recommend reserved concurrency allocations for critical functions.
prompt_short: Find functions that could exhaust account concurrency and throttle all others.
prompt_filtered: Concurrency limits only. Reserved vs unreserved concurrency allocation.

title: Find serverless functions with no observability instrumentation
prompt: Identify functions that emit no custom metrics, no distributed trace spans, and no structured logs beyond default invocation logs. Find functions where a failure would be invisible until a downstream system reports an error. Recommend minimum instrumentation — invocation count, error rate, p99 duration — for all functions.
prompt_short: Find functions with no custom metrics, traces, or structured logs.
prompt_filtered: Observability gaps only. Functions invisible to monitoring.

title: Evaluate function versioning and alias-based deployment strategy
prompt: Assess whether functions use versioned deployments with aliases for blue/green or canary rollouts. Find functions deployed directly to $LATEST with no versioned alias, making rollback a full redeployment. Recommend an alias-based deployment model with traffic shifting for canary releases.
prompt_short: Find functions deployed to latest with no alias-based rollback path.
prompt_filtered: Function versioning only. Alias-based deployment and rollback strategy.

title: Identify functions sharing data through filesystem or tmp storage
prompt: Find functions that write data to /tmp as a way to pass data between invocations or between function instances. /tmp is per-invocation ephemeral storage — it is not shared across concurrent instances and not guaranteed to persist between warm invocations. Identify patterns relying on /tmp for inter-invocation communication that silently fail in concurrent scenarios.
prompt_short: Find functions using /tmp for inter-invocation state that fails under concurrency.
prompt_filtered: /tmp usage only. Ephemeral storage misused for shared state.

title: Assess function composition patterns and workflow orchestration
prompt: Find multi-step workflows implemented as chained Lambda or serverless function calls — where one function invokes another synchronously. Identify workflows that would be better expressed as orchestrated state machines — AWS Step Functions or Temporal — with built-in retry, error handling, and visibility. Assess the longest chain depth for latency and error propagation risk.
prompt_short: Find chained function calls that should be a managed workflow orchestrator.
prompt_filtered: Function chaining only. Orchestration candidates for state machine migration.
