title: Audit every WebSocket connection in the system for reconnection correctn
prompt: Audit every WebSocket connection in the system for reconnection correctness. Verify exponential backoff with jitter is applied, reconnection has a stop condition, and state is reconciled after reconnect. Find connections that miss events during the reconnect gap and recommend a sequence-number or replay strategy.

title: Identify all real-time channels or topics and verify subscription author
prompt: Identify all real-time channels or topics and verify subscription authorization is enforced server-side. Find any channel where the name is guessable and authorization is not checked, allowing a subscriber to receive events meant for another user or tenant.

title: Assess real-time fan-out scalability for the highest-subscriber event ty
prompt: Assess real-time fan-out scalability for the highest-subscriber event type. Find whether delivery is sequential or parallel. Recommend a pub/sub broker architecture that maintains sub-100ms delivery at 10x subscriber count.

title: Find all real-time features and verify each has a graceful degradation p
prompt: Find all real-time features and verify each has a graceful degradation path when the transport is unavailable. For each feature missing a fallback, design a polling interval that maintains acceptable freshness without overwhelming the API.

title: Identify every WebSocket or SSE connection in the system with no heartbe
prompt: Identify every WebSocket or SSE connection in the system with no heartbeat or keepalive mechanism. For each, assess the risk of a silent connection drop where the client and server both believe the connection is active but no messages are flowing. Find connections where a silent drop would leave the client displaying stale data without a visual indicator. Recommend heartbeat intervals and reconnection strategies for each connection type. Verify that the heartbeat implementation detects both client-side and server-side silent drops.

title: Audit all real-time room or channel implementations for cleanup correctn
prompt: Audit all real-time room or channel implementations for cleanup correctness. Identify rooms that are created on connection but never explicitly destroyed when all members disconnect. Find rooms that accumulate state such as message history, presence lists, and typing indicators without a maximum size limit or TTL. Assess the memory growth rate if rooms are created at the current rate without cleanup. Recommend cleanup triggers such as last-member-disconnect, TTL-based expiry, or explicit close message for each room type.

title: Evaluate the authentication and authorization enforcement on WebSocket u
prompt: Evaluate the authentication and authorization enforcement on WebSocket upgrade requests. Identify WS endpoints that validate the authentication token at upgrade time but do not re-validate on subsequent messages, allowing a token to expire while the connection remains active. Find WS endpoints that pass authentication credentials as query parameters where they appear in server access logs in plaintext. Assess whether authorization is enforced per-message for connections that can perform multiple distinct operations. Recommend an authentication re-validation strategy for long-lived connections.

title: Review the message ordering and delivery guarantee design for all real-t
prompt: Review the message ordering and delivery guarantee design for all real-time channels. Identify channels where clients assume strict message ordering but the server delivers from multiple producers with no sequence number. Find channels where reconnection after a network gap causes missed messages with no catch-up mechanism to replay events that occurred during the disconnection. Assess whether message sequence numbers or cursor-based catch-up is implemented for channels where missed messages cause incorrect client state. Recommend a delivery guarantee and catch-up strategy appropriate for each channel's data criticality.

title: Assess backpressure handling for high-frequency real-time event streams
prompt: Assess backpressure handling for high-frequency real-time event streams. Identify channels where the server pushes events to clients at a rate the client may not be able to process, causing client-side queue buildup or dropped messages. Find server implementations that have no per-connection send queue limit, allowing a slow client to accumulate an unbounded backlog that consumes server memory. Assess whether clients implement any form of flow control. Recommend backpressure strategies such as client-side debouncing, server-side send queue limits, and adaptive event rate based on client acknowledgment rate for the highest-frequency event streams.

title: Evaluate the entire real-time layer for reliability and correctness
prompt: Evaluate the entire real-time layer for reliability and correctness. Inventory every WebSocket connection, SSE endpoint, and long-polling route in the system. For each, verify that reconnection is automatic with exponential backoff and that state is reconciled after reconnect to prevent missed events. Assess whether subscription authorization is enforced server-side for all channels. Find channels where guessable names allow unauthorized subscriptions. Identify real-time event streams with no ordering guarantee used by clients that assume order. Assess whether presence tracking correctly removes users on unclean disconnects. Find fan-out paths that would bottleneck under a large simultaneous subscriber burst. Summarize the top five real-time reliability and security gaps.

title: Assess the scalability of the real-time infrastructure at 10x current co
prompt: Assess the scalability of the real-time infrastructure at 10x current connection count. Identify whether WebSocket servers require sticky sessions for message routing — if so, adding nodes does not horizontally scale the broadcast path. Find whether a cross-node pub/sub broker is in use or whether scaling requires every node to hold every connection. Evaluate the memory footprint per connection including Durable Object state, subscription registries, and per-connection buffers, and project the memory cost at 10x load. Identify the first component to saturate under connection growth. Recommend the architectural changes that enable horizontal scaling of the real-time layer with no sticky session requirement.

title: You are a real-time systems engineer reviewing all WebSocket and SSE imp
prompt: You are a real-time systems engineer reviewing all WebSocket and SSE implementations in a production application. Begin by enumerating every real-time connection type, the events each carries, the expected connection duration, and the maximum concurrent connections at peak load. For each connection type, assess the authentication mechanism at connection establishment and whether authentication is re-validated during the connection lifetime for long-lived connections where tokens may expire. Review the heartbeat and keepalive configuration, whether both the client and server can detect a silent connection drop, and whether the reconnection logic handles all disconnect scenarios. Identify connections with no reconnection logic that leave the client in a permanently disconnected state without a visible error. Assess whether missed messages during a reconnection gap are recovered through a catch-up mechanism or silently lost. Evaluate the per-connection memory footprint and whether the total memory usage scales acceptably with the expected peak connection count. Conclude with a real-time infrastructure readiness assessment covering authentication, heartbeat, reconnection, message delivery, and memory scalability.

title: Evaluate the scalability of the real-time infrastructure
prompt: Evaluate the scalability of the real-time infrastructure. Assess whether the WebSocket server is stateless or stateful, whether a client that reconnects to a different server instance loses its session context or can resume seamlessly. Identify state that must be shared across server instances such as room membership, presence data, and in-flight message queues, and assess whether the current state sharing mechanism scales to the expected peak connection count. Find room or channel implementations that store message history in memory rather than a persistent store, losing history on server restart. Assess the fan-out performance for broadcast events, whether a message sent to a high-membership room creates a write-per-connection bottleneck that degrades under load. Evaluate the infrastructure configuration for horizontal scaling, whether connection load balancing is session-aware or requires sticky routing. Identify the connection count ceiling of the current infrastructure and estimate when that ceiling will be reached at current growth. Recommend the specific state sharing, fan-out, and load balancing changes that would double the scalable connection capacity with the least architectural change.

title: Audit the security design of all real-time communication channels
prompt: Audit the security design of all real-time communication channels. Assess whether WebSocket upgrade requests validate the Origin header against an allowlist of known origins, preventing cross-origin WebSocket hijacking where a malicious page establishes a connection authenticated by the victim's cookies. Identify WS endpoints that accept authentication tokens as URL query parameters, which appear in server access logs and browser history. Verify that message payloads are validated on the server side for every received message and not just at connection time, preventing a client from sending malformed or oversized messages that crash the handler. Assess whether room membership authorization is enforced on every message, whether a client who joins room A can send messages to room B by crafting a message with a different room identifier. Identify connections with no rate limiting on message frequency, allowing a single client to flood the server or all other clients in a shared room. Review whether sensitive message content is encrypted end-to-end or only encrypted in transit. Recommend the specific Origin validation, token handling, message validation, and rate limiting changes that eliminate the highest-severity real-time security risks.

title: Audit CI pipeline for missing quality gates
prompt: Review the CI pipeline configuration. Identify missing checks — type checking, lint, unit tests, security scanning, license checks. Flag any merge path that bypasses required gates.
prompt_short: Find missing quality gates in the CI pipeline configuration.
prompt_filtered: CI gate coverage only. No deployment or runtime analysis.

title: Find the slowest CI jobs and bottlenecks
prompt: Analyze CI job durations. Identify the top 5 slowest jobs and why — missing parallelism, unoptimized test suites, large artifact downloads, unneeded cache invalidation. Estimate time saved per optimization.
prompt_short: Find the slowest CI jobs and the root cause of each delay.
prompt_filtered: CI performance only. Job duration and parallelism opportunities.

title: Identify flaky tests destabilizing the CI pipeline
prompt: Find tests that fail intermittently without code changes. Identify the root cause for each — timing dependencies, shared state, external service calls, race conditions. Prioritize by frequency and team trust impact.
prompt_short: Find all flaky tests in CI and identify the root cause of each.
prompt_filtered: Flaky tests only. Intermittent failures, not permanent failures.

title: Review CI secrets management and exposure risks
prompt: Audit how secrets are provided to CI jobs. Identify secrets printed to logs, passed as build args baked into images, or available to untrusted PR branches. Assess the blast radius if CI secrets were leaked.
prompt_short: Audit how secrets are handled in CI to prevent exposure.
prompt_filtered: Secrets in CI only. Log exposure, build arg risks, branch access.

title: Find PRs that can merge without CI passing
prompt: Review branch protection rules and required status checks. Identify any path that allows merging to main without all CI checks passing — admin bypass, missing required checks, or checks configured as optional.
prompt_short: Find all ways to merge code without passing CI checks.
prompt_filtered: Branch protection only. Required status check gaps.

title: Optimize CI cache strategy to cut build times
prompt: Audit the CI cache configuration across all jobs. Identify missing caches for package managers, build outputs, and Docker layers. Find cache keys that are too broad (causing stale hits) or too narrow (causing frequent misses). Estimate the build time reduction from each cache fix.
prompt_short: Find missing or misconfigured caches in CI that are wasting build time.
prompt_filtered: Cache configuration only. Package manager, build output, and Docker layer caches.

title: Design a matrix build strategy for multi-platform coverage
prompt: Assess which CI jobs should run in parallel across multiple OS, Node, or runtime version combinations. Identify test gaps caused by only running on one platform. Recommend a matrix strategy that maximizes coverage without multiplying total build cost.
prompt_short: Design a matrix build strategy for multi-platform and multi-version coverage.
prompt_filtered: Matrix builds only. Parallelization across OS and runtime versions.

title: Set up flaky test quarantine and detection pipeline
prompt: Design a system to automatically detect and quarantine flaky tests. Define the failure-rate threshold for quarantine, the re-evaluation period for re-entry, and the alert that notifies the owning team. Ensure quarantined tests cannot block merges but are still tracked.
prompt_short: Design a flaky test quarantine system that prevents merge blocks without losing signal.
prompt_filtered: Flaky test quarantine only. Detection threshold and re-entry criteria.

title: Audit CI runner costs and right-size compute
prompt: Review CI runner configurations across all workflows. Identify jobs using large runners for work that completes in under 2 minutes, jobs that could be parallelized to reduce wall time on smaller machines, and workflows that spin up runners for no-op paths. Estimate monthly cost savings.
prompt_short: Find CI jobs using oversized runners and estimate the cost of right-sizing.
prompt_filtered: Runner cost only. Compute size vs job duration and workload analysis.

title: Review artifact retention policies and storage cost
prompt: Audit what build artifacts are retained, for how long, and at what cost. Find artifacts kept longer than needed (coverage reports, test logs, build binaries). Recommend retention windows per artifact type that balance debugging needs with storage cost.
prompt_short: Audit CI artifact retention and find artifacts kept longer than needed.
prompt_filtered: Artifact retention only. Types, duration, and storage cost analysis.

title: Enforce dependency change review in CI
prompt: Add a CI check that fails when lockfiles change without a corresponding dependency audit step. Ensure every dependency addition or upgrade produces a diff in the audit log that a human must acknowledge. Identify workflows where dependency changes pass without any review gate.
prompt_short: Add a CI gate that flags unreviewed lockfile changes.
prompt_filtered: Dependency review gate only. Lockfile change detection in CI.

title: Validate CI pipeline configuration with schema linting
prompt: Set up CI configuration linting (actionlint for GitHub Actions, yamllint for generic YAML) to catch syntax errors, invalid action references, and deprecated syntax before they reach production. Identify the current pipeline configs lacking this validation.
prompt_short: Add schema linting for CI pipeline config files to catch errors before they run.
prompt_filtered: Config linting only. Pipeline YAML syntax and schema validation.

title: Add build reproducibility verification to CI
prompt: Assess whether CI builds are reproducible — same source produces identical output on every run. Identify sources of non-determinism: timestamps, random IDs, network fetches during build, and environment-variable leaks into build artifacts. Recommend hermetic build practices.
prompt_short: Verify CI builds are reproducible and find sources of non-determinism.
prompt_filtered: Build reproducibility only. Non-deterministic build inputs and outputs.

title: Audit notification and alerting on CI failure
prompt: Review how CI failures are communicated to the team. Find workflows with no failure notification, notifications going to a channel nobody watches, or notifications so noisy that they are ignored. Recommend targeted alerts by job type and severity.
prompt_short: Audit CI failure notifications for gaps and noise that cause failures to be ignored.
prompt_filtered: Failure alerting only. Notification routing and signal-to-noise ratio.

title: Map test parallelization opportunities to reduce total CI time
prompt: Analyze the test suite structure and identify test files that can be split across multiple CI workers. Calculate the optimal shard count to minimize total wall time without exceeding the parallelism budget. Identify tests with external dependencies that cannot be safely parallelized.
prompt_short: Find how to shard the test suite across CI workers to minimize wall time.
prompt_filtered: Test parallelization only. Shard strategy and external dependency constraints.

title: Implement status badge and pipeline health reporting
prompt: Add CI status badges to the README for all critical pipeline stages. Set up a pipeline health report that aggregates pass rate, average duration, and flakiness over the last 30 days per job. Send a weekly digest to the engineering team so trends are visible before they become incidents.
prompt_short: Add CI status badges and a weekly pipeline health digest for the team.
prompt_filtered: Pipeline reporting only. Status badges, pass rate, and duration trends.

title: Audit dependency install step for reproducibility and speed
prompt: Review how dependencies are installed in CI. Find pipelines using npm install (non-deterministic) instead of npm ci (lockfile-exact). Identify install steps that do not cache node_modules between runs. Measure the install step duration and estimate the time saved by combining frozen installs with layer caching.
prompt_short: Replace npm install with npm ci and add layer caching to reduce install time.
prompt_filtered: Dependency install only. npm ci adoption and install step caching.

title: Separate lint, type check, and test into parallel jobs
prompt: Audit whether lint, type checking, and tests run sequentially in one job. Splitting them into parallel jobs lets fast checks (lint) fail immediately while slower checks (integration tests) continue. Map the current sequential duration against the projected parallel wall time after splitting.
prompt_short: Split lint, type check, and tests into parallel CI jobs to reduce wall time.
prompt_filtered: Job parallelization only. Sequential vs parallel job structure comparison.

title: Add SAST scanning for security vulnerabilities in CI
prompt: Integrate a static application security testing (SAST) tool into the CI pipeline. Configure it to scan on every PR, fail on high-severity findings, and report results as inline PR comments. Identify the SAST tool best suited to this language and framework and the rule set that minimizes false positives.
prompt_short: Add SAST scanning to CI with PR comments and high-severity failure gates.
prompt_filtered: SAST only. Static security scanning integration and severity thresholds.

title: Set up pipeline change validation with a dry-run mode
prompt: Implement a mechanism to validate changes to the CI pipeline configuration before they land on main. Run a dry-run or syntax validation job that is triggered on any PR touching workflow files. Identify configurations where a pipeline change can only be tested by merging it to main.
prompt_short: Add dry-run validation for CI pipeline config changes before they reach main.
prompt_filtered: Pipeline config validation only. Workflow file change safety testing.

title: Fill in Missing Data Gaps
prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

title: Gain full understanding before completing
prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

title: Find What's Broken
prompt: Find What's Broken

title: Recommend Next Steps
prompt: Recommend Next Steps

title: Score for Production Readiness
prompt: Score for Production Readiness

title: Audit Pipeline Quality Gates
prompt: Audit Pipeline Quality Gates

title: Find Slowest CI Jobs
prompt: Find Slowest CI Jobs

title: Identify Flaky Tests
prompt: Identify Flaky Tests

title: Check Branch Protection Rules
prompt: Check Branch Protection Rules

title: Map Build Dependencies
prompt: Map Build Dependencies

title: Find Missing Test Coverage
prompt: Find Missing Test Coverage

title: Audit CI Cache Efficiency
prompt: Audit CI Cache Efficiency

title: Audit the CI pipeline for missing quality gates
prompt: Audit the CI pipeline for missing quality gates. Identify every merge path that can bypass required checks. Flag absent type-checking, lint, security scanning, or test steps. Order findings P0 to P3 by risk of shipping broken code. For each gap, specify the exact config file and change needed.

title: Find the slowest jobs in the CI pipeline
prompt: Find the slowest jobs in the CI pipeline. Identify the root cause for each — missing parallelism, large artifact downloads, or unoptimized test suites. Estimate time saved per optimization. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by time saved. Include the specific job name and the configuration change needed.

title: Identify all flaky tests destabilizing the CI pipeline
prompt: Identify all flaky tests destabilizing the CI pipeline. Determine the root cause of each — timing issues, shared state, or external service calls. Prioritize by failure frequency and team trust impact. Output a numbered list with root cause, affected test path, and recommended fix.

title: Review CI secrets management and exposure risks
prompt: Review CI secrets management and exposure risks. Find secrets that appear in logs, error messages, or build artifacts. Identify pipelines passing secrets as unmasked environment variables. Output findings in full paragraphs with secret type, exposure path, and remediation step.

title: Audit all ways to merge code without CI passing
prompt: Audit all ways to merge code without CI passing. Identify admin bypass paths, optional-check misconfigurations, and direct push access. For each bypass path, output the exact branch protection setting that closes it. Order by frequency of exploit in the git log.

title: Audit CI notification routing for signal-to-noise ratio
prompt: Audit CI notification routing for signal-to-noise ratio. Find jobs that send alerts to channels no one monitors actively. Identify alert fatigue patterns — builds that fail so often the team ignores the signal. Output a numbered list of notification gaps with the job name, current routing, and recommended escalation path.

title: Identify CI jobs that duplicate work already done in another job
prompt: Identify CI jobs that duplicate work already done in another job. Find linting, type checking, or test steps running in multiple workflows for the same codebase on the same commit. Estimate the per-commit compute waste from duplicated jobs. Output findings in full paragraphs with the job names, overlap description, and recommended consolidation approach.

title: Review CI permission scopes for principle of least privilege
prompt: Review CI permission scopes for principle of least privilege. Find jobs running with admin tokens when read-only tokens suffice, workflows that can access production secrets from a PR branch, and GITHUB_TOKEN permissions granted beyond what each job needs. Output a numbered list of over-permissioned steps with the job name, current permission, and minimum required permission.

title: Act as a senior platform engineer auditing this CI pipeline for producti
prompt: Act as a senior platform engineer auditing this CI pipeline for production readiness. Walk every job, every gate, and every merge path. Identify gaps where broken code could reach main — missing type checks, absent security scans, unprotected branches, or checks marked optional when they must be required. For each gap, state the job name, the risk it creates, a real example of what could slip through, and the one-line config change that closes it. Then assess the pipeline's performance: find the top three slowest jobs, identify parallelism opportunities, and estimate the total time savings achievable without changing the test suite. Output as a single report with an executive summary followed by numbered findings ordered by severity.

title: Evaluate this CI pipeline as if you are preparing it for a compliance au
prompt: Evaluate this CI pipeline as if you are preparing it for a compliance audit. Check that every merge to main requires passing checks, that no admin account can bypass protections, and that all secrets are masked in logs. Identify any job that runs with more permissions than needed. Check that test results are stored as artifacts and that failed pipelines produce actionable error messages. Verify that the pipeline configuration is version-controlled and reviewed like code. Flag any step that relies on an external service without a timeout or fallback. Output findings in full paragraphs grouped by risk category, with the highest-risk findings first.

title: Perform a full flakiness audit of this CI pipeline
prompt: Perform a full flakiness audit of this CI pipeline. Find every test that fails intermittently — defined as failing without a corresponding code change. For each flaky test, identify the root cause: timing dependency, global state pollution, external API call, or race condition. Estimate how many developer-hours per week are lost to flakiness triage and reruns. Recommend a remediation strategy for each cause category, not each individual test. Then identify the systemic changes — test isolation rules, retry policies, quarantine queues — that would eliminate recurrence. Output as a numbered remediation plan ordered by expected impact.

title: Act as a DevSecOps engineer auditing this CI pipeline for security and s
prompt: Act as a DevSecOps engineer auditing this CI pipeline for security and supply chain integrity. Evaluate every job for privilege escalation opportunities — workflows triggered by pull request events that can access organization secrets, jobs running with write permissions when read access suffices, and any step downloading external actions without pinning them to an immutable SHA. Find steps that execute user-controlled code in a privileged context. Assess whether the pipeline verifies artifact provenance before using it in a downstream step. Identify any code path where a fork-based pull request could exfiltrate secrets or modify production artifacts. Output a security-focused report with numbered findings ordered by exploitability, including the exact workflow file, job name, step name, and the minimum configuration change that eliminates each risk.

title: Evaluate the complete CI pipeline for developer experience quality
prompt: Evaluate the complete CI pipeline for developer experience quality. Measure how long a developer waits from pushing a commit to seeing a red or green signal. Identify the longest sequential chains of jobs that could be parallelized. Find jobs that output cryptic failure messages forcing developers to read raw logs rather than receiving actionable guidance. Assess whether failing jobs provide links to the relevant section of the test output, the line of code that triggered the failure, and a suggested fix. Find the top five sources of developer friction — long queues, flaky reruns, unclear failures, or missing caches — and estimate the weekly engineering hours lost to each. Output as a numbered prioritized improvement plan with time savings per fix.

title: Audit the deployment pipeline for manual failure points
prompt: Map every step in the deployment pipeline. Identify manual steps that create inconsistency, delay, or human error risk. Recommend which steps to automate first based on failure frequency.
prompt_short: Find manual steps in the deployment pipeline that should be automated.
prompt_filtered: Manual steps only. Automation gap analysis.

title: Verify every deployment has a tested rollback path
prompt: For each service deployment, verify there is a documented and tested rollback procedure. Identify deployments where rollback is not possible (irreversible migrations, destructive operations) and verify mitigation plans exist.
prompt_short: Verify all deployments have a tested rollback procedure.
prompt_filtered: Rollback availability only. Flag irreversible deployments.

title: Find missing post-deployment verification checks
prompt: Review what happens immediately after a deployment completes. Identify deployments with no automated smoke test, no health check verification, and no alerting configured to catch regressions within the first 15 minutes.
prompt_short: Find deployments with no automated verification after they complete.
prompt_filtered: Post-deploy checks only. Smoke test and health check gaps.

title: Assess environment promotion gates and approvals
prompt: Review how code moves from dev → staging → production. Identify missing approval gates, environments that auto-promote without sign-off, and cases where staging is skipped entirely. Recommend a minimum viable promotion gate for each stage.
prompt_short: Review environment promotion gates and find missing approval steps.
prompt_filtered: Promotion gates only. Env-to-env approval and validation requirements.

title: Review zero-downtime deployment strategy for each service
prompt: Assess how each service handles rolling, blue-green, or canary deployments. Identify services deployed with a hard restart that causes downtime, and those with no traffic draining before shutdown. Recommend zero-downtime strategies per service.
prompt_short: Find services deployed with downtime and recommend zero-downtime strategies.
prompt_filtered: Deployment strategy only. Zero-downtime and traffic draining.

title: Design a canary deployment strategy with automatic rollback
prompt: Define a canary deployment process for the highest-risk services. Specify the initial traffic percentage, the error-rate and latency thresholds that trigger automatic rollback, the observation window, and the promotion criteria to reach 100% traffic.
prompt_short: Design a canary deployment strategy with automated rollback triggers.
prompt_filtered: Canary strategy only. Traffic percentage, thresholds, and rollback automation.

title: Audit GitOps config drift between declared and live state
prompt: Compare the declared desired state in the GitOps repository against the current live state of each service. Identify resources that have drifted — manual edits applied directly to the cluster, deleted resources not reflected in Git, and pending sync failures.
prompt_short: Find drift between GitOps declared state and live production state.
prompt_filtered: GitOps drift only. Declared vs live state comparison.

title: Set up progressive delivery with feature-flag-gated rollout
prompt: Design a deployment process where code ships to all instances simultaneously but feature flags control user exposure. Define the rollout percentages, the metrics to evaluate at each step, and the flag kill-switch procedure for incidents.
prompt_short: Design progressive delivery using feature flags as the traffic control mechanism.
prompt_filtered: Feature-flag delivery only. Code deploy vs feature exposure separation.

title: Implement deployment frequency and lead time tracking
prompt: Instrument the CD pipeline to track DORA metrics: deployment frequency, lead time for changes, change failure rate, and mean time to restore. Identify which metric is the current bottleneck and the single pipeline change that would improve it most.
prompt_short: Add DORA metric tracking to the CD pipeline and find the biggest bottleneck.
prompt_filtered: DORA metrics only. Deployment frequency, lead time, failure rate, MTTR.

title: Audit database migration deployment ordering
prompt: Review how database migrations are applied relative to application deployments. Identify any sequence where a new migration runs before the old application is fully drained, causing schema incompatibility during rollout. Recommend a backward-compatible migration strategy.
prompt_short: Find migration and deployment ordering issues that cause schema incompatibility.
prompt_filtered: Migration ordering only. Schema compatibility during rolling deploys.

title: Verify deployment pipeline produces immutable artifacts
prompt: Confirm that the CD pipeline builds an artifact once and promotes it through environments unchanged. Identify pipelines that rebuild for each environment (introducing divergence), or that modify configuration by mutating the artifact rather than injecting it at runtime.
prompt_short: Verify the pipeline promotes a single immutable artifact through all environments.
prompt_filtered: Artifact immutability only. Build-once, promote-everywhere pattern compliance.

title: Review change-freeze and deploy-lock procedures
prompt: Audit how deployment freezes (holiday periods, major events) are enforced. Identify gaps where a deployment could proceed during a freeze, missing communication channels for freeze announcements, and the process to request an emergency exception.
prompt_short: Review how deployment freezes are enforced and find gaps in the process.
prompt_filtered: Freeze enforcement only. Lock mechanisms and exception handling.

title: Map deployment dependencies and find hidden coupling
prompt: Identify services that must be deployed in a specific order due to API contract dependencies, shared database schemas, or configuration hand-offs. Find cases where this ordering is undocumented and relied on by institutional memory rather than pipeline enforcement.
prompt_short: Find undocumented deployment ordering dependencies between services.
prompt_filtered: Deployment coupling only. Service ordering dependencies and enforcement.

title: Assess deployment pipeline security and supply chain integrity
prompt: Audit who can trigger deployments, what actions they can take without approval, and whether artifacts are signed and verified before production deployment. Identify steps where a compromised pipeline runner could inject malicious code into a production artifact.
prompt_short: Audit CD pipeline access and artifact integrity for supply chain security.
prompt_filtered: Pipeline security only. Deployment access control and artifact signing.

title: Build a multi-region deployment promotion strategy
prompt: Design a deployment strategy that promotes code through regions sequentially — primary region first, observe error rates for a defined window, then promote to remaining regions. Define the automatic halt criteria and the manual override procedure.
prompt_short: Design a sequential multi-region deployment strategy with automatic halts.
prompt_filtered: Multi-region only. Region-by-region promotion and halt criteria.

title: Implement deployment audit log for compliance
prompt: Ensure every production deployment is recorded with timestamp, artifact version, deployer identity, environment, and outcome. Store the audit log in an immutable store. Verify the log is queryable for compliance reviews and that it captures rollbacks as well as forward deployments.
prompt_short: Add an immutable deployment audit log capturing who deployed what and when.
prompt_filtered: Deployment audit log only. Immutable record of all production deployments.

title: Design a feature-branch deployment strategy for parallel teams
prompt: Design a deployment model that allows multiple teams to deploy their feature branches to isolated staging namespaces simultaneously. Define the resource limits per namespace, the cleanup policy when branches are merged or closed, and the promotion path from namespace to shared staging to production.
prompt_short: Enable parallel team deployments using isolated staging namespaces per feature branch.
prompt_filtered: Namespace isolation only. Per-branch staging and cleanup policy.

title: Automate database migration verification before every deploy
prompt: Add an automated pre-deploy check that runs migration dry-runs and schema validation against a staging database before any production deployment proceeds. The check must verify that the migration is reversible, that no destructive schema change is present, and that the migration runtime is under the defined SLA.
prompt_short: Run migration dry-runs and reversibility checks before every production deploy.
prompt_filtered: Migration verification only. Dry-run, reversibility, and runtime checks.

title: Assess CD pipeline observability and trace propagation
prompt: Review whether deployment events are emitted to the observability stack as structured events. Verify that trace IDs from deployment events can be correlated with application traces to attribute performance changes or error spikes to a specific deployment.
prompt_short: Add structured deployment events to the observability stack with trace correlation.
prompt_filtered: CD observability only. Deployment event emission and trace correlation.

title: Implement deployment gating based on SLO burn rate
prompt: Configure the deployment pipeline to check the current SLO burn rate before promoting to production. If the error budget burn rate exceeds a threshold, halt the deployment automatically and require an explicit override with justification.
prompt_short: Block production deployments when the SLO error budget burn rate is too high.
prompt_filtered: SLO-gated deploy only. Error budget burn rate as a deployment gate.

title: Audit Deployment Approvals
prompt: Audit Deployment Approvals

title: Audit the deployment pipeline for manual steps that cause failures
prompt: Audit the deployment pipeline for manual steps that cause failures. Identify every hand-off, approval gate, or human action in the deploy path. Assess which manual steps can be automated safely. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by frequency of failure. Include the exact pipeline stage and recommended automation approach.

title: Verify every deployment has a tested rollback path
prompt: Verify every deployment has a tested rollback path. Identify services with no rollback procedure or an untested one. Find deployments where rollback would cause data loss. Output a numbered list of rollback gaps with the service name, rollback risk, and recommended fix.

title: Find all missing post-deployment verification checks
prompt: Find all missing post-deployment verification checks. Identify deployments that declare success before confirming the service is healthy. Find missing smoke tests, health check polls, and traffic validation steps. Output findings in full paragraphs with the deployment stage and specific check needed.

title: Audit environment promotion gates between staging and production
prompt: Audit environment promotion gates between staging and production. Identify gates with no automated quality criteria — only time-based holds or manual approval. Find promotions that skip staging entirely. Output a numbered list of gate gaps ordered by blast radius if a broken build reaches production.

title: Review zero-downtime deployment strategy for each service
prompt: Review zero-downtime deployment strategy for each service. Identify services using hard restarts that cause brief outages. Find services with no traffic draining before shutdown. Output findings in full paragraphs with the service name, current strategy, downtime estimate, and recommended replacement approach.

title: Audit the CD pipeline for compliance with the four-eyes principle on pro
prompt: Audit the CD pipeline for compliance with the four-eyes principle on production deployments. Identify deployments that can reach production with approval from only the deployer, without a second engineer's sign-off. Find workflows that allow emergency bypasses without audit logging who performed the bypass and why. Output findings in full paragraphs with the pipeline stage, bypass path, and recommended control.

title: Map every secret and credential used across the CD pipeline
prompt: Map every secret and credential used across the CD pipeline. Identify secrets with overly broad scope — a deploy key that can push to all repositories when it only needs one, or a service account that has admin rights when read-write suffices. Find secrets without automatic rotation. Output a numbered list of over-scoped credentials with the pipeline step, current scope, and recommended minimum scope.

title: Identify deployment pipeline steps that are not idempotent
prompt: Identify deployment pipeline steps that are not idempotent. Find steps that would produce different results if run twice on the same artifact — creating duplicate database records, doubling a counter, or sending duplicate notifications. Assess what a failed deploy retry would produce. Output findings in full paragraphs with the step name, the non-idempotent behavior, and the redesign needed to make it safe to retry.

title: Act as a deployment reliability engineer auditing the full CD pipeline f
prompt: Act as a deployment reliability engineer auditing the full CD pipeline for production safety. Walk every stage from artifact build to live traffic. Identify manual steps that introduce failure risk, missing health checks that allow broken deploys to succeed, rollback procedures that are untested or absent, and promotion gates between environments that have no automated quality bar. For each finding, state the pipeline stage, the failure mode it enables, the frequency of that failure mode based on the git and deployment log, and the specific automation or configuration change that eliminates it. Output as a numbered findings list ordered by risk of causing a production incident.

title: Evaluate this deployment pipeline as if a production incident just occur
prompt: Evaluate this deployment pipeline as if a production incident just occurred and you are tracing the root cause. Map the full deploy path for the affected service. Identify where a bad artifact could have been caught — linting, type checks, integration tests, staging validation, canary traffic — and where it was not. Find gaps in post-deployment verification that allowed the incident to go undetected. Recommend specific instrumentation, automated rollback triggers, and traffic validation steps that would have caught the issue before it reached full production. Output findings in full paragraphs with the detection point, gap, and remediation.

title: Perform a zero-downtime readiness audit across all services in this depl
prompt: Perform a zero-downtime readiness audit across all services in this deployment pipeline. For each service, determine its current deployment strategy and whether it produces any downtime — hard restarts, missing readiness probes, absent traffic draining, or no rolling update configuration. Calculate the aggregate downtime minutes per deployment cycle across the entire system. Rank services by downtime impact and recommend the minimum configuration change for each to achieve zero-downtime deploys. Output as a numbered service-by-service report followed by a priority order for implementation.

title: Perform a deployment blast radius assessment for this CD pipeline
prompt: Perform a deployment blast radius assessment for this CD pipeline. For each service with a deployment configured, determine the maximum number of users affected if a bad deployment reaches production before being caught. Identify deployments with no canary or staged rollout — shipping directly to all production traffic at once. Find deployments where a bad change to a shared library or platform service would cascade to multiple dependent services simultaneously. Assess whether the blast radius of each deployment is documented anywhere and whether the rollout strategy is matched to the service's blast radius. Output a service-by-service table showing current strategy, blast radius estimate, and recommended strategy adjustment.

title: Act as an SRE auditing this deployment pipeline for mean time to recover
prompt: Act as an SRE auditing this deployment pipeline for mean time to recover from a bad deployment. Measure how quickly the pipeline can roll back: identify whether rollback is automated or manual, how many steps it requires, and whether the rollback procedure has been tested in the last 90 days. Find deployments where rollback would not restore the previous working state — irreversible migrations, data model changes, or external API calls that cannot be undone. Assess whether the pipeline has a defined recovery time objective and whether the current rollback mechanism can meet it. Output findings in full paragraphs with each service, its rollback mechanism, estimated time to recover, and blocking factors.

title: Audit test coverage gaps across the codebase
prompt: Review test coverage across unit, integration, and end-to-end layers. Identify the most critical untested code paths — auth flows, payment processing, data mutations. Prioritize coverage additions by risk.
prompt_short: Find the highest-risk untested code paths and prioritize coverage additions.
prompt_filtered: Coverage gaps only. Critical paths with no test coverage.

title: Find tests that test implementation not behavior
prompt: Identify tests tightly coupled to implementation details — mocking private methods, testing internal state, or breaking when code is refactored without changing behavior. Recommend behavior-driven rewrites.
prompt_short: Find implementation-coupled tests that break on refactoring.
prompt_filtered: Test design only. Implementation coupling vs behavior testing.

title: Review integration test coverage for external dependencies
prompt: Assess whether external dependencies (databases, APIs, queues) are tested with real integrations or only mocked. Identify cases where mocks diverged from the real behavior and caused production bugs.
prompt_short: Find mocked dependencies that should use real integration tests.
prompt_filtered: Integration test coverage only. Mock vs real dependency assessment.

title: Identify missing edge case and boundary tests
prompt: Review the test suite for coverage of edge cases — empty inputs, null values, maximum values, concurrent access, timeout conditions. Identify the top 10 edge cases that are untested and most likely to cause production bugs.
prompt_short: Find the most likely untested edge cases that could cause production bugs.
prompt_filtered: Edge cases only. Boundary values, nulls, concurrency, timeouts.

title: Assess test data management and fixture quality
prompt: Review how test data is created, managed, and cleaned up. Identify tests with hardcoded IDs, shared mutable fixtures, or tests that depend on execution order. Recommend isolated, deterministic test data patterns.
prompt_short: Audit test data fixtures for shared state, order dependency, and hardcoded IDs.
prompt_filtered: Test data only. Fixture isolation and determinism.

title: Apply mutation testing to find inadequate assertions
prompt: Run mutation testing on the core business logic modules. Identify surviving mutants — code changes that don't cause any test to fail — as evidence of tests that execute code without asserting the result. Prioritize adding assertions that kill the highest-value survivors.
prompt_short: Use mutation testing to find tests that pass without asserting the right thing.
prompt_filtered: Mutation testing only. Surviving mutants in critical business logic.

title: Design a consumer-driven contract testing strategy
prompt: For each API boundary between services, define consumer-driven contract tests using Pact or a similar framework. Identify the highest-risk integrations where a schema change could silently break a consumer. Recommend the minimum contract coverage to catch breaking changes before deploy.
prompt_short: Design contract tests for all internal service API boundaries.
prompt_filtered: Contract testing only. API consumer-producer compatibility verification.

title: Audit E2E test suite for flake budget and maintenance cost
prompt: Review the end-to-end test suite for tests that exceed the flake budget — failing more than 1% of runs without a code change. Calculate the total engineering time spent triaging E2E failures per sprint. Recommend the maximum E2E suite size given the current flake rate.
prompt_short: Calculate the true cost of E2E test flakiness and set a sustainable flake budget.
prompt_filtered: E2E flake budget only. Failure rate, triage cost, and suite size limit.

title: Add snapshot testing for UI components without visual regression coverage
prompt: Identify UI components with no snapshot or visual regression test. For each, assess the risk of silent visual regressions reaching production. Add serialized snapshot tests for component output, and flag where pixel-level visual diffing would add more value.
prompt_short: Find UI components with no snapshot coverage and add baseline snapshots.
prompt_filtered: Snapshot testing only. Component render output and visual regression gaps.

title: Build a test pyramid compliance report
prompt: Count the number of unit, integration, and end-to-end tests currently in the suite. Calculate the ratio and compare to the recommended test pyramid (70% unit, 20% integration, 10% E2E). Identify which layer is over- or under-invested and the highest-priority tests to add or remove.
prompt_short: Measure the current test pyramid ratio and find which layer is out of balance.
prompt_filtered: Test pyramid ratio only. Unit vs integration vs E2E count and balance.

title: Identify tests that require a running database to be meaningful
prompt: Find unit tests that mock database calls in ways that diverge from real database behavior — returning structs without constraint checks, ignoring transaction semantics, or simulating queries that would fail on real data. Recommend promoting these to integration tests against a real database.
prompt_short: Find unit tests using database mocks that diverge from real database behavior.
prompt_filtered: Database mock fidelity only. Tests that need real DB to be trustworthy.

title: Review load and performance test coverage for critical paths
prompt: Identify the application's most performance-critical paths — high-traffic endpoints, report generation, batch jobs. For each, verify there is a performance test that asserts a latency budget and throughput minimum. Find paths with no performance baseline that would let regressions go undetected.
prompt_short: Find critical paths with no performance test or latency baseline.
prompt_filtered: Performance tests only. Latency budget and throughput assertions.

title: Add property-based tests to replace hand-written edge cases
prompt: Identify pure functions with complex input domains where hand-written test cases are missing edge cases. Apply property-based testing (fast-check, Hypothesis) to generate inputs automatically. Define the invariants — properties that must hold for all inputs — and let the framework find counterexamples.
prompt_short: Replace hand-written edge case tests with property-based tests for complex functions.
prompt_filtered: Property-based testing only. Invariant definition and input generation.

title: Set up test coverage gates with per-module thresholds
prompt: Implement coverage gates that enforce different thresholds per module — 90% for payment and auth code, 70% for utilities, 50% for UI components. Configure CI to fail on regressions without blocking new work in low-priority areas. Report coverage trends over time.
prompt_short: Implement per-module coverage thresholds and enforce them in CI.
prompt_filtered: Coverage gates only. Per-module thresholds and CI enforcement.

title: Audit accessibility testing integration in the test suite
prompt: Assess whether automated accessibility checks (axe-core, Lighthouse, jest-axe) are integrated into the test suite for UI components and page-level tests. Identify the most-used components and flows with no accessibility assertion. Recommend where to add automated a11y checks with the highest coverage-to-effort ratio.
prompt_short: Find UI components and flows with no automated accessibility testing.
prompt_filtered: Accessibility testing only. axe-core and ARIA assertion coverage.

title: Add API schema validation tests for all external endpoints
prompt: For every external API the application consumes, write a test that validates the response schema against the expected shape. Run these tests in CI to catch breaking schema changes before they cause production failures. Use a schema registry or OpenAPI spec as the source of truth.
prompt_short: Add schema validation tests for all external API responses the application consumes.
prompt_filtered: Schema validation only. External API response structure tests.

title: Measure test suite maintenance cost per module
prompt: Calculate how often tests in each module are modified versus how often the module itself changes. A high test-change-to-code-change ratio indicates brittle tests. Identify the 5 modules with the worst ratio and refactor their tests to be more resilient to implementation changes.
prompt_short: Find modules with brittle tests that change more often than the code they test.
prompt_filtered: Test brittleness only. Test-to-code change ratio per module.

title: Implement test impact analysis to run only affected tests
prompt: Integrate test impact analysis so that CI runs only the tests affected by a given code change rather than the full suite. Map source files to the tests that cover them. Estimate the reduction in CI time and the risk tolerance for skipping unaffected tests.
prompt_short: Run only the tests affected by each code change using test impact analysis.
prompt_filtered: Test impact analysis only. Affected test selection and coverage mapping.

title: Establish test coverage trend monitoring over time
prompt: Set up coverage trend tracking that records and graphs test coverage over time. Configure alerts when coverage drops more than 2 percentage points between merges. Integrate the coverage trend chart into the PR review flow so reviewers can see whether a PR improves or degrades coverage.
prompt_short: Track coverage trends over time and alert on regressions between merges.
prompt_filtered: Coverage trending only. Historical coverage tracking and regression alerts.

title: Audit test naming conventions for discoverability
prompt: Review naming conventions across the test suite. Find tests with names describing implementation details rather than behavior, tests with generic names that give no context on failure, and duplicate names across modules. Enforce a naming convention (Given/When/Then or should/when format) that makes failing tests immediately actionable.
prompt_short: Standardize test naming to make failures immediately actionable without reading code.
prompt_filtered: Test naming only. Behavior-describing names and failure discoverability.

title: Fill in Missing Data Gaps
prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

prompt: Find What's Broken

title: Recommend Next Steps
prompt: Recommend Next Steps

prompt: Score for Production Readiness

prompt: Audit Test Coverage Gaps

title: Find Behavior-Testing Opportunities
prompt: Find Behavior-Testing Opportunities

title: Identify Missing Integration Tests
prompt: Identify Missing Integration Tests

title: Find Flaky Tests
prompt: Find Flaky Tests

title: Audit Test Data Fixtures
prompt: Audit Test Data Fixtures

title: Map Missing Edge Cases
prompt: Map Missing Edge Cases

title: Check Test Suite Speed
prompt: Check Test Suite Speed

title: Audit test coverage gaps across the codebase
prompt: Audit test coverage gaps across the codebase. Identify the most critical paths with no test coverage. Flag tests that cover implementation details instead of behavior. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by risk of undetected regression. Include file paths and the specific scenario each missing test should cover.

title: Find tests that test implementation rather than behavior
prompt: Find tests that test implementation rather than behavior. Identify tests tightly coupled to internal function names, private state, or specific return structures rather than observable outcomes. Output a numbered list of brittle tests with the behavior they should assert instead.

title: Review integration test coverage for all external dependencies
prompt: Review integration test coverage for all external dependencies. Find external services, databases, and APIs that are mocked in tests but never hit in any integration suite. Output findings in full paragraphs with the dependency name, risk of drift, and recommended integration test approach.

title: Identify missing edge case and boundary tests across the critical paths
prompt: Identify missing edge case and boundary tests across the critical paths. For each critical function or endpoint, list the boundary conditions — empty inputs, max values, concurrent requests, error states — that have no test coverage. Output a numbered list ordered by likelihood of production failure.

title: Assess test data management and fixture quality across the test suite
prompt: Assess test data management and fixture quality across the test suite. Find tests that share mutable state, depend on execution order, or use hardcoded production IDs. Output findings in full paragraphs with the test file, the data hazard it creates, and the recommended fixture isolation approach.

title: Audit the test suite for dependency on external services that are not mo
prompt: Audit the test suite for dependency on external services that are not mocked. Find integration tests that call live third-party APIs, real payment processors, or production databases during CI runs. Identify tests that fail due to external service downtime rather than code bugs. Output a numbered list of external dependencies in the test suite with the test file, service called, and recommended mock or contract test replacement.

title: Map test coverage to the highest-traffic code paths using production tel
prompt: Map test coverage to the highest-traffic code paths using production telemetry. Find the top 10 endpoints or functions by call volume and verify each has meaningful test coverage. Identify paths that handle the most user-facing requests but have the fewest tests. Output findings in full paragraphs with the code path, production call volume rank, current test count, and coverage gap description.

title: Review the test suite for tests that assert happy paths only
prompt: Review the test suite for tests that assert happy paths only. Find functions with multiple error branches — validation failures, database errors, permission denials — where only the success case has a test. Identify the 10 most critical error paths with no test coverage. Output a numbered list with the function name, the untested error branch, and the minimum assertion needed to cover it.

title: Act as a testing lead auditing this test suite for production confidence
prompt: Act as a testing lead auditing this test suite for production confidence. Walk every layer — unit, integration, end-to-end — and assess coverage for the most critical user paths. Identify paths with no tests, tests that assert implementation details instead of behavior, and integration tests that mock every external dependency rather than testing real integration. For each gap, state the path, the failure mode it leaves undetected, and the minimum test that would catch it. Then assess the suite's speed and reliability: find the slowest test files, identify flakiness sources, and estimate the total time lost to reruns per week. Output as a single report with numbered findings ordered by risk to the production release process.

title: Evaluate this test suite as a quality signal for production deployments
prompt: Evaluate this test suite as a quality signal for production deployments. Determine whether passing tests is a meaningful indicator that the system works. Identify tests that pass even when the feature is broken — over-mocked integration tests, tautological unit tests, or tests asserting the wrong thing. Find paths where a genuine regression would reach production undetected by any test in the suite. Output findings in full paragraphs with the gap, the regression scenario it misses, and the recommended test type and scope to close it.

title: Perform a test data audit across the entire test suite
prompt: Perform a test data audit across the entire test suite. Identify every source of shared mutable state — global fixtures, database seeds that accumulate across tests, in-memory caches not reset between tests, and external service state left over from prior runs. Map the execution-order dependencies that result from shared state. Estimate the false-negative rate caused by state pollution — tests that should fail but pass because prior state masks the bug. Recommend a test isolation strategy, fixture cleanup approach, and data factory pattern that eliminates shared state entirely. Output as a numbered remediation plan ordered by impact on suite reliability.

title: Evaluate this test suite as a decision-making tool for safe production d
prompt: Evaluate this test suite as a decision-making tool for safe production deployments. Answer whether a passing test suite gives the engineering team justified confidence that the system works correctly for real users. Identify the most significant gaps between what the tests verify and what users actually do — untested user flows, untested error recovery scenarios, and untested concurrency conditions. Find the category of production bugs that appears most often in incident history and assess whether the current test suite would catch future occurrences. Determine whether the integration test environment is representative enough of production that integration test results are meaningful. Output a confidence assessment per system layer with specific gaps and the test investment that would close each gap.

title: Perform a test suite architecture audit focused on long-term maintainabi
prompt: Perform a test suite architecture audit focused on long-term maintainability. Assess the ratio of test code to production code and evaluate whether the test suite is growing sustainably. Identify patterns that make tests fragile — tests that import implementation details, tests that depend on timing, tests that share state across files, and tests that assert on generated IDs or timestamps. Find modules where the test code is more complex than the production code it tests, signaling over-engineering. Assess whether there is a clear test taxonomy — what to unit test, what to integration test, and what to end-to-end test — and whether that taxonomy is applied consistently across the team. Output findings in full paragraphs with specific examples and a recommended set of testing principles for this codebase.

title: Audit branching strategy for consistency and clarity
prompt: Review the branching model in use. Identify branches that have diverged significantly from main, long-lived feature branches at risk of merge conflict, and inconsistencies in naming conventions across the team.
prompt_short: Audit branches for divergence, naming inconsistency, and merge conflict risk.
prompt_filtered: Branch strategy only. Divergence and naming conventions.

title: Find commits that should have been separate PRs
prompt: Review recent large commits and PRs. Identify changes that bundled unrelated work — making it harder to bisect bugs, revert individual changes, or understand the history. Recommend a commit splitting strategy.
prompt_short: Find large commits that mixed unrelated changes and should be split.
prompt_filtered: Commit hygiene only. Scope and atomicity of recent commits.

title: Identify secrets or sensitive data in git history
prompt: Scan the git history for accidentally committed secrets — API keys, passwords, tokens, PII. For any found, assess when they were committed, whether they are still active, and the steps to purge them from history and rotate credentials.
prompt_short: Scan git history for accidentally committed secrets or sensitive data.
prompt_filtered: Secret detection only. Git history scan for credentials.

title: Review tag and release versioning conventions
prompt: Audit how releases are tagged in version control. Verify semantic versioning is used correctly, all production deployments correspond to a tag, and the tagging convention is documented and enforced.
prompt_short: Audit release tags for semantic versioning compliance and completeness.
prompt_filtered: Release tagging only. Tag conventions and coverage.

title: Find stale branches that should be archived or deleted
prompt: Identify branches that haven't been updated in 30+ days. For each, determine if the work was merged (safe to delete), abandoned (needs a decision), or still in progress (needs a status update). Never delete — archive per project policy.
prompt_short: Find stale branches and determine their status for cleanup decisions.
prompt_filtered: Stale branches only. Age and merge status analysis.

title: Evaluate trunk-based development vs. git-flow for this team
prompt: Assess whether the team's current branching model matches their release cadence and team size. If feature branches live longer than two days on average, evaluate whether trunk-based development with feature flags would reduce merge conflict cost and improve deployment frequency.
prompt_short: Assess whether trunk-based development would outperform the current branching model.
prompt_filtered: Branching model only. Trunk-based vs git-flow trade-off analysis.

title: Enforce commit signing for all contributors
prompt: Audit whether commits to main are signed with GPG or SSH keys. Find the current percentage of unsigned commits in the git log. Set up branch protection to require signed commits and provide a guide for contributors to configure commit signing locally.
prompt_short: Find unsigned commits and set up commit signing enforcement on main.
prompt_filtered: Commit signing only. GPG/SSH signature enforcement and coverage.

title: Review monorepo vs. polyrepo boundaries for this codebase
prompt: Evaluate whether the current repository structure (mono or poly) is appropriate for the team's deployment cadence and ownership model. Identify services that would benefit from independent versioning, or shared code that would benefit from colocation. Quantify the cross-repo coordination overhead.
prompt_short: Assess whether the current mono/polyrepo structure matches team needs.
prompt_filtered: Repo structure only. Monorepo vs polyrepo trade-off for this codebase.

title: Audit submodule or subtree dependencies for staleness
prompt: Find all git submodules or subtrees in use. For each, check whether it is pinned to a specific commit or floating on a branch, when it was last updated, and whether the dependent code is compatible with the latest version of the submodule. Identify update blockers.
prompt_short: Find stale or floating git submodules and recommend pinning or updating.
prompt_filtered: Submodules only. Pin status, staleness, and compatibility.

title: Generate a contributor activity report for bus factor analysis
prompt: Analyze the git log to find the percentage of commits per contributor per module. Identify modules where more than 70% of commits come from a single author — high bus factor risk. Recommend knowledge-sharing strategies (pair programming, documentation sprints, code review rotation) for each high-risk module.
prompt_short: Find modules with high bus factor based on contributor concentration in git log.
prompt_filtered: Bus factor only. Commit authorship concentration per module.

title: Standardize commit message format with commitlint
prompt: Audit the last 100 commits for adherence to a consistent message format (Conventional Commits or custom). Identify the most common format violations. Configure commitlint in the repo with the agreed-upon rules and add it as a git commit-msg hook and CI check.
prompt_short: Enforce commit message format with commitlint and fix current violations.
prompt_filtered: Commit message format only. Conventional Commits compliance and linting setup.

title: Set up protected tag rules for release artifacts
prompt: Audit whether release tags can be moved or deleted by any contributor. Configure protected tag rules so that version tags (v*.*.*) can only be created by the release automation or a designated role, and cannot be deleted or force-moved once created.
prompt_short: Protect release tags from deletion or mutation by unauthorized contributors.
prompt_filtered: Tag protection only. Release tag immutability and access rules.

title: Review pre-commit hooks for consistency across developer machines
prompt: Audit the pre-commit hook configuration. Verify hooks are installed consistently across developer machines (via husky, pre-commit framework, or lefthook), that the hooks match what CI enforces, and that there is no way to skip them without an explicit override.
prompt_short: Audit pre-commit hooks for consistent enforcement across developer machines and CI.
prompt_filtered: Pre-commit hooks only. Hook installation and CI parity.

title: Create a git history cleanup plan for a leaner repository
prompt: Analyze the repository size and identify large binary files, removed assets still in history, and oversized commits. Estimate the repository size reduction from running git-filter-repo on identified blobs. Provide a step-by-step cleanup plan that preserves the meaningful commit history.
prompt_short: Find large blobs in git history and plan a cleanup to reduce repository size.
prompt_filtered: History size only. Large blobs and binary files in git history.

title: Audit merge strategy for consistency and history clarity
prompt: Review the merge strategy in use — merge commits, squash, or rebase. Identify inconsistencies where different team members use different strategies for the same type of work. Recommend a single strategy per PR type (feature, fix, dependency) and enforce it via branch protection settings.
prompt_short: Standardize the merge strategy for each PR type and enforce it in branch protection.
prompt_filtered: Merge strategy only. Merge commit vs squash vs rebase consistency.

title: Implement automated changelog generation from commit history
prompt: Configure conventional-changelog or release-please to auto-generate CHANGELOG.md entries from commit messages on every release. Verify the commit message convention is enforced in CI so the changelog is always accurate. Identify releases in the current history with no changelog entry.
prompt_short: Auto-generate changelogs from Conventional Commits on every release.
prompt_filtered: Changelog generation only. Commit-to-changelog pipeline and convention enforcement.

title: Set up CODEOWNERS for mandatory reviewer assignments
prompt: Create or audit the CODEOWNERS file to assign mandatory reviewers for each critical path. Verify that branch protection enforces CODEOWNERS review requirements and that reviews cannot be self-approved for owned paths.
prompt_short: Create CODEOWNERS assignments for critical paths and enforce them in branch protection.
prompt_filtered: CODEOWNERS only. Mandatory reviewer assignments and enforcement.

title: Review force-push policies and rebase safety rules
prompt: Audit whether force pushes are allowed to any shared branch. Find recent force pushes in the git reflog and assess whether they caused integration problems. Configure branch protection to disallow force pushes on all shared branches except under explicit emergency procedure.
prompt_short: Disallow force pushes to shared branches and audit the git reflog for past occurrences.
prompt_filtered: Force push policy only. Branch protection and reflog audit.

title: Map release branch lifecycle and long-term support strategy
prompt: Define the lifecycle of release branches — how long they receive bug fixes, when they reach end-of-life, and how security patches are backported. Identify currently active release branches with no defined EOL date. Document the backport process and automate cherry-picks via a bot where possible.
prompt_short: Define release branch EOL dates and automate security patch backporting.
prompt_filtered: Release branch lifecycle only. EOL policy and backport automation.

title: Audit repository access permissions and outside collaborators
prompt: Review who has write or admin access to the repository. Find outside collaborators who were granted access for a specific project and never removed. Identify service accounts with admin rights that only need write access. Recommend minimum permissions per contributor type and implement access review as a quarterly scheduled task.
prompt_short: Audit repository access and remove unnecessary write and admin permissions.
prompt_filtered: Access audit only. Contributor permissions and outside collaborator review.
