title: Check Commit Message Quality
prompt: Check Commit Message Quality

title: Audit the branching strategy for consistency and clarity
prompt: Audit the branching strategy for consistency and clarity. Identify branches that violate naming conventions or purpose boundaries. Find long-lived feature branches that have diverged significantly from main. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by merge risk. Include the branch name, age, and recommended action.

title: Find commits that should have been separate PRs
prompt: Find commits that should have been separate PRs. Identify commits mixing unrelated concerns — feature code with bug fixes, dependency bumps with logic changes, or refactors bundled with features. Output a numbered list of commits by hash with the recommended split strategy for each.

title: Scan the full git history for secrets and sensitive data
prompt: Scan the full git history for secrets and sensitive data. Identify commits that introduced API keys, tokens, passwords, or PII into tracked files — even if later removed. Output findings in full paragraphs with commit hash, file path, and whether the secret is still reachable in history.

title: Review tag and release versioning conventions for consistency
prompt: Review tag and release versioning conventions for consistency. Find releases that skip versions, use inconsistent tag formats, or lack a corresponding changelog entry. Output a numbered list of versioning gaps with the tag, expected convention, and recommended fix.

title: Find stale branches that should be archived or cleaned up
prompt: Find stale branches that should be archived or cleaned up. For each branch inactive for 30 or more days, determine whether the work was merged, abandoned, or still in progress. Output findings in full paragraphs with branch name, last commit date, status assessment, and recommended action.

title: Analyze the git history for commits that would make incident investigati
prompt: Analyze the git history for commits that would make incident investigation harder. Find commits with no description of why the change was made, commits that mix formatting changes with logic changes making diffs unreadable, and commits with no linked issue or ticket. Output a numbered list of the worst-quality commits in the last 90 days with the commit hash, the quality problem, and what information is now permanently lost.

title: Review the repository's gitignore coverage for completeness
prompt: Review the repository's gitignore coverage for completeness. Identify file types that should be ignored but are not — IDE config directories, OS-generated files, build outputs, and generated code. Find any tracked files that should be untracked. Verify there are no patterns so broad they accidentally ignore important source files. Output findings in full paragraphs with the missing pattern, the risk of accidentally committing that type of file, and the recommended gitignore entry.

title: Assess the repository's branch protection settings against a minimum sec
prompt: Assess the repository's branch protection settings against a minimum security baseline. Verify that main requires passing CI, at least one non-author approval, stale review dismissal on new commits, and no direct pushes. Find any setting that is weaker than the baseline. Output a numbered list of protection gaps with the specific setting, current value, and recommended value.

title: Act as a version control strategist auditing the git history and branchi
prompt: Act as a version control strategist auditing the git history and branching model for this project. Analyze the commit graph to determine how work flows from creation to merge. Identify branches that are long-lived beyond their purpose, commits that mix unrelated concerns, and merge patterns that create difficult-to-read history. Find secrets or sensitive data introduced and later removed from history — their presence in history still constitutes exposure. Evaluate the tagging and versioning conventions for consistency and whether they support reliable rollbacks and changelogs. Output a full report with numbered findings ordered by risk to team velocity and release reliability.

title: Evaluate the git commit history as a diagnostic tool for understanding h
prompt: Evaluate the git commit history as a diagnostic tool for understanding how this codebase evolved. Identify patterns that indicate poor hygiene — force pushes to main, merge commits without context, large commits that bundle unrelated changes, and commit messages that describe what rather than why. Find the commits most likely to cause confusion during incident investigation. Assess whether the current branching strategy supports parallel work without frequent conflicts. Recommend specific workflow changes — commit message standards, branch naming, PR size limits, and squash policies — that would make the history a reliable audit trail. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by impact on team productivity.

title: Perform a secrets exposure audit across the full git history of this rep
prompt: Perform a secrets exposure audit across the full git history of this repository. Search for patterns matching API keys, tokens, connection strings, private keys, and credential formats in every commit — including commits where the file was later deleted or the secret was overwritten. For each finding, state the commit hash, the file path, the type of secret, whether it is still reachable in the current tree, and the remediation step. Assess whether history rewriting is needed and what the blast radius of each exposed secret was. Output as a numbered findings list followed by a prioritized remediation plan.

title: Evaluate the repository's contribution workflow from the perspective of
prompt: Evaluate the repository's contribution workflow from the perspective of a new team member joining today. Assess whether the branching conventions, commit message standards, PR description requirements, and review process are documented clearly enough for a new contributor to follow without asking for help. Identify where tribal knowledge fills gaps that should be codified. Find the most common mistakes new contributors make — based on review comment patterns and revert history — and assess whether those mistakes could be prevented by tooling, templates, or better documentation. Recommend the minimum set of guardrails — hooks, templates, CI checks, and onboarding documentation — that would reduce new contributor friction while maintaining quality standards. Output a gap analysis with specific recommendations ordered by new-contributor impact.

title: Perform a bus factor analysis using the git contribution history
prompt: Perform a bus factor analysis using the git contribution history. For each module or directory in the repository, calculate the percentage of commits attributed to each contributor over the last 12 months. Identify modules where a single contributor authored more than 70 percent of all commits — knowledge silos where that contributor's departure would leave the team unable to maintain the code safely. Assess whether the concentration is increasing or decreasing over time. Find modules that have received no contributions from more than two different engineers in the entire project history. Recommend specific knowledge-transfer strategies — code review rotation, pair programming assignments, documentation sprints — and estimate the time investment needed to reduce bus factor risk in the top five highest-risk modules. Output as a prioritized module list with author concentration data and recommended actions.

title: Create a code review checklist for this codebase
prompt: Generate a code review checklist tailored to this codebase's tech stack and known failure patterns. Include: correctness, security, performance, test coverage, naming, error handling, and documentation. Make it concise enough to use on every PR.
prompt_short: Generate a focused code review checklist for this specific codebase.
prompt_filtered: Checklist generation only. Tailored to this tech stack and failure patterns.

title: Find security issues that reviewers commonly miss
prompt: Identify the security-related issues most likely to slip through standard code reviews in this codebase — auth bypass patterns, injection vectors, insecure direct object reference, missing rate limiting. Create a security-focused review checklist for these specific risks.
prompt_short: List security issues commonly missed in code reviews for this codebase.
prompt_filtered: Security review only. High-miss-rate vulnerabilities specific to this stack.

title: Review this PR for correctness and hidden regressions
prompt: Review the current diff for logical correctness, hidden regressions in adjacent code, missing test coverage for new paths, and any change that could have unintended side effects in other parts of the system.
prompt_short: Review this diff for correctness, regressions, and missing test coverage.
prompt_filtered: Correctness only. Logic errors and regression risks in the diff.

title: Audit recent PRs for patterns of recurring issues
prompt: Review the last 20 merged PRs for recurring review feedback. Identify the most common issues raised — and assess whether they should be automated (linting rule, CI check) rather than caught manually in every review.
prompt_short: Identify recurring review issues that should be automated in CI.
prompt_filtered: Recurring issues only. What to automate vs continue reviewing manually.

title: Check PR size and review quality metrics
prompt: Assess the average PR size and review throughput. Identify PRs too large for effective review (>500 lines changed), PRs approved with no feedback, and review turnaround times exceeding 24 hours that block delivery.
prompt_short: Audit PR size and review quality to find where reviews are ineffective.
prompt_filtered: PR metrics only. Size, feedback rate, and turnaround time.

title: Establish a review SLA and escalation policy
prompt: Define a code review SLA — the maximum time from PR open to first review and from review to merge. Identify PRs in the last 60 days that exceeded the SLA. Recommend an escalation path (reviewer reassignment, async approval, engineering manager escalation) for PRs stuck beyond the SLA.
prompt_short: Set a review SLA and find PRs in the last 60 days that violated it.
prompt_filtered: Review SLA only. Time-to-first-review and time-to-merge compliance.

title: Implement AI-assisted first-pass review for common issues
prompt: Design a bot that runs on every PR and flags the categories of issues that human reviewers consistently raise — missing error handling, large function size, missing test coverage, TODO comments. The bot should comment inline rather than blocking merge, freeing reviewers for judgment-level feedback.
prompt_short: Build an AI review bot that auto-flags common review issues on every PR.
prompt_filtered: AI review automation only. Bot comment scope and human reviewer complement.

title: Create a security-focused review checklist for auth and data changes
prompt: Generate a targeted security review checklist for PRs that touch authentication, authorization, payment flows, or user data. The checklist should be short enough to complete in 10 minutes and specific enough to catch the vulnerability classes most common in this codebase.
prompt_short: Create a 10-minute security review checklist for high-risk code changes.
prompt_filtered: Security checklist only. Auth, payment, and data handling PRs.

title: Audit reviewer distribution for knowledge silo risk
prompt: Analyze which engineers are reviewing which modules over the last 90 days. Identify modules where reviews are concentrated on one or two people — creating a review bottleneck and knowledge silo. Recommend code ownership rotation and cross-training through review assignments.
prompt_short: Find modules where code review is concentrated on too few reviewers.
prompt_filtered: Reviewer distribution only. Silo detection and rotation recommendations.

title: Find PRs that should require mandatory security review
prompt: Identify all code paths that, when changed, should require a mandatory security-aware reviewer — auth flows, cryptographic code, admin APIs, data export endpoints, and payment processing. Verify that CODEOWNERS or branch protection rules enforce this for each path today.
prompt_short: Set up mandatory security review for high-risk code paths via CODEOWNERS.
prompt_filtered: Mandatory reviewers only. CODEOWNERS and branch protection for sensitive paths.

title: Measure and reduce time-to-feedback on draft PRs
prompt: Analyze how teams use draft PRs. Find cases where drafts stay open for more than 5 days with no review feedback, indicating the draft workflow is being misused as a parking lot. Recommend a draft-to-review promotion policy and a maximum draft-open time before automatic escalation.
prompt_short: Audit draft PR usage and find cases where drafts are blocking progress.
prompt_filtered: Draft PRs only. Promotion to ready-for-review and maximum open time.

title: Add automated style and complexity checks to pre-review
prompt: Configure automated checks to run before human review begins — cyclomatic complexity limits, function length limits, and style enforcement. Ensure these checks post inline comments so reviewers see the violations without manually searching. Identify the checks that would eliminate the most common review comments.
prompt_short: Add automated complexity and style checks to reduce manual review burden.
prompt_filtered: Pre-review automation only. Complexity and style checks before human review.

title: Design a post-merge code review retrospective process
prompt: Implement a lightweight monthly review of production bugs and outages to trace them back to the code review that missed them. For each escaped bug, identify the review gap — which checklist item would have caught it. Update the team review checklist based on learnings.
prompt_short: Trace production bugs back to missed review items and update the checklist.
prompt_filtered: Review retrospective only. Escaped defect analysis and checklist improvement.

title: Enforce description and test plan requirements on all PRs
prompt: Audit recent PRs for description quality — how many have no description, a single sentence, or no test plan. Configure a PR template that requires a summary, linked issue, test plan, and screenshots for UI changes. Add a CI check that fails on empty descriptions.
prompt_short: Enforce PR description quality with a template and CI check.
prompt_filtered: PR description only. Template enforcement and description quality metrics.

title: Review self-merge and bypass patterns in branch protection
prompt: Audit the git log for PRs merged by the same person who opened them, direct pushes to main bypassing PRs, and admin override usage. For each instance, assess whether it was a legitimate exception or a process gap. Recommend tightening branch protection to require at least one non-author approval.
prompt_short: Find self-merges and direct pushes that bypassed the review process.
prompt_filtered: Bypass detection only. Self-merge, direct push, and admin override audit.

title: Implement inline feedback templates for common review patterns
prompt: Create a set of reviewer comment templates for the most common feedback types — missing error handling, untested edge case, security concern, naming issue, and performance concern. Templates provide consistent links to relevant documentation and the specific fix expected.
prompt_short: Create reviewer comment templates for the most common review feedback categories.
prompt_filtered: Review templates only. Consistent comment format for recurring feedback types.

title: Measure and reduce PR cycle time end-to-end
prompt: Define and measure the full PR cycle time from first commit to merged. Break it into phases: time in draft, time waiting for review, time in review, time waiting for CI, and time waiting for merge. Identify which phase contributes the most time and the specific process change that reduces it.
prompt_short: Measure PR cycle time by phase and find the highest-impact reduction opportunity.
prompt_filtered: Cycle time only. PR phase breakdown from first commit to merge.

title: Require linked issues on all PRs for traceability
prompt: Configure branch protection or a CI check to require that every PR links to at least one open issue or ticket. Find PRs in the last 90 days with no linked issue. Assess the impact on change management and compliance audits when changes cannot be traced to a business requirement.
prompt_short: Require every PR to link a tracked issue for change traceability.
prompt_filtered: Issue traceability only. PR-to-issue linking requirement and CI enforcement.

title: Add review analytics to identify high-variance reviewer quality
prompt: Track code review metrics per reviewer: average comments per PR, comment-to-approval ratio, turnaround time, and percentage of PRs reviewed where bugs escaped to production post-merge. Identify reviewers with very high or very low comment rates and use the data to improve reviewer training.
prompt_short: Track review quality metrics per reviewer and use data to improve training.
prompt_filtered: Reviewer analytics only. Comment rate, turnaround, and escaped defect correlation.

title: Create a review rotation schedule to spread knowledge
prompt: Implement a structured review rotation where engineers are assigned to review modules outside their primary ownership area at least once per sprint. Track which engineers have reviewed which modules over the last quarter. Identify modules never reviewed by anyone outside the owning team.
prompt_short: Rotate code review assignments across modules to prevent knowledge silos.
prompt_filtered: Review rotation only. Cross-module reviewer assignment and silo detection.

prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

prompt: Build Code Review Checklist

title: Find Security Review Gaps
prompt: Find Security Review Gaps

title: Review This PR
prompt: Review This PR

title: Audit Recent PR Patterns
prompt: Audit Recent PR Patterns

title: Check PR Size Metrics
prompt: Check PR Size Metrics

title: Find Rubber-Stamp Approvals
prompt: Find Rubber-Stamp Approvals

title: Identify Review Bottlenecks
prompt: Identify Review Bottlenecks

title: Create a code review checklist for this codebase
prompt: Create a code review checklist for this codebase. Identify the most common review comments from recent PRs and the recurring issues that slip through to production. Output a numbered checklist with each item as an actionable yes/no check a reviewer can apply to any PR in this project.

title: Find security issues that reviewers commonly miss in this codebase
prompt: Find security issues that reviewers commonly miss in this codebase. Identify injection risks, missing auth checks, improper error exposure, and unsafe data handling patterns. Output findings in full paragraphs with the vulnerability type, an example from the codebase, and the review heuristic that catches it.

title: Review this PR for correctness and hidden regressions
prompt: Review this PR for correctness and hidden regressions. Trace every changed function to understand its callers and side effects. Identify any behavior change not covered by the accompanying tests. Output a numbered list of concerns ordered by severity with the line reference and the specific regression risk.

title: Audit recent PRs for patterns of recurring issues
prompt: Audit recent PRs for patterns of recurring issues. Find the most common types of bugs, style violations, or missing considerations that appear across multiple reviews. Output findings in full paragraphs with the issue type, frequency, and a review automation or checklist item that prevents recurrence.

title: Assess PR size and review quality across the last 90 days of merged PRs
prompt: Assess PR size and review quality across the last 90 days of merged PRs. Find PRs too large for effective review, PRs approved with no substantive feedback, and review turnaround times that block delivery. Output findings in full paragraphs with the specific PRs, the bottleneck cause, and the process change that addresses it.

title: Measure reviewer load distribution across the engineering team over the
prompt: Measure reviewer load distribution across the engineering team over the last 90 days. Find engineers reviewing more than 40 percent of all PRs, signaling a bottleneck. Find engineers who have reviewed fewer than 5 percent, signaling under-utilization or siloing. Output findings in full paragraphs with each reviewer's load percentage, the modules they dominate, and the rebalancing approach that reduces concentration without losing quality.

title: Audit the PR description quality for the last 50 merged PRs
prompt: Audit the PR description quality for the last 50 merged PRs. Identify descriptions with no summary, no test plan, no linked issue, or only a single sentence. Find PRs where reviewers had to ask what the change does — visible in the review comment history. Output a numbered list of the weakest descriptions with the PR number, what information was missing, and the template item that would have required it.

title: Find review patterns that indicate reviewers are not reading the code
prompt: Find review patterns that indicate reviewers are not reading the code. Identify PRs approved within 2 minutes of being opened, PRs approved with only positive emoji reactions and no substantive comments, and PRs where the reviewer approved a change that later caused a production bug. Output findings in full paragraphs with specific PR numbers as evidence and the pattern each illustrates.

title: Act as an engineering lead auditing the code review process for this tea
prompt: Act as an engineering lead auditing the code review process for this team. Analyze recent merged PRs to assess whether reviews are producing quality outcomes. Identify PRs that were approved with no feedback — a signal of rubber-stamping. Find PRs so large that effective review is impossible. Flag categories of issues that reviewers consistently miss — security, error handling, edge cases, or test coverage. Then evaluate the review process itself: turnaround times, reviewer load distribution, and whether the right people are reviewing the right code. Output a numbered set of findings with specific PRs as evidence, followed by recommended process and tooling changes ordered by expected quality improvement.

title: Evaluate this codebase's code review standards as a security control
prompt: Evaluate this codebase's code review standards as a security control. Determine whether the review process would catch the most common vulnerability classes — injection, broken auth, sensitive data exposure, insecure deserialization, and missing input validation. Review the last 20 merged PRs for examples of each vulnerability class passing review undetected. Assess whether there is a security checklist in use and whether reviewers have the training to apply it. Output findings in full paragraphs with vulnerability type, example from the PR history, and the specific review practice or automated check that would have caught it.

title: Perform a review quality audit across all PRs merged in the last 60 days
prompt: Perform a review quality audit across all PRs merged in the last 60 days. For each PR, assess whether the review produced meaningful feedback, whether the description gave reviewers enough context to review effectively, and whether the test coverage was verified before approval. Identify the reviewers with the highest and lowest feedback rates. Find PRs where the reviewer and author are the same person, where self-merges occurred, or where merge happened within minutes of approval suggesting the process was bypassed. Output a full report with numbered findings ordered by process risk.

title: Evaluate the code review process as a knowledge transfer mechanism
prompt: Evaluate the code review process as a knowledge transfer mechanism. Beyond catching bugs, code review is the primary mechanism for spreading architectural understanding, coding conventions, and domain knowledge across the team. Assess whether the current review process supports this goal — are junior engineers regularly assigned to review complex PRs with senior guidance? Are reviewers required to understand the full context of a change rather than approving based on surface syntax? Find patterns where rubber-stamp approvals pass code that the reviewer could not explain. Identify modules where only one or two engineers are capable of giving meaningful reviews because all domain knowledge is concentrated. Recommend specific changes to the review assignment process, reviewer preparation requirements, and review comment quality standards that would make code review a genuine knowledge-transfer event rather than a compliance step.

title: Perform a retrospective audit of code reviews for escaped production def
prompt: Perform a retrospective audit of code reviews for escaped production defects. For each production incident in the last 12 months, trace the root cause to a specific code change. Identify the PR that introduced the bug, the reviewer who approved it, and the review comment that should have caught it but did not. For each escaped defect, determine whether the gap was a knowledge problem, a process problem, or a tooling problem. Aggregate the findings across all incidents to identify the top three recurring review failure modes. Output a root cause analysis with specific PRs, followed by a prioritized set of changes to the review process, checklist, or tooling that would have prevented each class of escaped defect.

title: Audit all active feature flags and their owners
prompt: List every active feature flag. For each, identify: the owner, when it was created, what it controls, whether it has an expiry date, and what percentage of traffic it currently affects. Flag any with no owner or no expiry.
prompt_short: List all active feature flags with owner, age, and expiry status.
prompt_filtered: Flag inventory only. No code or behavior analysis.

title: Find stale flags that should have been cleaned up
prompt: Identify feature flags that are 100% rolled out (no longer gating anything), flags controlling deprecated features, and flags that have been in the codebase for more than 90 days without changes. Generate a cleanup priority list.
prompt_short: Find feature flags that are fully rolled out or expired and can be removed.
prompt_filtered: Stale flags only. 100% rollout and age-based identification.

title: Identify flag dependencies that create removal risk
prompt: Find feature flags that are nested inside other feature flags, or flags whose removal requires coordinated changes across multiple services. Identify flags that cannot be safely removed without a migration plan.
prompt_short: Find feature flags with complex dependencies that make removal risky.
prompt_filtered: Flag dependencies only. Removal risk and cross-service coupling.

title: Review flag targeting rules for correctness
prompt: Audit the targeting rules for all active flags. Identify rules with logic errors, rules that target more users than intended, and rules that could expose a beta feature to the wrong user segment. Verify each rule matches its stated intent.
prompt_short: Audit feature flag targeting rules for logic errors and scope creep.
prompt_filtered: Targeting rules only. Rule logic and intended audience alignment.

title: Find code paths gated by removed flags still in code
prompt: Search the codebase for references to feature flags that no longer exist in the flag management system. These create dead code paths that may be unreachable but still affect bundle size, test coverage, and readability.
prompt_short: Find code still referencing deleted or non-existent feature flags.
prompt_filtered: Dead code only. References to removed flags in source.

title: Design a kill-switch strategy for every active feature
prompt: For every feature currently behind a flag, verify there is a tested kill switch — a flag value that reverts to the previous behavior without a deployment. Identify features where the kill-switch path has not been tested and would produce errors if triggered during an incident.
prompt_short: Verify every flagged feature has a tested kill switch that works without a deploy.
prompt_filtered: Kill switches only. Tested reversion path for each active flag.

title: Implement sticky bucketing for consistent user experiences
prompt: Audit flags using percentage-based rollout for consistent user assignment. Find any flag that could assign the same user to different variants across sessions — creating a flickering experience. Implement sticky bucketing based on a stable user attribute (user ID hash) for all experiment flags.
prompt_short: Fix flags that can assign different variants to the same user across sessions.
prompt_filtered: Sticky bucketing only. Consistent variant assignment per user.

title: Create an environment override system for flag testing
prompt: Implement a mechanism for engineers to override individual flag values in their local or staging environment without changing the flag's production configuration. Verify the override mechanism is scoped to non-production environments and cannot accidentally reach production.
prompt_short: Add local and staging environment flag overrides without touching production config.
prompt_filtered: Environment overrides only. Non-production flag value injection.

title: Audit flag debt and estimate cleanup cost
prompt: Count the total number of feature flags currently in the codebase. Calculate the number of conditional branches introduced per flag. Estimate the total lines of code that could be deleted if all shipped flags were cleaned up. Rank cleanup candidates by lines-of-code reduction and removal safety.
prompt_short: Calculate total feature flag debt and rank cleanup candidates by code reduction.
prompt_filtered: Flag debt only. LOC reduction estimate and cleanup priority ranking.

title: Set up flag evaluation performance monitoring
prompt: Identify flags evaluated on every request in hot paths. Measure or estimate the latency added by flag evaluation SDK calls, especially for flags that make network calls to a remote flag service. Recommend local caching, SDK streaming updates, or edge-evaluated flags to keep latency under 1ms per evaluation.
prompt_short: Find flags in hot paths with evaluation overhead and recommend caching strategies.
prompt_filtered: Flag evaluation latency only. SDK call cost in high-traffic request paths.

title: Define a flag lifecycle policy with automatic expiry
prompt: Create a policy that assigns a maximum age to each flag type — experiment flags expire after 30 days, ops flags after 90 days, and long-term kill switches are exempt. Configure the flag system to warn owners 14 days before expiry and auto-disable the flag if not extended by the owner.
prompt_short: Define flag lifecycle policies with automatic expiry and owner notifications.
prompt_filtered: Flag lifecycle only. Expiry policy by flag type and auto-disable behavior.

title: Review flag evaluation logging for audit compliance
prompt: Assess whether flag evaluations for compliance-relevant features (payment gating, access control, data visibility) are logged with the user ID, flag value assigned, and timestamp. Verify logs are retained for the required compliance window and are queryable for audit requests.
prompt_short: Verify flag evaluations for access-control features are logged for compliance.
prompt_filtered: Flag audit logging only. Evaluation logs for compliance-relevant flags.

title: Find flags controlling multiple unrelated behaviors in one key
prompt: Identify flags that are used to gate more than one logically distinct feature or behavior. These create entanglement — you cannot enable one behavior without the other, and rollback affects both. Recommend splitting each into single-purpose flags.
prompt_short: Find flags gating multiple unrelated behaviors that should be split.
prompt_filtered: Flag scope only. Single-responsibility principle for flag design.

title: Implement a flag change approval workflow
prompt: Design a workflow where changes to flags affecting more than 10% of production traffic require a second engineer to approve before the change takes effect. Define the approval interface, notification method, and the audit log entry that records who approved and when.
prompt_short: Require approval for high-impact flag changes above a traffic threshold.
prompt_filtered: Flag change approval only. Workflow for high-traffic flag modifications.

title: Assess flag system vendor lock-in and migration path
prompt: Evaluate how tightly the codebase is coupled to the current feature flag vendor's SDK. Find direct SDK calls throughout the codebase versus a thin wrapper layer. Estimate the migration effort to switch vendors. Recommend a flag abstraction layer that decouples business logic from the flag SDK.
prompt_short: Assess vendor lock-in in the feature flag SDK and recommend an abstraction layer.
prompt_filtered: Vendor coupling only. SDK call concentration and abstraction strategy.

title: Design a flag rollout playbook for safe progressive release
prompt: Create a standardized flag rollout playbook: start at 1% for internal users, then 5% for beta users, then 20% with error budget monitoring, then 50% after 24 hours with no incidents, then 100%. Define the metrics to check at each stage and the rollback trigger criteria.
prompt_short: Write a staged flag rollout playbook with metrics and rollback criteria at each step.
prompt_filtered: Rollout playbook only. Staged percentage rollout with metric checks.

title: Audit flags for experiment collision in shared user segments
prompt: Find cases where multiple active experiment flags target the same user segment simultaneously. Assess whether the experiments could interfere with each other. Recommend mutual exclusion groups for experiments that test related features and could confound each other results.
prompt_short: Find experiment flags that may interfere with each other in overlapping user segments.
prompt_filtered: Experiment collision only. Overlapping flag targeting and mutual exclusion.

title: Implement flag state serialization for client-side rendering
prompt: Audit how feature flag values are delivered to the client-side application. Find cases where flags are evaluated server-side and the initial render differs from the hydrated client render, causing layout shifts or flicker. Implement server-to-client flag state serialization so the client hydrates with the same flag values the server used.
prompt_short: Fix flag-caused hydration mismatches by serializing server flag state to the client.
prompt_filtered: Client hydration only. Server-to-client flag state serialization.

title: Set up automated flag cleanup PRs for fully-shipped features
prompt: Configure a bot that opens a cleanup PR automatically when a feature flag reaches 100% rollout for more than 14 days. The PR should remove the flag check, delete the off-path code, and update tests. Flag the PR for the owning engineer to review.
prompt_short: Automate cleanup PRs when flags reach 100% rollout for more than 14 days.
prompt_filtered: Flag cleanup automation only. Bot-generated cleanup PRs for shipped features.

title: Measure flag evaluation latency impact on request p99
prompt: Instrument the flag SDK to measure the latency added by flag evaluation per request at the p99 level. Find request paths where 5 or more flags are evaluated sequentially. Parallelize independent evaluations, add a local in-process cache with a short TTL, and verify the p99 latency reduction.
prompt_short: Measure and reduce p99 latency from sequential feature flag evaluations per request.
prompt_filtered: Flag latency only. Evaluation time at p99 and parallelization opportunities.

title: Assess Flag Removal Risk
prompt: Assess Flag Removal Risk

title: Check Flag Ownership
prompt: Check Flag Ownership

title: Audit all active feature flags and their current owners
prompt: Audit all active feature flags and their current owners. Identify flags with no assigned owner, no documented purpose, or no planned removal date. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by staleness. Include the flag key, creation date, and the team responsible for its lifecycle.

title: Find stale feature flags that should have been cleaned up
prompt: Find stale feature flags that should have been cleaned up. Identify flags that are fully rolled out to all users, flags referencing features that shipped more than 90 days ago, and flags with no recent evaluation in the flag system. Output a numbered list of stale flags with the flag key, rollout status, and removal safety assessment.

title: Identify feature flag dependencies that create removal risk
prompt: Identify feature flag dependencies that create removal risk. Find flags that control other flags, flags checked in multiple code paths with different effects, and flags whose removal would require coordination across teams. Output findings in full paragraphs with the dependency graph for each complex flag.

title: Review flag targeting rules for correctness and security
prompt: Review flag targeting rules for correctness and security. Find flags targeting production users with rules that were meant for staging, flags with overlapping percentage rollouts that could double-count users, and flags with no kill switch if the feature causes incidents. Output a numbered list of targeting issues with the flag key and the corrected rule.

title: Find all code still referencing removed feature flags
prompt: Find all code still referencing removed feature flags. Search for flag keys that no longer exist in the flag management system. Identify which code paths are now unreachable dead code. Output findings in full paragraphs with the flag key, the file and line of each reference, and the cleanup steps needed.

title: Audit all feature flags for documentation completeness
prompt: Audit all feature flags for documentation completeness. Find flags with no description of what they control, no rollout history, no owner contact, and no removal criteria. Identify flags that a new engineer could not understand without reading the source code that references them. Output a numbered list of undocumented flags with the flag key and the minimum documentation required for each.

title: Identify feature flags that have been enabled in production for more tha
prompt: Identify feature flags that have been enabled in production for more than 180 days without being cleaned up. For each, determine whether the feature is fully shipped, still being evaluated, or abandoned. Find flags where the off-branch code path is the fallback for a feature that no one has used in production in the last 90 days. Output findings in full paragraphs with the flag key, production age, current state, and recommended cleanup action.

title: Review the feature flag SDK integration for performance and reliability
prompt: Review the feature flag SDK integration for performance and reliability. Find every location where a flag is evaluated and assess whether the evaluation is synchronous with the request path, whether it falls back gracefully if the flag service is unavailable, and whether the local cache has an appropriate TTL. Output a numbered list of integration points with the evaluation pattern, fallback behavior, and any latency concern.

title: Act as a feature flag program manager auditing the full flag inventory f
prompt: Act as a feature flag program manager auditing the full flag inventory for this system. Identify every active flag, its owner, its purpose, its current rollout state, and its planned removal date. Flag flags with no owner, no documented purpose, or no removal plan. Assess flags that have been fully rolled out for more than 90 days — these are candidates for permanent enablement and code cleanup. Find flags with complex targeting rules that could produce unintended behavior for edge-case users. Map any flag dependencies where disabling one flag affects the behavior of another. Output a full inventory with health status per flag and a prioritized cleanup backlog.

title: Evaluate the technical debt created by this codebase's feature flag usag
prompt: Evaluate the technical debt created by this codebase's feature flag usage. Find every place in the source code where a feature flag is checked. Identify conditional branches that have accumulated over multiple releases and now represent permanent dead code, flag checks in hot paths that add measurable latency, and flags whose logic is duplicated across multiple files rather than centralized. Estimate the lines of code that could be deleted if all fully-shipped flags were cleaned up. Output findings in full paragraphs with specific file paths, followed by a recommended cleanup order starting with the lowest-risk removals.

title: Perform a feature flag security audit
prompt: Perform a feature flag security audit. Identify flags that control access to privileged features — admin capabilities, payment flows, or data export — and assess whether the targeting rules are tight enough to prevent unauthorized access. Find flags that could be exploited by manipulating user attributes, cookie values, or URL parameters to gain access to unreleased features. Check whether flag evaluation is logged for audit purposes. Assess whether there is a kill switch process for flags that cause incidents in production. Output a numbered findings list ordered by security risk.

title: Evaluate the feature flag system as a risk management tool
prompt: Evaluate the feature flag system as a risk management tool. Assess whether the organization uses flags consistently to decouple deployment from feature exposure, or whether flags are only added for large risky features while smaller changes ship directly to all users. Identify the classes of changes that bypass flagging — configuration changes, schema migrations, A-B experiments initiated without a flag — that could benefit from the same progressive exposure model. Assess whether the kill switch for every active feature has been tested in the last 90 days. Find flags where the off-branch code path has diverged so significantly from the on-path that toggling the kill switch would produce a jarring user experience. Output a risk assessment covering flag coverage gaps, kill switch readiness, and recommended policies for when a flag is required versus optional.

title: Perform an experiment integrity audit across all active A-B test feature
prompt: Perform an experiment integrity audit across all active A-B test feature flags. Assess whether the experiments currently running are designed to produce statistically valid results. Find experiments with no defined sample size, no defined success metric, no planned end date, and no statistical test for significance. Identify experiments that have been running for more than 60 days without a decision — signals that the experiment is a zombie with no owner driving a conclusion. Assess whether experiments are using the correct assignment unit — user-level for conversion experiments, session-level for engagement experiments — and whether there is any risk of contamination between experiments targeting the same user segment. Output findings as a numbered list of active experiments with their integrity assessment and recommended action per experiment.

title: Find all hardcoded secrets and credentials in source
prompt: Scan the entire codebase for hardcoded secrets — API keys, passwords, tokens, connection strings, private keys. Flag by severity based on what the secret grants access to. Include config files, test files, and comments.
prompt_short: Scan source code for all hardcoded secrets and credentials.
prompt_filtered: Hardcoded secrets only. Source code, config files, and comments.

title: Audit which services have access to which secrets
prompt: Map every secret to the services that consume it. Identify services with access to secrets they don't need (principle of least privilege violations). Flag any secret shared across more services than necessary.
prompt_short: Map secret access to services and find least-privilege violations.
prompt_filtered: Secret access mapping only. Which service needs which secret.

title: Review secret rotation procedures and last rotation dates
prompt: For each production secret, verify there is a documented rotation procedure and record of when it was last rotated. Identify secrets that have never been rotated, secrets past their rotation policy, and secrets with no rotation runbook.
prompt_short: Audit secret rotation procedures and find overdue rotations.
prompt_filtered: Rotation status only. Last rotation date and procedure existence.

title: Check secrets are not logged or exposed in error messages
prompt: Review error handling and logging code for patterns that could expose secret values — logging the full environment, printing exception details that include config values, or returning internal config in API error responses.
prompt_short: Find places where secret values could appear in logs or error responses.
prompt_filtered: Secret exposure in logs only. Error handling and logging patterns.

title: Verify secrets are scoped to the correct environment
prompt: Confirm that production secrets are not used in development or staging environments and vice versa. Identify any cross-environment secret sharing, and assess the blast radius if a development environment is compromised.
prompt_short: Verify production secrets are never shared with dev or staging.
prompt_filtered: Environment scoping only. Cross-env secret leakage risks.

title: Define secret rotation cadence by sensitivity tier
prompt: Classify every production secret by sensitivity tier — database credentials, third-party API keys, signing keys, webhook tokens. Define a maximum rotation interval per tier (e.g., database credentials rotate every 30 days, webhook tokens every 90 days) and verify the current rotation schedule meets each threshold.
prompt_short: Define rotation cadence by secret type and find secrets past their rotation deadline.
prompt_filtered: Rotation cadence only. Tier classification and interval enforcement.

title: Integrate gitleaks into CI for pre-push secret scanning
prompt: Configure gitleaks or trufflehog to run in CI and block pushes that introduce secret patterns into tracked files. Define custom rules for this project's secret formats (internal API key prefixes, proprietary token formats). Test the integration with a synthetic secret to verify detection.
prompt_short: Add gitleaks to CI to block commits that contain secret patterns.
prompt_filtered: Secret scanning only. CI-integrated gitleaks or trufflehog setup.

title: Implement Vault or cloud secret manager for dynamic secrets
prompt: Design a migration from static long-lived credentials to dynamic secrets using HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, or GCP Secret Manager. Identify the highest-value secrets to migrate first — database credentials that can be issued per-service with short TTLs. Define the renewal and revocation flow.
prompt_short: Design a migration to dynamic short-lived secrets using a secret manager.
prompt_filtered: Dynamic secrets only. Vault or cloud SM integration for database credentials.

title: Audit CI/CD pipeline for secret exposure via logs or artifacts
prompt: Review every CI/CD pipeline step for commands that could print environment variables to build logs, copy secrets into build artifacts, or pass secrets as build arguments that get baked into Docker layers. Verify that secret masking is enabled on the CI platform and test that it prevents exposure.
prompt_short: Find all ways CI pipeline steps could leak secrets into logs or build artifacts.
prompt_filtered: CI secret exposure only. Log printing, build args, and artifact inclusion.

title: Create a secret compromise response runbook
prompt: Write a runbook for responding to a secret compromise: detect, contain (rotate immediately), assess blast radius, audit access logs for unauthorized use during the exposure window, notify affected parties if required, and post-mortem. Assign owners for each step and verify the runbook is tested quarterly.
prompt_short: Write and test a secret compromise response runbook with assigned owners.
prompt_filtered: Incident response only. Compromise detection, rotation, and blast radius assessment.

title: Audit service-to-service authentication for shared secret anti-patterns
prompt: Review how internal services authenticate to each other. Find services using a shared API key passed in headers (any service with the key can impersonate any other), and recommend migrating to short-lived mutual TLS certificates or workload identity tokens (SPIFFE/SPIRE) that provide cryptographic identity per service.
prompt_short: Replace shared service-to-service API keys with workload identity or mTLS.
prompt_filtered: Service auth only. Shared key anti-patterns and workload identity migration.

title: Implement sealed secrets for Kubernetes secret management
prompt: Audit whether Kubernetes secrets are stored in plain YAML in the git repository. Configure Bitnami Sealed Secrets or External Secrets Operator to encrypt secret values before committing to source control. Define the key rotation procedure and test decryption after a controller restart.
prompt_short: Replace plain Kubernetes secret YAML with encrypted sealed secrets in git.
prompt_filtered: Kubernetes secrets only. Sealed secrets or External Secrets Operator setup.

title: Measure time-to-revoke for all production secrets
prompt: For each production secret, measure or estimate how quickly it can be revoked if compromised — from detection to the secret being invalid. Identify secrets that require a deployment to rotate (slow) versus secrets managed by a secret store with hot reload (fast). Target a time-to-revoke under 15 minutes for all critical secrets.
prompt_short: Measure time-to-revoke for each production secret and find the slowest ones.
prompt_filtered: Revocation speed only. Time from compromise detection to secret invalidation.

title: Enforce secret presence validation at service startup
prompt: Add startup assertions that verify all required secrets are present and non-empty before the service accepts traffic. Verify the error message on missing secrets is informative but does not reveal which secret or its expected format. Test that a misconfigured deployment fails fast rather than starting with silent fallback behavior.
prompt_short: Add startup validation that fails fast when required secrets are missing.
prompt_filtered: Startup validation only. Missing secret detection before first request.

title: Produce a secrets inventory report for compliance
prompt: Generate a complete inventory of all secrets in use — type, storage location, services with access, last rotation date, and owner. Format the inventory for a SOC 2 or ISO 27001 compliance review. Identify gaps where owner is unknown, rotation date is missing, or storage location is non-compliant.
prompt_short: Create a secrets inventory report formatted for SOC 2 or ISO 27001 compliance.
prompt_filtered: Secrets inventory only. Type, owner, rotation date, and storage location.

title: Implement short-lived credentials for all service-to-service calls
prompt: Audit all service-to-service authentication. Replace long-lived shared API keys with short-lived credentials issued by a central authority. Define the maximum TTL per credential type and the renewal mechanism.
prompt_short: Replace long-lived service API keys with short-lived credentials from a central issuer.
prompt_filtered: Credential TTL only. Short-lived service-to-service credential issuance.

title: Audit secrets access in third-party CI integrations
prompt: Review which third-party tools (bots, code scanners, analytics integrations) have been granted access to the CI platform and what secrets they can access. Find integrations with broader secret access than needed. Revoke over-privileged integrations and re-grant with minimum required scopes.
prompt_short: Audit third-party CI integrations for over-privileged secret access.
prompt_filtered: Third-party CI access only. Bot and integration secret scope review.

title: Set up secret scanning alerts on every push with auto-block
prompt: Enable GitHub Advanced Security or an equivalent secret scanning tool that blocks pushes containing detected secrets in real time. Configure custom patterns for internal secret formats. Set up alerts to the security team when a scan detects and blocks a commit.
prompt_short: Enable push-blocking secret scanning with alerts for every detected credential.
prompt_filtered: Push-block scanning only. Real-time secret detection that blocks the push.

title: Review secret access patterns for anomaly detection
prompt: Instrument the secret manager to log every secret access with the accessor identity, timestamp, and source IP. Configure anomaly detection alerts for access outside normal hours, from unexpected IPs, or at volumes above the baseline. Verify logs are retained for the required compliance period.
prompt_short: Add anomaly detection to secret access logs and alert on unusual access patterns.
prompt_filtered: Access anomaly detection only. Secret manager audit log and alert configuration.

title: Evaluate and enforce secrets manager client-side caching policy
prompt: Review how applications cache secrets retrieved from the secret manager. Find services that cache secrets indefinitely, services that re-fetch on every request, and services with no cache invalidation on rotation event. Define a caching policy with TTL equal to half the rotation interval and rotation event invalidation.
prompt_short: Set a secrets cache TTL equal to half the rotation interval with rotation event invalidation.
prompt_filtered: Secrets caching only. Client-side cache TTL and rotation event handling.

title: Verify Environment Scoping
prompt: Verify Environment Scoping

title: Find Over-Privileged Secrets
prompt: Find Over-Privileged Secrets

title: Audit Secret Store Coverage
prompt: Audit Secret Store Coverage

title: Find all hardcoded secrets and credentials in the source code
prompt: Find all hardcoded secrets and credentials in the source code. Scan every file including config files, test files, and comments for API keys, passwords, tokens, and connection strings. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by severity based on what access the secret grants. Include the file path and the remediation step for each.

title: Audit which services have access to which secrets
prompt: Audit which services have access to which secrets. Map every secret to the services that consume it. Identify services with access to secrets they do not need. Output a numbered list of least-privilege violations with the service name, secret name, and the minimum required scope.

title: Review secret rotation procedures and last rotation dates
prompt: Review secret rotation procedures and last rotation dates. For each production secret, verify there is a documented rotation procedure and a record of when it was last rotated. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by rotation urgency. Flag secrets that have never been rotated or are past their rotation policy.

title: Check whether secrets appear in logs or error messages
prompt: Check whether secrets appear in logs or error messages. Find logging statements that could print environment variables, request headers, or exception details containing credentials. Output a numbered list of log exposure risks with the file, line, and the data masking approach needed.

title: Verify secrets are scoped to the correct environment
prompt: Verify secrets are scoped to the correct environment. Identify any production secret used in development or staging, and any staging secret with access to production resources. Output findings in full paragraphs with the secret name, the cross-environment usage, and the remediation to enforce correct scoping.

title: Audit secret access logs for anomalous access patterns
prompt: Audit secret access logs for anomalous access patterns. Find service accounts accessing secrets they have never accessed before, secrets accessed outside of deployment windows, and secrets accessed at rates higher than expected for normal operation. Output findings in full paragraphs with the secret name, access pattern, and whether the pattern is consistent with a known deployment or a potential credential leak.

title: Review the secret rotation history for all production credentials
prompt: Review the secret rotation history for all production credentials. Find secrets that have not been rotated in more than 90 days, secrets with no rotation schedule, and secrets last rotated by a departed team member. Assess the risk of each unrotated secret — how many systems use it and what access it grants. Output a numbered list of overdue rotations ordered by risk, with the secret name, age, and recommended rotation approach.

title: Identify secrets that are duplicated across multiple secret stores or co
prompt: Identify secrets that are duplicated across multiple secret stores or configuration systems. Find the same credential appearing in both environment variables and a secret manager, or the same API key injected into multiple services from different storage locations. Assess the risk of the copies drifting out of sync. Output findings in full paragraphs with the duplicate locations and the recommended single source of truth.

title: Act as a secrets management auditor performing a full inventory of how c
prompt: Act as a secrets management auditor performing a full inventory of how credentials are stored, accessed, rotated, and revoked across this system. Identify every secret in use — API keys, database credentials, signing keys, OAuth client secrets, and webhook tokens. For each, assess whether it is stored in a proper secret store or hardcoded somewhere in the codebase, whether it follows least-privilege access, whether there is a documented rotation procedure with a record of last rotation, and whether it is scoped correctly to only the environments that need it. Output a numbered findings list with the secret type, current storage and access state, rotation status, and remediation priority.

title: Evaluate the blast radius if this system's secrets were fully compromise
prompt: Evaluate the blast radius if this system's secrets were fully compromised. For each production secret, determine the maximum damage an attacker could cause with that credential — data exfiltration, lateral movement, financial fraud, or service disruption. Identify which secrets provide the highest-value attack surface. Assess whether revocation is possible within minutes for each secret and what the recovery procedure looks like. Find any secrets that cannot be revoked — such as long-lived API keys with no rotation mechanism. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by blast radius, with the secret type, maximum impact, revocability, and recommended hardening.

title: Perform a secrets exposure audit across all the system's log outputs, er
prompt: Perform a secrets exposure audit across all the system's log outputs, error messages, and monitoring pipelines. Trace how sensitive values flow through the application — from environment variables through middleware, request handlers, and error formatters. Identify any code path where a secret value could appear in a log line, a Sentry event, a debug endpoint response, or a monitoring dashboard. Assess whether the logging library is configured to mask sensitive fields by default. Find any CI/CD pipeline where secrets are echoed to build logs. Output a numbered list of exposure vectors with severity, the specific code path, and the masking or redaction fix needed.

title: Evaluate the secrets management posture of this system against the princ
prompt: Evaluate the secrets management posture of this system against the principle of least privilege. For each service, assess the set of secrets it can access at runtime and whether every secret it has access to is actually used. Find services with overly broad secret access — a read-only API service that has access to a write-capable database credential, or a frontend service that can read backend service-to-service authentication tokens. Assess whether secrets are scoped to the environment they serve — production secrets inaccessible from staging, staging secrets inaccessible from development. Find any configuration where a secret is injected into a service by default and then selectively disabled, rather than requiring explicit opt-in. Output a least-privilege audit with findings per service, specific excess permissions, and the refactored access model.

title: Perform a secrets exposure audit focusing on the paths by which a secret
prompt: Perform a secrets exposure audit focusing on the paths by which a secret could leak from the secret store to an unintended recipient. Trace each secret from its storage location through injection into a service, through the service's handling at runtime, to the points where it could appear in a log, an error message, a stack trace, an API response, or a monitoring dashboard. Identify secrets passed as command-line arguments — visible in process listings — secrets logged in verbose mode, secrets included in exception messages sent to error tracking services, and secrets returned in API responses when the system is misconfigured. For each leakage path found, state the secret type, the leakage mechanism, and the code or configuration change that closes it. Output as a numbered leakage path analysis ordered by ease of exploitation.

title: Find the highest-value manual tasks to automate now
prompt: Survey all manual recurring tasks across development, deployment, and operations. Rank by frequency × time cost × error rate. Identify the top 5 automation opportunities with the highest ROI and lowest implementation complexity.
prompt_short: Find the top 5 manual tasks worth automating based on time and error cost.
prompt_filtered: Automation candidates only. Frequency and time cost analysis.

title: Audit existing automation for reliability and coverage
prompt: Review all existing automation scripts, workflows, and cron jobs. Identify those with no error handling, no alerting on failure, no documentation, and those that silently succeed even when they didn't actually complete their task.
prompt_short: Audit existing automation for silent failures and missing error handling.
prompt_filtered: Automation reliability only. Silent failures and missing error handling.

title: Identify automation running more often than needed
prompt: Review scheduled jobs and event-driven automation. Identify tasks that run more frequently than their actual need — wasteful in cost and adding unnecessary load. Recommend the minimum viable schedule for each.
prompt_short: Find automation jobs running too frequently and wasting compute.
prompt_filtered: Frequency optimization only. Schedule vs actual need alignment.

title: Find manual approval steps that block automation flow
prompt: Identify where automation pipelines stall waiting for human approval. For each, assess whether the approval is adding real value or is a rubber stamp. Recommend which approvals to keep, automate with checks, or eliminate.
prompt_short: Find manual approval steps in automation pipelines that could be removed.
prompt_filtered: Approval bottlenecks only. Which approvals add value vs block flow.

title: Review automation documentation and runbook coverage
prompt: For every automated process, verify there is documentation explaining what it does, when it runs, what it touches, and what to do if it fails. Identify automation that is opaque to the team and would cause confusion during an incident.
prompt_short: Find automation with no documentation or runbook for incident recovery.
prompt_filtered: Documentation only. Automation opacity and incident response gaps.

title: Audit scheduled job drift and clock skew across services
prompt: Review all scheduled tasks (cron jobs, Celery beats, cloud schedulers) for clock drift — tasks that should run at the same time across services but are offset due to timezone mismatches or scheduler jitter. Identify tasks that overlap and contend for the same resources.
prompt_short: Find scheduled job timing drift and resource contention across services.
prompt_filtered: Job scheduling only. Clock skew, timezone, and resource contention.

title: Ensure all automation jobs are idempotent
prompt: Review every automated job for idempotency — verify that running the job twice produces the same outcome as running it once. Identify jobs that would cause duplicates, double-charges, or corrupt state if triggered more than once (due to at-least-once delivery or manual reruns).
prompt_short: Verify all automation jobs are idempotent and safe to rerun without side effects.
prompt_filtered: Idempotency only. Duplicate execution safety for all automated jobs.

title: Map choreography vs. orchestration in event-driven workflows
prompt: Audit the event-driven automation architecture. Identify workflows using pure choreography (services react to events independently) and assess whether the lack of a central coordinator makes tracing failures difficult. Recommend where orchestration (a workflow engine with explicit state) would improve observability and error recovery.
prompt_short: Assess whether choreography or orchestration is the right pattern for each workflow.
prompt_filtered: Workflow pattern only. Choreography vs orchestration trade-off analysis.

title: Review automation for missing dead-letter queue handling
prompt: Find all message queues and event buses in the automation pipeline. Identify queues with no dead-letter queue (DLQ) configured — meaning failed messages are silently dropped. Verify each DLQ has alerting and a defined reprocessing procedure.
prompt_short: Find queues with no dead-letter queue and add DLQ alerting and reprocessing.
prompt_filtered: DLQ coverage only. Message queue failure handling and DLQ setup.

title: Identify automation that bypasses application business logic
prompt: Find automation scripts that write directly to the database or call internal functions, bypassing the application layer's validation, audit logging, and event emission. Assess the risk of data corruption or missed audit trail entries. Recommend routing automation through the application API instead.
prompt_short: Find automation that writes directly to the DB and bypasses app-layer validation.
prompt_filtered: Bypass risk only. Automation that skips application validation and audit logging.

title: Design rate limiting and backpressure for high-volume automation
prompt: Identify automation jobs that can generate spikes of outbound API calls or database writes during batch runs. For each, implement rate limiting (token bucket or fixed window), exponential backoff on failures, and backpressure mechanisms that pause the job when downstream systems signal saturation.
prompt_short: Add rate limiting and backpressure to batch automation jobs that can overload downstream systems.
prompt_filtered: Rate limiting only. Backpressure and throttling for high-volume automation.

title: Audit automation for hard-coded credential and endpoint risks
prompt: Scan all automation scripts, workflow definitions, and scheduled job configs for hardcoded credentials, hardcoded base URLs, and hardcoded environment assumptions. Identify jobs that would send production data to a development endpoint if deployed in the wrong environment.
prompt_short: Find hardcoded credentials and endpoints in automation scripts.
prompt_filtered: Config safety only. Hardcoded values in automation that vary by environment.

title: Set up automation observability with structured logging
prompt: Review current automation logging. Find jobs that produce no logs on success (making it impossible to verify they ran), jobs that log raw objects (making logs unsearchable), and jobs with no correlation ID linking log lines to a specific run. Implement structured JSON logging with run_id, job_name, start_time, duration, and outcome for every job.
prompt_short: Add structured logging with run_id and outcome to all automation jobs.
prompt_filtered: Automation logging only. Structured log format and run traceability.

title: Test automation failure recovery with chaos injection
prompt: Design and run chaos tests for critical automation workflows — inject failures at each step (downstream API timeout, database connection reset, partial write) and verify the automation correctly retries, records the failure, and leaves the system in a consistent state. Document the failure modes that are currently unhandled.
prompt_short: Run chaos tests on automation jobs to find unhandled failure modes.
prompt_filtered: Failure recovery only. Chaos injection at each automation step.

title: Build an automation health dashboard
prompt: Create a dashboard that shows the status of every scheduled and event-driven automation job: last run time, duration, success rate over the last 7 days, and current status (healthy, degraded, failing). Add alerts for jobs that have not run within 2× their scheduled interval and for jobs with a success rate below 95%.
prompt_short: Build a health dashboard for all automation jobs with alerting on missed runs.
prompt_filtered: Automation monitoring only. Job health metrics, run frequency, and success rate.

title: Implement workflow orchestration for multi-step automation
prompt: Identify multi-step automation workflows currently implemented as chained scripts with no visibility into intermediate state. Migrate the highest-risk workflows to an orchestration engine (Temporal, Conductor, or Step Functions) that provides durable execution, automatic retries, and a history of every step for debugging.
prompt_short: Migrate complex multi-step automation to a workflow orchestration engine.
prompt_filtered: Orchestration only. Workflow engine selection and migration for stateful automation.

title: Audit automation for time-zone and DST sensitivity
prompt: Review all scheduled jobs for time-zone assumptions. Find jobs defined in local time that will shift by one hour on daylight saving time transitions, causing either skipped runs or double runs. Convert all job schedules to UTC and verify that job logic does not assume wall-clock time of day.
prompt_short: Fix scheduled jobs that shift on DST transitions by converting all schedules to UTC.
prompt_filtered: DST sensitivity only. Timezone handling in scheduled job definitions.

title: Add cost tracking to automation job resource consumption
prompt: Instrument all automation jobs to emit resource consumption metrics per run. Aggregate these into a cost report per job per month. Identify the 3 most expensive jobs by resource cost and find the optimization with the highest cost-reduction-to-effort ratio.
prompt_short: Track and report resource consumption per automation job to identify costly outliers.
prompt_filtered: Automation cost only. Per-job resource metrics and monthly cost reporting.

title: Design a canary deployment for automation job updates
prompt: Implement a canary pattern for high-risk automation jobs: run the new version of the job against a 5% random sample of the workload alongside the old version, compare outputs, and only promote the new version to 100% if outputs match and error rates stay within threshold.
prompt_short: Run automation job updates as canaries against a small workload sample before full rollout.
prompt_filtered: Automation canary only. Side-by-side job version comparison before promotion.

title: Build a self-healing automation pattern for common failures
prompt: Identify the 5 most common automation failure modes and for each implement a self-healing handler: auto-rotate the expired credential, increase timeout and retry on downstream timeout, reject and quarantine malformed input, clear disk space, and back off for the rate limit window.
prompt_short: Implement self-healing handlers for the 5 most common automation failure modes.
prompt_filtered: Self-healing only. Automated recovery handlers for known failure patterns.
