title: Map Automation Dependencies
prompt: Map Automation Dependencies

title: Find Automation Failure Risks
prompt: Find Automation Failure Risks

title: Find the highest-value manual tasks to automate now
prompt: Find the highest-value manual tasks to automate now. Survey the team's most time-consuming repeatable work. Identify tasks done more than weekly that follow the same steps each time. Output a numbered list ordered by time saved per week with the task name, current frequency, estimated automation effort, and recommended tooling.

title: Audit existing automation for reliability and coverage gaps
prompt: Audit existing automation for reliability and coverage gaps. Find automated processes that fail silently, have no alerting on failure, or are not monitored for drift. Output findings in full paragraphs with the automation name, failure mode, and the monitoring or alerting addition needed.

title: Identify automation running more often than needed
prompt: Identify automation running more often than needed. Find scheduled jobs whose output is the same for consecutive runs, jobs triggered by events that fire far more frequently than the job can keep up with, and jobs running at high frequency with low impact. Output a numbered list with the job name, current schedule, recommended schedule, and estimated compute savings.

title: Find manual approval steps blocking automation flow
prompt: Find manual approval steps blocking automation flow. Identify places in the system where a human must act before automation can proceed — deployments waiting on Slack approvals, data pipelines paused for manual review, or cron jobs that email a report instead of taking action. Output findings in full paragraphs with the bottleneck, the risk of automating it, and the recommended approach.

title: Review automation documentation and runbook coverage
prompt: Review automation documentation and runbook coverage. For every automated process, verify documentation exists covering what it does, when it runs, what it touches, and what to do when it fails. Output a numbered list of undocumented automation ordered by incident risk if it fails without a runbook.

title: Audit automation scripts for error handling completeness
prompt: Audit automation scripts for error handling completeness. Find scripts that exit with status 0 even when a step fails, scripts that swallow exceptions silently, and scripts that continue past a failed command instead of stopping. Identify the automation failures that could produce a system in a partially-applied state. Output a numbered list of error handling gaps with the script name, the problematic step, and the recommended failure behavior.

title: Review all scheduled automation jobs for observability
prompt: Review all scheduled automation jobs for observability. Find jobs with no logging, no alerting on failure, and no way to determine the last successful run time without reading log files manually. Identify jobs that are critical to operations but have no runbook explaining what they do and how to recover if they fail. Output findings in full paragraphs with the job name, current observability level, and recommended minimum monitoring.

title: Identify automation that has drifted from the manual process it was mean
prompt: Identify automation that has drifted from the manual process it was meant to replace. Find scripts that implement an older version of a workflow that the team has since updated manually but not updated in the automation. Identify the divergence by comparing the script steps to the current runbook. Output a numbered list of drifted automations with the script, the divergent step, and the updated procedure it should follow.

title: Act as an automation engineer auditing the full landscape of automated p
prompt: Act as an automation engineer auditing the full landscape of automated processes in this system. Inventory every scheduled job, triggered automation, webhook handler, and batch process. For each, assess its reliability — does it have failure alerting, does it have idempotency guarantees, does it have a runbook for when it fails? Identify automation that runs silently and would be undetected for hours if it broke. Find automation that is over-scheduled for its actual output change rate. Map dependencies between automated processes so you know which failures cascade. Output a full inventory with a health rating per process and a prioritized improvement backlog.

title: Evaluate the automation coverage for this team's most repetitive manual
prompt: Evaluate the automation coverage for this team's most repetitive manual work. Interview the engineering workflow to identify tasks done more than once a week that follow the same steps each time — deployments, data exports, permission grants, environment resets, release notes generation. For each candidate, assess the automation value as time saved per week times the team size, the implementation effort in days, and the risk of automating incorrectly. Recommend the highest-ROI automation targets with a proposed implementation approach. Output as a numbered prioritized list with value estimate, effort estimate, and first step to automate.

title: Perform a reliability audit of all existing automation in this system
prompt: Perform a reliability audit of all existing automation in this system. For each automated process, trace what happens when it fails — does it retry, does it alert, does it leave data in an inconsistent state? Identify automation that is not idempotent and would cause duplicate effects if retried. Find processes with no timeout that could block indefinitely. Assess whether automation failures are observable in monitoring or would only be noticed when a downstream system breaks. Recommend specific reliability improvements — idempotency keys, timeout configuration, failure alerting, and dead-letter queues — for each at-risk process. Output as a numbered findings list ordered by blast radius of failure.

title: Evaluate the automation strategy for this system holistically
prompt: Evaluate the automation strategy for this system holistically. Assess what percentage of routine operational tasks are automated and what percentage still require manual execution. Identify the tasks that are run manually most frequently — based on runbook usage, Slack message patterns, or on-call handoff notes — and assess whether each one is a good automation candidate. Evaluate the quality of existing automation: whether scripts are idempotent, whether they validate pre-conditions before executing, whether they leave the system in a known good state on failure, and whether they are tested before being used in production. Find automation that is used so rarely that engineers no longer remember how to run it correctly. Output a full automation coverage assessment with findings ordered by operational risk from untested or unreliable automation.

title: Perform a runbook-to-automation gap analysis for this system
prompt: Perform a runbook-to-automation gap analysis for this system. For every operational runbook in the repository, assess whether the steps described could be automated. Categorize each step as: already automated, automatable with low effort, automatable with high effort, or requires human judgment and cannot be automated. Calculate the total human-hours per month spent on manual runbook steps across the engineering team. Identify the top three runbooks by time consumed that could be partially or fully automated in the next quarter. For each, outline the automation approach, the estimated implementation effort, and the monthly time savings. Output as a prioritized automation backlog with business case per item.

title: Audit first-clone setup for friction and missing steps
prompt: Walk through the entire first-clone setup process as a new developer. Identify every step that requires tribal knowledge, an undocumented tool, or help from a teammate. List them in order of blocking impact.
prompt_short: Find all first-clone setup steps that require tribal knowledge to complete.
prompt_filtered: Onboarding setup only. Steps not covered in existing docs.

title: Find the slowest parts of the local dev feedback loop
prompt: Assess the time from code change to seeing the result in local dev. Identify the bottlenecks — slow builds, missing hot reload, slow test runs, large seed datasets. Estimate the time lost per developer per day.
prompt_short: Find what makes local development slow and quantify the daily cost.
prompt_filtered: Feedback loop speed only. Time from change to visible result.

title: Identify dev environment inconsistencies causing bugs
prompt: Find differences between developer machines, CI, and production environments that cause "works on my machine" bugs — OS differences, tool versions, environment variables, database seed state, service dependencies.
prompt_short: Find environment inconsistencies that cause different behavior on different machines.
prompt_filtered: Env parity only. Dev vs CI vs production divergence.

title: Review error messages and debugging experience quality
prompt: Assess whether error messages in development mode are useful. Identify errors that show generic messages, missing stack traces, or cryptic codes that send developers to Stack Overflow instead of pointing to the root cause.
prompt_short: Audit development error messages for clarity and actionability.
prompt_filtered: Error message quality only. Dev-mode error clarity and context.

title: Find missing tooling that would speed up development
prompt: Survey the team's development workflow and identify missing tools — code generators, database seeders, local service emulators, test data factories, browser dev extensions. Estimate time savings per tool per week.
prompt_short: Identify missing developer tools that would significantly speed up workflow.
prompt_filtered: Missing tooling only. Gap analysis, not existing tool review.

title: Define and measure developer experience metrics
prompt: Define the DX metrics this team will track: time to first local run for a new engineer, time from git push to CI result, build cold-start time, and hot-reload latency. Establish current baseline values for each. Set improvement targets for the next quarter and identify the single change with the largest expected impact.
prompt_short: Define DX metrics, measure baselines, and set improvement targets.
prompt_filtered: DX metrics only. Time to first run, CI feedback time, and hot-reload speed.

title: Create a golden path for common development tasks
prompt: Identify the 5 most common development tasks in this codebase — adding a new API endpoint, creating a component, writing a migration, adding a test, and deploying to staging. For each, document the canonical steps and automate as much as possible with a script or generator. The golden path should complete in under 5 minutes.
prompt_short: Document and automate the golden path for the 5 most common dev tasks.
prompt_filtered: Golden paths only. Canonical automated workflows for common development tasks.

title: Reduce local service dependency startup complexity
prompt: Audit the services a developer needs to run locally to work on any feature. Find services that require complex manual setup, services that need production credentials, and services that have no local emulator. Introduce docker-compose profiles or service stubs that let developers start only the services relevant to their current task.
prompt_short: Reduce local development service startup to only what each task needs.
prompt_filtered: Local service setup only. Startup complexity and docker-compose profiles.

title: Standardize code scaffolding with generators
prompt: Review how developers create new components, routes, services, and tests. Find places where scaffolding is done by copying an existing file and manually editing it — introducing copy-paste errors and inconsistency. Implement code generators (Plop, hygen, or custom scripts) for each repeated scaffold pattern.
prompt_short: Replace copy-paste scaffolding with automated code generators for common patterns.
prompt_filtered: Scaffolding only. Generator implementation for repeated file patterns.

title: Audit IDE and editor configuration consistency across the team
prompt: Review whether the project enforces consistent editor settings — via .editorconfig, .vscode/settings.json, or recommended extensions lists. Find settings that differ between developer machines causing lint passes locally but CI failures. Commit the minimal set of editor configs that produce consistent formatting without constraining personal preferences.
prompt_short: Enforce consistent editor settings across all developer machines.
prompt_filtered: Editor config only. .editorconfig, .vscode/settings.json, and extension recommendations.

title: Reduce time to production readiness for new features
prompt: Map the full lifecycle of a new feature from code complete to production-ready: code review, QA sign-off, staging deploy, performance test, security review, documentation, and feature flag setup. Identify the steps that take the longest and recommend parallelizing or automating each to cut total time to production.
prompt_short: Map and reduce the time from feature-complete to production-ready.
prompt_filtered: Feature lifecycle only. Steps between code complete and production deployment.

title: Create an inner loop benchmark and track it in CI
prompt: Define a synthetic inner loop benchmark: the time from running a specific test file to seeing results, and the time from saving a source file to seeing the change reflected in a running local server. Automate the benchmark run in CI on every merge to main so regressions in developer feedback speed are caught immediately.
prompt_short: Benchmark the developer inner loop and track it in CI to prevent regressions.
prompt_filtered: Inner loop benchmarking only. Save-to-result and test-to-result time tracking.

title: Improve local database seeding for realistic development data
prompt: Audit the current database seeding strategy for local development. Find seeds that are too minimal to exercise edge cases, seeds that are hardcoded rather than generated, and seeds that require manual cleanup between runs. Design a realistic seed dataset that covers all major entities and relationships and can be reset to a clean state with one command.
prompt_short: Improve the local database seed to be realistic and resettable in one command.
prompt_filtered: Seed data only. Local development database fixture quality and reset workflow.

title: Add developer-facing error pages with actionable context
prompt: Review error pages and messages shown in development mode. Find errors that show a generic message in development the same as production, stack traces that don't link to source files, and runtime errors with no hint about which configuration is missing. Add development-only error overlays with the full context, relevant documentation links, and the exact fix for common errors.
prompt_short: Add development-mode error pages with context, links, and actionable hints.
prompt_filtered: Dev error messages only. Error clarity and documentation links in development mode.

title: Conduct a developer experience survey and prioritize improvements
prompt: Design a 10-question developer experience survey covering setup, feedback loop speed, tooling satisfaction, documentation quality, and incident response tooling. Run the survey, aggregate scores by area, and identify the top 3 friction points by average severity. Build a roadmap of DX improvements ordered by impact score from the survey.
prompt_short: Run a DX survey to find the highest-friction areas and build an improvement roadmap.
prompt_filtered: DX survey only. Survey design, friction identification, and improvement prioritization.

title: Implement developer portal with service catalog and runbooks
prompt: Build or configure a developer portal that catalogs every service with its owner, tech stack, deployment runbook, on-call rotation, and SLO. Ensure the catalog is kept current via automated sync from service repositories. Measure portal adoption by tracking page views per service.
prompt_short: Deploy a developer portal with a service catalog, owners, and runbooks.
prompt_filtered: Developer portal only. Service catalog structure and automated sync.

title: Reduce PR review friction with better tooling integration
prompt: Audit the PR review flow for friction — reviewers switching between the PR interface and a separate IDE, insufficient context about what changed and why, and no way to run changed code without pulling the branch locally. Integrate review tools that provide richer in-browser context and reduce context switches.
prompt_short: Reduce PR review friction by adding tooling that provides richer in-browser context.
prompt_filtered: PR tooling only. Review context richness and context-switch reduction.

title: Add intelligent test selection to the pre-commit hook
prompt: Configure the pre-commit hook to run only the tests that cover the files being committed, rather than the full test suite. Use coverage data or a file-to-test mapping to select the relevant tests. Target a pre-commit test run time under 10 seconds.
prompt_short: Run only the tests covering changed files in pre-commit for under-10-second feedback.
prompt_filtered: Pre-commit testing only. Coverage-based test selection for fast feedback.

title: Create a developer productivity metrics dashboard
prompt: Instrument and display key developer productivity metrics on a shared dashboard: PR cycle time, CI pass rate, test suite duration, build time, and deploy frequency. Track these metrics weekly and highlight regressions. Review the dashboard in sprint retrospectives.
prompt_short: Build a developer productivity dashboard tracking PR cycle time, build speed, and deploy frequency.
prompt_filtered: Productivity metrics only. Dashboard for DX health indicators and trend tracking.

title: Automate stale branch cleanup with developer notifications
prompt: Set up a weekly automated job that identifies branches inactive for more than 30 days and notifies the owning engineer. After 60 days of inactivity with no response, archive the branch by adding a prefix. Never delete — only archive. Include branch age, last commit author, and last commit message in the notification.
prompt_short: Auto-notify engineers of stale branches and archive after 60 days of inactivity.
prompt_filtered: Stale branch cleanup only. Notification, archive workflow, and no-delete policy.

prompt: Check Documentation Completeness

title: Audit the first-clone setup experience for new engineers
prompt: Audit the first-clone setup experience for new engineers. Walk every step from cloning the repo to running the first successful test. Identify missing prerequisites, unclear instructions, environment setup steps that fail silently, and tools that require manual configuration not documented anywhere. Output a numbered list of friction points with the step, the failure mode, and the fix.

title: Find the slowest parts of the local development feedback loop
prompt: Find the slowest parts of the local development feedback loop. Identify the time between saving a file and seeing the result — build time, test run time, server restart time, browser refresh delay. Find the top three bottlenecks and estimate the time saved per day if each were cut in half. Output findings in full paragraphs with the specific step and the recommended optimization.

title: Identify developer environment inconsistencies causing bugs that only ap
prompt: Identify developer environment inconsistencies causing bugs that only appear on some machines. Find dependencies pinned differently per developer, environment variables with different defaults, OS-specific behavior in build scripts, and tools at different versions between team members. Output a numbered list of inconsistencies with the recommended standardization approach.

title: Review error messages and debugging experience quality across the develo
prompt: Review error messages and debugging experience quality across the development workflow. Find error messages that do not explain what went wrong or how to fix it, stack traces that obscure the actual cause, and silent failures that require log spelunking to diagnose. Output findings in full paragraphs with the specific error, why it is confusing, and the improved message or diagnostic output.

title: Find missing tooling that would significantly speed up the development w
prompt: Find missing tooling that would significantly speed up the development workflow. Identify the most repetitive manual tasks that developers do multiple times per day — resetting test data, switching between feature branches, generating boilerplate, or querying the local database. Output a numbered list of missing tools ordered by estimated daily time saved per developer.

title: Audit the local development setup for time-to-first-commit for a new eng
prompt: Audit the local development setup for time-to-first-commit for a new engineer. Map every step from cloning the repository to running the full test suite locally — including environment setup, dependency installation, configuration, and any manual steps in a readme. Find steps that are undocumented, require tribal knowledge, or fail on a fresh machine. Output a numbered list of setup friction points with the step, the failure mode on a fresh machine, and the automation or documentation fix.

title: Measure the feedback loop speed for a typical code change
prompt: Measure the feedback loop speed for a typical code change. Time the full cycle from saving a file to seeing the result in a running local environment. Identify the slowest steps — long hot-reload times, slow test runners, dependencies that re-install on every change. Find tools or configurations that would reduce the feedback cycle below five seconds for the most common types of changes. Output findings in full paragraphs with each bottleneck, its contribution to total cycle time, and the recommended optimization.

title: Review the developer tooling standardization across the team
prompt: Review the developer tooling standardization across the team. Identify the tools each engineer is expected to use and where there is significant variation — different editors, different linters, different test runners. Find cases where tooling inconsistency causes friction: a linting rule enforced in CI that no local editor checks, a formatter that runs in CI but not locally, or a debugging tool that only works in one engineer's environment. Output a numbered list of standardization gaps with the tool, current variation, and recommended standard.

title: Act as a developer experience engineer auditing the full development wor
prompt: Act as a developer experience engineer auditing the full development workflow for this project. Walk every phase from initial setup through daily development to shipping. Identify friction points that slow down new engineers getting productive, slow down experienced engineers in their daily cycle, and cause inconsistencies between developer environments that lead to works-on-my-machine bugs. For each friction point, assess the daily time cost across the team, the implementation effort to fix it, and the specific tooling or documentation change that addresses it. Output a full report with numbered findings ordered by aggregate team time saved per week.

title: Evaluate the developer feedback loop for this project
prompt: Evaluate the developer feedback loop for this project. Measure or estimate the time between making a code change and seeing the result — in unit tests, in integration tests, in a local running server, and in a deployed staging environment. Identify every step where that loop is longer than it needs to be — slow builds, missing hot reload, full test suite runs where only affected tests should run, or manual steps that could be automated. Recommend specific changes to each phase of the feedback loop with estimated time savings. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by impact on developer velocity.

title: Perform a new-engineer onboarding audit for this project
prompt: Perform a new-engineer onboarding audit for this project. Document the full sequence of steps required to go from zero to submitting a first meaningful PR. For each step, assess whether the documentation is current and accurate, whether the step can fail in ways not covered by the docs, and whether the step could be automated or simplified. Identify the most common stumbling blocks based on the README, setup scripts, and any onboarding issues in the issue tracker. Estimate the total setup time for a new engineer and recommend the changes that would cut it in half. Output as a numbered step-by-step audit with findings and recommendations per step.

title: Act as a developer experience engineer auditing the full development wor
prompt: Act as a developer experience engineer auditing the full development workflow for this project from the perspective of a mid-level engineer who joined the team 30 days ago. Identify every point of friction encountered in a typical day: environment setup issues, unclear documentation, slow tooling, inconsistent local and CI behavior, missing test fixtures, and unclear ownership of modules. Assess whether the project's documentation accurately reflects the current state of the codebase or whether it describes a past version of the tooling. Find tasks that engineers report completing inconsistently — where two engineers following the same process get different results. Evaluate the time cost of each friction point. Output a DX audit report with numbered findings ordered by time cost to engineers, followed by a recommended set of investments in documentation, tooling, and automation that would recover the most engineering time per week.

title: Evaluate the testing experience from the developer's perspective
prompt: Evaluate the testing experience from the developer's perspective. Assess whether running the test suite locally is fast enough to be part of the development workflow rather than only a CI gate. Identify test configurations that require manual setup — database seeds, external service credentials, specific environment variables — that are not scripted. Find tests that are too slow to run during development but are the most useful for catching the bugs engineers actually introduce. Assess whether engineers can run a subset of tests scoped to the code they are changing without running the full suite. Evaluate the quality of test failure messages — whether a failing test tells the engineer what went wrong and where to look, or whether it requires reading the test implementation to understand. Output findings in full paragraphs with specific test configuration issues and recommended DX improvements for the test workflow.

title: Audit all internal tools for active use and maintenance
prompt: Inventory all internal tools — admin dashboards, scripts, CLI tools, internal APIs. For each, determine the last time it was used, who owns it, and whether it is still maintained. Identify tools that should be decommissioned.
prompt_short: Inventory all internal tools and find those unused or unmaintained.
prompt_filtered: Tool inventory only. Usage and maintenance status.

title: Find manual admin operations with no internal tool
prompt: Identify recurring admin operations done via direct database access, API calls, or manual scripts. For each, assess the risk of human error and recommend building a simple internal tool or admin UI to reduce that risk.
prompt_short: Find admin operations done manually that need an internal tool.
prompt_filtered: Missing tools only. Recurring manual admin operations.

title: Review internal tool access control and audit logging
prompt: Audit who has access to each internal tool and what actions they can take. Verify sensitive operations (deleting users, modifying payments, overriding flags) require elevated access and produce an audit log entry.
prompt_short: Audit internal tool access control and verify audit logging for sensitive actions.
prompt_filtered: Access control only. Internal tool permissions and audit trails.

title: Assess internal tool documentation and discoverability
prompt: Evaluate whether team members know which internal tools exist and how to use them. Identify tools with no documentation, no onboarding for new users, or tools that duplicate functionality because people couldn't find the existing one.
prompt_short: Find internal tools that are undiscoverable or undocumented.
prompt_filtered: Discoverability only. Documentation and awareness gaps.

title: Find internal tools creating production risk
prompt: Identify internal tools that operate directly on production data or services without a staging equivalent. Flag tools with no confirmation step before destructive actions, no rate limiting, and no way to audit what was done after the fact.
prompt_short: Find internal tools that can cause irreversible production damage.
prompt_filtered: Production risk only. Destructive actions with no safeguards.

title: Assess internal tool bus factor and knowledge concentration
prompt: Identify internal tools where only one engineer knows how to use, maintain, or deploy them. For each high-bus-factor tool, assess the cost if that engineer were unavailable. Recommend documentation sprints, knowledge-sharing sessions, or cross-training assignments to reduce concentration.
prompt_short: Find internal tools known by only one engineer and reduce the bus factor.
prompt_filtered: Bus factor only. Single-engineer knowledge concentration in internal tools.

title: Evaluate build vs. buy decisions for internal tooling
prompt: For each major internal tool, assess whether it should continue to be built in-house or replaced by a commercial solution. Calculate the total cost of ownership (engineering time to maintain, outage cost, onboarding cost for new engineers) versus the cost of a SaaS alternative. Recommend buy for commodity tools and build for competitive differentiators.
prompt_short: Evaluate build vs. buy for each major internal tool by total cost of ownership.
prompt_filtered: Build vs buy only. Internal tool TCO versus commercial alternative cost.

title: Standardize internal CLI tooling for common operations
prompt: Audit the set of internal CLI scripts and tools used by engineers. Find tools with inconsistent argument conventions, no --help flag, no version output, and no error messages on invalid input. Recommend a unified CLI framework (Click, Cobra, oclif) and a standard for flags, output formats, and error codes.
prompt_short: Standardize all internal CLI tools with consistent flags, help text, and error codes.
prompt_filtered: CLI consistency only. Internal tool argument conventions and help text standards.

title: Implement dogfooding to improve internal tool quality
prompt: Identify internal tools that the team building them does not use in their own day-to-day workflow. Tools not dogfooded by their builders accumulate usability debt fastest. Recommend a dogfooding rotation where tool owners use each other's tools as their primary workflow for one sprint and log friction points.
prompt_short: Set up a dogfooding rotation to surface usability issues in internal tools.
prompt_filtered: Dogfooding only. Internal tool usage by builder teams and friction logging.

title: Add versioning and changelog to all internal tools
prompt: Find internal tools with no version number and no changelog. When an internal tool changes behavior without a version bump or announcement, dependent teams break silently. Implement semantic versioning for every internal tool and a changelog entry for every behavior-changing release.
prompt_short: Add semantic versioning and changelogs to internal tools to prevent silent breaks.
prompt_filtered: Internal tool versioning only. Semver, changelog, and breaking change signaling.

title: Audit internal tool access controls and permission scoping
prompt: Review who can access each internal tool and what level of access they have. Find tools where all authenticated users have full access including destructive operations. Recommend role-based access with least privilege — read-only access for observers, write access for operators, destructive actions restricted to designated roles with an explicit justification field.
prompt_short: Add role-based access control to internal tools with least-privilege scoping.
prompt_filtered: Internal tool access only. RBAC and destructive action restrictions.

title: Identify internal tools that should become product features
prompt: Review internal tools that were built to serve a need that also exists for end users. Identify where an internal tool solves a problem users currently work around manually. Assess the effort to productize the tool — adding auth, rate limiting, a public API, and user-facing documentation — versus the market value of the capability.
prompt_short: Find internal tools that solve user problems and could become product features.
prompt_filtered: Productization only. Internal tools with user-facing feature potential.

title: Create an internal tool catalog and discovery system
prompt: Audit whether engineers know what internal tools exist and how to find them. Find tools built to solve a problem where another tool already existed — duplicated because the first was undiscoverable. Build a simple internal tool catalog (a searchable wiki page, Notion database, or internal portal) with tool name, purpose, owner, and link to documentation.
prompt_short: Build a searchable catalog of all internal tools to prevent duplication.
prompt_filtered: Tool discovery only. Catalog structure and ownership documentation.

title: Review internal tool incident history and failure patterns
prompt: Audit incident reports and on-call logs for incidents caused by internal tools — incorrect admin actions, tool bugs that corrupted data, or tools that produced misleading output. For each, identify the safeguard that would have prevented the incident: confirmation prompts, dry-run modes, immutable audit logs, or stricter input validation.
prompt_short: Review incidents caused by internal tools and add the safeguards that would have prevented them.
prompt_filtered: Incident review only. Internal tool failure modes and preventive safeguards.

title: Measure internal tool adoption rates and deprecation candidates
prompt: Instrument all internal tools to log usage events — which tool, which operation, which team, at what frequency. After 30 days, identify tools with zero usage (deprecation candidates), tools used by only one team (could be that team's responsibility), and tools used heavily but by a different team than the owner (ownership transfer candidates).
prompt_short: Add usage instrumentation to internal tools and find deprecation candidates.
prompt_filtered: Tool adoption only. Usage frequency, team distribution, and deprecation assessment.

title: Implement graceful deprecation for internal tools
prompt: Define a deprecation policy for internal tools: announce 90 days before EOL, provide a migration guide to the replacement, show a deprecation banner in the UI, and disable the tool after the EOL date. Identify tools currently in use that should be deprecated with no announced timeline.
prompt_short: Define and enforce a 90-day deprecation policy for internal tools being retired.
prompt_filtered: Tool deprecation only. Deprecation timeline, migration guide, and EOL enforcement.

title: Build a dry-run mode for all destructive internal tool actions
prompt: For every internal tool action that modifies or deletes data, add a --dry-run flag that shows exactly what would change without applying the change. Require dry-run output to be reviewed before the live action is available. Verify the dry-run output is accurate by comparing it to the actual change in staging.
prompt_short: Add --dry-run mode to all destructive internal tool actions before they execute.
prompt_filtered: Dry-run mode only. Preview of destructive actions without execution.

title: Standardize internal tool error handling and recovery guidance
prompt: Audit error messages produced by internal tools. Find tools that surface stack traces to non-engineers, tools that produce cryptic error codes without context, and tools that fail without indicating whether the operation partially completed. Implement structured error messages: what failed, what state the system is in, and the exact command to recover.
prompt_short: Improve internal tool error messages to include state and recovery instructions.
prompt_filtered: Error messages only. Internal tool failure clarity and recovery guidance.

title: Add rate limiting to internal tools that hit production APIs
prompt: Identify internal tools that make unbounded calls to production APIs or databases when operated by a human. Add client-side rate limiting so that a runaway internal tool cannot overload the production API or exhaust database connection pools. Set the limit based on the maximum safe request rate for each downstream system.
prompt_short: Add rate limiting to internal tools that call production APIs to prevent overload.
prompt_filtered: Internal tool rate limiting only. Client-side throttle for production API calls.

title: Review internal tools for accessibility and screen reader support
prompt: Audit all internal web-based tools for basic accessibility compliance — keyboard navigation, screen reader labels on interactive elements, and sufficient color contrast. Run axe-core against each tool and fix all critical violations. Internal tools are often built without accessibility considerations, creating barriers for team members with disabilities.
prompt_short: Audit internal web tools for accessibility and fix all keyboard navigation gaps.
prompt_filtered: Internal tool a11y only. Keyboard navigation and screen reader compliance.

prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

prompt: Find What's Broken

title: Recommend Next Steps
prompt: Recommend Next Steps

prompt: Score for Production Readiness

prompt: Audit Internal Tool Inventory

prompt: Find Manual Admin Operations

prompt: Review Tool Access Controls

title: Assess Tool Documentation
prompt: Assess Tool Documentation

title: Find Production Risk Tools
prompt: Find Production Risk Tools

title: Map Tool Usage Patterns
prompt: Map Tool Usage Patterns

title: Check Audit Logging Coverage
prompt: Check Audit Logging Coverage

title: Audit all internal tools for active use and maintenance status
prompt: Audit all internal tools for active use and maintenance status. Identify tools with no usage in the last 90 days, tools with no identified owner, and tools that have not been updated since the system they operate on changed significantly. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by risk of operating on stale assumptions.

title: Find manual admin operations with no internal tool support
prompt: Find manual admin operations with no internal tool support. Identify the most common admin actions — user management, data correction, permission grants, cache purges — that are done by running raw scripts or database queries with no tooling wrapper. Output a numbered list ordered by frequency with the operation, current execution method, and recommended tool to build.

title: Review internal tool access control and audit logging
prompt: Review internal tool access control and audit logging. Find tools accessible to more people than necessary, tools with no access control at all, and tools that perform sensitive operations without recording who did what and when. Output findings in full paragraphs with the tool name, access gap, and recommended access control and audit log approach.

title: Assess internal tool documentation and discoverability
prompt: Assess internal tool documentation and discoverability. Find tools that exist but are unknown to most of the team, tools whose documentation is outdated since a major system change, and tools with no usage examples. Output a numbered list of documentation gaps with the tool name and the minimum documentation needed for a new engineer to use it safely.

title: Find internal tools creating production risk
prompt: Find internal tools creating production risk. Identify tools that operate on production data with no staging equivalent, tools with destructive actions and no confirmation step, and tools with no rate limiting that could cause a runaway operation. Output findings in full paragraphs with the tool name, the risk, and the safeguard needed.

title: Identify internal tools that are being used by teams other than their in
prompt: Identify internal tools that are being used by teams other than their intended owner. Find tools built for one team that spread organically to other teams without the original maintainer's awareness, creating undocumented dependencies. Assess whether each tool can handle its actual current user base. Output findings in full paragraphs with the tool name, intended vs. actual user base, current reliability level, and recommended governance approach.

title: Audit all internal tools for authentication and authorization controls
prompt: Audit all internal tools for authentication and authorization controls. Find tools that accept any request from inside the internal network without authentication, tools with hardcoded admin credentials, and tools where any authenticated user can perform any action regardless of their role. Output a numbered list of access control gaps with the tool name, the missing control, and the recommended implementation.

title: Review the internal tooling for documentation completeness
prompt: Review the internal tooling for documentation completeness. Find tools with no user guide, no API reference, no runbook for common failures, and no explanation of what the tool does and who owns it. Identify tools that can only be operated by the engineer who built them. Output a numbered list of documentation gaps with the tool name, the missing documentation type, and the minimum documentation needed for safe operation by any team member.

title: Act as an internal tooling engineer auditing the full internal tool land
prompt: Act as an internal tooling engineer auditing the full internal tool landscape for this system. Inventory every admin tool, dashboard, script, and CLI used by the team to operate the system. For each tool, assess its access control — who can use it and whether that scope is appropriate, its audit trail — whether actions are logged with actor, timestamp, and outcome, its safety — whether destructive operations require confirmation and have rollback paths, and its maintenance state — whether it still works correctly given recent system changes. Output a full inventory with a risk rating per tool and a prioritized improvement backlog.

title: Evaluate the internal tooling gap for this system
prompt: Evaluate the internal tooling gap for this system. Identify the most common operations that engineers perform by connecting directly to the database, running ad-hoc scripts, or manually calling internal APIs. These are the highest-risk gaps — raw access with no safety wrapper, no audit trail, and no mistake prevention. For each gap, assess the frequency of the operation, the blast radius of a mistake, and the effort to build a proper internal tool. Recommend the highest-priority tools to build, ordered by risk reduction. Output findings in full paragraphs with specific operation examples and implementation recommendations.

title: Perform a production safety audit of all existing internal tools
prompt: Perform a production safety audit of all existing internal tools. For each tool that touches production data or services, trace what happens if it is used incorrectly — what is the worst case action a user could take with this tool and what is the blast radius? Identify tools with no confirmation step before irreversible operations, tools that do not check whether the target record exists before modifying it, tools that can modify records in bulk without preview, and tools with no way to recover from a mistake. Assess whether each tool has a staging-equivalent that can be used for testing before production use. Output a numbered findings list ordered by maximum blast radius.

title: Evaluate the internal tooling portfolio for strategic alignment and main
prompt: Evaluate the internal tooling portfolio for strategic alignment and maintainability. Catalog every internal tool in use and assess whether each one solves a problem that could not be addressed by an existing external tool or a configuration change to current systems. Find tools that were built to work around a limitation that has since been resolved — tools that are now redundant but continue to be used because no one has migrated off them. Assess the total maintenance burden across the portfolio: how many hours per week are spent keeping internal tools running, debugging internal tool failures, and onboarding new engineers to internal tool usage. Find tools with no assigned maintainer — running on technical debt with no one responsible for keeping them current. Output a portfolio audit with findings ordered by maintenance cost per tool and a recommended rationalization strategy.

title: Perform a reliability audit of the internal tools that are on the critic
prompt: Perform a reliability audit of the internal tools that are on the critical path for engineering productivity. Identify the internal tools that, if unavailable for more than one hour, would block engineers from doing their work — CI dashboards, deployment tools, secret management interfaces, code review automation, and monitoring systems. For each critical tool, assess its current reliability: uptime history, failure mode when unavailable, fallback procedure, and whether the fallback is documented and practiced. Find critical tools with no defined SLA and no incident response procedure. Assess whether the reliability of internal tools is measured and tracked with the same rigor as production user-facing services. Output as a criticality-ranked list of internal tools with reliability assessment and recommended SLA targets.

title: Audit all packages for security vulnerabilities
prompt: Run a full dependency audit across all package manifests. Identify packages with known CVEs, assess severity and exploitability in the context of this application. Prioritize fixes by CVSS score and actual reachability.
prompt_short: Audit all dependencies for known CVEs and rank by exploitability.
prompt_filtered: Security vulnerabilities only. CVE severity and reachability.

title: Find unused packages adding unnecessary bundle weight
prompt: Identify packages listed as dependencies that are not imported anywhere in the codebase. Also find packages imported once for a small utility when a native alternative would suffice. Quantify bundle size impact.
prompt_short: Find unused packages and those replaceable with native alternatives.
prompt_filtered: Unused packages only. Imports analysis and bundle impact.

title: Review package version pinning and lockfile health
prompt: Assess the lockfile health — is it committed, up-to-date, and consistent across environments? Identify packages with unpinned version ranges that could auto-upgrade to a breaking or vulnerable version.
prompt_short: Review lockfile health and find packages with unsafe unpinned versions.
prompt_filtered: Version pinning only. Lockfile consistency and range safety.

title: Identify packages with incompatible licenses
prompt: Audit the licenses of all direct and transitive dependencies. Identify packages with GPL, AGPL, or other copyleft licenses that may be incompatible with commercial distribution. Flag any license that requires attribution or code disclosure.
prompt_short: Audit all dependency licenses for compatibility with commercial use.
prompt_filtered: License compliance only. Copyleft and attribution requirements.

title: Find packages that should be moved to devDependencies
prompt: Identify packages listed in production dependencies that are only used in tests, build scripts, or development tooling. Moving them to devDependencies reduces production bundle size and attack surface.
prompt_short: Find dev-only packages incorrectly listed in production dependencies.
prompt_filtered: Dependency classification only. Dev vs production dependency split.

title: Audit lockfile for supply chain integrity
prompt: Verify the lockfile is committed and up-to-date. Identify any discrepancy between the declared version ranges in package.json and the resolved versions in the lockfile. Find packages where the resolved URL or integrity hash differs from the expected registry entry, which could indicate a dependency confusion or substitution attack.
prompt_short: Verify lockfile integrity and find discrepancies that suggest supply chain tampering.
prompt_filtered: Lockfile integrity only. Registry URL and hash verification.

title: Evaluate npm vs. pnpm vs. yarn for this project's needs
prompt: Assess the current package manager against alternatives based on install speed, disk space efficiency, strict dependency isolation, and workspace support. If the project uses a monorepo, evaluate pnpm workspaces specifically. Calculate the install time and node_modules size difference for this exact project.
prompt_short: Compare npm, pnpm, and yarn for this project and recommend the best fit.
prompt_filtered: Package manager selection only. Speed, disk, and workspace support comparison.

title: Set up a private registry mirror for build reproducibility
prompt: Assess the risk of public registry outages affecting builds. Configure Verdaccio, npm Enterprise, or a cloud registry (Artifactory, GitHub Packages) as a local mirror or proxy for critical packages. Identify the packages most frequently fetched in CI that would benefit from caching in a private registry.
prompt_short: Set up a private registry mirror to protect builds from public registry outages.
prompt_filtered: Registry mirror only. Cache and proxy setup for CI build reliability.

title: Enforce peer dependency resolution consistency
prompt: Find packages with peer dependency warnings in the install log. Identify cases where different parts of the dependency tree require incompatible peer versions of the same package. Verify the resolved peer is the version that all peers expect, and document explicit peer resolutions in the package manager config.
prompt_short: Resolve peer dependency conflicts and document explicit peer version resolutions.
prompt_filtered: Peer dependencies only. Conflict detection and explicit resolution.

title: Measure and optimize total node_modules size
prompt: Calculate the current node_modules size. Identify the top 10 packages by disk footprint including their transitive dependencies. Find packages with multiple versions installed in the dependency tree (version duplication) that could be deduplicated. Estimate the disk size reduction from deduplication and from replacing the largest packages with lighter alternatives.
prompt_short: Find the largest node_modules packages and reduce size through deduplication.
prompt_filtered: Module size only. Disk footprint, version duplication, and deduplication opportunities.

title: Review package.json scripts for consistency and safety
prompt: Audit all package.json scripts across all workspaces. Find scripts that use different environment handling (NODE_ENV set inconsistently), scripts that work only on certain operating systems (using & instead of && or path separators), and scripts that have no error propagation. Standardize with cross-env and npm-run-all where needed.
prompt_short: Audit package.json scripts for cross-platform safety and environment consistency.
prompt_filtered: Package scripts only. Cross-platform compatibility and environment handling.

title: Identify packages with known prototype pollution vulnerabilities
prompt: Scan the dependency tree for packages with prototype pollution vulnerabilities — particularly in utility packages that merge, extend, or clone objects (lodash.merge, deepmerge, defaults). Verify whether the vulnerable code path is reachable in this application and whether the installed version has a patched release available.
prompt_short: Find packages with prototype pollution vulnerabilities and check reachability.
prompt_filtered: Prototype pollution only. Object utility packages and patch availability.

title: Set up automated Dependabot or Renovate for dependency updates
prompt: Configure Dependabot or Renovate to open PRs for dependency updates on a defined cadence. Set grouping rules to batch patch updates for non-breaking changes, separate PRs for minor and major upgrades, and auto-merge for patch updates that pass CI. Define a reviewers list and SLA for dependency update PRs.
prompt_short: Configure Renovate or Dependabot with grouping rules and auto-merge for patch updates.
prompt_filtered: Automated updates only. Dependabot/Renovate config for cadence and grouping.

title: Audit packages with post-install scripts for security risk
prompt: Find all packages in the dependency tree that define postinstall, preinstall, or install scripts. Review the code each script executes. Identify scripts that download binaries, make network requests, or execute untrusted code. Evaluate whether any can be disabled via --ignore-scripts or replaced with a safer alternative.
prompt_short: Find packages with postinstall scripts and assess their execution risk.
prompt_filtered: Install scripts only. Postinstall code review and disable options.

title: Generate an SBOM for compliance and audit purposes
prompt: Generate a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) in CycloneDX or SPDX format for this project. Include all direct and transitive dependencies with their version, license, and source URL. Integrate SBOM generation into the release pipeline so every production artifact has an accompanying SBOM. Identify the packages with missing or ambiguous license data.
prompt_short: Generate a CycloneDX or SPDX SBOM and integrate it into the release pipeline.
prompt_filtered: SBOM only. Bill of materials generation for compliance and audit.

title: Enforce package version consistency across monorepo workspaces
prompt: Find packages installed at different versions across workspaces in the monorepo. Inconsistent versions cause subtle bugs when shared utilities behave differently depending on which workspace is executing. Configure syncpack or pnpm catalog to enforce a single version for shared packages across all workspaces.
prompt_short: Enforce consistent package versions across all monorepo workspaces with syncpack.
prompt_filtered: Version consistency only. Cross-workspace package version alignment.

title: Audit package publish configuration for accidental public release
prompt: Review the publishConfig and private field in all package.json files. Find internal packages that could be accidentally published to the public npm registry because the private field is missing. Verify all internal packages have private: true or are scoped to a private registry.
prompt_short: Verify all internal packages have private: true to prevent accidental public publish.
prompt_filtered: Publish config only. private field and publishConfig.access for internal packages.

title: Review optional and peer dependency ranges for compatibility
prompt: Audit all optional and peer dependencies for version ranges that allow incompatible combinations. Find cases where a peer dependency range accepts a version that is known to have breaking behavior. Test the install across the full declared peer range to verify all combinations produce a working installation.
prompt_short: Test all declared peer dependency ranges for compatibility across the full version range.
prompt_filtered: Peer compatibility only. Peer range testing across all accepted versions.

title: Implement package boundary enforcement with dependency rules
prompt: Configure eslint-plugin-import or a similar tool to enforce that packages only import from within their declared dependencies. Phantom dependencies (imports of packages not in the local package.json) break when the package is installed independently. Find and fix all phantom dependency imports.
prompt_short: Find and fix phantom dependency imports that bypass declared package boundaries.
prompt_filtered: Phantom dependencies only. Imports not declared in the local package.json.

title: Measure and optimize package install time in CI and onboarding
prompt: Measure the current package install time in CI (cold and warm cache) and on a new developer machine. Identify the packages that contribute the most to install time. Estimate the time saved by enabling a package manager with content-addressable storage (pnpm) and a registry mirror.
prompt_short: Measure install time with cold and warm cache, and find the top time contributors.
prompt_filtered: Install performance only. Cold vs warm cache time and top install-time contributors.

title: Review Version Pinning

title: Identify License Issues
prompt: Identify License Issues

title: Find Misclassified Dependencies
prompt: Find Misclassified Dependencies

title: Map Package Bundle Impact
prompt: Map Package Bundle Impact

title: Check Lockfile Health
prompt: Check Lockfile Health

title: Audit all packages for known security vulnerabilities
prompt: Audit all packages for known security vulnerabilities. Run a full dependency audit and identify packages with CVEs. Prioritize by severity and whether the vulnerable code path is reachable in this application. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by exploitability. Include the package name, CVE, and the upgrade or patch that resolves it.

title: Find unused packages adding unnecessary bundle weight and attack surface
prompt: Find unused packages adding unnecessary bundle weight and attack surface. Identify packages installed but never imported in source code. Find packages imported in only one place where the functionality could be inlined. Output a numbered list ordered by bundle size contribution with the package name, size, and removal recommendation.

title: Review package version pinning and lockfile health
prompt: Review package version pinning and lockfile health. Find packages pinned to ranges that could silently upgrade on install, packages whose lockfile entry diverges from the declared version range, and packages where the lockfile has not been updated in over 90 days. Output findings in full paragraphs with the package name, current pinning, and recommended pin strategy.

title: Identify packages with incompatible or risky licenses
prompt: Identify packages with incompatible or risky licenses. Find packages under licenses that conflict with this project's license or impose distribution restrictions. Identify packages under copyleft licenses that could affect proprietary code if included incorrectly. Output a numbered list of license risks with the package name, license, and recommended action.

title: Find packages that should be moved to devDependencies
prompt: Find packages that should be moved to devDependencies. Identify packages in production dependencies that are only imported by test files, build scripts, or development tooling. Moving them reduces production bundle size and narrows the attack surface. Output findings in full paragraphs with the package name, where it is imported, and the classification that applies.

title: Audit the package registry configuration for security
prompt: Audit the package registry configuration for security. Find projects fetching packages from registries without TLS, projects using public registries for internal packages that should be private, and projects with no integrity verification on downloaded packages. Output a numbered list of registry security gaps with the configuration file, the risk, and the recommended fix.

title: Identify packages installed but not referenced in the production code pa
prompt: Identify packages installed but not referenced in the production code path. Find devDependencies installed into production images, packages listed in dependencies that are only used in tests or scripts, and packages imported in one file and never used elsewhere. Estimate the bundle size and attack surface reduction from removing each category. Output findings in full paragraphs with the package name, where it is installed, where it is actually used, and the removal path.

title: Review the package versioning strategy for consistency
prompt: Review the package versioning strategy for consistency. Find packages pinned to an exact version alongside packages using a loose range in the same project. Find packages where the lockfile version and the declared range are significantly diverged. Assess whether the current strategy produces reproducible installs across all developer machines and CI environments. Output a numbered list of versioning inconsistencies with the package name, current spec, recommended approach, and the risk each inconsistency creates.

title: Act as a supply chain security engineer auditing the full package depend
prompt: Act as a supply chain security engineer auditing the full package dependency graph for this project. Identify every direct and transitive dependency. For each dependency, assess its security posture — known CVEs, maintainer activity, number of maintainers, last publish date, and whether it has been flagged in any public vulnerability database. Identify packages that are unmaintained or have a single maintainer with no succession plan. Find packages where the declared version range allows silent upgrades that could introduce breaking changes or vulnerabilities. Output a numbered findings list ordered by supply chain risk with the package name, risk type, and recommended action.

title: Evaluate the production bundle composition for this project
prompt: Evaluate the production bundle composition for this project. Identify all packages that are included in the production bundle. Find packages that add significant size but whose functionality could be replaced by a smaller alternative or a native implementation. Find packages included only for one or two functions that could be inlined. Assess the total bundle size impact of all unused or redundant packages. Calculate the bundle size reduction achievable by removing the top five largest unnecessary packages. Output findings in full paragraphs with package names, size contributions, and replacement recommendations ordered by size impact.

title: Perform a license compliance audit across the full dependency tree — dir
prompt: Perform a license compliance audit across the full dependency tree — direct and transitive. For each unique license in the dependency graph, assess whether it is compatible with this project's stated license and usage model. Identify any copyleft licenses that could affect the distribution rights of the application if the dependency is bundled incorrectly. Find any packages with no license — which defaults to all-rights-reserved and may not be legally usable. Assess any packages with dual licensing where the commercial terms may apply. Output a full compliance report with the license type, the packages under it, the compliance status, and the recommended action for each non-compliant dependency.

title: Evaluate the package management strategy for this project from a securit
prompt: Evaluate the package management strategy for this project from a security and supply chain perspective. Assess how the project discovers, evaluates, and approves new dependencies before they are added. Find the process — or lack thereof — for reviewing a new package's maintainer reputation, license, download count, and security history before it is added as a dependency. Identify packages added without any documented rationale, making it impossible to evaluate whether they are still needed. Assess whether the lockfile is committed and whether CI uses the lockfile exactly rather than resolving versions fresh on each run. Find packages where the installed version is significantly older than the latest stable release, creating an exposure window. Output a supply chain readiness assessment with specific risk findings and a recommended dependency governance process.

title: Perform a package audit focused on transitive dependencies
prompt: Perform a package audit focused on transitive dependencies. Identify the total number of unique packages installed including all transitive dependencies. Find packages that appear in the transitive dependency graph but not in the direct dependency list, creating invisible attack surface. Identify transitive dependencies with known CVEs that are not flagged by any current tooling. Assess whether the project pins transitive dependencies for reproducibility or relies entirely on lockfile resolution. Find the packages with the largest transitive footprint — single additions that pull in dozens of sub-dependencies. Output findings in full paragraphs with the transitive risk assessment, the most concerning transitive dependencies, and the recommended dependency hygiene practices that reduce invisible supply chain risk.

title: Find circular dependencies causing import issues
prompt: Identify circular import chains in the codebase. For each cycle, map the modules involved, assess the impact (initialization order bugs, test isolation failures, bundle splitting problems), and recommend a resolution.
prompt_short: Find circular imports and explain the risk each cycle creates.
prompt_filtered: Circular imports only. Module dependency cycle detection.

title: Identify outdated dependencies past end-of-life
prompt: Find all dependencies that have reached end-of-life or have a maintained successor. Assess the risk of running EOL software (no security patches) and create an upgrade priority list based on public exposure.
prompt_short: Find EOL dependencies and rank upgrade priority by security exposure.
prompt_filtered: EOL dependencies only. No version range or lockfile analysis.

title: Map all external service dependencies and their SLAs
prompt: List every external service the application depends on at runtime — APIs, databases, payment processors, email providers. For each, document the SLA, what happens when it's unavailable, and whether a fallback exists.
prompt_short: Map all external runtime dependencies and their failure impact.
prompt_filtered: External services only. SLA and failure impact mapping.

title: Review dependency upgrade strategy and migration plan
prompt: Assess the team's approach to dependency upgrades. Identify dependencies many major versions behind, and estimate the migration effort to get current. Recommend a sustainable upgrade cadence (automated PRs, scheduled upgrade sprints).
prompt_short: Find heavily outdated dependencies and create a migration priority plan.
prompt_filtered: Upgrade strategy only. Version lag and migration effort estimation.

title: Identify single-maintainer dependencies with bus factor risk
prompt: Find dependencies maintained by a single person or with low contributor counts. Assess the risk if the maintainer abandons the project. For critical dependencies, recommend forking, vendoring, or switching to a more actively maintained alternative.
prompt_short: Find dependencies with single maintainers and evaluate abandonment risk.
prompt_filtered: Bus factor only. Maintainer count and activity status.

title: Conduct a supply chain attack surface assessment
prompt: Identify the ways this project could be compromised through its dependency chain — typosquatting of popular packages, dependency confusion attacks using internal package names, compromised maintainer accounts on widely-used transitive dependencies. For each vector, recommend the control that reduces exposure: namespace scoping, registry allow-lists, or hash pinning.
prompt_short: Assess supply chain attack vectors and recommend controls for each.
prompt_filtered: Supply chain attacks only. Typosquatting, confusion attacks, and maintainer compromise.

title: Perform a full license audit with SPDX compliance output
prompt: Generate a complete license report for all direct and transitive dependencies in SPDX format. Identify any package with a license incompatible with commercial distribution (GPL-3.0, AGPL-3.0), any package with no license declared, and any package using a non-standard or custom license. Produce a compliance report suitable for a legal review.
prompt_short: Generate an SPDX license report and flag incompatible or missing licenses.
prompt_filtered: License audit only. SPDX format, GPL/AGPL detection, and missing licenses.

title: Map transitive dependency depth and identify over-reliance
prompt: Analyze the transitive dependency graph and identify chains deeper than 5 levels. Find dependencies that pull in hundreds of transitive packages for minimal functionality. Identify the single direct dependency whose removal would reduce the total transitive count the most. Recommend alternatives with shallower dependency trees.
prompt_short: Find deep transitive dependency chains and recommend flatter alternatives.
prompt_filtered: Transitive depth only. Dependency chain depth and transitive package count.

title: Assess Node.js native addon dependencies for compatibility risk
prompt: Find all dependencies that include native addons (bindings.gyp, .node files, node-pre-gyp). Assess whether each addon has prebuilt binaries for all target platforms and Node.js versions in use, or requires a build toolchain at install time. Identify addons that could block deployments if a prebuilt binary is unavailable.
prompt_short: Find native addon dependencies and assess their cross-platform compatibility.
prompt_filtered: Native addons only. Prebuilt binary availability and build toolchain requirements.

title: Review dependency version constraints for range safety
prompt: Audit every dependency's version constraint in package.json. Find dependencies pinned to exact versions (good for reproducibility but missing security patches), ranges too wide (^major allows breaking changes), and ranges too narrow (= pin creates lockfile churn on every patch). Recommend the appropriate constraint strategy per dependency category.
prompt_short: Review version constraint strategy per dependency type and fix unsafe ranges.
prompt_filtered: Version constraints only. Exact pins, caret ranges, and tilde ranges by category.

title: Detect dependency confusion risks for internal packages
prompt: Find all internal package names used in this project that are not scoped with a private namespace (e.g., @company/package-name). Assess whether these names are registered on the public npm registry by a third party — which could allow a dependency confusion attack where the package manager resolves to the public registry over the private one.
prompt_short: Find unscoped internal package names vulnerable to dependency confusion attacks.
prompt_filtered: Dependency confusion only. Unscoped internal packages on public registries.

title: Audit dependency change velocity and regression risk
prompt: Review how frequently dependencies are updated in this project. Find dependencies updated more than once per week on average — high churn that increases regression risk. Find dependencies not updated in over a year — accumulating security debt. Recommend a controlled update cadence that balances security and stability.
prompt_short: Find dependencies updated too frequently or not enough and set a healthy cadence.
prompt_filtered: Update velocity only. High-churn and high-staleness dependency identification.

title: Identify dependencies with known typosquatting variants
prompt: For the top 20 most critical direct dependencies by usage, generate a list of common typosquatting variants (transposed letters, added hyphens, .js suffix). Check whether any variants are registered on the public npm registry. Verify the install configuration uses registry integrity checks to prevent accidental installation of typosquatted packages.
prompt_short: Check for registered typosquatting variants of critical dependencies.
prompt_filtered: Typosquatting only. Common variant names registered on public registries.

title: Review patching strategy for dependencies with no upstream fix
prompt: Identify CVEs in the dependency tree where no upstream fix is available. For each, assess whether the vulnerable code path is reachable. If reachable, evaluate the options: vendoring and patching the source, using patch-package for a temporary fix, or replacing the dependency entirely. Document the chosen mitigation and the review cadence to check for an upstream fix.
prompt_short: Find CVEs with no upstream fix and choose a patching or mitigation strategy.
prompt_filtered: Unpatched CVEs only. Vendor patch, patch-package, or replacement options.

title: Set up integrity hash pinning for all critical dependencies
prompt: Verify that the lockfile stores the integrity hash (SHA-512) for every installed package. Configure the package manager to verify integrity hashes on every install and fail if they do not match. Identify any dependencies installed with --no-integrity or where the lockfile entry is missing the integrity field.
prompt_short: Verify all lockfile entries have integrity hashes and enforce hash verification on install.
prompt_filtered: Integrity hashes only. Lockfile hash coverage and verification enforcement.

title: Assess vendor lock-in depth for each critical dependency
prompt: For each mission-critical dependency, measure the coupling depth: how many files import it directly, whether there is an abstraction layer, and the estimated migration effort to switch to an alternative. Recommend wrapping the top 3 highest-lock-in dependencies behind interfaces to reduce future migration cost.
prompt_short: Measure vendor lock-in per critical dependency and wrap the riskiest behind interfaces.
prompt_filtered: Lock-in depth only. Direct import count and abstraction layer assessment.

title: Review dependency tree for known malware injection patterns
prompt: Audit the dependency tree for known malware injection patterns: packages recently transferred to new maintainers, packages with sudden major version jumps after long inactivity, and packages with install scripts added in recent releases. Cross-reference against published malware incident databases.
prompt_short: Check the dependency tree for packages with recent maintainer transfers or sudden activity.
prompt_filtered: Malware patterns only. Recent ownership changes and sudden script additions.

title: Map dependency version compatibility with the Node.js LTS schedule
prompt: Identify which version of Node.js this project currently targets and which dependencies have dropped support for that version in recent releases. Create a compatibility matrix and recommend an upgrade path for Node.js alongside compatible dependency versions.
prompt_short: Map dependency compatibility with the current and next Node.js LTS versions.
prompt_filtered: Node.js compatibility only. Dependency support matrix across LTS versions.

title: Evaluate the cost of removing each major dependency
prompt: For each major direct dependency, estimate the cost of replacing it with native platform APIs or a lighter alternative. Find dependencies added years ago for features now available in the language or runtime natively. Prioritize replacements by dependency size and native API coverage.
prompt_short: Find dependencies replaceable with native platform APIs and estimate replacement effort.
prompt_filtered: Dependency replacement only. Native API alternatives to heavy library dependencies.

title: Set up a dependency vulnerability response SLA
prompt: Define a vulnerability response SLA by severity: critical CVEs patched within 24 hours, high within 7 days, medium within 30 days, low within 90 days. Verify the current backlog of open CVEs against this SLA. Identify the team member responsible for vulnerability triage and the escalation path when the SLA cannot be met.
prompt_short: Define vulnerability response SLAs by severity and find current SLA violations.
prompt_filtered: Vuln SLA only. Response time targets by severity and current backlog compliance.

prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps
