prompt: Review Upgrade Strategy

title: Find Single-Maintainer Risks
prompt: Find Single-Maintainer Risks

title: Audit Transitive Dependency Depth
prompt: Audit Transitive Dependency Depth

title: Check Dependency Update Cadence
prompt: Check Dependency Update Cadence

title: Find circular dependencies causing import issues
prompt: Find circular dependencies causing import issues. Identify modules that import each other directly or through an import chain. Assess the build and runtime impact of each cycle. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by complexity of the cycle with the modules involved and a recommended refactor to break the cycle.

title: Identify outdated dependencies past their end-of-life or with no recent
prompt: Identify outdated dependencies past their end-of-life or with no recent security patches. Find packages whose last release was more than 18 months ago and are still in active use. Assess the risk of staying on each. Output a numbered list ordered by security exposure with the package name, last release date, and recommended replacement.

title: Map all external service dependencies and their SLAs
prompt: Map all external service dependencies and their SLAs. Identify every third-party API, database, message queue, and external service this system depends on. For each, note the SLA, whether there is a circuit breaker, and what happens to this system if the dependency is unavailable. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by blast radius of unavailability.

title: Review the dependency upgrade strategy and migration plan
prompt: Review the dependency upgrade strategy and migration plan. Identify dependencies where major version upgrades are available but have been deferred. Find upgrades blocked by breaking changes with no migration path documented. Output a numbered list of pending major upgrades ordered by security risk of staying on the current version.

title: Identify single-maintainer dependencies with abandonment risk
prompt: Identify single-maintainer dependencies with abandonment risk. Find critical dependencies with only one active maintainer or contributor. Assess the blast radius if each were abandoned. Output findings in full paragraphs with the package name, usage criticality, maintainer status, and whether forking or replacing is the recommended mitigation.

title: Identify dependencies that have not been updated in more than 12 months
prompt: Identify dependencies that have not been updated in more than 12 months. For each, assess whether there are known CVEs in the current installed version, whether the latest version has breaking changes that require migration effort, and whether the delay is intentional or due to neglect. Output a numbered list of stale dependencies with the package, current version, latest version, known CVEs, and recommended update action.

title: Audit dependencies for license compliance
prompt: Audit dependencies for license compliance. Find packages with licenses incompatible with the project's intended distribution model — copyleft licenses in a proprietary project, or commercial-use-restricted licenses in a product sold to customers. Find transitive dependencies whose licenses are not surfaced by current tooling. Output findings in full paragraphs with the package name, license type, compatibility issue, and recommended replacement or legal review action.

title: Find dependencies used in only one file or one function that could be re
prompt: Find dependencies used in only one file or one function that could be replaced with a native language feature or a small inline implementation. Identify packages added for a single utility function — format a date, parse a URL, deep clone an object — where the language or standard library now provides an equivalent. Estimate the bundle size reduction and dependency count reduction from removing each. Output a numbered list of replaceable dependencies with the package, the usage location, and the native equivalent.

title: Act as a dependency health engineer auditing the full dependency graph —
prompt: Act as a dependency health engineer auditing the full dependency graph — direct and transitive — for this project. Identify dependencies past their end-of-life, dependencies with known critical vulnerabilities in the version range in use, and dependencies with no releases in the last 18 months that are still actively used. For each at-risk dependency, assess the blast radius if it is compromised or abandoned, the effort to migrate to an actively maintained alternative, and whether a vendored fork is a viable short-term mitigation. Map the dependency update cadence — how frequently are dependencies upgraded on average, and how far behind the latest release is each major dependency. Output a full health report with numbered findings ordered by risk.

title: Evaluate the dependency architecture of this project for structural heal
prompt: Evaluate the dependency architecture of this project for structural health. Identify circular import chains and assess their impact on build performance, testability, and readability. Find dependency clusters — groups of modules so tightly coupled through imports that they effectively form a monolith. Identify external dependencies used only by a single module where the dependency could be encapsulated behind an interface to reduce future migration cost. Assess whether the dependency structure would allow progressive replacement of a core dependency without a full rewrite. Output findings in full paragraphs with specific import chains identified and recommended refactors.

title: Perform an external service dependency audit for this system
prompt: Perform an external service dependency audit for this system. Map every external API, SaaS platform, database, message broker, and cloud service that this system depends on. For each, assess the following: whether there is a circuit breaker or timeout configured, what the documented SLA is and whether this system's own SLA can be met if the dependency degrades, whether there is a fallback or degraded-mode behavior, and whether there is a runbook for when the dependency is unavailable. Identify dependencies with no timeout configured — these are the most dangerous because an unavailable dependency will cause this system to hang indefinitely rather than fail fast. Output a numbered dependency map ordered by blast radius of unavailability.

title: Evaluate the dependency management lifecycle for this project
prompt: Evaluate the dependency management lifecycle for this project. Assess whether there is a documented process for the full lifecycle of a dependency: how it is evaluated and approved before addition, how updates are monitored and applied, how CVEs are triaged and remediated, and how dependencies are removed when no longer needed. Find gaps in each lifecycle phase — dependencies added without approval, no automated CVE monitoring, no process for removing unused dependencies. Assess the current dependency count against industry benchmarks for the project type and identify whether the project is trending toward a bloated dependency graph or a lean one. Output a lifecycle gap analysis with specific process recommendations for each phase.

title: Perform a dependency health audit using the framework of bus factor, act
prompt: Perform a dependency health audit using the framework of bus factor, activity, and maintenance signals. For each direct dependency, assess the health of its upstream project: how many maintainers it has, when the last commit was made, whether issues are responded to within a reasonable time, and whether there is a clear release cadence. Find dependencies whose upstream projects have been effectively abandoned — no commits in 12 months, open CVEs with no response, no active maintainer identified. Assess the risk of each abandoned dependency: how deeply embedded it is in the codebase and how difficult it would be to replace. Identify forks or alternatives that are actively maintained. Output a dependency health scorecard with the highest-risk abandoned or low-activity dependencies and a recommended migration plan.

title: Audit environment parity between dev staging and prod
prompt: Compare the configuration, services, and data between development, staging, and production. Identify differences that create "works in staging, broken in prod" scenarios — missing services, different scale, different config values.
prompt_short: Find configuration and service differences between dev, staging, and prod.
prompt_filtered: Env parity only. Configuration and service differences between environments.

title: Find environment variables missing from any environment
prompt: Compare required environment variables across all deployment targets. Identify variables that exist in production but are missing in staging (causing staging to behave differently) or missing in CI (causing test failures that pass locally).
prompt_short: Find environment variables missing in some but not all environments.
prompt_filtered: Missing env vars only. Cross-environment variable coverage.

title: Review environment-specific config for correctness
prompt: Audit all environment-specific configuration values. Identify values that look wrong for their environment — production debug mode enabled, staging pointing to production data, test credentials in staging.
prompt_short: Check environment configs for values inappropriate for that environment.
prompt_filtered: Config correctness only. Wrong values for the environment context.

title: Assess staging environment reliability and data freshness
prompt: Evaluate how closely staging mirrors production in terms of data volume, service configuration, and reliability. Identify gaps that cause staging to give false confidence before production deployments.
prompt_short: Assess how closely staging mirrors production for reliable pre-deploy testing.
prompt_filtered: Staging fidelity only. Data and config mirror accuracy.

title: Find environment configuration stored in wrong place
prompt: Identify environment-specific values hardcoded in source code instead of environment variables, configuration files committed with secrets instead of using a secrets manager, and config that should vary per environment but is set globally.
prompt_short: Find environment config that is hardcoded in source or stored incorrectly.
prompt_filtered: Config location only. Hardcoded env values and secrets in wrong stores.

title: Implement ephemeral preview environments for every PR
prompt: Design a system that provisions a full-stack preview environment for every open pull request. The environment should start from the PR branch, use isolated databases seeded from staging data, and be automatically destroyed when the PR is merged or closed. Identify the infrastructure and tooling required and estimate the cost per PR-day.
prompt_short: Design ephemeral PR preview environments that spin up and tear down automatically.
prompt_filtered: Preview environments only. Per-PR provisioning and automatic cleanup.

title: Audit environment variable sprawl and consolidate into a config schema
prompt: Count the total number of environment variables consumed by all services. Find variables that are duplicated across services with the same name but potentially different values, variables with no documentation of their purpose, and variables no longer read by any code but still set in the deployment config. Define a config schema (Zod or Joi) that validates all required env vars at startup.
prompt_short: Audit env var sprawl and add a startup config schema that validates all required vars.
prompt_filtered: Env var schema only. Duplication, undocumented vars, and startup validation.

title: Enforce environment-specific rate limits and quotas
prompt: Review whether rate limits and quotas are configured per environment. Find services where the production rate limit is applied in staging (preventing realistic load testing) or where there is no rate limit in development (allowing tests to exhaust third-party API quotas). Define appropriate limits per environment for each external service.
prompt_short: Set environment-specific rate limits so staging and dev use appropriate quotas.
prompt_filtered: Rate limits per environment only. Production vs staging vs dev quota differences.

title: Test configuration validation with chaos engineering
prompt: Inject configuration errors into a staging deployment and verify the system fails fast with a clear error rather than starting and failing silently later. Test missing required env vars, wrong-type values, invalid URLs, and expired credentials. Verify the failure message is actionable and does not expose the expected secret value.
prompt_short: Inject config errors in staging to verify the system fails fast with clear messages.
prompt_filtered: Config chaos testing only. Missing and malformed env var injection in staging.

title: Review secrets-per-environment isolation in the secret manager
prompt: Audit the secret manager configuration to verify that development, staging, and production secrets are stored in separate namespaces or accounts with no cross-environment access. Identify any service account that has read access to secrets from more than one environment. Recommend the IAM and namespace structure that enforces strict isolation.
prompt_short: Verify secret manager namespaces are isolated per environment with no cross-access.
prompt_filtered: Secret isolation only. Cross-environment secret access in secret manager.

title: Create an environment promotion checklist for production releases
prompt: Define a promotion checklist that must be completed before any staging-to-production promotion: staging smoke tests passing, database migration dry-run complete, feature flags set to intended production values, rollback plan documented, on-call engineer notified, and monitoring dashboards open. Integrate the checklist into the CD pipeline as a required approval step.
prompt_short: Create and enforce a staging-to-production promotion checklist in the CD pipeline.
prompt_filtered: Promotion checklist only. Required steps before staging-to-production deployment.

title: Audit third-party service sandbox vs. production configuration
prompt: For every third-party service (payment processor, email provider, SMS gateway, analytics), verify that staging and development use sandbox or test-mode credentials — never production. Identify services where staging sends real emails, charges real cards, or posts to production webhooks. Assess the cost and compliance risk of each misconfiguration.
prompt_short: Find third-party services where staging accidentally uses production credentials.
prompt_filtered: Third-party sandbox only. Staging vs production credential separation per service.

title: Implement environment-aware feature flag defaults
prompt: Audit whether feature flags have sensible default values per environment — new features defaulting to enabled in development, behind a rollout in staging, and off in production until explicitly enabled. Find flags with the same default across all environments, which may enable unreleased features in production if the flag service is unavailable.
prompt_short: Set environment-specific defaults for feature flags so outages fail safely.
prompt_filtered: Flag defaults only. Per-environment default values for feature flags.

title: Reduce environment-specific configuration to a minimum
prompt: Assess how much of the application's behavior changes between environments. Find behavior changes driven by environment-specific code branches (if process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production') rather than environment variables. Recommend replacing all environment-specific code branches with externalized configuration so the same code artifact runs in every environment.
prompt_short: Replace environment-specific code branches with externalized configuration.
prompt_filtered: Config externalization only. Removing env-conditional logic from source code.

title: Design a disaster recovery test for environment reconstruction
prompt: Design and schedule a quarterly disaster recovery test where the production environment is reconstructed from scratch using only the documented runbooks, infrastructure-as-code, and secret manager. Identify any manual step, tribal knowledge, or undocumented dependency that prevents clean reconstruction. Every step that requires a human to remember something is a DR gap.
prompt_short: Run a DR test to find steps that require tribal knowledge to reconstruct production.
prompt_filtered: Disaster recovery only. Environment reconstruction from runbooks and IaC only.

title: Implement environment-aware logging verbosity
prompt: Review logging configuration across all services and environments. Find services that log at DEBUG level in production, services that log at ERROR level only in staging, and services with no log-level configuration at all. Define the expected log level per environment and enforce it via deployment config.
prompt_short: Set and enforce environment-appropriate log levels across all services.
prompt_filtered: Log level config only. Per-environment verbosity settings and enforcement.

title: Audit environment bootstrapping scripts for idempotency
prompt: Review all scripts that set up environment infrastructure — database migrations, seed scripts, initial secret provisioning, and service account creation. Verify each is idempotent: running it twice against an already-configured environment must produce no errors and no duplicate resources.
prompt_short: Verify all environment bootstrapping scripts are safe to run multiple times.
prompt_filtered: Bootstrap idempotency only. Rerun safety for setup and migration scripts.

title: Create an environment dependency graph and start-order documentation
prompt: Document the startup order dependencies between all services in each environment. Find services that assume other services are already running and fail silently when they are not. Implement health-check-based startup ordering in the docker-compose or orchestration config to enforce the correct start sequence.
prompt_short: Document service startup order and enforce it with health-check-based dependencies.
prompt_filtered: Startup ordering only. Service dependency graph and health-check-based sequencing.

title: Implement environment promotion dry-run validation
prompt: Before any environment promotion, run a dry-run that compares the configuration diff, verifies all required secrets are present in the target environment, and checks that all downstream dependencies are reachable. Fail the promotion if any check fails and surface a detailed report of what is missing.
prompt_short: Add a dry-run validation step that checks config and secrets before every promotion.
prompt_filtered: Promotion dry-run only. Config diff, secret presence, and connectivity checks.

title: Review cost allocation tagging across all environments
prompt: Audit whether cloud resources in all environments are tagged with the environment name, owning team, service name, and cost center. Find resources with missing or inconsistent tags. Implement tag enforcement via cloud policy so untagged resources are rejected at creation time.
prompt_short: Enforce cost allocation tags on all cloud resources and reject untagged resource creation.
prompt_filtered: Cost tagging only. Environment and team tag coverage and enforcement policy.

title: Find Misplaced Config
prompt: Find Misplaced Config

title: Map Config Drift Between Environments
prompt: Map Config Drift Between Environments

title: Check Config Validation at Startup
prompt: Check Config Validation at Startup

title: Audit environment parity between development, staging, and production
prompt: Audit environment parity between development, staging, and production. Find services or configuration that exist in production but not in staging, causing staging to be an unreliable test environment. Output findings in full paragraphs ordered by parity gap severity with the missing component and the impact on staging confidence.

title: Find environment variables missing from any environment
prompt: Find environment variables missing from any environment. Identify variables required by the application that are not set in one or more environments. Find variables present in production but absent in staging, causing behavior differences. Output a numbered list of missing variables with the environment, the variable name, and the impact of its absence.

title: Review environment-specific configuration for correctness
prompt: Review environment-specific configuration for correctness. Find configuration values set to production values in staging or development — rate limits too high, email delivery active in staging, payment processors pointing at live APIs in test environments. Output findings in full paragraphs with the config key, the incorrect environment value, and the correct value for each environment.

title: Assess staging environment reliability and data freshness
prompt: Assess staging environment reliability and data freshness. Determine how old the staging data is, whether the staging environment reflects the production schema, and whether staging is stable enough for meaningful pre-production testing. Output findings in full paragraphs with the staleness assessment, schema drift findings, and recommended data refresh strategy.

title: Find environment configuration stored in the wrong place
prompt: Find environment configuration stored in the wrong place. Identify environment-specific values hardcoded in source code, config files committed with secrets, and configuration that should vary per environment but is set globally. Output a numbered list of misplaced config items with the location, what it should be, and the recommended storage approach.

title: Audit environment parity between staging and production
prompt: Audit environment parity between staging and production. Find configuration values that differ unexpectedly between the two environments — different feature flag states, different connection pool sizes, different timeout values, or different versions of a shared service. For each difference, assess whether it is intentional and documented or an accidental divergence. Output a numbered list of parity gaps with the configuration key, staging value, production value, and whether the difference is intentional.

title: Review the environment promotion process from staging to production
prompt: Review the environment promotion process from staging to production. Find steps in the promotion checklist that are performed manually without verification, approval gates with no defined criteria for passing, and promotions that proceed even when the staging health check is failing. Output findings in full paragraphs with the promotion step, the gap in the current process, and the automated or documented check that would close it.

title: Identify environment-specific configuration that is hardcoded in source
prompt: Identify environment-specific configuration that is hardcoded in source code instead of injected at runtime. Find connection strings, API base URLs, timeout values, and feature behavior that changes per environment but is set as a constant in the code. Assess the deployment implications — a code change is required to change environment behavior, rather than a configuration update. Output a numbered list of hardcoded environment configuration items with the file, value, and recommended injection mechanism.

title: Act as a platform engineer auditing the environment configuration strate
prompt: Act as a platform engineer auditing the environment configuration strategy for this system. Map every configuration value across all environments — development, staging, and production. Identify values hardcoded in source code that should be environment variables, environment variables that differ unexpectedly between staging and production causing behavior divergence, and secrets stored in configuration files that should be in a secret store. Assess whether the application validates all required configuration at startup and fails fast with a clear error if any required value is missing. Find configuration that is duplicated across services rather than centralized. Output a full configuration audit with numbered findings ordered by risk of misconfiguration causing a production incident.

title: Evaluate the staging environment as a production readiness gate
prompt: Evaluate the staging environment as a production readiness gate. Determine whether staging is reliable enough to catch production issues before they ship. Identify gaps between staging and production — different configuration values, different service versions, different data volumes, or missing third-party integrations. Find places where tests pass in staging but would fail in production due to environment differences. Assess whether the staging database schema matches production and whether it is refreshed with realistic data on a regular cadence. Output findings in full paragraphs with specific staging-production divergences and their impact on pre-production confidence.

title: Perform a configuration validation audit for this system
prompt: Perform a configuration validation audit for this system. Trace how each service reads its configuration at startup. Identify services that do not validate required configuration at startup — meaning a misconfigured deployment would start successfully and only fail when the missing config is actually needed, potentially hours into operation. Find configuration that is read at request time rather than at startup, delaying misconfiguration discovery. Assess whether there is a configuration schema that is checked against the actual environment values before deployment. Recommend a configuration validation strategy — startup assertions, schema validation, and environment-specific checks — that would catch every misconfiguration before a single request is served. Output findings in full paragraphs with specific services and the validation gaps identified.

title: Evaluate the development environment setup experience for this project
prompt: Evaluate the development environment setup experience for this project. Assess how long it takes a new engineer to have a fully functioning local environment from a fresh clone. Identify every external dependency that must be running locally — databases, message queues, external services, secrets management systems — and assess whether each has a documented setup procedure that works reproducibly on a fresh machine. Find configuration values that are required for the application to start but are not included in the project's environment template. Assess whether the local environment accurately represents production behavior or whether engineers regularly encounter bugs in staging or production that do not reproduce locally due to environment differences. Output a setup experience audit with the time estimate per setup step, the failure points on a fresh machine, and the automation or documentation fixes that would reduce total setup time below 30 minutes.

title: Perform a secrets and configuration segregation audit across all environ
prompt: Perform a secrets and configuration segregation audit across all environments. Verify that production secrets cannot be accessed from the development or staging environment under any configuration. Find cases where the same credentials are used across multiple environments, where a developer can modify staging configuration with their personal credentials, or where a misconfiguration in the staging environment could affect production. Assess whether environment boundaries are enforced at the infrastructure level or only by convention and honor system. Find the weakest link — the single misconfiguration or policy gap that would allow staging to affect production. Output a segregation audit with specific findings and the infrastructure-level controls that would enforce the environment boundaries without relying on human discipline.

title: Find critical paths with no monitoring coverage
prompt: Map every critical user-facing path. For each, verify there is a corresponding monitor — uptime check, synthetic transaction, or real user metric. Identify paths that fail silently with no alert.
prompt_short: Find critical user paths with no monitoring or alerting configured.
prompt_filtered: Monitoring gaps only. Critical path coverage.

title: Audit alert thresholds for false positive rate
prompt: Review all configured alerts. Identify alerts firing more than twice per week without user impact (alert fatigue sources) and alerts with thresholds so high they wouldn't fire until service is degraded for all users.
prompt_short: Audit alert thresholds for false positives and dangerously high limits.
prompt_filtered: Alert threshold only. False positive rate and sensitivity.

title: Identify metrics missing from the monitoring dashboard
prompt: Review the monitoring dashboard against the system's key performance indicators. Identify metrics that should be visible during an incident but aren't — error rate, queue depth, cache hit rate, p99 latency by endpoint.
prompt_short: Find metrics that should be on the dashboard but are currently missing.
prompt_filtered: Dashboard gaps only. Missing metrics for incident response.

title: Review on-call alerting and escalation policy
prompt: Audit the on-call routing. Verify every critical alert has an on-call owner, escalation policy, and acknowledgment timeout. Identify alerts that route to nobody, alerts that never escalate, and services with no on-call coverage.
prompt_short: Verify all critical alerts have an owner, escalation, and timeout.
prompt_filtered: Alert routing only. On-call ownership and escalation paths.

title: Assess synthetic monitoring for critical user flows
prompt: Evaluate whether synthetic monitors (automated user flow tests running against production) cover the most critical paths — sign-up, login, purchase, core feature usage. Identify flows with no synthetic test.
prompt_short: Find critical user flows not covered by synthetic monitoring in production.
prompt_filtered: Synthetic monitoring only. Critical flow coverage gaps.

title: Design SLO-based alerting to replace threshold alerts
prompt: Identify which existing alerts fire on raw thresholds (CPU > 80%) rather than SLO burn rate. Propose SLO-based alerting using a multi-window burn rate model — 1h fast burn and 6h slow burn — for the top 5 user-facing services.
prompt_short: Replace raw threshold alerts with SLO burn-rate alerting for user-facing services.
prompt_filtered: SLO alerting only. Burn rate model design per service.

title: Audit golden signal coverage across all services
prompt: For each service, verify all four golden signals are instrumented and dashboarded — latency (p50/p95/p99), traffic (requests/sec), errors (rate by type), and saturation (CPU, memory, queue depth). Flag any signal missing per service.
prompt_short: Verify all four golden signals are visible for every production service.
prompt_filtered: Golden signals only. Latency, traffic, errors, saturation coverage.

title: Measure mean time to detect across recent incidents
prompt: Review the last 10 incidents. For each, calculate MTTD — the time from when the problem started to when an alert fired or a human noticed. Identify the detection method (alert, user complaint, manual) and whether monitoring could have detected it earlier.
prompt_short: Calculate MTTD for recent incidents and identify detection improvement opportunities.
prompt_filtered: MTTD only. Detection method and improvement per incident.

title: Identify monitoring blind spots for third-party dependencies
prompt: List all third-party services the application depends on. For each, assess whether latency, error rate, and availability are monitored from the application's perspective. Identify dependencies where failures would only be detected via user complaints.
prompt_short: Find third-party dependencies with no application-side monitoring.
prompt_filtered: Third-party monitoring only. Dependency observability from the caller side.

title: Review alert noise reduction and deduplication strategy
prompt: Audit the alerting pipeline for deduplication, grouping, and inhibition rules. Identify alert storms where a single root cause fires dozens of child alerts. Recommend grouping rules that surface the root cause alert and suppress derivatives.
prompt_short: Find alert storms and recommend grouping and inhibition rules.
prompt_filtered: Alert deduplication only. Grouping, inhibition, and storm reduction.

title: Assess business metric monitoring alongside technical metrics
prompt: Verify that business-level metrics — conversion rate, sign-up rate, revenue per minute — are monitored alongside technical metrics. A purely technical monitoring stack can miss incidents that degrade business outcomes without triggering technical alerts.
prompt_short: Find business metrics not monitored that could catch user-impacting incidents earlier.
prompt_filtered: Business metrics only. Conversion and revenue signal coverage.

title: Review heartbeat and dead-man-switch checks for batch jobs
prompt: Identify all cron jobs and batch processes. Verify each has a heartbeat or dead-man-switch check — an alert fires if the job does NOT run within its expected window. Identify jobs that can silently stop running with no alert.
prompt_short: Find cron and batch jobs with no dead-man-switch check.
prompt_filtered: Heartbeat checks only. Cron and batch job silence detection.

title: Audit multi-region monitoring for coverage consistency
prompt: If the system runs in multiple regions, verify monitoring coverage is equivalent across all regions. Identify regions with fewer dashboards, missing alerts, or different sampling rates. A regional failure should be as detectable as a global one.
prompt_short: Verify monitoring coverage is consistent across all deployed regions.
prompt_filtered: Multi-region monitoring only. Coverage equivalence per region.

title: Evaluate time-to-detect for latency degradation vs errors
prompt: Assess whether the monitoring stack detects gradual latency degradation (p99 rising over 30 minutes) as well as it detects hard errors (500 rate spike). Many stacks alert on errors quickly but miss slow latency regressions that degrade user experience.
prompt_short: Verify monitoring catches gradual latency regression as well as error spikes.
prompt_filtered: Latency degradation detection only. Trend-based alerting for p99.

title: Define and instrument error budget monitoring
prompt: For each SLO, calculate the monthly error budget (1 - SLO target). Design a dashboard showing budget remaining, burn rate, and projected depletion date. Alert when budget is 50% consumed at the current burn rate with more than half the window remaining.
prompt_short: Design error budget dashboards and burn-rate depletion alerts per SLO.
prompt_filtered: Error budget only. Remaining budget, burn rate, and depletion alerts.

title: Review monitoring coverage for security events
prompt: Assess whether security-relevant events are monitored — authentication failures above threshold, unusual access patterns, privilege escalation attempts, rate limit hits. Identify security events that generate logs but have no associated alert.
prompt_short: Find security events that are logged but have no monitoring alert configured.
prompt_filtered: Security monitoring only. Auth and access event alerting gaps.

title: Assess on-call rotation health and burnout risk
prompt: Review on-call schedules for alert volume, after-hours alert rate, and rotation fairness. Identify engineers receiving more than 5 alerts per on-call shift on average, or shifts with predominantly overnight pages — both are burnout signals.
prompt_short: Assess on-call alert volume and after-hours rate for burnout risk.
prompt_filtered: On-call health only. Alert frequency, timing, and rotation fairness.

title: Verify production monitoring coverage post-deployment
prompt: After a major deployment, verify that all new code paths, new services, and new dependencies have monitoring configured before declaring the deploy complete. New endpoints should have latency/error monitors; new jobs should have heartbeat checks.
prompt_short: Define a post-deployment monitoring checklist for new code and services.
prompt_filtered: Post-deploy monitoring only. New code and service coverage verification.

title: Identify metrics with stale or incorrect recording rules
prompt: Audit recording rules in the metrics pipeline. Identify rules that aggregate data no longer emitted, rules whose expressions produce incorrect results after schema changes, and pre-computed aggregations that are never queried by any dashboard or alert.
prompt_short: Find stale or incorrect recording rules that produce misleading metrics.
prompt_filtered: Recording rules only. Staleness and correctness of pre-computed aggregations.

title: Review runbook linkage from alerts to resolution steps
prompt: Verify every production alert links directly to a runbook with specific investigation steps. Identify alerts with no runbook link, runbooks that are outdated, and alerts whose runbook says 'see engineer' with no actionable steps — all of which slow incident response.
prompt_short: Verify every alert links to an actionable, current runbook.
prompt_filtered: Runbook linkage only. Alert-to-runbook coverage and freshness.

title: Fill in Missing Data Gaps
prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

title: Gain full understanding before completing
prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

prompt: Find What's Broken

title: Recommend Next Steps
prompt: Recommend Next Steps

title: Score for Production Readiness
prompt: Score for Production Readiness

title: Audit Alert Coverage
prompt: Audit Alert Coverage

title: Check Metric Gaps
prompt: Check Metric Gaps

title: Map Alert Coverage Gaps
prompt: Map Alert Coverage Gaps

title: Identify Stale Dashboards
prompt: Identify Stale Dashboards

title: Audit Heartbeat Checks
prompt: Audit Heartbeat Checks

title: Review SLO Burn Rate
prompt: Review SLO Burn Rate

title: Find Orphaned Alerts
prompt: Find Orphaned Alerts

title: Identify all production services with no monitoring coverage
prompt: Identify all production services with no monitoring coverage. For each, describe what failure mode would go completely undetected. Recommend the minimum viable monitoring setup — one alert per service — that would catch the most common failure type.

title: Audit all existing alerts for alert fatigue signals
prompt: Audit all existing alerts for alert fatigue signals. Find alerts that fire more than five times per week without resulting in a page or remediation. For each noisy alert, recommend tuning the threshold or converting it to a trend-based alert.

title: Find the three most critical user-facing flows with no end-to-end latenc
prompt: Find the three most critical user-facing flows with no end-to-end latency monitor. For each, define the SLO — the latency threshold and the acceptable error budget. Recommend the specific metric and alert configuration to enforce it.

title: Map every cron job and batch process to a heartbeat or dead-man-switch c
prompt: Map every cron job and batch process to a heartbeat or dead-man-switch check. Find jobs that can stop running without any alert firing. For each, specify the expected run window and the alert condition. Recommend the tooling integration that would implement the check with the least engineering effort. Output a prioritized list ordered by business impact of the job stopping silently.

title: Audit the on-call rotation for alert volume and fairness
prompt: Audit the on-call rotation for alert volume and fairness. Calculate the average number of pages per engineer per shift across the past 30 days. Identify the top three noisiest services. Recommend specific tuning changes that would reduce total alert volume by at least 30 percent. Prioritize recommendations by expected page reduction per change.

title: Review SLO error budget status across all user-facing services
prompt: Review SLO error budget status across all user-facing services. Identify services burning their monthly budget faster than the current window allows. For each at-risk service, describe the likely user-visible impact if the budget is exhausted. Recommend the fastest mitigation available without a full release. Output findings ordered by budget burn rate.

title: Identify all monitoring rules that reference deprecated metric names or
prompt: Identify all monitoring rules that reference deprecated metric names or decommissioned services. Audit recording rules and alert expressions for metric labels no longer emitted. Find dashboards referencing non-existent panel data sources. Recommend a cleanup plan that removes noise without affecting legitimate coverage. Output a table of stale rules with recommended actions.

title: Design a monitoring coverage scorecard for each production service
prompt: Design a monitoring coverage scorecard for each production service. Score each service on five dimensions: golden signal coverage, SLO definition, alert routing, runbook linkage, and synthetic monitoring. Weight dimensions by user impact. Output a ranked table with scores and top-priority improvements per service.

title: Review the monitoring strategy across all production services
prompt: Review the monitoring strategy across all production services. Identify every service and classify its monitoring coverage — fully monitored with SLOs, partially monitored, or completely dark. For services with partial coverage, identify the failure modes that would go undetected. Assess whether monitoring covers all three pillars: availability, latency, and error rate. Find any critical user journey — checkout, login, core API calls — that has no end-to-end monitor. Review the on-call alert configuration for noise level and actionability. Identify alerts that are informational rather than actionable and recommend converting them to dashboards. Summarize the five monitoring gaps with the highest incident detection risk.

title: Evaluate the operational readiness of the monitoring stack itself
prompt: Evaluate the operational readiness of the monitoring stack itself. Assess whether the monitoring infrastructure is redundant or whether a single failure could make it dark while production incidents occur. Check whether alerting notifications have fallback channels — if PagerDuty is down, does the team still receive alerts. Find monitoring configurations with no ownership — alerts that fire to a group with no designated responder. Review whether monitoring thresholds are reviewed periodically or set once and forgotten. Recommend the monitoring reliability improvements that would ensure the team never misses a production incident due to monitoring infrastructure failure.

title: Conduct a comprehensive audit of alert quality across all production ser
prompt: Conduct a comprehensive audit of alert quality across all production services. Begin by classifying every alert as actionable, informational, or noise. An actionable alert requires immediate human intervention; an informational alert provides context but no action; a noise alert fires without meaningful signal and degrades responder trust. For each noise alert, identify the misconfigured threshold, missing deduplication rule, or absent grouping policy responsible. Assess whether alert routing ensures that every actionable alert reaches the right on-call engineer within two minutes and escalates to a secondary responder if unacknowledged. Evaluate runbook linkage, verifying whether every alert provides a direct link to a resolution procedure that an on-call engineer unfamiliar with the service could follow. Review whether alerting distinguishes between symptom alerts visible to users and cause alerts indicating internal degradation, and whether suppression rules prevent downstream alerts when the root cause alert is already firing. Identify any alert with no owner, any service with no alert coverage at all, and any critical dependency whose failure would produce no alert. Conclude with a ranked list of the ten alert quality improvements that would most reduce mean time to acknowledge and mean time to resolve.

title: Perform a holistic review of the monitoring stack's reliability and self
prompt: Perform a holistic review of the monitoring stack's reliability and self-health. Assess whether the monitoring infrastructure itself is redundant, whether an outage of the primary metrics backend, alerting engine, or notification channel would leave the team blind to production incidents. Identify single points of failure in the monitoring pipeline, including the scrape agents, ingest endpoints, storage nodes, and delivery channels. Review whether monitoring data retention is sufficient for post-incident analysis, whether a team investigating an incident from three days ago has access to the granular data they need. Examine whether monitoring coverage degrades after deployments, with new services spun up without corresponding alerts, new endpoints without latency monitors, and new cron jobs without heartbeat checks. Assess whether the team has a process for reviewing monitoring coverage as part of the deployment checklist, or whether gaps accumulate silently. Evaluate the cost and complexity of the monitoring stack, whether redundant or overlapping tools create maintenance burden without coverage benefit. Recommend the monitoring infrastructure improvements that would make the stack itself a reliable foundation rather than an additional failure surface.

title: Evaluate monitoring maturity against industry best practices
prompt: Evaluate monitoring maturity against industry best practices. Identify which of the four monitoring maturity levels the organization currently occupies: reactive monitoring where alerts fire after user impact, proactive monitoring where anomaly detection catches issues before users notice, predictive monitoring where trend analysis flags approaching limits, and autonomous monitoring where self-healing actions trigger automatically. For each service, assess which maturity level is appropriate given its criticality and user impact. Find the specific gaps between current capability and the appropriate target level. Review whether synthetic transactions run continuously in production to validate end-to-end functionality, and whether those synthetics alert fast enough to catch failures before a significant fraction of users are affected. Assess whether machine-learning-based anomaly detection is applied to the most volatile metrics, or whether all thresholds are manually configured and therefore slow to adapt to changing traffic patterns. Recommend a phased maturity improvement plan starting with the highest-impact services.

title: Audit distributed trace coverage across all services
prompt: Verify that all services emit distributed traces and that trace context propagates correctly across service boundaries. Identify services that break the trace chain or emit no spans.
prompt_short: Verify distributed traces propagate correctly across all service boundaries.
prompt_filtered: Tracing coverage only. Span emission and context propagation.

title: Find the top unanswerable production debugging questions
prompt: Think through the last 5 production incidents. For each, identify the question that was hardest to answer with the available telemetry. Use that to define specific observability gaps — missing spans, missing log fields, missing metrics.
prompt_short: Find what questions current telemetry cannot answer during an incident.
prompt_filtered: Debugging gaps only. Unanswerable questions from existing telemetry.

title: Assess correlation ID coverage across service calls
prompt: Verify that a correlation ID is generated at the entry point of every request and propagated through all downstream calls, logs, and events. Identify services that drop or fail to forward the correlation ID.
prompt_short: Verify correlation IDs propagate correctly through all service calls.
prompt_filtered: Correlation ID only. Propagation gaps in logs and traces.

title: Review log sampling rates and trace completeness
prompt: Assess whether sampling rates are configured appropriately. High sampling rates for error paths (100%) and lower for high-volume happy paths. Identify services sampling traces at rates too low to catch intermittent issues.
prompt_short: Review sampling rates to ensure errors are always captured completely.
prompt_filtered: Sampling configuration only. Error path vs happy path rates.

title: Identify observability gaps in background and async jobs
prompt: Assess telemetry coverage for background workers, cron jobs, and async processors. Identify jobs that produce no traces, no structured logs, and no metrics — making them invisible when they fail silently.
prompt_short: Find background jobs and async processors with no observability coverage.
prompt_filtered: Async job observability only. Background and cron telemetry gaps.

title: Audit trace context propagation across service boundaries
prompt: Verify that W3C TraceContext headers (traceparent, tracestate) are forwarded on every outbound HTTP call, message queue publish, and gRPC request. Identify service pairs where the trace chain breaks and a new root span is created instead.
prompt_short: Find service boundaries where distributed trace context is dropped.
prompt_filtered: Context propagation only. Trace header forwarding across boundaries.

title: Assess high-cardinality label usage in the metrics pipeline
prompt: Identify metric labels with high cardinality — user ID, request ID, session ID — that cause metric explosion in time-series databases. Recommend moving high-cardinality dimensions to trace span attributes or structured log fields instead.
prompt_short: Find high-cardinality metric labels causing time-series database explosion.
prompt_filtered: Cardinality only. High-cardinality label identification and remediation.

title: Design exemplar linking between metrics and traces
prompt: Assess whether histogram metrics include exemplars — trace ID references embedded in metric data points that allow jumping from a p99 spike on a dashboard directly to a representative slow trace. Identify histograms that lack exemplar emission.
prompt_short: Find histograms missing exemplar links to representative traces.
prompt_filtered: Exemplars only. Metric-to-trace linkage coverage.

title: Apply RED method instrumentation to all request handlers
prompt: Verify every request-handling service instruments Rate (requests/sec), Errors (error rate by type), and Duration (latency distribution) as standard spans and metrics. Identify services where one or more RED signals are missing.
prompt_short: Verify RED method instrumentation across all request-handling services.
prompt_filtered: RED method only. Rate, errors, and duration coverage per service.

title: Apply USE method to all infrastructure resources
prompt: For each infrastructure resource — CPU, memory, disk, network, connection pools — verify Utilization, Saturation, and Errors are instrumented. Identify resources where saturation is not measured (filling up silently) or errors are not counted.
prompt_short: Verify USE method coverage for every infrastructure resource.
prompt_filtered: USE method only. Utilization, saturation, and errors per resource.

title: Review span attribute completeness for debugging utility
prompt: Audit span attributes on the most critical trace types. Verify each span includes enough context to debug without querying a database — user ID, resource ID, operation type, result status, and key input parameters. Identify spans too sparse to be useful.
prompt_short: Find spans with insufficient attributes to debug incidents from traces alone.
prompt_filtered: Span attributes only. Completeness and debugging utility per span type.

title: Assess tail-based sampling strategy for critical traces
prompt: Evaluate whether tail-based sampling is implemented to retain 100% of error traces and slow traces regardless of overall sampling rate. Head-based sampling at 1% discards 99% of error traces — quantify how many incidents this has caused.
prompt_short: Assess whether tail-based sampling retains all error and slow traces.
prompt_filtered: Tail sampling only. Error and slow trace retention strategy.

title: Identify missing instrumentation in third-party library calls
prompt: Audit auto-instrumentation coverage for third-party libraries — HTTP clients, database drivers, message queue clients. Identify libraries making outbound calls without producing spans, making them invisible in traces even when they are the latency source.
prompt_short: Find third-party library calls missing auto-instrumentation spans.
prompt_filtered: Library instrumentation only. Outbound call span coverage.

title: Design a service map from trace data
prompt: Using the distributed trace data, generate a service dependency map showing which services call which, the volume per edge, and the error rate per edge. Identify service pairs with high error rates or unexpectedly high call volume not reflected in architecture diagrams.
prompt_short: Generate a service dependency map with volume and error rate per edge.
prompt_filtered: Service map only. Dependency topology and error rate per call path.

title: Assess observability cost and data volume optimization
prompt: Estimate the monthly cost of the observability stack — metrics storage, trace storage, log ingestion. Identify the top 3 cost drivers and recommend specific volume reduction strategies: sampling, aggregation, shorter retention for verbose services.
prompt_short: Find the top cost drivers in the observability stack and reduce volume.
prompt_filtered: Observability cost only. Ingestion volume and storage spend per signal type.

title: Review dashboard coverage for SRE on-call needs
prompt: Assess dashboards from the perspective of a new on-call engineer. Verify there is a single overview dashboard showing overall system health, and per-service dashboards showing all four golden signals. Identify services with no dedicated on-call dashboard.
prompt_short: Verify on-call dashboards exist and show all golden signals per service.
prompt_filtered: On-call dashboards only. Overview and per-service coverage.

title: Identify observability gaps introduced by recent refactoring
prompt: Review recent large refactors — service splits, library migrations, framework upgrades. Verify telemetry was preserved or re-instrumented after the change. Identify services that lost observability coverage as a side effect of structural changes.
prompt_short: Find services that lost telemetry coverage after recent refactoring.
prompt_filtered: Refactor telemetry only. Coverage regressions from structural changes.

title: Design structured event logging for business-critical flows
prompt: Identify the 5 most business-critical user flows — checkout, signup, subscription upgrade. Design structured event records (not trace spans) capturing each step's outcome, timing, and relevant IDs. These events enable business analytics in addition to technical debugging.
prompt_short: Design structured event logs for the 5 most critical business flows.
prompt_filtered: Business event logging only. Structured events for critical user flows.

title: Audit alert-to-trace correlation for incident investigation
prompt: Verify that every production alert includes a direct link or query that surfaces the relevant traces at the time the alert fired. Identify alerts where the investigator must manually find relevant traces — a friction point that slows every incident.
prompt_short: Verify alerts include direct links to relevant traces at fire time.
prompt_filtered: Alert-to-trace links only. Investigation friction and correlation gaps.

title: Assess feature flag observability and rollout monitoring
prompt: Verify that feature flag evaluations are captured in traces or structured logs, and that rollout dashboards exist showing error rate and latency for flag-on vs flag-off cohorts. Identify flags rolled out with no comparative monitoring.
prompt_short: Verify feature flag rollouts have comparative error and latency monitoring.
prompt_filtered: Feature flag observability only. Rollout cohort comparison monitoring.

title: Audit Exemplar Coverage

title: Review Sampling Strategy
prompt: Review Sampling Strategy

title: Identify all services producing no distributed traces
prompt: Identify all services producing no distributed traces. For each, describe the cross-service debugging scenario that is impossible without traces. Recommend the instrumentation change and the expected debugging capability it unlocks.

title: Audit metric cardinality across all observability pipelines
prompt: Audit metric cardinality across all observability pipelines. Identify high-cardinality labels — user IDs, request IDs, or URLs — embedded in metric names. For each, explain the storage cost and recommend a cardinality reduction strategy.

title: Find all user-facing errors that surface to the end user but produce no
prompt: Find all user-facing errors that surface to the end user but produce no corresponding internal trace or log entry. For each gap, describe the debugging scenario where this silence causes unnecessary MTTR increase. Recommend the instrumentation fix.

title: Design an observability maturity assessment for all services
prompt: Design an observability maturity assessment for all services. Score each service on metrics coverage, trace coverage, and log coverage using a 1-5 scale. Identify services scoring below 3 on any dimension. For each low-scoring service, recommend the single highest-impact instrumentation addition. Output a ranked improvement roadmap.

title: Audit the distributed trace pipeline for data loss risk
prompt: Audit the distributed trace pipeline for data loss risk. Identify buffer configurations, queue sizes, and backpressure behaviors at each stage from SDK emission to storage. Find stages where trace data can be silently dropped under load. Quantify the estimated trace loss rate at 2x current throughput. Recommend buffer sizing or alternative transport configurations.

title: Review span naming conventions across all instrumented services
prompt: Review span naming conventions across all instrumented services. Identify inconsistencies where some services use HTTP method plus path, others use operation names, and others use class and method names. Design a canonical span naming standard. Specify the migration approach and estimate the query and dashboard changes required after standardization.

title: Assess the observability gap introduced by third-party SaaS dependencies
prompt: Assess the observability gap introduced by third-party SaaS dependencies. Identify dependencies where the application has no visibility into internal performance, only seeing the response from the outside. Recommend correlation strategies: client-side timing spans, webhook latency tracking, and provider-supplied tracing integrations. Prioritize by the frequency of user-impacting incidents originating from each dependency.

title: Evaluate the cost efficiency of the observability stack relative to its
prompt: Evaluate the cost efficiency of the observability stack relative to its debugging value. Calculate cost per gigabyte stored for each signal type: metrics, traces, and logs. Identify the top three cost-by-volume contributors in each category. Determine whether the data retained beyond 30 days is queried for incident investigation or only held for compliance. Recommend a tiered retention strategy that reduces storage cost without eliminating the data needed for postmortems.

title: Evaluate the observability design for completeness and actionability
prompt: Evaluate the observability design for completeness and actionability. Identify whether all three pillars — metrics, logs, and traces — are collected across every service. Find services that produce metrics but no traces, making it impossible to correlate a metric spike with a specific request. Assess whether distributed traces are sampled at a rate that captures enough data to diagnose intermittent issues. Review the correlation strategy between logs and traces — whether a trace ID is injected into every log entry so that all log lines for a request can be retrieved from a single trace ID. Check whether business-level metrics — order count, signup rate, feature adoption — are tracked alongside infrastructure metrics. Summarize the five observability gaps that most impede incident diagnosis.

title: Review the tooling and infrastructure supporting observability
prompt: Review the tooling and infrastructure supporting observability. Assess whether the observability stack scales to the current data volume without dropping samples under load. Identify whether log retention and trace retention policies are set appropriately for the incident review and compliance requirements of the organization. Find any observability tool that is a single point of failure — where its unavailability would leave the team blind during an incident. Check whether observability data is accessible during a production incident — that dashboards remain responsive when the production system is under stress. Recommend the observability infrastructure improvements that would most reduce mean time to diagnosis during the next major incident.

title: Perform a deep assessment of trace quality and completeness across the p
prompt: Perform a deep assessment of trace quality and completeness across the production system. Identify every service boundary where a distributed trace must propagate, covering HTTP calls, gRPC calls, message queue publishes, and event stream publishes, and verify that trace context crosses each boundary without creating orphaned root spans. For services using auto-instrumentation, assess whether the auto-instrumentation library captures sufficient span attributes or whether manual instrumentation must supplement it. Review the span lifecycle for common sources of trace data loss: spans dropped by an SDK sampling decision before reaching the collector, spans buffered locally and lost on process restart, and spans arriving at the storage backend after the retention window closes. Assess whether error spans capture the full exception stack, the request inputs that caused the error, and the user context needed to reproduce the failure. Evaluate whether trace data is accessible during an incident with acceptable query latency, whether a p99 latency spike query across all services in the past hour returns results within seconds or minutes. Identify the three most critical trace quality improvements ordered by the debugging scenarios they unlock.

title: Review the integration between observability signals, treating metrics,
prompt: Review the integration between observability signals, treating metrics, traces, and logs as a unified debugging surface rather than three separate tools. Assess whether every error log entry contains a trace ID allowing the investigator to jump directly from the log to the full request trace. Verify whether metrics dashboards include exemplar data linking a p99 spike to a specific representative trace. Evaluate whether alert notifications include pre-built queries that surface the relevant traces and logs from the exact time window of the alert. Identify the investigative workflows that require switching between tools manually, losing context with each switch, and design the correlation improvements that would reduce the average number of tool transitions per incident. Assess whether a new on-call engineer unfamiliar with the specific service could diagnose the cause of an alert using only the observability tooling available, without requiring a senior engineer to interpret raw data. Recommend the correlation improvements that most reduce investigation time.

title: Assess the observability coverage for the system's most critical busines
prompt: Assess the observability coverage for the system's most critical business transactions from an end-to-end perspective. Identify the five highest-value user journeys, covering account creation, purchase completion, subscription upgrade, core feature activation, and data export, and trace each through every service boundary from the browser request to the final data store write. Verify that a complete distributed trace exists for each journey, capturing every service touched, every database query executed, every external API called, and every message enqueued. Find gaps where any segment of the journey produces no span, making it impossible to attribute latency or errors to a specific step. Assess whether business-level attributes such as transaction amount, product category, user tier, and experiment cohort are annotated on the relevant spans to enable business impact analysis from trace data. Evaluate whether the team can quantify the user impact of a latency increase, expressing it as affected transactions per minute and revenue at risk, using observability data alone. Recommend the instrumentation additions that most improve business outcome visibility.

title: Audit log structure for consistency across all services
prompt: Review log output across all services. Identify services using unstructured string logs instead of structured JSON, inconsistent field names for the same concepts, and missing required fields like timestamp, level, and correlation ID.
prompt_short: Audit all services for structured JSON logging and field consistency.
prompt_filtered: Log structure only. JSON format and field naming consistency.

title: Find PII and sensitive data logged in plain text
prompt: Scan all log statements for PII exposure — emails, phone numbers, names, addresses, payment card data, auth tokens. Identify where sensitive values are logged in full rather than redacted or hashed.
prompt_short: Find all logs exposing PII or sensitive values in plain text.
prompt_filtered: PII in logs only. Sensitive data exposure in log statements.

title: Identify missing log coverage for critical operations
prompt: Map critical operations — auth events, payment events, admin actions, data mutations. For each, verify there is a log entry with enough context to reconstruct what happened. Identify operations that leave no audit trail.
prompt_short: Find critical operations with no log entry or insufficient context.
prompt_filtered: Audit trail only. Critical operations with missing or weak logging.

title: Review log retention policy and storage costs
prompt: Assess the log retention configuration for all services. Identify logs retained longer than necessary (wasting cost and creating compliance risk), and logs not retained long enough to support incident postmortems or audit requirements.
prompt_short: Audit log retention periods for compliance and cost optimization.
prompt_filtered: Retention policy only. Retention period vs requirement alignment.

title: Find services logging at wrong verbosity levels
prompt: Identify services that log DEBUG-level data in production (performance and cost waste), and services that log so little that incidents become undebuggable. Recommend the right log level per service and environment.
prompt_short: Find services logging too much or too little in production.
prompt_filtered: Log level only. Verbosity appropriateness per service and environment.

title: Implement dynamic log-level adjustment without restarts
prompt: Assess whether log verbosity can be raised to DEBUG on a specific service instance during an incident without restarting it. If not, design an API or feature-flag-driven log level override so operators can get debug-level logs in production on demand.
prompt_short: Design a runtime log-level override to enable debug logging during incidents.
prompt_filtered: Dynamic log level only. Runtime verbosity control per service.

title: Audit log aggregation pipeline for data loss risk
prompt: Trace the log pipeline from emission to storage: agent collection, transport (Fluentd/Logstash/Vector), indexing, storage. Identify points where logs can be dropped under backpressure — buffer overflows, agent crashes, indexing lag. Estimate tolerable loss rate.
prompt_short: Find points in the log pipeline where data can be lost under load.
prompt_filtered: Log pipeline reliability only. Drop risk and backpressure behavior.

title: Review log sampling strategy for high-volume services
prompt: Identify services emitting more than 10K log lines per second. Evaluate whether sampling is appropriate for INFO-level happy-path logs while preserving 100% of WARN and ERROR entries. Design a sampling policy that reduces volume without hiding problems.
prompt_short: Design a log sampling policy for high-volume services that preserves all errors.
prompt_filtered: Log sampling only. Volume reduction strategy preserving error fidelity.

title: Standardize log field names across the entire system
prompt: Inventory log field names across all services. Identify where the same concept uses different field names — userId vs user_id vs uid, timestamp vs time vs @timestamp. Define a canonical schema and the migration path to enforce it.
prompt_short: Define a canonical log field schema and find all inconsistencies to fix.
prompt_filtered: Schema consistency only. Field name standardization across services.

title: Verify PII tokenization and redaction in the log pipeline
prompt: Assess whether PII redaction happens at the source (in application code before emit) or in the pipeline (post-emit scrubbing). Pipeline scrubbing is unreliable and may miss edge cases. Identify which PII fields are only scrubbed in the pipeline and not at source.
prompt_short: Find PII fields redacted in the pipeline instead of at source.
prompt_filtered: PII redaction only. Source-level vs pipeline-level scrubbing gaps.

title: Design error log enrichment with request context
prompt: Identify error log entries that lack the request context needed to reproduce the failure — missing user ID, missing endpoint, missing request payload summary, missing tenant ID. Design a middleware that auto-injects request context into every ERROR log.
prompt_short: Find error logs missing request context and design auto-enrichment middleware.
prompt_filtered: Error log context only. Request metadata injection on error events.

title: Audit log storage costs and top volume contributors
prompt: Identify the top 5 services or log categories by ingested volume and cost. For each, determine whether the volume is justified by debugging value. Recommend specific log statements to remove, downsample, or move to a cheaper cold storage tier.
prompt_short: Find the top 5 log cost contributors and recommend volume reduction.
prompt_filtered: Log cost only. Volume per service and reduction recommendations.

title: Review log-based metric extraction for key events
prompt: Identify high-value events in logs — payment processed, user registered, error category X — that should be converted to metrics via log-based metric extraction (grok patterns in Datadog, log metrics in CloudWatch). List all events not yet promoted to metrics.
prompt_short: Find log events that should be extracted as metrics but currently aren't.
prompt_filtered: Log-to-metric extraction only. Event promotion candidates.

title: Verify immutable audit log integrity controls
prompt: For compliance-relevant audit logs, verify they are write-once and cannot be modified or deleted by any application or operator. Identify audit log stores with no immutability control — S3 without Object Lock, databases where DELETE is possible by the app user.
prompt_short: Verify audit logs are immutable and cannot be tampered with.
prompt_filtered: Audit log integrity only. Immutability controls and tamper prevention.

title: Assess cross-service log correlation without distributed tracing
prompt: For services that do not emit distributed traces, assess how logs from different services can be correlated to reconstruct a request. Verify correlation IDs are logged consistently and queryable. Identify request flows that cannot be reconstructed from logs alone.
prompt_short: Find request flows where logs from multiple services cannot be correlated.
prompt_filtered: Log correlation only. Cross-service request reconstruction without traces.

title: Review logging in test environments for signal pollution
prompt: Assess whether test, staging, and canary environment logs are mixed with production logs in the same index or storage bucket. Test noise inflates error rates and hides real signals. Verify environment labels are present and filtering is applied in all dashboards.
prompt_short: Verify production logs are cleanly separated from test environment noise.
prompt_filtered: Environment isolation only. Log separation and environment labeling.

title: Design a structured log schema for new microservices
prompt: Define the canonical structured log schema all new microservices must emit from day one: required fields (timestamp, level, service, version, trace_id, span_id, env), recommended fields (user_id, request_id, duration_ms), and prohibited patterns (string concatenation, nested JSON strings).
prompt_short: Define the required structured log schema for all new services.
prompt_filtered: Log schema design only. Required fields and prohibited patterns.

title: Identify slow log queries causing dashboard latency
prompt: Audit the most-used log dashboard queries. Identify queries scanning large time ranges without index fields, queries missing environment or service filters, and queries that take more than 5 seconds — signs of unindexed fields and inefficient log store usage.
prompt_short: Find slow log queries on dashboards and optimize them.
prompt_filtered: Log query performance only. Dashboard query efficiency and indexing.

title: Review logging configuration for secret and credential leakage
prompt: Scan logging configuration and log formatter code for patterns that might inadvertently serialize entire request/response objects — including Authorization headers, cookie values, and request bodies containing passwords. Identify formatters with no field exclusion list.
prompt_short: Find log formatters that may serialize credentials from request objects.
prompt_filtered: Credential leakage only. Log formatter and object serialization risks.

title: Define log-level conventions for the entire engineering team
prompt: Draft engineering-wide log level conventions: ERROR for actionable failures requiring paging, WARN for unexpected states not yet impacting users, INFO for significant business events, DEBUG for developer-only diagnostic data. Include examples from this codebase for each level.
prompt_short: Draft log level conventions with codebase-specific examples for each level.
prompt_filtered: Log level conventions only. Definitions and codebase examples per level.
