title: Review Log Pipeline Reliability
prompt: Review Log Pipeline Reliability

title: Map Audit Trail Coverage
prompt: Map Audit Trail Coverage

title: Find Missing Request Context
prompt: Find Missing Request Context

title: Audit log coverage across all services
prompt: Audit log coverage across all services. Identify services that log no request metadata — no status code, no latency, no user ID. For each gap, recommend the minimum structured log fields needed to support incident investigation.

title: Find all log statements that contain sensitive data — passwords, tokens,
prompt: Find all log statements that contain sensitive data — passwords, tokens, PII, or internal secrets. For each, recommend the redaction strategy — field omission, masking, or hashing — appropriate to the data type and compliance requirement.

title: Assess log retention and search infrastructure
prompt: Assess log retention and search infrastructure. Identify whether logs are retained long enough to support post-incident review and compliance audits. Find any service whose logs are not indexed for search, requiring raw file access for debugging.

title: Define a canonical structured log schema for the entire engineering orga
prompt: Define a canonical structured log schema for the entire engineering organization. Specify required fields including timestamp in ISO 8601, severity level, service name, version, trace ID, span ID, and environment. Specify recommended fields for request handlers including HTTP method, path, status code, latency in milliseconds, and user ID. Identify the top three schema violations currently present across services. Recommend a migration plan with a target date for full schema compliance.

title: Audit log level usage across all services
prompt: Audit log level usage across all services. Identify services logging at DEBUG level in production. Calculate the volume contribution of DEBUG logs as a percentage of total log ingestion cost. Find services where WARN and ERROR levels are used interchangeably without clear criteria. Draft a log level policy document with concrete examples from the codebase for each level. Recommend a log level audit gate to be added to the CI pipeline.

title: Review the logging configuration for secret and credential leakage risk
prompt: Review the logging configuration for secret and credential leakage risk. Identify log formatters that serialize entire request or response objects without field exclusion lists. Find services that log Authorization headers, Set-Cookie values, or request bodies containing password fields. Test whether error log formatters capture exception context that might include stack frames revealing secrets. Recommend the formatter changes and field exclusion patterns that eliminate the risk.

title: Assess cross-service log correlation capability
prompt: Assess cross-service log correlation capability. Identify whether a correlation ID generated at the API gateway is propagated through all downstream service calls and included in every log entry. Find service boundaries where the correlation ID is dropped. Design the middleware or SDK wrapper that would enforce automatic correlation ID propagation. Estimate the reduction in MTTR from having complete cross-service log chains for every user request.

title: Design a log-based alerting strategy for business-critical events
prompt: Design a log-based alerting strategy for business-critical events. Identify the five events that should trigger immediate notification when they appear in logs, including payment failure with retry exhausted, account lockout after brute force, data export completion failure, and webhook delivery failure. For each, design the log query, the alert threshold, and the routing target. Specify the log metric extraction approach that converts log events to time-series signals suitable for SLO tracking.

title: Review the logging strategy across all services for completeness and use
prompt: Review the logging strategy across all services for completeness and usefulness. Identify every service and whether it produces structured logs in a consistent format that can be queried programmatically. Find services that log only errors and miss informational context needed to reconstruct the sequence of events leading to a failure. Assess whether log levels are used consistently — debug, info, warn, error — and whether the production log level captures enough detail without producing noise. Check whether request correlation IDs are propagated through all service calls and logged consistently. Identify any log aggregation gaps where logs from some services are not collected into the central log store. Summarize the five logging gaps that most impede post-incident analysis.

title: Evaluate log security and compliance across the system
prompt: Evaluate log security and compliance across the system. Find log statements that contain sensitive data — passwords, authentication tokens, credit card numbers, or other PII — that should never appear in logs. Assess whether log access is restricted to authorized personnel and whether access itself is audited. Check whether logs are immutable once written — protected from tampering that could hide evidence of a security incident. Review log retention policies against compliance requirements — GDPR, SOC 2, PCI — to verify minimum and maximum retention windows are respected. Recommend the log security hardening changes that would most reduce compliance risk and protect user data.

title: Evaluate the logging infrastructure's reliability and its role as a sour
prompt: Evaluate the logging infrastructure's reliability and its role as a source of truth for incident investigation. Assess whether the log aggregation pipeline can handle peak production log volume without dropping entries, whether backpressure from slow indexing causes log agents to discard data rather than buffer it. Identify the maximum data loss scenario: if the log pipeline fails during a one-hour incident, what fraction of log data would be unrecoverable. Review whether logs are written to a durable local buffer before forwarding, ensuring that a collector restart does not lose log data in flight. Assess whether logs from all environments, including production, staging, and canary, are clearly separated in the storage backend and whether dashboards enforce environment filtering by default. Evaluate the log search experience under incident load, whether query latency degrades when multiple engineers simultaneously search the same time window. Identify single points of failure in the pipeline that could make logs unavailable exactly when they are needed most. Recommend the pipeline reliability improvements that would make logs a dependable incident investigation foundation.

title: Review logging practices for the system's most sensitive operations and
prompt: Review logging practices for the system's most sensitive operations and assess their compliance with relevant data protection requirements. Identify the complete set of operations that must be logged for compliance purposes, including user authentication events, permission changes, data access to sensitive records, payment processing steps, and administrative actions. Verify that each required event produces a log entry with sufficient detail to satisfy an auditor: who performed the action, what was changed, when it occurred, from which system, and what the outcome was. Assess whether these compliance-critical logs are stored in an immutable, tamper-evident format separate from operational logs, with retention periods that meet regulatory minimums. Find any compliance-required event currently not logged, any log entry that lacks required fields for audit purposes, and any audit log stored in a location where it could be modified or deleted. Evaluate whether log access itself is audited, whether the team can answer who queried sensitive log data and when. Recommend the specific logging additions and infrastructure changes needed to achieve full compliance audit readiness.

title: Perform a cost-benefit analysis of the current logging volume and retent
prompt: Perform a cost-benefit analysis of the current logging volume and retention strategy. Calculate the total monthly cost of log ingestion, processing, and storage, broken down by service and by log level. Identify the top five cost contributors and assess whether their log volume is justified by the debugging and compliance value they provide. Find services logging at verbosities that produce significant volume but are never queried during incidents, such as high-frequency DEBUG or TRACE output that inflates cost without improving observability. Review the retention policy for each log category, assessing whether 90-day retention is necessary for operational logs or whether 30 days covers all practical incident review needs, and whether compliance logs genuinely need seven-year retention or whether a shorter period satisfies the applicable regulation. Design a tiered retention strategy that routes high-value logs to hot storage with fast query access, aging logs to warm storage with acceptable query latency, and compliance-only logs to cold archive storage at minimum cost. Estimate the cost reduction achievable through sampling, retention tiering, and verbosity reduction without compromising incident investigation capability.

title: Find the top 5 slowest endpoints by p99 latency
prompt: Identify the slowest API endpoints by p99 response time. For each, trace the latency to its source — slow DB query, N+1 problem, missing cache, synchronous external call. Estimate the improvement potential for each fix.
prompt_short: Find the 5 slowest endpoints and trace each latency to its root cause.
prompt_filtered: Endpoint latency only. p99 ranking and root cause tracing.

title: Identify N+1 query patterns in database access
prompt: Find N+1 query patterns where a single request generates a query for each item in a list. Identify the specific code paths, estimate the query count at scale, and recommend eager loading or batching fixes.
prompt_short: Find all N+1 query patterns and estimate database impact at scale.
prompt_filtered: N+1 queries only. Database query patterns per request.

title: Audit bundle size and client-side load performance
prompt: Analyze the JavaScript bundle size. Identify the largest contributors — unoptimized images, missing code splitting, imported-but-unused exports, large JSON data embedded in JS. Estimate the page load improvement from each fix.
prompt_short: Audit JavaScript bundle size and find the highest-impact reduction opportunities.
prompt_filtered: Bundle size only. JS payload and code splitting analysis.

title: Find missing indexes causing full table scans
prompt: Identify database queries performing full table scans on large tables. Find the columns in WHERE, ORDER BY, and JOIN clauses that lack indexes. Estimate query time reduction from adding targeted indexes.
prompt_short: Find queries causing full table scans and recommend specific indexes.
prompt_filtered: Database indexes only. Missing index identification and impact.

title: Assess render performance and unnecessary re-renders
prompt: Identify components that re-render more often than necessary — missing memoization, unstable prop references, state updates that trigger full subtree re-renders. Estimate the frame rate and interaction latency improvement from each fix.
prompt_short: Find components re-rendering unnecessarily and why.
prompt_filtered: Re-render analysis only. Component render frequency and memoization.

title: Analyze p99 vs p999 tail latency and outlier causes
prompt: Compare p99 and p999 latency distributions for the top 10 endpoints. A large p99-to-p999 gap indicates outlier requests with fundamentally different behavior — garbage collection pauses, lock contention, cold cache hits, or synchronous I/O spikes. Identify the cause for each gap.
prompt_short: Find p99-to-p999 latency gaps and trace each to its root cause.
prompt_filtered: Tail latency only. p999 outlier cause identification per endpoint.

title: Profile GC pause impact on request latency
prompt: Correlate garbage collection pause events with request latency spikes. Identify GC pause duration, frequency, and the latency percentile primarily affected. Recommend heap sizing, allocation rate reduction, or GC tuning parameters to reduce pause impact.
prompt_short: Correlate GC pause events with request latency spikes and recommend tuning.
prompt_filtered: GC impact only. Pause duration, frequency, and latency correlation.

title: Identify lock contention bottlenecks in concurrent code
prompt: Find synchronization primitives (mutexes, read-write locks, semaphores) with high contention — high hold time, high wait time, or low concurrency. Identify whether the locked critical section can be reduced in scope, replaced with a lock-free structure, or eliminated via partitioning.
prompt_short: Find high-contention locks and recommend scope reduction or elimination.
prompt_filtered: Lock contention only. Mutex wait time and critical section analysis.

title: Generate a flame graph analysis for CPU hotspots
prompt: Describe how to capture a CPU flame graph for this runtime — profiler flag, duration, and output format. Then interpret the resulting flame graph: identify the widest plateau functions (CPU hotspots), whether they are application code vs framework vs serialization, and the highest-value optimization target.
prompt_short: Capture and interpret a CPU flame graph to find the top optimization target.
prompt_filtered: Flame graph only. CPU hotspot identification and optimization priority.

title: Audit serialization and deserialization overhead
prompt: Identify the most frequent serialization operations — JSON.parse, JSON.stringify, protobuf decode, database row mapping. Measure or estimate their CPU cost relative to total request processing time. Recommend schema-specific serializers, binary formats, or lazy deserialization where overhead is highest.
prompt_short: Find the highest-cost serialization operations and recommend optimizations.
prompt_filtered: Serialization only. Format, frequency, and CPU cost per operation type.

title: Find connection pool exhaustion causing queued requests
prompt: Identify whether database or HTTP client connection pools are exhausted during load — requests queuing for a connection instead of failing fast. Measure queue depth, wait time, and pool utilization. Recommend pool sizing or circuit breaking to prevent cascading delays.
prompt_short: Find connection pool exhaustion causing request queuing under load.
prompt_filtered: Pool exhaustion only. Queue depth and wait time analysis.

title: Assess cache effectiveness with hit rate and eviction analysis
prompt: Measure cache hit rate, eviction rate, and TTL effectiveness for each cache layer. Identify caches with hit rates below 70% — wasted infrastructure cost. Find caches with high eviction rates indicating undersizing, and TTLs too short to provide meaningful hit rates.
prompt_short: Assess cache hit rate, eviction rate, and TTL effectiveness per cache layer.
prompt_filtered: Cache efficiency only. Hit rate, eviction, and TTL analysis.

title: Identify chatty API patterns with excessive round trips
prompt: Find client-server interaction patterns that make multiple sequential API calls to complete a single user action — fetching a list, then details for each item, then metadata for each detail. Recommend API composition, GraphQL, or batching endpoints to reduce round trips.
prompt_short: Find chatty API patterns making multiple round trips for a single user action.
prompt_filtered: API round trips only. Chatty patterns and batching opportunities.

title: Measure cold start latency for serverless and container workloads
prompt: Identify serverless functions or containers with significant cold start latency. Measure cold start frequency (scale-to-zero events) and p99 cold start duration. Recommend provisioned concurrency, warm pools, or keep-alive strategies for latency-sensitive paths.
prompt_short: Measure cold start frequency and latency for serverless and container workloads.
prompt_filtered: Cold start only. Frequency, duration, and mitigation per workload type.

title: Audit image and media optimization for load performance
prompt: Review all images and media assets served by the application. Identify images served without WebP/AVIF conversion, without responsive srcset, oversized for their display dimensions, or without lazy loading below the fold. Estimate LCP improvement from addressing each issue.
prompt_short: Find images not optimized for format, size, and lazy loading.
prompt_filtered: Image optimization only. Format, sizing, and lazy loading gaps.

title: Identify synchronous filesystem or blocking I/O in request paths
prompt: Scan request handlers for synchronous I/O operations — blocking file reads, synchronous DNS lookups, or CPU-intensive work running on the event loop. Each blocks the thread for all concurrent requests. Identify, measure impact, and recommend async alternatives.
prompt_short: Find blocking I/O in request handlers that stalls concurrent request processing.
prompt_filtered: Blocking I/O only. Synchronous operations in async request paths.

title: Review HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 adoption for multiplexing gains
prompt: Assess whether services use HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 for client-to-server and service-to-service communication. HTTP/1.1 with many small requests suffers head-of-line blocking. Identify connections still using HTTP/1.1 that would benefit most from multiplexing.
prompt_short: Find HTTP/1.1 connections that would benefit from upgrading to HTTP/2.
prompt_filtered: HTTP version only. Multiplexing adoption and head-of-line blocking.

title: Analyze query plan changes causing performance regressions
prompt: Identify slow queries that recently regressed in performance — increased execution time, plan change from index scan to sequential scan. Analyze whether the regression is due to data volume growth, statistics staleness, or a schema change that invalidated an index.
prompt_short: Find recently regressed queries and diagnose the query plan change.
prompt_filtered: Query regression only. Plan changes and statistics staleness.

title: Measure and optimize time-to-first-byte for server-rendered pages
prompt: Profile TTFB for server-rendered pages. Break down the server processing time: auth check, database queries, template rendering, response serialization. Identify the largest time sink. Recommend caching, query optimization, or streaming rendering to reduce TTFB.
prompt_short: Profile TTFB for server-rendered pages and find the largest processing cost.
prompt_filtered: TTFB only. Server-side processing breakdown and reduction strategy.

title: Establish performance budgets and regression detection
prompt: Define performance budgets for the 5 most critical endpoints — maximum p99 latency, maximum bundle size, maximum LCP. Design an automated check that runs in CI and blocks merge if any budget is exceeded, with a clear owner for each budget.
prompt_short: Define performance budgets and CI checks that block regressions.
prompt_filtered: Performance budgets only. Thresholds, CI enforcement, and ownership.

title: Identify the three slowest API endpoints by p99 latency
prompt: Identify the three slowest API endpoints by p99 latency. For each, trace the latency through the call stack — network, compute, database, and downstream calls. Pinpoint the specific operation consuming the most time and recommend a targeted optimization.

title: Find all N+1 query patterns in the codebase
prompt: Find all N+1 query patterns in the codebase. For each, calculate the query count difference between the current implementation and a batched alternative at 100 items. Estimate the database load reduction and recommend the specific fix.

title: Audit database query plans for the five most frequently executed queries
prompt: Audit database query plans for the five most frequently executed queries. Identify queries performing full table scans on large tables. For each, recommend the index or query rewrite that eliminates the scan, and estimate the latency improvement.

title: Profile memory allocation patterns for the top three services by heap us
prompt: Profile memory allocation patterns for the top three services by heap usage. Identify the data structures allocating the most memory per request. Determine whether allocations are GC-eligible or held in long-lived caches. For each high-allocation pattern, recommend a reduction strategy including pooling, streaming, or lazy instantiation. Estimate the GC pause reduction from implementing each change.

title: Audit third-party SDK performance overhead for all production integratio
prompt: Audit third-party SDK performance overhead for all production integrations. Measure initialization cost, per-request overhead, and background thread usage for each SDK. Identify SDKs adding more than 5 milliseconds of overhead to the p99 request path. For each, assess whether a lighter-weight alternative exists or whether the integration can be moved to a background worker. Recommend the SDK optimizations with the highest latency improvement.

title: Review client-side rendering performance for the five most-visited pages
prompt: Review client-side rendering performance for the five most-visited pages. Measure Time to Interactive, Largest Contentful Paint, and Cumulative Layout Shift for each. Identify the top contributor to each metric, covering render-blocking scripts, unoptimized images, and layout-shifting late-loading elements. Recommend the specific optimization for each page and estimate the Core Web Vitals improvement. Prioritize by page visit volume.

title: Assess connection pool configuration across all database and HTTP client
prompt: Assess connection pool configuration across all database and HTTP client connections. Identify services with pool sizes set to defaults rather than tuned to actual concurrency. Measure pool wait time at peak load, the time a request waits for an available connection. Find services where pool exhaustion causes queued requests to add latency. Recommend pool sizes based on observed concurrency and recommend circuit-breaking behavior when the pool is full.

title: Design a performance regression detection system integrated into the CI
prompt: Design a performance regression detection system integrated into the CI pipeline. Define performance budgets for the five most critical endpoints, including maximum p99 latency, maximum database query count per request, and maximum bundle size increment. Specify the load test scenario used to evaluate each budget. Define the CI gate condition to fail the build if any budget is exceeded. Assign a named owner for each budget and the process for intentional budget increases.

title: Analyze system performance for the most critical user-facing operations
prompt: Analyze system performance for the most critical user-facing operations. Identify the ten highest-traffic API endpoints and their current p50, p95, and p99 latency under production load. Find the endpoints where latency is degrading over time as data volume grows. Profile the most expensive operations — database queries, external API calls, CPU-bound computations — and identify the top optimization opportunities. Look for synchronous blocking operations that could be parallelized or moved to background processing. Assess whether performance regressions would be detected before they affect users. Summarize the five highest-impact performance improvements ranked by user experience benefit.

title: Evaluate database performance specifically
prompt: Evaluate database performance specifically. Identify the most frequently executed queries and their execution plans. Find queries that perform full table scans on tables with significant row counts. Identify N+1 query patterns where a loop generates one query per item rather than a single batched query. Assess index coverage — whether the columns most frequently used in WHERE, ORDER BY, and JOIN clauses are indexed. Look for queries that lock rows or tables for longer than necessary. Review connection pool configuration to ensure it is sized appropriately for peak concurrent query load. Recommend the database changes — new indexes, query rewrites, connection pool tuning — that would most reduce database load.

title: Conduct a comprehensive analysis of the application's memory usage patte
prompt: Conduct a comprehensive analysis of the application's memory usage patterns and their impact on performance and reliability. Identify the services with the highest heap usage and the most frequent garbage collection events. Profile the allocation patterns of each high-usage service, whether memory is consumed by long-lived caches, by large per-request allocations that are quickly released, or by data structures that grow unbounded over time. Assess whether heap sizes are configured appropriately for observed usage, or whether services operate near their memory limits causing frequent GC pauses. Identify memory leaks, objects that are allocated but never freed, by comparing heap snapshots taken at different points in a service's lifetime. Review whether in-process caches are bounded with eviction policies or whether they grow without limit until the process is restarted. Evaluate whether memory-intensive operations such as large dataset processing, image resizing, and audio transcoding are isolated to services with appropriate memory limits rather than running in shared application processes. Recommend the memory optimization changes that would most reduce GC pause duration and eliminate out-of-memory risk.

title: Perform a full audit of the application's caching effectiveness across a
prompt: Perform a full audit of the application's caching effectiveness across all cache layers. Identify every cache in use, including browser cache, CDN edge cache, application-level in-process cache, distributed Redis cache, and database query cache, and measure the hit rate for each. A hit rate below 80 percent suggests either a TTL that is too short, a cache key that is too granular, or content being cached that changes too frequently to benefit from caching. Find cache stampede risks, high-traffic cache keys with no lock or probabilistic early expiration, where a simultaneous expiry causes all concurrent requests to hit the origin simultaneously. Assess whether cache invalidation is implemented correctly or whether stale data persists beyond its valid window. Review whether cache sizing is appropriate: caches that are too small evict entries before they can be reused, while caches that are too large waste memory. Evaluate whether read-through, write-through, and cache-aside patterns are applied appropriately to the data access characteristics of each cached resource. Recommend the specific cache configuration changes, covering TTL adjustments, key redesign, size increases, and stampede protection, that would most improve hit rates and reduce origin load.

title: Analyze the application's behavior under sustained load and during traff
prompt: Analyze the application's behavior under sustained load and during traffic spikes to identify performance characteristics that only emerge at scale. Run a load test targeting 2x current peak traffic and measure how key performance indicators evolve as load increases: does p99 latency grow linearly with request rate, or does it degrade sharply at a specific inflection point indicating a bottleneck. Identify the system component that saturates first, whether it is CPU on an application server, connection pool slots to the database, maximum throughput of a message queue, or rate limits imposed by a third-party API. Assess whether the performance degradation at saturation is graceful, where requests queue briefly before completing successfully, or abrupt, where requests fail immediately when capacity is exceeded. Review whether auto-scaling responds quickly enough to traffic spikes to prevent user-visible degradation. Evaluate whether performance benchmarks are run before major deployments to catch regressions before they reach users. Recommend the performance infrastructure investments, covering load testing tooling, benchmark CI gates, and autoscaling trigger tuning, that would give the team confidence in predictable performance at any traffic level.

title: Identify the first bottleneck that breaks at 10x load
prompt: Walk through the system architecture at 10x current load. Identify the component that hits its limit first — DB connections, API rate limits, CPU on a single service, queue depth, memory on a singleton. Estimate the actual traffic threshold.
prompt_short: Find the first component that breaks at 10x current traffic.
prompt_filtered: Scalability ceiling only. Bottleneck identification at load multiples.

title: Find stateful components that block horizontal scaling
prompt: Identify services or components that cannot scale horizontally because they hold state in memory, on local disk, or via sticky sessions. For each, recommend a stateless refactor using distributed caching, object storage, or external session management.
prompt_short: Find stateful services that cannot scale horizontally.
prompt_filtered: Horizontal scaling blockers only. In-memory and local state.

title: Assess database connection pool sizing for scale
prompt: Review database connection pool configuration across all services. Identify services likely to exhaust the connection pool under load, and assess whether connection pooling middleware (PgBouncer, RDS Proxy) is needed.
prompt_short: Assess connection pool sizes and find exhaustion risk under load.
prompt_filtered: Connection pool only. Pool size vs concurrent load analysis.

title: Identify synchronous patterns that limit throughput
prompt: Find request paths that perform work synchronously that could be decoupled — sending emails during request handling, writing to multiple databases in series, calling external APIs inline. Estimate throughput increase from async decoupling.
prompt_short: Find synchronous work in request paths that should be async.
prompt_filtered: Sync-to-async candidates only. Request path throughput limiters.

title: Review load balancing and traffic distribution strategy
prompt: Assess the load balancing configuration. Verify health checks are configured correctly so unhealthy instances are removed from rotation, load is distributed evenly across instances, and connection draining is configured for graceful shutdown.
prompt_short: Review load balancer health checks, distribution, and draining config.
prompt_filtered: Load balancing only. Health checks and distribution correctness.

title: Design auto-scaling triggers based on leading indicators
prompt: Assess current auto-scaling triggers. If scaling on CPU, evaluate whether CPU is a lagging indicator — services often degrade before CPU visibly spikes. Design triggers based on leading indicators: request queue depth, connection pool utilization, or custom application metrics.
prompt_short: Replace lagging CPU auto-scaling triggers with leading indicator metrics.
prompt_filtered: Auto-scaling triggers only. Leading indicator design per service.

title: Identify data-layer sharding candidates for write scale
prompt: Identify database tables receiving the highest write throughput. For each, assess whether horizontal sharding by a natural partition key (user_id, tenant_id, date) is feasible. Estimate the write throughput per shard and whether the single-node limit will be reached in 12 months.
prompt_short: Find write-heavy tables that need sharding and recommend a partition key.
prompt_filtered: Sharding candidates only. Write throughput and partition key analysis.

title: Evaluate vertical vs horizontal scaling tradeoffs per service
prompt: For each service approaching its scale limit, compare vertical scaling (larger instance) vs horizontal scaling (more instances). Consider the effort to make the service stateless, the cost differential, and whether the bottleneck is CPU, memory, or I/O — each favors a different strategy.
prompt_short: Compare vertical vs horizontal scaling options for services near their limit.
prompt_filtered: Scaling strategy only. Vertical vs horizontal tradeoffs per service.

title: Assess queue depth growth and consumer scaling strategy
prompt: Monitor message queue depth trends for each queue. Identify queues growing faster than consumers can process. Assess whether consumers can scale horizontally, whether message ordering constraints prevent parallelism, and whether the queue is a bottleneck or a symptom.
prompt_short: Find queues growing faster than consumers can drain and design scaling.
prompt_filtered: Queue consumer scaling only. Depth trends and parallelism constraints.

title: Design a rate limiting strategy for the public API
prompt: Design a tiered rate limiting strategy for the public API: per-IP for unauthenticated requests, per-user-ID for authenticated requests, per-tenant for B2B clients. Define limit values, window sizes, and the response format including Retry-After header semantics.
prompt_short: Design tiered rate limits for public API covering IP, user, and tenant dimensions.
prompt_filtered: Rate limiting design only. Tier structure, limits, and response semantics.

title: Audit caching strategy for database read relief
prompt: Identify the top 10 most frequent database read queries. For each, assess whether the result can be cached (TTL, cache invalidation strategy, consistency tolerance). Estimate the database load reduction from caching each and recommend a cache layer placement.
prompt_short: Find the top database queries suitable for caching and estimate load reduction.
prompt_filtered: Database read cache only. Top queries, TTL strategy, and load reduction.

title: Evaluate CDN offload ratio for static and cacheable content
prompt: Measure what percentage of total traffic is served from CDN cache vs origin. For any cacheable content (assets, public API responses, images) not hitting CDN, identify missing Cache-Control headers, CDN bypass routes, or dynamic rendering of content that could be statically cached.
prompt_short: Measure CDN hit rate and find cacheable content bypassing CDN.
prompt_filtered: CDN offload only. Cache-Control headers and origin bypass analysis.

title: Identify synchronous fan-out patterns limiting throughput
prompt: Find request handlers that synchronously fan out to N downstream calls — calling all microservices in sequence before responding. Assess which downstream calls can be parallelized with Promise.all or equivalent, and which can be made async via event publishing.
prompt_short: Find synchronous fan-out patterns and recommend parallelization or async decoupling.
prompt_filtered: Fan-out patterns only. Sequential call chains and parallelization opportunities.

title: Assess read replica usage and routing correctness
prompt: Verify read replicas are being used for read-heavy queries and that replica lag is monitored. Identify queries routed to the primary unnecessarily, and verify code that requires strong consistency is explicitly routed to primary. Estimate primary load reduction from correct replica routing.
prompt_short: Verify read replicas are used for reads and that replica lag is monitored.
prompt_filtered: Read replica routing only. Primary vs replica query distribution.

title: Design graceful degradation at 5x and 10x load
prompt: For each service, define what happens at 5x and 10x peak load if auto-scaling cannot keep up. Design graceful degradation behaviors — rate-limit low-priority users first, serve stale cached data, disable non-essential features — so the core experience survives overload.
prompt_short: Design graceful degradation behaviors at 5x and 10x load per service.
prompt_filtered: Overload degradation only. Feature shedding order and core experience preservation.

title: Review stateless service guarantees for safe horizontal scaling
prompt: For each service scaled horizontally, verify it is truly stateless: no in-process caches that diverge between instances, no local filesystem writes, no instance-specific scheduled work. Identify any state that would cause inconsistency when requests route to different instances.
prompt_short: Verify services claimed as stateless have no per-instance state divergence.
prompt_filtered: Statelessness verification only. In-process and filesystem state per service.

title: Estimate infrastructure cost projection at 10x scale
prompt: Using current infrastructure costs and traffic-to-cost ratios, project the monthly cost at 10x current load. Identify the cost drivers that scale super-linearly (egress, storage IOPS, per-transaction pricing) and recommend architectural changes to reduce them before reaching that scale.
prompt_short: Project infrastructure cost at 10x scale and find super-linear cost drivers.
prompt_filtered: Cost scaling only. Super-linear drivers and cost optimization before growth.

title: Assess Kubernetes resource requests and limits correctness
prompt: Audit Kubernetes resource requests and limits for all pods. Identify pods with no limits set (noisy neighbor risk), pods with requests far below actual usage (scheduled poorly), and pods being OOM-killed (limits too low). Recommend requests and limits based on observed usage.
prompt_short: Audit K8s resource requests and limits against actual pod usage.
prompt_filtered: K8s resources only. Requests, limits, and OOM kill analysis.

title: Find global state preventing multi-region deployment
prompt: Identify application components that cannot be deployed in multiple regions due to global state dependencies — single-region databases, global locks, centralized queues, or single-region third-party services. For each, design a strategy to either replicate or regionalize the dependency.
prompt_short: Find global state dependencies preventing multi-region deployment.
prompt_filtered: Multi-region blockers only. Global state and single-region dependencies.

title: Design a load test plan for the next scale milestone
prompt: Design a realistic load test plan targeting the next scale milestone — 2x, 5x, or 10x current peak. Define the traffic shape (ramp-up curve, user scenarios), success criteria (p99 latency thresholds, error rate), and which infrastructure components to instrument during the test.
prompt_short: Design a load test plan targeting the next scale milestone with clear success criteria.
prompt_filtered: Load test design only. Traffic shape, scenarios, and success criteria.

prompt: Identify Stateful Service Blockers

title: Find Database Write Ceilings

title: Audit CDN Offload Ratio
prompt: Audit CDN Offload Ratio

title: Review Queue Consumer Lag
prompt: Review Queue Consumer Lag

title: Map Multi-Region Gaps
prompt: Map Multi-Region Gaps

title: Identify the component that would be first to fail at 10x current traffi
prompt: Identify the component that would be first to fail at 10x current traffic. Trace its current capacity ceiling. Recommend the specific architectural or infrastructure change that raises that ceiling to 50x without requiring a full redesign.

title: Audit horizontal scaling readiness across all services
prompt: Audit horizontal scaling readiness across all services. Identify services with local state, sticky sessions, or in-memory caches that prevent adding instances without breaking functionality. For each, recommend the change needed to make the service stateless and horizontally scalable.

title: Model database growth over the next 12 months based on current write rat
prompt: Model database growth over the next 12 months based on current write rates. Identify which tables will hit storage or performance limits first. Recommend data lifecycle policies — archiving, partitioning, or offloading — for the top three growth-bound tables.

title: Map every user-facing feature to the database tables it reads and writes
prompt: Map every user-facing feature to the database tables it reads and writes. Identify the three tables receiving the highest combined read and write throughput. Project their row count and storage size at 10x current user growth. Recommend the table-level optimizations, including indexing, partitioning, read replicas, or archival, that would sustain performance at that scale without requiring a full schema rewrite.

title: Audit every queue in the system for consumer scaling constraints
prompt: Audit every queue in the system for consumer scaling constraints. Identify queues where message ordering requirements prevent parallelism. Find queues with consumers that hold external locks during processing, preventing concurrent consumption. For each constrained queue, estimate the maximum throughput ceiling given current constraints. Recommend architectural changes that would raise the ceiling by at least 5x.

title: Review multi-region deployment readiness for the three most critical ser
prompt: Review multi-region deployment readiness for the three most critical services. Identify data stores with no geographic replication that would prevent active-active or active-passive multi-region operation. For each, assess replication lag tolerance, whether the application can tolerate eventual consistency or requires strong consistency. Recommend the specific replication strategy and conflict resolution approach for each data store.

title: Design a traffic forecasting model for the next 12 months based on curre
prompt: Design a traffic forecasting model for the next 12 months based on current growth metrics. Identify the growth rate of daily active users, API request volume, storage consumption, and outbound data transfer. Project each at 6 and 12 months. Identify the infrastructure components that would require upgrades within 6 months at the current rate. Recommend the scaling investments needed before those limits are reached.

title: Assess whether the application's architecture supports zero-downtime hor
prompt: Assess whether the application's architecture supports zero-downtime horizontal scaling, adding instances without restarting existing ones and without user-visible impact. Identify components that require coordinated restarts during scaling events. Find services that perform initialization work at startup that causes elevated error rates for the first 30 seconds after instance launch. Recommend changes to enable transparent horizontal scaling with no user-visible impact during scale-out events.

title: Assess scalability headroom across the entire system
prompt: Assess scalability headroom across the entire system. Identify the current peak load for each service and how much headroom remains before hitting capacity limits. Find services that scale vertically — requiring larger machines — rather than horizontally — adding more instances. Assess whether the database can scale independently of the application layer, or whether scaling requires simultaneous changes to both. Identify shared resources — connection pools, caches, rate limiters — that become bottlenecks before individual services saturate. Look for any architectural decisions made at small scale that would require significant rework to support 10x growth. Summarize the five highest-priority scalability investments based on proximity to current limits.

title: Evaluate the scalability planning process
prompt: Evaluate the scalability planning process. Assess whether the team has defined capacity thresholds that trigger scaling events before user impact occurs. Identify whether load testing is performed before major feature launches that change traffic patterns. Check whether auto-scaling policies are configured and tested — particularly whether scale-down is tested as carefully as scale-up. Find any service that requires manual intervention to scale, creating an operational bottleneck during traffic spikes. Review the data retention strategy for growing tables — whether old data is archived, partitioned, or left to accumulate without limit. Recommend the scalability planning improvements that would give the team confidence in handling 3x growth without an emergency response.

title: Conduct a detailed analysis of the application's data access patterns an
prompt: Conduct a detailed analysis of the application's data access patterns and their implications for long-term scalability. Identify the read-to-write ratio for each major data store and assess whether the current architecture is optimized for that ratio. A read-heavy workload benefits from read replicas, aggressive caching, and CDN offload, while a write-heavy workload requires attention to replication lag, connection pool sizing, and write throughput limits. Find the specific database queries that contribute the most to read throughput, and assess whether their results could be cached, precomputed, or served from a read replica without consistency issues. Identify write patterns that could be made asynchronous, moving synchronous database writes to event-driven updates that decouple the user-facing response time from the persistence operation. Evaluate whether the application performs well on the current database size and whether query performance degrades predictably as table sizes grow. Review whether data that is no longer actively needed is archived or deleted, preventing table bloat that slows all queries on those tables. Recommend the data access pattern improvements that would sustain current query performance at 5x data volume.

title: Evaluate the cost efficiency of the current infrastructure and its cost
prompt: Evaluate the cost efficiency of the current infrastructure and its cost scaling behavior as traffic grows. Identify the services with the highest cost per request and assess whether that cost is driven by compute, memory, storage I/O, or egress. Find infrastructure costs that scale super-linearly with traffic, where doubling traffic more than doubles cost, versus costs that scale linearly or sub-linearly. Assess whether the application uses spot or preemptible instances for batch and background workloads where interruptions are tolerable, reducing costs significantly compared to on-demand pricing. Review whether auto-scaling policies scale down as aggressively as they scale up, since over-provisioned instances running at low utilization represent pure waste. Evaluate whether read-heavy traffic could shift to cheaper CDN delivery instead of origin compute for cacheable responses. Identify the infrastructure cost optimizations that would reduce the cost per active user while maintaining current performance and reliability SLOs. Recommend a cost efficiency improvement plan with estimated savings and implementation effort for each action.

title: Assess the organization's capacity planning process and its effectivenes
prompt: Assess the organization's capacity planning process and its effectiveness at preventing capacity-related incidents. Identify whether the engineering team has a documented process for reviewing infrastructure capacity against projected growth on a regular cadence, with quarterly reviews of storage, compute, and network headroom. Evaluate whether capacity planning is reactive, triggered by incidents, or proactive, triggered by defined utilization thresholds. Find infrastructure components currently operating above 70 percent peak utilization that have no scheduled capacity increase, representing imminent risk. Review whether load testing is conducted before major feature launches that introduce new traffic patterns, such as file uploads, video processing, or bulk data exports, that could saturate infrastructure components sized for prior workloads. Assess whether on-call engineers have the authority and runbook access to add capacity during an incident without waiting for a management approval chain. Recommend the capacity planning process improvements that would eliminate capacity-related incidents and give the team visibility into approaching limits before users are affected.

title: Map all failure modes and their user-visible impact
prompt: Enumerate every component that can fail. For each, describe the failure mode, the user-visible symptom, the blast radius, and whether degradation is graceful or a hard failure. Prioritize by combination of likelihood and impact.
prompt_short: Map all failure modes and their user-visible impact and blast radius.
prompt_filtered: Failure mode analysis only. Impact and blast radius mapping.

title: Review retry logic for exponential backoff and jitter
prompt: Audit all retry implementations. Verify they use exponential backoff with jitter (not fixed intervals), have a maximum retry count, and don't retry on 4xx errors. Identify retries that could cause thundering herd on a recovering service.
prompt_short: Audit retry logic for backoff, jitter, and thundering herd prevention.
prompt_filtered: Retry logic only. Backoff strategy and non-retryable error handling.

title: Identify operations that are not idempotent but should be
prompt: Find critical operations — charge payment, send email, create record — that are not idempotent. Assess the consequence of double-execution (double charge, duplicate email, duplicate record). Recommend idempotency key patterns for each.
prompt_short: Find non-idempotent operations where double-execution causes real harm.
prompt_filtered: Idempotency gaps only. Critical operations with double-execution risk.

title: Assess graceful degradation for non-critical features
prompt: Identify features whose failure should result in graceful degradation rather than a full service error. Verify these features have fallback behavior implemented and that their failure is isolated from the critical path.
prompt_short: Find non-critical features that should degrade gracefully but currently fail hard.
prompt_filtered: Graceful degradation only. Feature isolation and fallback behavior.

title: Review timeout configuration across all service calls
prompt: Audit timeout configuration for every outbound service call — databases, external APIs, queue operations, file storage. Identify calls with no timeout (blocking indefinitely) and calls with timeouts shorter than valid slow operations.
prompt_short: Find all outbound calls with missing or misconfigured timeouts.
prompt_filtered: Timeout configuration only. Missing and inappropriate timeout values.

title: Design error budget policy for SLO breaches
prompt: For each defined SLO, design an error budget policy: what actions are required when 50% of budget is consumed (reduce deploy frequency), 75% (freeze features), 100% (all hands incident). Define who owns the decision and how it is communicated to stakeholders.
prompt_short: Define error budget policies and escalation actions at 50%, 75%, 100% burn.
prompt_filtered: Error budget policy only. Actions and ownership at budget thresholds.

title: Design a chaos engineering experiment for this system
prompt: Design a safe, scoped chaos experiment for this system: the steady-state hypothesis, the failure injection (kill a pod, throttle a dependency, drop packets), the blast radius containment strategy, and the rollback procedure if the hypothesis is violated.
prompt_short: Design a scoped chaos experiment with hypothesis, injection, and rollback.
prompt_filtered: Chaos experiment only. Hypothesis, injection, containment, and rollback.

title: Audit circuit breaker configuration and thresholds
prompt: Review circuit breaker configurations for all outbound calls. Verify trip thresholds are calibrated to real error rates (not so tight they trip on normal variance, not so loose they let cascades propagate), half-open probe behavior is correct, and breaker state is monitored.
prompt_short: Audit circuit breaker thresholds, probe behavior, and monitoring coverage.
prompt_filtered: Circuit breakers only. Threshold calibration and state monitoring.

title: Verify bulkhead isolation between services
prompt: Assess whether bulkheads isolate failure domains — separate thread pools or connection pools per downstream dependency so one slow dependency cannot exhaust resources for all others. Identify shared pools that allow one failing service to starve all outbound calls.
prompt_short: Find shared connection pools that allow one failing dependency to starve others.
prompt_filtered: Bulkhead isolation only. Shared resource failure domain analysis.

title: Assess fallback behavior for degraded dependencies
prompt: For each critical dependency, define the fallback behavior when it is unavailable: serve stale cache, return empty results, show a placeholder, or fail fast. Identify dependencies where failure causes a hard error to users instead of graceful degradation.
prompt_short: Define fallback behaviors for each dependency when it is unavailable.
prompt_filtered: Fallback behavior only. Dependency unavailability response per service.

title: Review jitter implementation in all retry and backoff logic
prompt: Inspect retry implementations for jitter — random delay added to backoff to prevent synchronized retry storms. Identify retries using fixed-interval or pure exponential backoff without jitter. Calculate the theoretical retry storm magnitude and recommend full jitter or decorrelated jitter.
prompt_short: Find retry implementations missing jitter and calculate thundering herd risk.
prompt_filtered: Retry jitter only. Fixed-interval and jitter-free backoff implementations.

title: Define and instrument SLIs for each user-facing service
prompt: For each user-facing service, define the Service Level Indicators: availability (successful requests / total requests), latency (% of requests below threshold), and quality (% of responses with correct data). Verify each SLI is measured from the user's perspective, not the server's.
prompt_short: Define SLIs measured from the user perspective for each user-facing service.
prompt_filtered: SLI definition only. User-perspective measurement per service.

title: Assess health check endpoint accuracy and completeness
prompt: Review health check endpoints for all services. Verify they check actual readiness — database connectivity, dependency reachability, warm cache state — not just that the process is running. Identify health checks that return 200 even when the service cannot handle requests.
prompt_short: Find health checks returning 200 when the service is actually unable to serve traffic.
prompt_filtered: Health check accuracy only. Readiness vs liveness check completeness.

title: Map failure propagation paths and cascade risk
prompt: Trace how a failure in each critical service propagates through the system. Identify services that call multiple downstream services synchronously — where one failure becomes N failures upstream. Map all cascade paths and recommend circuit breakers or async decoupling at high-risk points.
prompt_short: Map failure cascade paths and identify the highest-risk propagation points.
prompt_filtered: Cascade analysis only. Failure propagation paths and circuit breaker placement.

title: Review deployment pipeline for reliability gate coverage
prompt: Audit the deployment pipeline for reliability gates: load testing, canary analysis, synthetic check validation, and rollback automation. Identify pipelines that deploy directly to 100% traffic with no automated reliability validation — relying entirely on manual monitoring post-deploy.
prompt_short: Find deployment pipelines missing automated reliability gates before full rollout.
prompt_filtered: Deployment gates only. Canary, load test, and rollback automation gaps.

title: Quantify reliability improvement ROI from top three fixes
prompt: Identify the top three reliability improvements available for this system. For each, estimate the expected reduction in incidents per month, the engineering effort to implement, and the user-impact reduction in minutes of degradation per month. Prioritize by ROI.
prompt_short: Rank the top three reliability fixes by estimated ROI in incidents and engineering hours.
prompt_filtered: Reliability ROI only. Incident reduction vs implementation effort.

title: Assess dependency version pinning for reproducible deployments
prompt: Verify all dependencies — language packages, OS packages, container base images — are pinned to exact versions in lock files or image digests. Identify any unpinned dependencies that could introduce a breaking change on the next deployment without a code change.
prompt_short: Find unpinned dependencies that could break deployments without code changes.
prompt_filtered: Dependency pinning only. Version locks and image digest usage.

title: Design a kill switch strategy for new features
prompt: Design a kill switch framework for new features: a centralized feature flag store, fast evaluation at the edge, a clear owner per flag, and a documented on-call runbook step to disable any feature within 60 seconds if it causes a reliability incident.
prompt_short: Design a kill switch framework for disabling features within 60 seconds.
prompt_filtered: Kill switch design only. Flag evaluation, ownership, and runbook integration.

title: Audit dependency health and version freshness
prompt: Audit the age of all major dependencies. Identify packages more than 2 major versions behind the current release — these accumulate unpatched vulnerabilities and make migration increasingly expensive. Build a prioritized upgrade plan starting with dependencies on the critical path.
prompt_short: Find major dependencies significantly behind current release and prioritize upgrades.
prompt_filtered: Dependency freshness only. Major version lag and upgrade priority.

title: Review multi-tenancy isolation for reliability between tenants
prompt: Assess whether one tenant's heavy usage can degrade service for other tenants — shared connection pools, shared queues, shared rate limiters. Identify the isolation boundaries and whether a noisy tenant can trigger alerts or SLO misses for unrelated tenants.
prompt_short: Find shared resources where one tenant can degrade reliability for others.
prompt_filtered: Tenant isolation only. Noisy neighbor risk and shared resource analysis.

title: List all services with no defined SLO
prompt: List all services with no defined SLO. For each, identify the most appropriate SLO type — availability, latency, or error rate — based on user impact. Recommend a starting SLO target and the alert threshold that would give the team a meaningful error budget.

title: Identify services that have SLOs defined but no automated error budget b
prompt: Identify services that have SLOs defined but no automated error budget burn rate alerts. For each, describe the scenario where an SLO is breached without the team receiving any notification. Recommend the burn rate alert configuration that would detect breaches early.

title: Audit the reliability of the most critical service in the system over th
prompt: Audit the reliability of the most critical service in the system over the past 30 days. Calculate actual availability, p99 latency compliance, and error rate against defined targets. Identify the specific incidents that most consumed error budget and recommend the architectural changes that would prevent recurrence.

title: Identify all synchronous calls from user-facing services to internal or
prompt: Identify all synchronous calls from user-facing services to internal or external dependencies that have no timeout configured. For each, calculate the worst-case impact on user experience, the maximum time a user would wait if the dependency hung indefinitely. Recommend timeout values based on p99 response time plus a safety margin. Specify the fallback behavior when each timeout fires.

title: Design a failure mode and effects analysis for the payment processing fl
prompt: Design a failure mode and effects analysis for the payment processing flow. Enumerate every step from user submitting payment to funds confirmed. For each step, define the failure mode, the probability estimate, the user-visible impact, and the current mitigation. Identify steps with no mitigation. Recommend the reliability improvements that would reduce total payment failure rate by 50 percent.

title: Audit retry logic implementations across all services for thundering her
prompt: Audit retry logic implementations across all services for thundering herd risk. Identify retries using fixed intervals rather than exponential backoff with full jitter. Calculate the maximum simultaneous retry volume at a recovering service for each fixed-interval retry configuration. Recommend the full-jitter backoff formula and the maximum retry budget per service type. Specify how non-retryable error codes are identified and excluded.

title: Map all single points of failure in the production architecture, compone
prompt: Map all single points of failure in the production architecture, components whose failure would cause a complete service outage rather than degraded service. For each, assess whether eliminating the SPOF requires active-active redundancy, active-passive failover, or graceful degradation. Prioritize by the probability of failure multiplied by the outage duration. Recommend the SPOF elimination investments ordered by expected reliability improvement.

title: Review the change management process for reliability gate coverage
prompt: Review the change management process for reliability gate coverage. Identify recent deployments that caused production incidents and assess whether a reliability gate, including load test, canary analysis, or synthetic validation, would have detected the issue before full rollout. Design a deployment gate checklist specifying the gates required for each change category. Estimate the percentage of recent incidents that would have been prevented.

title: Review reliability design across all production services
prompt: Review reliability design across all production services. Identify whether SLOs are defined for every user-facing service — covering availability, latency, and error rate. Find services with no SLO where failures go unnoticed until a user complaint arrives. Assess whether SLOs are enforced with error budget tracking and burn rate alerts, or whether they exist only as documentation. Look for services that habitually exceed their error budgets without triggering a reliability investment response. Evaluate whether reliability investments are prioritized based on SLO breach history or driven by developer preference. Summarize the five reliability design gaps with the highest user impact potential.

title: Evaluate the resilience patterns applied across the system
prompt: Evaluate the resilience patterns applied across the system. Identify whether retries, timeouts, circuit breakers, and bulkheads are applied consistently or only in isolated areas. Find services that make outbound calls without any timeout configured — relying on the remote service to respond in a reasonable time. Assess whether retry logic uses exponential backoff with jitter, or whether fixed-interval retries would create thundering herd conditions under load. Look for services that call multiple downstream dependencies in sequence rather than in parallel, amplifying latency when one is slow. Recommend the resilience pattern additions that would most improve mean time to recovery during the next major dependency failure.

title: Perform a thorough analysis of the system's resilience under partial fai
prompt: Perform a thorough analysis of the system's resilience under partial failure conditions, scenarios where some components fail while others continue operating. Identify the failure isolation boundaries in the current architecture and assess whether a failure in any single component is truly contained or whether it propagates to adjacent services through shared resources, synchronous call chains, or shared database connections. Evaluate whether the application degrades gracefully when non-critical features fail, whether a recommendation engine outage shows empty results rather than crashing the page, whether a search service outage falls back to cached results rather than returning a 500, and whether a third-party analytics SDK failure is caught and logged without blocking page rendering. Assess the use of the bulkhead pattern, whether separate thread pools or connection pools are used for different downstream dependencies. Review whether circuit breakers are configured with appropriate thresholds calibrated to normal variance rather than tripping on transient errors, and whether breaker state changes are monitored and alerted. Evaluate the retry strategy for all service-to-service calls. Recommend the resilience pattern additions that would most improve the user experience during the next partial infrastructure failure.

title: Assess the system's SLO compliance history and the organizational respon
prompt: Assess the system's SLO compliance history and the organizational response to error budget consumption. Retrieve the actual availability, latency, and error rate for each defined SLO over the past 90 days and compare against the defined targets. Identify services that have breached their SLO in the past quarter and assess the organizational response, whether error budget overruns triggered reliability investments or were acknowledged without remediation. Evaluate whether error budget policies are enforced, whether feature development slows and reliability investments are prioritized when a service approaches budget exhaustion. Review whether SLOs are defined from the user perspective, measuring the experience at the point of consumption rather than internal metrics. Assess whether the SLO targets are calibrated appropriately, neither so tight that the error budget is constantly exhausted preventing feature development, nor so loose that user-impacting outages fall within budget. Identify the SLO compliance improvements that would give users consistent, predictable service quality while preserving development velocity.

title: Evaluate the reliability testing practices used to validate the system's
prompt: Evaluate the reliability testing practices used to validate the system's resilience before and after changes. Assess whether chaos engineering experiments are conducted in production or only in staging, and whether staging experiments provide meaningful signal about production resilience given differences in traffic volume and data characteristics. Review the results of the most recent chaos experiment and the reliability improvements it prompted. Identify failure scenarios that have never been tested, including network partition between services, database primary failover, deployment pipeline failure during a canary rollout, and third-party API degradation rather than complete failure. Assess whether the team has defined a steady-state hypothesis for each critical service, a measurable definition of normal behavior that serves as the basis for chaos experiment success criteria. Evaluate whether reliability testing is integrated into the deployment process for high-risk changes or conducted only on a scheduled basis. Recommend a chaos engineering roadmap that progressively tests higher-risk failure scenarios in production, starting with blast-radius-limited experiments and building toward full regional failover drills.

title: Create a runbook for the most likely production incident
prompt: Based on the system architecture and known failure modes, write a runbook for the most likely production incident. Include: detection steps, initial triage, escalation path, common root causes, mitigation steps, and rollback commands.
prompt_short: Write a runbook for the most likely production failure in this system.
prompt_filtered: Runbook creation only. Most likely failure scenario.

title: Define incident severity levels and response SLAs
prompt: Draft incident severity level definitions (P0-P3) with clear criteria for this system. For each severity, define the expected response time, communication cadence, and who must be involved. Tailor to the actual services and user impact scenarios.
prompt_short: Define P0-P3 incident severity levels with response SLAs for this system.
prompt_filtered: Severity classification only. No runbook or tooling analysis.

title: Audit incident communication templates and channels
prompt: Review the templates and channels used for incident communication — internal Slack updates, external status page updates, customer emails. Identify missing templates and communication gaps that cause confusion during incidents.
prompt_short: Audit incident communication templates for completeness and clarity.
prompt_filtered: Communication templates only. Internal and external incident updates.

title: Review postmortem process and action item completion rate
prompt: Assess the postmortem process. Verify postmortems are blameless, follow a consistent template, result in actionable items with owners, and that action items are actually completed. Identify incidents with no postmortem or postmortems with no follow-through.
prompt_short: Assess postmortem quality and action item completion rate.
prompt_filtered: Postmortem process only. Blameless culture and action item follow-through.

title: Find services with no defined incident owner
prompt: List every service in production. For each, verify there is a documented owner (team or individual) responsible for incidents. Identify services that are orphaned — built, deployed, and running but with no clear owner on call.
prompt_short: Find production services with no defined incident owner or on-call.
prompt_filtered: Ownership gaps only. Service to owner mapping.

title: Design incident severity classification criteria
prompt: Draft precise, unambiguous criteria for each severity level (SEV1–SEV4) in terms of user impact, revenue impact, and data risk. Criteria should be objective enough that any on-call engineer reaches the same classification for the same incident without needing a manager.
prompt_short: Write objective SEV1–SEV4 classification criteria any on-call engineer can apply.
prompt_filtered: Severity criteria only. User impact, revenue, and data risk thresholds.

title: Audit the incident command structure and IC role handoff process
prompt: Assess whether the Incident Commander role is clearly defined, whether handoff between ICs is documented for long incidents, and whether the IC is empowered to make decisions without manager approval during a high-severity incident. Identify gaps in the IC role definition.
prompt_short: Assess the Incident Commander role definition and handoff process for long incidents.
prompt_filtered: IC role only. Definition, authority, and handoff process.

title: Define the external communication protocol during incidents
prompt: Design the external communication protocol for customer-facing incidents: who approves status page updates, what template to use at each severity level, how frequently to update, and what language to use when root cause is unknown. Include escalation to executive communication.
prompt_short: Design the external communication protocol for customer-facing incidents.
prompt_filtered: External comms only. Status page, update frequency, and language templates.

title: Review on-call rotation fairness and coverage gaps
prompt: Audit on-call schedules for all services. Identify rotations with fewer than 4 engineers (burnout risk from overloaded rotations), services with no weekend coverage, and rotations where the same engineer is always on-call for high-alert-volume shifts.
prompt_short: Find on-call rotations with fewer than 4 engineers or weekend coverage gaps.
prompt_filtered: Rotation health only. Engineer count, weekend coverage, and load distribution.

title: Assess mean time to resolve and identify top delay causes
prompt: Analyze the last 20 incidents. Calculate MTTR for each. Categorize the primary delay cause: slow detection, slow escalation, unclear ownership, missing runbook, difficult diagnosis, or slow rollback. Identify the delay category causing the most total lost time.
prompt_short: Calculate MTTR for recent incidents and identify the top delay category.
prompt_filtered: MTTR analysis only. Delay cause categorization across recent incidents.

title: Design a war room protocol for high-severity incidents
prompt: Design a war room protocol for SEV1 incidents: who is paged, which collaboration channel to use, how to divide investigation roles (IC, comms, technical lead), and what cadence to run update cycles. Include a first-30-minutes checklist.
prompt_short: Design a war room protocol with roles, channels, and a first-30-minutes checklist.
prompt_filtered: War room protocol only. Roles, channels, and initial response checklist.

title: Audit blameless postmortem template completeness
prompt: Review the current postmortem template. Verify it includes: timeline, contributing factors (not root cause — systems rarely have one), impact measurement, what went well, what went poorly, and action items with owners and due dates. Identify missing sections that lead to shallow postmortems.
prompt_short: Audit the postmortem template for completeness and blamelessness.
prompt_filtered: Postmortem template only. Required sections and blameless framing.

title: Find incidents with no postmortem and assess impact
prompt: Identify all SEV1 and SEV2 incidents in the past 90 days. For each, verify a postmortem was completed within 5 business days. Find incidents with no postmortem — these represent missed learning opportunities and recurring incidents that may have been prevented.
prompt_short: Find high-severity incidents in the past 90 days with no completed postmortem.
prompt_filtered: Postmortem coverage only. Unreviewed high-severity incidents.

title: Design a game day exercise for incident response readiness
prompt: Design a game day scenario that tests incident response readiness without production impact: inject a realistic failure in a staging environment, time the team's detection, classification, IC handoff, and resolution. Measure against defined MTTD and MTTR targets.
prompt_short: Design a game day exercise to test incident response readiness in staging.
prompt_filtered: Game day design only. Scenario, metrics, and staging injection approach.

title: Assess tooling availability during major infrastructure failures
prompt: Identify which incident response tools — monitoring dashboards, communication channels, runbook systems, deploy pipelines — depend on the same infrastructure that fails during a major outage. Define fallback tooling for each that does not share the failure domain.
prompt_short: Find incident tools that fail alongside the system they are used to fix.
prompt_filtered: Tooling resilience only. Failure domain overlap for response tools.

title: Review alert routing accuracy and escalation ladder testing
prompt: Test alert routing end-to-end by triggering a test alert for each critical service. Verify the alert reaches the correct on-call engineer within 2 minutes, escalates to a secondary within the defined timeout, and reaches a manager if neither responds. Identify routing failures.
prompt_short: Test end-to-end alert routing for all critical services and verify escalation.
prompt_filtered: Alert routing testing only. Delivery time, escalation, and manager fallback.

title: Establish incident response metrics and improvement targets
prompt: Define and baseline three core incident response metrics: MTTD (mean time to detect), MTTR (mean time to resolve), and repeat incident rate (same root cause recurs within 30 days). Set quarterly improvement targets and assign an owner to the metric improvement initiative.
prompt_short: Baseline MTTD, MTTR, and repeat incident rate and set improvement targets.
prompt_filtered: IR metrics only. Baseline values, improvement targets, and ownership.

title: Audit action item completion rate from past postmortems
prompt: Review all postmortem action items from the past 6 months. Calculate the completion rate (items done / items created). Identify action items older than 30 days with no progress, items assigned to individuals who have left the team, and patterns in action item types that are consistently not completed.
prompt_short: Find stale postmortem action items and calculate overall completion rate.
prompt_filtered: Action item completion only. Stale items, reassignment needs, and completion rate.

title: Design incident tagging taxonomy for trend analysis
prompt: Design an incident tagging taxonomy that enables trend analysis: category (availability, latency, data, security), trigger (deploy, config change, traffic spike, third-party), and affected component. Define tagging as a required postmortem field and build a monthly trend report template.
prompt_short: Design an incident taxonomy for trend analysis and make tagging mandatory.
prompt_filtered: Incident taxonomy only. Category, trigger, and component tagging schema.

title: Assess communication breakdown patterns across past incidents
prompt: Review postmortems for communication failures: unclear IC handoffs, delayed external updates, conflicting information in different channels, or stakeholders who were not notified. Identify the most common communication breakdown pattern and design a process fix.
prompt_short: Find the most common communication breakdown pattern across recent incidents.
prompt_filtered: Communication patterns only. Handoff, notification, and channel conflicts.

prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

title: Recommend Next Steps
prompt: Recommend Next Steps

prompt: Score for Production Readiness
