prompt: Identify Reproduction Blockers

title: Review Debugging Runbook Quality
prompt: Review Debugging Runbook Quality

title: Find MTTR Improvement Opportunities
prompt: Find MTTR Improvement Opportunities

title: Identify the three hardest categories of bugs to reproduce locally from
prompt: Identify the three hardest categories of bugs to reproduce locally from this codebase. For each, explain why they are hard to reproduce — timing-dependent, environment-specific, or data-dependent. Recommend the specific tooling or observability additions that would make them reproducible.

title: Audit the debugging tooling available for production issues
prompt: Audit the debugging tooling available for production issues. Find failure scenarios where the only debugging path is adding new log statements and redeploying. For each, recommend the observability addition — structured logging, trace sampling, or live query tools — that would enable diagnosis without deployment.

title: Review the last five bug reports
prompt: Review the last five bug reports. For each, reconstruct the debugging steps taken and identify where the team lost the most time. Find the common pattern across all five — missing context, missing logs, missing metrics — and recommend the single tooling investment that would address it.

title: Identify the five most time-consuming categories of production bug to di
prompt: Identify the five most time-consuming categories of production bug to diagnose based on past incident data. For each, determine the specific diagnostic bottleneck including insufficient log detail, missing distributed trace, lack of local reproduction environment, or access restrictions. Recommend the tooling or process change that would most reduce diagnosis time for each category. Estimate the total MTTR reduction across all incidents in the next quarter.

title: Design a debugging guide for the three most complex failure modes in thi
prompt: Design a debugging guide for the three most complex failure modes in this system. For each, specify the first five diagnostic steps any on-call engineer should take, the key signals to look for in logs and traces, the common misdiagnoses to avoid, and the escalation threshold. Verify each step is executable with currently available tooling and access.

title: Audit the local development environment for production debugging fidelit
prompt: Audit the local development environment for production debugging fidelity. Identify behaviors that are present in production but not reproducible locally, including environment-specific configuration, production-only data volumes, and third-party integrations. For each, recommend a simulation strategy using mocks, stubs, or staging environments that brings local debugging closer to production behavior. Prioritize by the frequency of production issues first diagnosed in production because local reproduction was impossible.

title: Review the debugging impact of recent observability improvements
prompt: Review the debugging impact of recent observability improvements. Identify the last three observability changes including new log fields, new trace spans, and new dashboards. For each, assess whether the change demonstrably reduced time to diagnosis in subsequent incidents. Identify observability changes that were implemented but are not used during incidents. Recommend a feedback loop that evaluates observability investments against actual debugging outcomes.

title: Design a knowledge base structure for recurring production issues
prompt: Design a knowledge base structure for recurring production issues. Identify the five issue types that have been debugged more than once using the same diagnostic approach. For each, create a knowledge base entry covering symptoms, triggering conditions, diagnostic steps, resolution, and prevention. Define the process for engineers to contribute new entries after resolving a novel issue. Estimate the MTTR reduction from a fully populated knowledge base for the most common issue types.

title: Assess the debugging strategy and tooling available for production issue
prompt: Assess the debugging strategy and tooling available for production issues. Identify the most common classes of bugs — data corruption, timing issues, integration failures — and evaluate whether the current observability stack provides the information needed to diagnose each type. Find failure modes where engineers must reproduce the issue locally because production debugging tools are insufficient. Assess whether remote debugging or production profiling is available for CPU or memory issues. Look for any recurring bug type that has been reported multiple times because the root cause was never fully understood. Recommend the debugging strategy improvements that would most reduce the average time from bug report to root cause identification.

title: Evaluate the team's ability to debug distributed system failures
prompt: Evaluate the team's ability to debug distributed system failures. Assess whether distributed traces provide enough context to follow a request across all service boundaries and identify where it failed. Find failure scenarios where a bug in one service manifests as a confusing error in a completely different service, with no trace connecting them. Check whether correlation IDs are propagated consistently so that all log entries for a single request can be retrieved together. Assess whether engineers can query production data safely without risk of data modification during debugging. Identify the top three distributed debugging scenarios that currently require heroic effort and recommend the tooling additions that would make them routine.

title: Conduct a systematic review of the debugging capabilities available to e
prompt: Conduct a systematic review of the debugging capabilities available to engineers during production incidents and assess their effectiveness. Evaluate the quality and completeness of diagnostic information available at the moment an incident is detected, whether the alerting notification provides enough context to begin investigation without querying multiple systems, whether the relevant traces and logs from the alert time window are immediately accessible, and whether the on-call engineer has the access permissions needed to query production data without requesting approval during the incident. Identify the debugging workflows that are most time-consuming, the steps that require the most context switching between tools, the most manual data correlation, or the most waiting for slow queries to return results. Assess whether the team has established first-principles debugging guides for the most common failure categories including database performance degradation, memory leak, cascading service failure, and authentication outage, that any engineer can follow regardless of their familiarity with the specific service. Review whether debugging tooling is accessible during an infrastructure outage, whether the observability stack is hosted on a separate failure domain from the system being debugged. Recommend the debugging capability improvements that would most reduce the time from incident detection to root cause identification.

title: Evaluate the effectiveness of post-incident debugging as a learning mech
prompt: Evaluate the effectiveness of post-incident debugging as a learning mechanism that improves future debugging speed. Assess whether postmortems include a section documenting the debugging approach used, the diagnostic steps taken, the tools used, the false leads followed, and the signal that ultimately identified the root cause. Identify whether this debugging narrative is preserved in a searchable knowledge base that engineers can reference when encountering similar symptoms in the future. Review whether the team conducts debugging retrospectives separate from reliability retrospectives, focusing specifically on whether the available tooling and information enabled efficient diagnosis or whether the investigation relied on tribal knowledge unavailable to a new on-call engineer. Evaluate whether recurring issues, the same bug type diagnosed multiple times over six months, have generated permanent diagnostic improvements such as new log fields, new dashboards, or new runbook sections. Assess whether debugging knowledge is distributed across the team or concentrated in senior engineers who become informal escalation paths for all complex incidents. Recommend the knowledge management and tooling improvements that would distribute debugging capability across the entire on-call rotation.

title: Review the debugging experience for the system's most complex distribute
prompt: Review the debugging experience for the system's most complex distributed transactions, operations that span multiple services and data stores. Identify the three most complex transaction types by the number of service boundaries crossed. For each, assess whether a complete end-to-end distributed trace is available from a real production sample, capturing every service, every database call, every external API invocation, and every message queue interaction. Evaluate whether the trace provides sufficient detail to answer the debugging question most commonly asked during incidents involving that transaction: which step was slow, which service returned an error, and what was the state of the data at each step. Identify the trace gaps, service boundaries where context is dropped, operations that produce no span, or events that are logged but not traced, that most impede diagnosis. Assess whether the team has a canonical debugging workflow for each complex transaction type, a documented sequence of queries and dashboards that efficiently narrows the root cause from a symptom to a specific line of code or configuration setting. Recommend the distributed tracing improvements that would make complex transaction debugging tractable for any on-call engineer.

title: Audit OWASP Top 10 coverage for this application
prompt: Assess the application against the OWASP Top 10. For each category — injection, broken auth, XSS, IDOR, security misconfiguration, etc. — identify whether the risk exists, how it's mitigated, and where coverage is missing.
prompt_short: Assess the application against all OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.
prompt_filtered: OWASP Top 10 only. Each category with mitigation status.

title: Find all user input that reaches the database unvalidated
prompt: Trace all user-controlled input from the API boundary to database queries. Identify any path where input reaches a query without parameterization, schema validation via Zod/Joi/Pydantic, or sanitization. Flag by injection severity.
prompt_short: Trace user input to database queries and find unparameterized paths.
prompt_filtered: Input validation only. User input to database path analysis.

title: Identify direct object references without authorization checks
prompt: Find all routes that accept a resource ID from the user and fetch that resource. Verify each checks that the authenticated user owns or has permission to access the requested resource. Flag IDOR vulnerabilities by severity.
prompt_short: Find routes accessing resources by ID without ownership verification.
prompt_filtered: IDOR only. Resource ID access without authorization check.

title: Review all third-party dependencies for known CVEs
prompt: Scan all direct and transitive dependencies for known CVEs. For each vulnerability, assess exploitability in this specific application's context, not just the raw CVSS score. Prioritize by actual reachability of the vulnerable code path.
prompt_short: Scan dependencies for CVEs and rank by reachability in this context.
prompt_filtered: CVE scanning only. Reachability-adjusted severity ranking.

title: Find security headers missing from HTTP responses
prompt: Audit HTTP response headers across all routes. Identify missing security headers — CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy. For each missing header, explain the attack it prevents and the recommended value.
prompt_short: Find missing HTTP security headers and explain each attack prevented.
prompt_filtered: Security headers only. Missing header identification and impact.

title: Perform threat modeling using STRIDE for the API surface
prompt: Apply STRIDE threat modeling to the API surface: identify threats for Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information disclosure, Denial of service, and Elevation of privilege. For each threat, rate the risk using DREAD and list the current mitigations and gaps.
prompt_short: Apply STRIDE threat modeling to the API and rate each threat with DREAD.
prompt_filtered: STRIDE modeling only. Threat identification and DREAD rating per category.

title: Audit secret scanning coverage in CI/CD pipeline
prompt: Verify a secret scanning tool (Trufflehog, GitLeaks, GitHub Secret Scanning) is configured in the CI pipeline to block commits containing API keys, credentials, or private keys. Identify repos with no secret scanning, and check the git history for any previously committed secrets.
prompt_short: Verify secret scanning blocks credential commits and audit history for leaks.
prompt_filtered: Secret scanning only. CI configuration and git history audit.

title: Assess supply chain security for npm and pip dependencies
prompt: Evaluate supply chain security for this project: are packages pinned by hash (not just version), is there a lock file committed, are packages sourced from public registries without integrity checks, and is there a policy review process for new dependencies? Identify the highest-supply-chain-risk dependencies.
prompt_short: Assess dependency supply chain security including hash pinning and integrity checks.
prompt_filtered: Supply chain only. Hash pinning, lock files, and registry integrity verification.

title: Audit CORS configuration for credential exposure risk
prompt: Review CORS configuration for all API origins. Identify responses with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * combined with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true (invalid but sometimes misconfigured), overly broad origin allowlists, and origins that are no longer in use but still allowed.
prompt_short: Find CORS misconfigurations that could allow cross-origin credential theft.
prompt_filtered: CORS only. Origin allowlist and credentials policy analysis.

title: Find unsafe deserialization vectors
prompt: Identify all points where the application deserializes data from untrusted sources — JSON.parse on request body, pickle.loads, YAML.load, Java ObjectInputStream. Assess whether deserialized type is validated before use, and whether gadget chains could enable remote code execution.
prompt_short: Find deserialization of untrusted data and assess gadget chain risk.
prompt_filtered: Deserialization only. Untrusted input and RCE gadget chain vectors.

title: Audit file upload validation for content-type bypass and path traversal
prompt: Review file upload handling. Verify content is validated by magic bytes (not just MIME type from the request), filename is sanitized to prevent path traversal (../ sequences), uploaded files are stored outside the web root, and executables cannot be served as static files.
prompt_short: Audit file upload validation for MIME bypass, path traversal, and execution risk.
prompt_filtered: File upload security only. Magic bytes, path traversal, and execution prevention.

title: Assess rate limiting for credential stuffing and brute force
prompt: Evaluate rate limiting on authentication endpoints. Verify IP-based rate limiting applies to login, password reset, and MFA challenge endpoints, account lockout after N failed attempts is implemented, and bot-traffic signals (user agent, timing) are used to differentiate credential stuffing from human error.
prompt_short: Verify rate limiting on auth endpoints prevents credential stuffing and brute force.
prompt_filtered: Auth rate limiting only. Login, reset, and MFA endpoint protection.

title: Review server-side request forgery (SSRF) exposure
prompt: Find all places where the application makes outbound HTTP requests using user-supplied URLs — webhooks, link previews, avatar fetches, integrations. Verify each uses an allowlist of allowed domains or schemes, blocks RFC-1918 addresses, and prevents access to cloud metadata endpoints.
prompt_short: Find user-supplied URL inputs and verify SSRF prevention per endpoint.
prompt_filtered: SSRF only. User-controlled URL inputs and allowlist enforcement.

title: Assess clickjacking protection beyond X-Frame-Options
prompt: Verify clickjacking protection beyond X-Frame-Options: CSP frame-ancestors directive (supersedes X-Frame-Options in modern browsers), frame-busting JavaScript as a defense-in-depth layer, and SameSite cookie attribute on session tokens to prevent CSRF via iframe embedding.
prompt_short: Verify clickjacking protection using CSP frame-ancestors and SameSite cookies.
prompt_filtered: Clickjacking only. Frame-ancestors, frame-busting, and SameSite defense layers.

title: Audit server-side rendering for XSS injection points
prompt: Review server-side rendered HTML for XSS injection points: user-provided data inserted without escaping, template literals with raw HTML interpolation, innerHTML assignments, dangerouslySetInnerHTML in React, v-html in Vue. Identify context (HTML, attribute, JS, URL) for each injection point.
prompt_short: Find XSS injection points in server-rendered HTML and identify context type.
prompt_filtered: XSS only. Raw HTML interpolation in templates and React dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

title: Review cryptographic algorithm choices for compliance and strength
prompt: Audit all cryptographic algorithm usage: hashing (MD5, SHA-1 are broken for security — use SHA-256+), encryption (3DES, RC4 are deprecated — use AES-256-GCM), key exchange (RSA-1024 is insufficient — use RSA-2048+ or ECDH P-256). Identify non-compliant algorithms in use.
prompt_short: Find deprecated cryptographic algorithms and recommend compliant replacements.
prompt_filtered: Crypto algorithms only. Hash, cipher, and key exchange compliance.

title: Assess API key management and rotation practices
prompt: Inventory all API keys used by the application — first-party and third-party. For each, verify: the key is scoped to minimum necessary permissions, stored in a secrets manager (not hardcoded), rotated on a defined schedule, and that a compromised key can be revoked without downtime.
prompt_short: Audit API key scoping, storage, rotation schedule, and revocation process.
prompt_filtered: API key management only. Scope, storage, rotation, and revocation per key.

title: Find insecure direct object reference vulnerabilities in file access
prompt: Identify endpoints that serve files by a user-supplied path, ID, or key. Verify each checks that the authenticated user is authorized to access the specific file — not just that they are logged in. Pay special attention to presigned URL generation where the key comes from the client.
prompt_short: Find file access endpoints missing per-file ownership authorization checks.
prompt_filtered: File IDOR only. Path-based and key-based file access without ownership verification.

title: Evaluate security posture of WebSocket connections
prompt: Review WebSocket connection security: verify the upgrade request validates the Origin header against an allowlist, authentication happens before any message is accepted (not just at connect time), and messages have a maximum size limit to prevent memory exhaustion attacks.
prompt_short: Audit WebSocket security for origin validation, auth, and message size limits.
prompt_filtered: WebSocket security only. Origin, authentication, and message validation.

title: Assess logging and monitoring for security event detection
prompt: Evaluate the security event detection capability: are auth failures, permission denials, admin actions, and data export events all logged with context (IP, user ID, resource)? Is there an alert for anomalous patterns — 100 auth failures from one IP in one minute, access at unusual hours? Identify detection gaps.
prompt_short: Assess security event logging coverage and anomaly detection alerting.
prompt_filtered: Security detection only. Event logging coverage and alert configuration.

prompt: Run OWASP Top 10 Check

title: Audit Input Validation
prompt: Audit Input Validation

title: Audit Input Validation Coverage
prompt: Audit Input Validation Coverage

title: Find Exposed Sensitive Endpoints
prompt: Find Exposed Sensitive Endpoints

title: Review CORS Configuration
prompt: Review CORS Configuration

title: Map Attack Surface
prompt: Map Attack Surface

title: Assess Secrets Rotation Policy
prompt: Assess Secrets Rotation Policy

title: Audit the application against the OWASP Top 10
prompt: Audit the application against the OWASP Top 10. For each category, identify the specific endpoints or code paths where that vulnerability class is most likely to appear. Output findings in order of exploitability, with the specific code location and recommended fix for each.

title: Find all locations where user-supplied data reaches a database query, sh
prompt: Find all locations where user-supplied data reaches a database query, shell command, file path, or template without sanitization. For each injection vector, construct a minimal proof-of-concept input that would demonstrate the vulnerability. Recommend the specific parameterization or escaping strategy for each.

title: Audit secret management across the entire codebase and infrastructure
prompt: Audit secret management across the entire codebase and infrastructure. Identify any secret hardcoded in source, committed to version control, or logged. Find secrets stored in environment variables accessible to untrusted processes. Recommend a secrets management approach that eliminates all identified risks.

title: Conduct an input validation audit for all API endpoints accepting user-s
prompt: Conduct an input validation audit for all API endpoints accepting user-supplied data. Identify endpoints with no schema validation where any JSON structure is accepted and deserialized without validation. For each, define the Zod or equivalent schema that enforces the required shape, types, and value constraints. Recommend the validation middleware placement that enforces constraints before any business logic executes.

title: Assess the secrets management posture across all services
prompt: Assess the secrets management posture across all services. Identify secrets stored in environment variables versus a secrets manager. Find secrets with no rotation policy. Assess whether secrets are scoped to the minimum required access, whether a secret granting read-write access to a database is used by a service that only reads. Recommend the secrets hygiene improvements ordered by risk reduction.

title: Review authentication and session management for all user-facing entry p
prompt: Review authentication and session management for all user-facing entry points. Identify session tokens without expiry, without secure and httpOnly flags, or transmitted over non-HTTPS connections. Find authentication flows missing rate limiting on credential attempts. Assess whether multi-factor authentication is available and enforced for administrative access. Recommend the session security improvements with the highest user impact.

title: Audit CORS configuration across all API origins
prompt: Audit CORS configuration across all API origins. Identify endpoints with wildcard CORS origins permitting requests from any domain. Find endpoints where CORS allows credentials with a wildcard origin. Assess whether CORS preflight caching is configured appropriately. Recommend the CORS policy changes that eliminate unnecessary cross-origin access without breaking legitimate frontend integrations.

title: Perform a dependency vulnerability scan across all package manifests
prompt: Perform a dependency vulnerability scan across all package manifests. Identify packages with known CVEs rated high or critical severity. For each, assess whether the vulnerability is exploitable given the application's usage of the package. Recommend the upgrade path and estimate the breaking change risk for each vulnerable dependency. Prioritize by exploitability and exposure in user-facing request paths.

title: Conduct a comprehensive security audit of the application
prompt: Conduct a comprehensive security audit of the application. Start with the OWASP Top 10 and identify whether each vulnerability class is present. For injection vulnerabilities — SQL, command, LDAP — trace every location where user input reaches a query or system call. For authentication weaknesses, review session management, token expiry, and password storage. For access control gaps, identify any endpoint that returns data or performs actions without verifying the requester has permission. Look for sensitive data exposure — PII, credentials, internal IPs — in API responses, logs, and error messages. Assess CSRF protection on all state-changing endpoints. Summarize the five highest-severity security findings with proof-of-concept scenarios and remediation steps.

title: Evaluate the security of data in transit and at rest
prompt: Evaluate the security of data in transit and at rest. Verify that all external communication uses TLS with strong cipher suites and certificate validation. Identify any internal service-to-service communication using unencrypted channels. Assess whether sensitive data fields — passwords, tokens, payment data — are encrypted at rest in the database or stored as plaintext. Review the key management strategy — whether encryption keys are rotated, how they are stored, and who has access. Check whether backups are encrypted with a key separate from the backup storage location. Find any data classification policy and assess whether it is consistently enforced in the codebase. Recommend the data protection changes that would most reduce the risk of a data breach.

title: Conduct a defense-in-depth assessment of the application's security post
prompt: Conduct a defense-in-depth assessment of the application's security posture, evaluating the layers of protection available to resist an attacker who has bypassed the outermost perimeter. Identify the security controls present at each layer: network perimeter controls that restrict which hosts can initiate connections, application-layer authentication and authorization that restricts which operations authenticated users can perform, input validation that prevents malformed or malicious data from reaching business logic, data-layer access controls that restrict database access to the minimum required by each service account, and audit logging that creates a record of security-relevant events. For each layer, assess whether the controls are correctly implemented, consistently applied, and tested for effectiveness. Identify the weakest layer, the one where a successful attacker would face the least resistance and could cause the most damage. Evaluate whether security controls are layered such that bypassing any single control does not grant full access, whether there are compensating controls that would detect or limit the impact. Recommend the defense-in-depth improvements that would most increase the cost and complexity of a successful attack.

title: Evaluate the security of the application's data handling practices from
prompt: Evaluate the security of the application's data handling practices from input to storage to output. Assess whether all user-supplied input is validated at the server side before use, whether validation happens before database queries, file operations, or command execution rather than relying on client-side validation that can be bypassed. Identify injection risk points: SQL injection in database queries, command injection in subprocess calls, template injection in rendering engines, and SSRF in URL-handling code. Review whether sensitive data including passwords, payment card information, health data, and authentication tokens is stored with appropriate protection: password hashing with an appropriate work factor, encryption of sensitive fields at rest, and tokenization of payment data. Assess whether data is encrypted in transit between all service pairs, not only for user-facing HTTPS but also for service-to-service communication and database connections within the internal network. Evaluate whether sensitive data is appropriately omitted from logs, error messages, and API responses, never exposing more information to a client than the operation strictly requires. Recommend the data security improvements that most reduce the risk of a data breach.

title: Review the application's authorization model for correctness and complet
prompt: Review the application's authorization model for correctness and completeness. Assess whether authorization decisions are made server-side on every request or whether the application trusts client-supplied authorization signals such as user ID in a request body or role in a header that could be manipulated. Identify the authorization scope: whether the application uses coarse-grained role-based access control, fine-grained permission-based access control, or attribute-based access control, and whether the chosen model is appropriate for the data sensitivity and user role diversity. Find authorization gaps, resources or operations that are accessible to any authenticated user when they should be restricted to specific roles, ownership checks that are missing for user-owned resources, and administrative endpoints without additional authentication beyond the standard user token. Assess whether authorization is consistently applied across all API endpoints, whether adding a new endpoint requires explicit authorization configuration or whether the default is permissive. Evaluate whether authorization decisions are logged for audit purposes. Recommend the authorization improvements that would most reduce the risk of unauthorized data access.

title: Audit the authentication flow for security gaps
prompt: Walk through every authentication path — password, OAuth, magic link, API key, SSO. For each, identify security weaknesses: token expiry, secure storage, rotation, timing attacks, enumeration risks, and missing MFA.
prompt_short: Audit every auth flow for token security, timing attacks, and enumeration risks.
prompt_filtered: Authentication only. Credential and token security analysis.

title: Find privilege escalation paths in the authorization model
prompt: Map the permission model and identify any path by which a lower-privileged user could gain higher-privileged access — parameter tampering, role assignment bugs, missing checks after role change, JWT claim manipulation.
prompt_short: Find paths where a lower-privilege user can gain higher-privilege access.
prompt_filtered: Privilege escalation only. Authorization model exploitation paths.

title: Review session management and token security
prompt: Audit session and token management. Verify tokens expire, are invalidated on logout, are stored securely (httpOnly cookies vs localStorage), and are not exposed in URLs, logs, or error messages. Check refresh token rotation.
prompt_short: Audit session tokens for secure storage, expiry, and invalidation.
prompt_filtered: Session management only. Token lifecycle and secure storage.

title: Identify routes missing authentication requirements
prompt: Enumerate all routes. For each, verify that the authentication requirement is explicit and correct — not relying on obscurity or assumed middleware application. Flag routes that expose data or perform actions without auth.
prompt_short: Find all routes that should require auth but currently don't.
prompt_filtered: Missing auth only. Unauthenticated routes that should be protected.

title: Assess OAuth integration for security compliance
prompt: Review the OAuth 2.0 / OIDC implementation. Verify state parameter is used (CSRF protection), redirect URIs are strictly validated, authorization code is exchanged server-side, tokens are not exposed to the client, and email_verified is checked.
prompt_short: Audit OAuth implementation for CSRF, redirect validation, and token security.
prompt_filtered: OAuth security only. CSRF, redirect URI, and token exposure risks.

title: Design a refresh token rotation strategy with rotation detection
prompt: Design refresh token rotation: each refresh issues a new refresh token and immediately invalidates the old one. Implement rotation theft detection — if an already-consumed refresh token is replayed, assume token theft and revoke the entire session family. Define the revocation mechanism and user notification.
prompt_short: Design refresh token rotation with theft detection and session revocation.
prompt_filtered: Refresh token rotation only. Single-use tokens and replay detection.

title: Audit JWT claim validation completeness
prompt: Review JWT validation code. Verify all required claims are validated: exp (expiry), nbf (not before), iss (issuer), aud (audience), and that the signing algorithm is explicitly specified rather than taken from the token header (algorithm confusion attack). Flag any claim that is decoded but not validated.
prompt_short: Audit JWT validation for exp, nbf, iss, aud, and algorithm confusion attacks.
prompt_filtered: JWT validation only. Claim verification and algorithm pinning.

title: Assess RBAC model for least privilege compliance
prompt: Audit the Role-Based Access Control model. For each role, list the permissions it grants. Identify roles with significantly more permissions than the actual function requires — violating least privilege. Recommend role splitting for overpowered roles and permission removal for unused grants.
prompt_short: Audit each RBAC role for least-privilege compliance and overpermissioning.
prompt_filtered: RBAC only. Role permission scope vs actual function requirement.

title: Design ABAC policies for fine-grained resource access
prompt: Assess whether RBAC is sufficient for this system or whether Attribute-Based Access Control is needed — where access depends on attributes of the user (department, clearance), resource (sensitivity, owner), and environment (time, location). Design ABAC policies for the 3 most complex access decisions.
prompt_short: Assess RBAC sufficiency and design ABAC policies for complex access decisions.
prompt_filtered: ABAC design only. Attribute policy design for complex authorization cases.

title: Review MFA implementation for bypass vulnerabilities
prompt: Audit MFA implementation for bypass paths: recovery code exhaustion rate limiting, social engineering of support team for account recovery, SIM-swap risk if SMS MFA is used, and whether MFA can be skipped via an older OAuth session. Identify each bypass path and its remediation.
prompt_short: Find MFA bypass paths including recovery codes, SMS, and OAuth session skipping.
prompt_filtered: MFA bypass only. Recovery, SMS, and legacy session bypass vectors.

title: Assess API key authorization granularity and scope enforcement
prompt: Review how API keys are scoped. Verify keys can be issued with read-only, write-only, or resource-specific scopes. Verify scope is enforced server-side — not just documented — and that a key issued for one resource cannot be used to access another. Identify keys with broader scope than the issuing use case required.
prompt_short: Verify API key scope enforcement and find keys with broader scope than needed.
prompt_filtered: API key scope only. Scope definition, enforcement, and over-scoping.

title: Design a zero-trust network access model for internal services
prompt: Assess whether internal services authenticate each other using mTLS or service tokens, or rely solely on network perimeter trust (a caller inside the VPC is trusted). Design a zero-trust model where each service authenticates its callers regardless of network origin.
prompt_short: Design mTLS or service token authentication between internal services.
prompt_filtered: Service-to-service auth only. Zero-trust model and mTLS design.

title: Audit session invalidation on password and permission changes
prompt: Verify that changing a user's password, email, or permission role immediately invalidates all existing sessions and tokens. Identify auth flows where a compromised account can be locked out but the attacker's existing token remains valid until natural expiry.
prompt_short: Verify all sessions are invalidated when password or permissions change.
prompt_filtered: Session invalidation only. Password and permission change propagation.

title: Review federated identity trust chain for SSO integration
prompt: For each SSO/SAML/OIDC integration, verify the full trust chain: certificate or public key used for assertion validation, clock skew tolerance, audience restriction, and what happens when the IdP issues an assertion for a user not in the local directory. Identify trust assumptions not explicitly verified.
prompt_short: Audit SSO trust chain validation including certificate, audience, and directory sync.
prompt_filtered: SSO trust only. SAML/OIDC assertion validation and IdP-local user reconciliation.

title: Assess account enumeration risk across all auth endpoints
prompt: Test all auth endpoints for account enumeration: does login return a different error for wrong email vs wrong password, does password reset confirm whether an email is registered, does registration return 409 for existing emails. Recommend uniform timing and generic responses to prevent enumeration.
prompt_short: Find auth endpoints that reveal whether an email is registered via error differences.
prompt_filtered: Enumeration only. Login, reset, and registration response uniformity.

title: Design a delegated authorization model for multi-tenant SaaS
prompt: Design a delegated authorization model where tenant admins can grant their users access to specific resources without platform admin involvement. Define the permission delegation chain, the audit trail for delegated grants, and the revocation mechanism when a delegated user leaves a tenant.
prompt_short: Design tenant-admin delegated authorization with audit trail and revocation.
prompt_filtered: Delegated authorization only. Tenant-admin grant chain and revocation.

title: Audit token storage practices for XSS and CSRF resistance
prompt: Review where auth tokens are stored in the client: localStorage (XSS-readable), sessionStorage (tab-scoped), memory-only (reset on refresh), or httpOnly cookie (XSS-safe, CSRF risk). For each storage choice, identify the attack class it is vulnerable to and recommend the appropriate countermeasure.
prompt_short: Audit client-side token storage for XSS and CSRF attack surface.
prompt_filtered: Token storage only. localStorage, cookie, and memory storage vulnerability analysis.

title: Review passwordless authentication implementation security
prompt: Assess the security of passwordless authentication (magic link, FIDO2, passkey). Verify magic links: single-use, short-lived (< 15 min), bound to the requesting device or IP, and not logged in plain text. For FIDO2, verify credential registration requires existing authentication before binding.
prompt_short: Audit magic link and FIDO2/passkey implementations for binding and expiry security.
prompt_filtered: Passwordless auth only. Magic link expiry, binding, and FIDO2 registration security.

title: Assess authorization check consistency across equivalent routes
prompt: For operations accessible via multiple routes — REST endpoint, GraphQL resolver, internal service call, admin API — verify authorization checks are applied consistently on all paths. Identify cases where an admin-only operation is properly guarded on the REST API but accessible without checks via the GraphQL equivalent.
prompt_short: Find operations where authorization is checked on one route but missing on an equivalent.
prompt_filtered: Authorization consistency only. Same operation across REST, GraphQL, and internal routes.

title: Design a secure credential recovery flow without knowledge-based authentication
prompt: Design an account recovery flow that does not use security questions (knowledge-based auth is easily defeated by social engineering). Use instead: verified backup email, backup phone for TOTP, recovery codes generated at MFA enrollment, or identity verification via a trusted social provider. Define the flow for each recovery option.
prompt_short: Design account recovery without security questions using modern verification methods.
prompt_filtered: Recovery flow only. Non-KBA options for secure account recovery.

prompt: Audit Auth Flows

title: Check Token Expiry Logic
prompt: Check Token Expiry Logic

title: Find Privilege Escalation Paths
prompt: Find Privilege Escalation Paths

title: Review Token Expiry Policy
prompt: Review Token Expiry Policy

title: Audit MFA Coverage
prompt: Audit MFA Coverage

title: Map Authorization Bypass Risk
prompt: Map Authorization Bypass Risk

title: Review OAuth Scope Grants
prompt: Review OAuth Scope Grants

title: Audit all authentication flows for common vulnerabilities
prompt: Audit all authentication flows for common vulnerabilities. Find flows where account enumeration is possible through distinct error messages or timing differences. Identify flows with no brute-force protection. Find token issuance that does not enforce expiry or that accepts expired tokens.

title: Review authorization enforcement across all API endpoints
prompt: Review authorization enforcement across all API endpoints. Identify endpoints that authenticate the caller but do not verify the caller has permission to access the specific resource requested — allowing horizontal privilege escalation. For each gap, recommend the specific ownership check to add.

title: Audit session and token lifecycle management
prompt: Audit session and token lifecycle management. Find tokens with no expiry, refresh tokens that are never rotated, and sessions that survive logout. For each, describe the attack scenario enabled by the gap and recommend the lifecycle management change that closes it.

title: Design a comprehensive authorization test suite for all API endpoints
prompt: Design a comprehensive authorization test suite for all API endpoints. For each endpoint, define the set of test cases: unauthenticated request returns 401, insufficient-role request returns 403, wrong-owner request returns 403, and authorized request returns expected response. Identify endpoints with no authorization test coverage. Recommend the test framework integration that would run these tests on every deployment.

title: Assess JWT implementation security across all services that issue or val
prompt: Assess JWT implementation security across all services that issue or validate tokens. Identify services accepting the none algorithm. Find services not validating token expiry on every request. Assess whether refresh token rotation is implemented, whether a used refresh token is invalidated to prevent replay. Recommend the JWT security improvements ordered by exploitation likelihood.

title: Review OAuth integration security for all third-party identity providers
prompt: Review OAuth integration security for all third-party identity providers. Identify OAuth flows missing PKCE for public clients. Find flows that do not validate the state parameter against session state. Assess whether the redirect_uri is validated against a strict allowlist. Review token storage, whether access tokens are stored in localStorage rather than httpOnly cookies. Recommend the OAuth security hardening changes.

title: Evaluate the principle of least privilege application across all service
prompt: Evaluate the principle of least privilege application across all service accounts and API keys. Identify service accounts with database roles granting more than the minimum required permissions. Find API keys with broad scope when only a subset of endpoints are called by the integration. Assess whether key rotation is implemented on a defined schedule. Recommend the privilege reduction changes with the highest blast radius reduction.

title: Audit multi-factor authentication coverage and enforcement
prompt: Audit multi-factor authentication coverage and enforcement. Identify user-facing roles including administrators, billing managers, and support agents where MFA is not enforced. Find authentication flows where MFA can be bypassed, including account recovery flows, trusted device flows, or API token flows that bypass MFA entirely. Assess the MFA methods supported and whether weaker methods like SMS OTP are used where phishing-resistant methods should be required. Recommend the MFA enforcement improvements.

title: Evaluate the authentication system for security and correctness
prompt: Evaluate the authentication system for security and correctness. Assess all authentication mechanisms in use — password, OAuth, API key, JWT — and verify each is implemented according to current best practices. Check password storage — whether bcrypt, Argon2, or an equivalent algorithm with appropriate work factor is used. Review JWT implementation — whether the algorithm is explicitly specified, whether none algorithm is rejected, and whether token claims are validated on every request. Find any authentication endpoint that does not use timing-safe comparison for credential verification. Assess whether account lockout is implemented for password-based auth and whether lockout state persists across server restarts. Summarize the five authentication vulnerabilities ordered by exploitability.

title: Review authorization design across the application
prompt: Review authorization design across the application. Identify whether authorization decisions are centralized in a consistent layer or scattered across individual route handlers. Find any endpoint that relies on client-provided data — user ID in a request body rather than extracted from a verified token — to determine what resource to return. Assess whether role-based or attribute-based access control is enforced consistently, or whether some routes check permissions while others implicitly trust any authenticated user. Look for privilege escalation paths — where an authenticated low-privilege user can reach a high-privilege endpoint by guessing or manipulating parameters. Recommend the authorization architecture changes that would most reduce the risk of horizontal and vertical privilege escalation.

title: Conduct a thorough security analysis of the authentication system coveri
prompt: Conduct a thorough security analysis of the authentication system covering the full lifecycle of user credentials from registration through session termination. Assess the password policy including minimum length, complexity requirements, and whether passwords are checked against known-breached credential databases before acceptance. Evaluate the password hashing implementation, whether a memory-hard algorithm such as Argon2 or bcrypt with an appropriate work factor is used, whether salting is applied per-user, and whether the hashing work factor is reviewed periodically to maintain resistance to hardware acceleration. Review the account recovery flow, whether password reset tokens are single-use with a short expiry, whether reset requests are rate-limited, and whether the reset flow is resistant to account enumeration by returning identical responses for registered and unregistered email addresses. Assess session management, whether session tokens are cryptographically random with sufficient entropy, whether they are invalidated on logout and password change, and whether concurrent session limits are enforced for sensitive account types. Evaluate brute force protection, whether credential stuffing is mitigated by rate limiting login attempts per IP and per account, and whether IP-based rate limiting accounts for proxied traffic. Recommend the authentication security improvements that would most reduce the risk of credential-based account takeover.

title: Evaluate the authorization model for completeness, correctness, and resi
prompt: Evaluate the authorization model for completeness, correctness, and resistance to privilege escalation. Identify the full set of resources and operations in the application and map each to the required authorization level. Assess whether authorization is checked at the point of data access, in the data layer, or only at the API entry point, which could allow internal service calls to bypass authorization. Find missing ownership checks: API endpoints that accept a resource ID as a parameter but verify only that the requester is authenticated, not that the authenticated user owns the specified resource. Evaluate whether horizontal privilege escalation is prevented, whether a user in role A can access data belonging to a user in role B by guessing or enumerating resource IDs. Assess whether vertical privilege escalation is prevented, whether a regular user can perform administrative operations by manipulating request parameters or headers. Review whether authorization logic is centralized in a reusable service or replicated in each endpoint where replication creates risk of inconsistency. Recommend the authorization model improvements that would eliminate the highest-risk privilege escalation paths.

title: Assess the security of API key and token management across all integrati
prompt: Assess the security of API key and token management across all integrations and service-to-service communication. Identify every API key and service token in use, including internal service credentials, third-party API keys, webhook signing secrets, and machine-to-machine OAuth tokens. For each, assess whether the key is stored in a secrets manager rather than application configuration files, whether the key has the minimum required scope, and whether the key rotation policy matches the sensitivity of the access it grants. Evaluate whether API keys are transmitted securely over HTTPS only, in headers rather than query parameters, and never logged in plaintext. Assess whether key compromise scenarios are tested, whether the team can revoke and rotate a specific key within 30 minutes without service disruption. Review whether long-lived API keys that should have been replaced by short-lived OAuth tokens are still in use. Identify keys that have not been rotated in more than one year. Recommend the token management improvements that would reduce the blast radius and detection latency of a key compromise.

title: Audit GDPR compliance gaps for user data handling
prompt: Assess the application against GDPR requirements. Identify gaps in: consent capture, right to access, right to erasure (data export and delete), data portability, breach notification readiness, and privacy policy accuracy vs actual data processing.
prompt_short: Find GDPR compliance gaps in consent, data access, and erasure flows.
prompt_filtered: GDPR only. Consent, access, erasure, and portability requirements.

title: Find all PII stored and verify retention policy
prompt: Inventory all PII collected and stored — names, emails, IP addresses, location data, payment info, behavioral data. For each, verify there is a defined retention policy, it is enforced technically, and users can request deletion.
prompt_short: Inventory all PII and verify each has an enforced retention policy.
prompt_filtered: PII inventory only. Data types, locations, and retention enforcement.

title: Review audit trail completeness for compliance events
prompt: Assess whether the system maintains tamper-evident audit logs for compliance-relevant events — data access, data export, admin actions, permission changes, login events. Identify events with no audit trail.
prompt_short: Find compliance-relevant events with no audit trail or log.
prompt_filtered: Audit trail only. Compliance event logging coverage.

title: Assess data residency and cross-border transfer compliance
prompt: Identify where user data is stored and processed geographically. Verify this meets the data residency requirements of your users' jurisdictions. Identify cross-border transfers and whether standard contractual clauses or equivalent mechanisms are in place.
prompt_short: Assess where user data is stored geographically and verify residency compliance.
prompt_filtered: Data residency only. Geographic storage and transfer legality.

title: Find consent collection gaps in the user journey
prompt: Trace the user journey for consent capture. Identify where consent for cookies, marketing, data processing, or third-party sharing should be captured but isn't. Verify consent is granular, optional, and reversible.
prompt_short: Find places in the user journey where required consent is not captured.
prompt_filtered: Consent gaps only. Missing consent capture points in user flows.

title: Assess SOC 2 Type II control coverage and evidence gaps
prompt: Map the system against SOC 2 Trust Service Criteria (Security, Availability, Confidentiality, Processing Integrity, Privacy). For each relevant criterion, identify the controls in place, the evidence that would satisfy an auditor, and gaps where a control is claimed but not demonstrably implemented.
prompt_short: Map SOC 2 Trust Service Criteria to existing controls and find evidence gaps.
prompt_filtered: SOC 2 only. Control mapping, evidence, and gap identification per criterion.

title: Audit HIPAA technical safeguard implementation
prompt: Assess the technical safeguards required by HIPAA for PHI: access controls (unique user IDs, auto-logoff, encryption), audit controls (activity logs for PHI access), integrity controls (data not altered in transit), and transmission security (TLS for PHI in transit). Identify gaps for each safeguard category.
prompt_short: Audit HIPAA technical safeguards for PHI access, logging, integrity, and transit.
prompt_filtered: HIPAA technical safeguards only. PHI access, audit, integrity, and transmission.

title: Review PCI-DSS scope reduction strategy for cardholder data
prompt: Assess whether cardholder data environment (CDE) scope is minimized — using tokenization to avoid storing PANs, redirect to hosted payment pages to avoid card data touching the server, and network segmentation to isolate any CDE components. Identify scope reduction opportunities.
prompt_short: Find PCI-DSS scope reduction opportunities via tokenization and hosted pages.
prompt_filtered: PCI scope only. CDE minimization through tokenization and segmentation.

title: Verify GDPR data subject access request fulfillment capability
prompt: Assess the technical capability to fulfill a GDPR Data Subject Access Request within 30 days: identify all data stores containing a user's PII, extract and format a complete data export, verify the export does not include other users' data, and confirm deletion removes data from all stores including backups.
prompt_short: Verify technical capability to fulfill GDPR DSAR in 30 days including backups.
prompt_filtered: DSAR capability only. Data location, extraction, and deletion completeness.

title: Audit third-party data processor agreements and DPAs
prompt: List all third-party services that receive user personal data — analytics, email providers, CDNs, error trackers, cloud platforms. Verify a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is in place with each, the DPA is current, and the processor is on the approved sub-processor list disclosed in the privacy policy.
prompt_short: Find third-party data processors missing a current Data Processing Agreement.
prompt_filtered: DPA coverage only. Third-party processors, agreements, and privacy policy disclosure.

title: Assess data minimization practices across collection points
prompt: Review all data collection points — registration forms, analytics events, API requests, logs. Identify fields collected that are not necessary for the stated purpose. Data minimization requires only collecting what is needed; excess collection creates unnecessary compliance scope and breach risk.
prompt_short: Find data collected beyond what is necessary for the stated processing purpose.
prompt_filtered: Data minimization only. Excess collection at forms, analytics, and API levels.

title: Review breach notification readiness and response playbook
prompt: Assess readiness to execute a data breach notification under GDPR (72-hour regulator notification), CCPA, and applicable state breach notification laws. Verify a playbook exists with: breach detection criteria, internal escalation, legal counsel notification, regulator filing template, and affected user notification template.
prompt_short: Verify breach notification playbook meets 72-hour GDPR and state law timelines.
prompt_filtered: Breach notification only. Detection, escalation, and regulatory filing process.

title: Audit cross-border data transfer mechanisms for adequacy
prompt: For each country where users' data flows, identify the legal mechanism authorizing the transfer: adequacy decision, Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs), Binding Corporate Rules, or consent. Identify transfers relying on Privacy Shield (invalidated) or no transfer mechanism at all.
prompt_short: Find cross-border data transfers lacking a valid legal transfer mechanism.
prompt_filtered: Transfer mechanisms only. Adequacy, SCCs, and invalid mechanism identification.

title: Assess cookie consent management platform implementation
prompt: Verify the Cookie Management Platform (CMP) correctly withholds non-essential cookies before consent is given — analytics, advertising, social media pixels. Verify the consent signal is passed to all third-party scripts, consent is stored for the required period, and consent preferences can be withdrawn.
prompt_short: Verify the CMP correctly blocks non-essential cookies before consent and supports withdrawal.
prompt_filtered: CMP implementation only. Pre-consent blocking, signal passing, and withdrawal.

title: Review children's data protection compliance
prompt: Assess whether the service could be accessed by users under 13 (COPPA), 16 (GDPR), or age of digital consent in applicable jurisdictions. If yes, verify age verification, parental consent mechanisms, and data handling restrictions for minors. Identify any processing of children's data without appropriate safeguards.
prompt_short: Identify children's data processing and verify COPPA/GDPR age consent compliance.
prompt_filtered: Children's data only. Age verification and parental consent mechanism coverage.

title: Audit vendor lock-in risk from compliance-scoped data
prompt: Identify compliance-scoped data (audit logs, consent records, access request records) stored in third-party SaaS tools. Assess whether data can be exported in a portable format if the vendor relationship ends. Vendor lock-in of compliance records creates a regulatory risk if the vendor is acquired or shut down.
prompt_short: Find compliance records in SaaS tools that cannot be exported if the vendor is lost.
prompt_filtered: Compliance data portability only. Export capability for audit and consent records.

title: Assess records of processing activities documentation
prompt: Verify a Records of Processing Activities (ROPA) document exists and is current. The ROPA must list each processing activity, its legal basis, data categories processed, retention period, and third-party recipients. Identify processing activities not documented in the ROPA.
prompt_short: Verify the ROPA is current and covers all active processing activities.
prompt_filtered: ROPA completeness only. Processing activities, legal basis, and retention documentation.

title: Review automated decision-making and profiling disclosures
prompt: Identify automated decisions that produce legal or similarly significant effects — credit scoring, content moderation, account suspension, pricing. Verify GDPR Article 22 requirements are met: user is informed, meaningful human review is available on request, and logic is disclosed in the privacy notice.
prompt_short: Find automated decisions with significant effects and verify GDPR Art. 22 compliance.
prompt_filtered: Automated decisions only. Disclosure, human review, and logic explanation.

title: Audit employee data handling for employment law compliance
prompt: Assess processing of employee personal data: monitoring of communications, location tracking, productivity monitoring, HR data transfers to payroll providers. Verify each has a legal basis under applicable employment law, employees are informed, and data minimization is applied.
prompt_short: Audit employee monitoring and HR data processing for employment law compliance.
prompt_filtered: Employee data only. Monitoring, HR transfers, and employment law legal basis.

title: Design a privacy-by-design review gate for new features
prompt: Design a privacy-by-design review gate embedded in the feature development process: a checklist that must be completed before any feature touching personal data is deployed. Include: data types collected, legal basis, retention, third-party sharing, DSAR impact, and privacy notice update requirement.
prompt_short: Design a privacy-by-design checklist gate for all features collecting personal data.
prompt_filtered: Privacy-by-design gate only. Feature checklist for new data collection.

prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

prompt: Gain full understanding before completing
