prompt: Audit Compliance Gaps

title: Check Data Residency
prompt: Check Data Residency

title: Audit Log Retention for Compliance
prompt: Audit Log Retention for Compliance

title: Find Unencrypted PII Stores
prompt: Find Unencrypted PII Stores

title: Map Data Subject Rights Coverage
prompt: Map Data Subject Rights Coverage

title: Review Vendor Compliance Status
prompt: Review Vendor Compliance Status

title: Identify Audit Evidence Gaps
prompt: Identify Audit Evidence Gaps

title: Identify which compliance frameworks apply to this system — GDPR, SOC 2,
prompt: Identify which compliance frameworks apply to this system — GDPR, SOC 2, PCI DSS, HIPAA, or others. For each applicable framework, list the five controls with the largest gap between the current implementation and the requirement. Order findings by audit risk.

title: Audit PII data flows through the system
prompt: Audit PII data flows through the system. Identify every location where PII is collected, stored, processed, or transmitted. For each location, verify that the data is handled according to the applicable compliance requirements — consent, retention limits, encryption, and access controls.

title: Review audit logging for compliance coverage
prompt: Review audit logging for compliance coverage. Identify all user actions, data accesses, and configuration changes that compliance requires to be logged with a tamper-evident record. Find any required audit event that is not currently logged. Recommend the logging additions needed to close the compliance gap.

title: Map all personal data flows from collection to deletion
prompt: Map all personal data flows from collection to deletion. Identify where personal data enters the system, how it is processed, where it is stored, who has access, and how deletion is executed. Find data flows that are not documented in the data processing record. Assess whether data minimization is applied, whether more personal data is collected than necessary for the stated purpose. Recommend the data flow changes that would reduce regulatory exposure.

title: Assess the technical implementation of GDPR data subject rights
prompt: Assess the technical implementation of GDPR data subject rights. For each right including access, rectification, erasure, portability, restriction, and objection, verify the technical capability exists and document the implementation. Identify rights that cannot be fulfilled within the regulatory time limit due to technical constraints. Recommend the implementation changes needed to achieve full data subject rights coverage.

title: Review the vendor and third-party data processor compliance status
prompt: Review the vendor and third-party data processor compliance status. Identify all vendors receiving personal data. Verify each has a signed Data Processing Agreement where required. Assess whether vendor security posture is reviewed at onboarding and annually thereafter. Find vendors with no DPA, no security certification, or whose data practices conflict with your privacy policy. Recommend the vendor compliance remediation actions.

title: Evaluate the consent management implementation for marketing and analyti
prompt: Evaluate the consent management implementation for marketing and analytics data. Identify consent collection points and verify they meet the requirements of the applicable regulation, including specific, informed, freely given, and unambiguous consent with equal prominence for accept and reject options. Find analytics or tracking tools active before consent is obtained. Assess whether consent withdrawal is implemented and propagates to all downstream systems. Recommend the consent management improvements.

title: Design a compliance audit evidence package for the most likely upcoming
prompt: Design a compliance audit evidence package for the most likely upcoming audit including SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, or GDPR supervisory authority inquiry. Identify the evidence categories required: access control logs, change management records, security training completion, incident response records, and vendor DPAs. Assess current evidence availability and identify gaps where evidence would need to be reconstructed from memory. Recommend the evidence collection automation that would make audit preparation less time-consuming.

title: Conduct a compliance readiness review for the applicable regulatory fram
prompt: Conduct a compliance readiness review for the applicable regulatory frameworks. Identify which frameworks govern this system based on the data it processes, the users it serves, and the jurisdictions it operates in. For each framework, assess the current control implementation against the requirements — noting which controls are fully implemented, partially implemented, or missing. Identify the data retention, deletion, and subject rights request handling — the ability to export or delete a specific user's data on demand. Review access controls on sensitive data stores — whether access is logged, restricted to authorized roles, and reviewed periodically. Summarize the five compliance gaps with the highest regulatory risk exposure.

title: Evaluate the data governance posture of the system
prompt: Evaluate the data governance posture of the system. Identify whether a data inventory exists — a mapping of what data is collected, where it is stored, who has access, and how long it is retained. Find data stores with no documented owner responsible for access review and data lifecycle management. Assess whether data classification — public, internal, confidential, restricted — is applied and enforced in access controls. Look for shadow data stores — spreadsheets, local databases, personal storage — that hold production data outside the governed systems. Review whether consent management is implemented for any data collection requiring user consent under applicable regulations. Recommend the data governance improvements that would most reduce regulatory and reputational risk.

title: Conduct a comprehensive compliance gap analysis against the primary regu
prompt: Conduct a comprehensive compliance gap analysis against the primary regulatory frameworks applicable to the organization. Identify the specific regulations and standards governing the application including GDPR for EU personal data, CCPA for California consumer data, HIPAA for health information, PCI-DSS for payment card data, and SOC 2 for enterprise customer trust. For each framework, enumerate the technical control requirements and assess the current implementation status: fully implemented, partially implemented, or not implemented. Prioritize gaps by the regulatory consequence of non-compliance, since frameworks with significant financial penalties and reputational damage for breaches warrant higher priority than those with lower enforcement intensity. Assess whether compliance controls are continuously monitored or point-in-time assessed, recognizing that a control that was compliant during the last audit may have drifted since then. Evaluate whether the organization has a compliance program with a designated owner, a regular review cadence, and a process for assessing new regulatory requirements as they emerge. Recommend the compliance investments that would most reduce the risk of a regulatory finding.

title: Evaluate the technical controls supporting data privacy compliance and t
prompt: Evaluate the technical controls supporting data privacy compliance and their adequacy for current and anticipated regulatory requirements. Assess whether data is classified by sensitivity level, including public, internal, confidential, and restricted, and whether security controls are applied proportionally to the classification. Review whether data retention schedules are documented, implemented technically, and enforced automatically, distinguishing between retention for operational purposes, retention for legal hold, and deletion required by regulation. Evaluate the pseudonymization and anonymization practices applied to data used in analytics, testing, and development environments, assessing whether the anonymization is sufficient to prevent re-identification given available auxiliary data. Assess whether the data breach notification process meets regulatory time requirements, whether the organization can detect a breach, assess its scope, and notify the relevant authority within 72 hours for GDPR or the applicable window for other regulations. Review the Privacy Impact Assessment process for new features that introduce or modify personal data processing. Recommend the privacy engineering improvements that would build compliance into the development process rather than treating it as a post-launch remediation task.

title: Assess the organizational readiness for a regulatory audit or external c
prompt: Assess the organizational readiness for a regulatory audit or external compliance examination. Evaluate whether audit evidence is collected and stored in a manner that makes it retrievable on demand, including access logs, change records, security scan results, training completion records, and incident reports. Identify evidence categories where the organization would need to reconstruct records from memory or disparate systems, which increases audit preparation cost and reduces confidence in completeness. Review whether compliance responsibilities are clearly assigned to individuals rather than distributed across the organization without ownership, assessing whether there is a named compliance officer or program owner who can speak authoritatively to regulators. Assess whether the engineering team understands its compliance obligations, whether developers know which regulatory requirements apply to the code they write and which controls they are responsible for implementing. Evaluate whether third-party auditors have previously identified compliance gaps and whether those findings have been fully remediated. Recommend the audit readiness improvements that would reduce preparation time and increase confidence in a clean audit outcome.

title: Audit architectural decisions for documentation coverage
prompt: Identify the top 15 architectural decisions made in this project. For each, check whether an ADR (Architecture Decision Record) exists. For decisions with no ADR, draft a one-paragraph summary of the decision and why it was made.
prompt_short: Find architectural decisions with no ADR and create summaries.
prompt_filtered: ADR coverage only. Undocumented architectural decisions.

title: Review code ownership and service responsibility assignment
prompt: Audit code ownership configuration (CODEOWNERS, team assignments). Identify code with no owner, services with unclear team responsibility, and critical paths where a change could merge without the right reviewers being required.
prompt_short: Find code and services with no clear owner or review requirement.
prompt_filtered: Code ownership only. CODEOWNERS and team responsibility gaps.

title: Find policy violations in dependency and license usage
prompt: Review all dependencies against the organization's approved license policy and approved package list. Identify packages added without following the approval process, packages with incompatible licenses, and dependencies from untrusted registries.
prompt_short: Find dependencies violating license or approval policy requirements.
prompt_filtered: Policy violations only. License and approved package compliance.

title: Assess technical debt tracking and prioritization
prompt: Review how technical debt is tracked — is it in a backlog, tagged in the codebase with TODO/FIXME, or only in people's heads? Identify the highest-impact untracked technical debt. Recommend a sustainable debt tracking and paydown process.
prompt_short: Audit how technical debt is tracked and find the biggest untracked items.
prompt_filtered: Technical debt tracking only. Documentation and prioritization gaps.

title: Review change approval process for critical systems
prompt: Assess the approval process for changes to critical systems — production databases, authentication services, payment processing. Identify gaps where high-impact changes can be made without appropriate review, testing, or change management process.
prompt_short: Find gaps in the approval process for changes to critical systems.
prompt_filtered: Change approval only. Critical system change process gaps.

title: Implement policy-as-code for infrastructure guardrails
prompt: Assess whether infrastructure policies are enforced as code — OPA/Rego for Kubernetes, AWS Config rules, Sentinel policies for Terraform. Identify policy requirements currently enforced only by process (documentation, reviews) rather than automated checks that block non-compliant infrastructure from being provisioned.
prompt_short: Find governance policies enforced only by process that should be automated as code.
prompt_filtered: Policy-as-code only. Manual policies suitable for OPA, Config rules, or Sentinel.

title: Audit RFC and design review process effectiveness
prompt: Assess the RFC/design review process. Verify there is a defined template, a required reviewer list per change type, a minimum review period, and a mechanism to track RFC outcomes. Identify significant changes that bypassed the RFC process and the consequences.
prompt_short: Audit the RFC process for template completeness, reviewer requirements, and bypass cases.
prompt_filtered: RFC process only. Template, reviewers, and bypass identification.

title: Design an Architecture Decision Record template and adoption plan
prompt: Create an ADR template covering: context, decision, consequences, alternatives considered, and decision status (proposed/accepted/deprecated/superseded). Design an adoption plan requiring ADRs for: new service creation, significant dependency additions, and any reversal of a previous ADR.
prompt_short: Create an ADR template and adoption plan requiring them for new services and dependencies.
prompt_filtered: ADR template only. Fields, adoption triggers, and deprecation lifecycle.

title: Assess CODEOWNERS coverage and required reviewer enforcement
prompt: Audit the CODEOWNERS file for coverage: every directory with production code should have at least 2 named owners. Verify required reviewers are actually enforced by the repository settings (branch protection rules), not just listed in CODEOWNERS without enforcement. Identify critical paths without required review.
prompt_short: Verify CODEOWNERS covers all critical code paths with enforced required reviewers.
prompt_filtered: CODEOWNERS coverage only. Directory ownership and branch protection enforcement.

title: Review API versioning and deprecation policy
prompt: Assess the API versioning strategy: how new versions are introduced, how long deprecated versions are supported, how clients are notified of deprecations, and whether sunset headers are used. Identify APIs where breaking changes were deployed without a deprecation notice or migration period.
prompt_short: Audit API versioning policy for deprecation notice, support windows, and sunset headers.
prompt_filtered: API versioning only. Deprecation timeline, notice, and sunset header usage.

title: Design a service catalog and ownership registry
prompt: Design a service catalog where every production service is registered with: owner team, on-call contact, SLO targets, dependencies, and documentation link. A catalog enables impact analysis (who is affected if service X goes down) and ownership lookup during incidents. Identify services not registered in any catalog.
prompt_short: Design a service catalog with ownership, SLOs, and dependencies for all services.
prompt_filtered: Service catalog only. Registry fields, ownership, and impact analysis enablement.

title: Audit technical roadmap alignment with engineering resource allocation
prompt: Compare the stated technical roadmap (features planned, tech debt items, infrastructure investments) against the actual engineering resource allocation from the past quarter. Identify roadmap items receiving zero resource allocation and reliability investments being consistently deprioritized in favor of feature work.
prompt_short: Find roadmap items with zero resource allocation and reliability work being skipped.
prompt_filtered: Roadmap alignment only. Planned vs actual resource allocation per category.

title: Review dependency approval and open-source license policy
prompt: Define a dependency approval policy: which open-source licenses are allowed (MIT, Apache-2.0: yes; GPL, AGPL: review required), the approval process for adding a new package, and how to handle a dependency that changes its license after adoption. Identify current dependencies outside the policy.
prompt_short: Define a dependency license policy and find current packages violating it.
prompt_filtered: License policy only. Allowed licenses, approval process, and violations.

title: Assess meeting and decision-making overhead in engineering teams
prompt: Audit recurring meetings and decision-making ceremonies for engineering teams. Identify meetings with no standing agenda, meetings making no decisions, and decisions that could be asynchronous. Estimate total engineering hours per week spent in meetings vs. focused work.
prompt_short: Find recurring meetings with no decisions and estimate engineering meeting overhead.
prompt_filtered: Decision overhead only. Recurring meetings without decisions or async alternatives.

title: Design a technical debt register and paydown cadence
prompt: Design a technical debt register: each item tagged with impact area (reliability, security, developer velocity), severity (blocks scaling, creates risk, slows development), and estimated remediation effort. Design a paydown cadence — dedicate 20% of each sprint to the top-priority debt items — with quarterly reviews.
prompt_short: Design a technical debt register with impact, severity, and a paydown sprint cadence.
prompt_filtered: Debt register only. Classification fields, severity rating, and paydown process.

title: Audit on-call documentation for new engineer onboarding
prompt: Review on-call documentation from the perspective of an engineer joining the on-call rotation for the first time: can they find the runbooks, dashboards, escalation contacts, and incident tooling without asking for help? Identify documentation gaps that require tribal knowledge to fill.
prompt_short: Audit on-call docs for a new engineer to be effective without asking for help.
prompt_filtered: On-call onboarding only. Runbook, dashboard, and tooling discoverability gaps.

title: Review security governance and vulnerability disclosure policy
prompt: Assess the security governance structure: is there a defined security review process for new features, a vulnerability disclosure policy (VDP) for external researchers, and a process for triaging and patching reported vulnerabilities with SLA targets? Identify governance gaps in each area.
prompt_short: Audit security governance for VDP, feature review process, and patch SLAs.
prompt_filtered: Security governance only. VDP, feature review, and vulnerability patch process.

title: Assess engineering culture health through cycle time and deployment frequency
prompt: Measure DORA metrics for the team: deployment frequency, lead time for changes, change failure rate, and mean time to restore. Compare against industry benchmarks (elite: deploys multiple times per day, MTTR < 1 hour). Identify the metric furthest from elite performance as the highest-priority governance intervention.
prompt_short: Measure DORA metrics and identify the lowest-performing for governance intervention.
prompt_filtered: DORA metrics only. Deployment frequency, lead time, failure rate, and MTTR.

title: Design a post-incident governance review for repeated failures
prompt: Design a governance escalation process for repeated incidents: if the same root cause category recurs within 90 days, trigger a mandatory governance review. The review assesses whether the original postmortem action items were completed, why they failed to prevent recurrence, and what systemic change is needed.
prompt_short: Design a governance escalation for incidents repeating within 90 days.
prompt_filtered: Repeat incident governance only. Escalation trigger and systemic review process.

title: Audit data governance and data ownership assignments
prompt: Identify every data domain in the system — user data, transaction data, analytics data, audit logs. For each, verify there is a named data owner responsible for: quality, retention policy, access control, and DSAR fulfillment. Identify data domains with no assigned owner.
prompt_short: Find data domains with no named owner and assign governance responsibility.
prompt_filtered: Data ownership only. Domain coverage and owner responsibility definition.

title: Assess Vendor Governance Maturity
prompt: Assess Vendor Governance Maturity

title: Identify all architectural decision records for this project
prompt: Identify all architectural decision records for this project. Find decisions made more than 12 months ago that have never been revisited. For each stale decision, assess whether the context that drove it has changed. Flag any decision that should be reversed or updated given current requirements.

title: Audit the governance process for major technical changes
prompt: Audit the governance process for major technical changes. Find the most recent significant architecture or infrastructure change and trace the decision path — who approved it, what alternatives were considered, and whether the decision was documented. Identify gaps in the governance process that allowed undocumented decisions.

title: Review vendor and dependency governance
prompt: Review vendor and dependency governance. Identify all third-party services and libraries the system depends on. Find dependencies with no documented evaluation, no approved alternative, and no exit strategy. Recommend the governance additions that would reduce vendor lock-in risk.

title: Identify all architectural decisions made in the past 12 months that lac
prompt: Identify all architectural decisions made in the past 12 months that lacked a formal review process. For each, assess the technical risk introduced, whether the decision created a scalability bottleneck, a security vulnerability, or a maintainability burden. Design an Architecture Decision Record template and a review gate process that applies proportional rigor based on the scope and risk of the decision.

title: Assess the technology standards governance process
prompt: Assess the technology standards governance process. Identify the approved technology list including approved languages, frameworks, databases, and cloud services, and assess whether it is current and enforced. Find services built on unapproved technologies. Review the exception process for teams needing to use a technology not on the approved list. Recommend the governance process improvements that balance innovation with standardization.

title: Review the data governance framework for completeness
prompt: Review the data governance framework for completeness. Identify the data classification policy, the data steward assignments for each data category, the approved use cases for each classification level, and the technical controls enforcing access restrictions. Find data stores with no assigned steward or no access review process. Recommend the data governance additions that would most reduce the risk of unauthorized data use.

title: Evaluate the software development lifecycle governance controls
prompt: Evaluate the software development lifecycle governance controls. Identify required reviews for production deployments including code review, security review, performance test, and compliance check. Find deployment types that bypass one or more required reviews. Assess whether review requirements are enforced by tooling or honored by convention. Recommend the SDLC governance controls that would provide the strongest assurance with the lowest developer friction.

title: Design a technology risk register for the organization
prompt: Design a technology risk register for the organization. Identify the top ten technology risks including outdated dependencies, single-region deployment, no DR testing program, insufficient access controls, and vendor lock-in. For each risk, document the likelihood, impact, current mitigations, and residual risk. Define the review cadence and ownership. Recommend the risk reduction investments ordered by residual risk after current mitigations.

title: Evaluate the technical governance framework in place
prompt: Evaluate the technical governance framework in place. Identify whether there is a defined process for making significant architectural decisions — who has decision authority, how alternatives are evaluated, and how decisions are documented. Find major decisions made in the past year with no documented rationale, creating knowledge loss risk when key engineers leave. Assess whether governance extends to dependency selection — whether new libraries and services are evaluated for security, license compatibility, and maintenance health before adoption. Review whether technical standards are maintained by a defined owner or allowed to drift. Recommend the governance improvements that would most reduce the risk of inconsistent or undocumented technical decisions.

title: Assess the change management process for infrastructure and architecture
prompt: Assess the change management process for infrastructure and architecture changes. Identify whether changes are reviewed for risk before deployment or deployed directly to production. Find changes in the past six months that bypassed the standard review process. Assess whether rollback procedures are required and tested before major changes are deployed. Review whether post-deployment validation is defined and performed — that changes are verified correct in production before the change is closed. Identify the governance gap most likely to cause the next significant production incident and recommend the process change that would close it.

title: Conduct a thorough assessment of the engineering governance framework an
prompt: Conduct a thorough assessment of the engineering governance framework and its effectiveness at ensuring consistent, high-quality technical decisions across the organization. Evaluate whether the organization has defined technology standards, including approved languages, frameworks, databases, cloud services, and architectural patterns, and whether those standards are enforced through tooling and process rather than social convention alone. Assess whether the architecture review process is well-defined and consistently applied, whether significant technical decisions receive structured review by experienced engineers before implementation begins, or whether architectural choices are made ad-hoc within individual teams without visibility to the broader organization. Review whether decision-making authority is clearly delegated, whether engineers understand which decisions they can make independently, which require team lead approval, and which require a formal architecture review. Evaluate the effectiveness of the exception process: whether teams with valid reasons to deviate from standards can do so through a documented process that captures the rationale and the risk acceptance, or whether exceptions are either denied reflexively or granted informally without record. Recommend the governance framework improvements that would provide consistent decision quality without creating bureaucratic friction that slows engineering velocity.

title: Evaluate the technical debt governance practices and the organization's
prompt: Evaluate the technical debt governance practices and the organization's ability to make informed decisions about debt accumulation and remediation. Assess whether the engineering organization maintains a technical debt register, a documented inventory of known architectural compromises, outdated dependencies, and deferred improvements, with associated risk assessments and remediation priorities. Review whether technical debt accumulation is measured over time and reported to leadership, giving visibility into whether the debt burden is growing, stable, or decreasing. Evaluate whether teams have allocated capacity for technical debt remediation, whether debt reduction is treated as legitimate engineering work with dedicated time in planning cycles or deferred indefinitely in favor of feature development. Assess whether technical debt is considered in product planning decisions, whether new features are evaluated against the debt they will accumulate and whether debt-heavy areas receive lower feature scope to enable remediation. Identify areas of the codebase with the highest debt burden and the highest risk of causing reliability or security incidents. Recommend the technical debt governance improvements that would give leadership visibility into the debt burden and enable informed decisions about remediation investment.

title: Review the vendor governance practices and their adequacy for managing t
prompt: Review the vendor governance practices and their adequacy for managing the risks introduced by third-party dependencies and service providers. Identify the complete inventory of vendors providing services that the application depends on, including cloud providers, SaaS tools, open-source libraries, and professional service providers. For each category, assess whether the vendor selection process includes security assessment, reliability track record review, contractual SLA verification, and data processing agreement execution. Evaluate whether the vendor relationship is monitored after onboarding, whether vendor security incidents trigger a response assessment, whether SLA performance is tracked, and whether the vendor's technology roadmap is reviewed for end-of-life risks that could force unplanned migrations. Assess whether vendor lock-in risk is evaluated before committing to proprietary services, whether the cost of migrating away from the vendor is understood and accepted as a deliberate architectural decision. Review whether critical vendor failures are addressed in the disaster recovery plan. Recommend the vendor governance improvements that would most reduce the risk of vendor-introduced disruption to production services.

title: Identify top 10 technical risks before production launch
prompt: Enumerate the top 10 technical risks in the current state of the system. For each, assess likelihood, potential impact, and the current mitigation status. Produce a prioritized risk register with recommended actions.
prompt_short: Enumerate the top 10 technical risks and their mitigation status.
prompt_filtered: Risk identification only. Likelihood, impact, and mitigation status.

title: Map third-party dependency risks and failure scenarios
prompt: List all third-party services the application depends on. For each, assess the risk of: outage, API breaking change, price increase, shutdown, data breach. Identify dependencies with no fallback and high business impact.
prompt_short: Map third-party dependencies and their failure or shutdown risk.
prompt_filtered: Third-party risk only. Outage, breaking change, and shutdown scenarios.

title: Assess blast radius of a compromised internal service
prompt: For each internal service, model the blast radius if it were compromised — what data can it access, what systems can it reach, what actions can it perform. Identify services with disproportionately high blast radius relative to their exposure.
prompt_short: Assess blast radius of compromised internal services for risk prioritization.
prompt_filtered: Blast radius only. Compromised service impact analysis.

title: Find risks from technical debt in the critical path
prompt: Identify technical debt in the critical request path that creates operational risk — old libraries with known bugs, complex code with no tests, patterns that are hard to change safely. Assess the probability each causes a production incident in the next 6 months.
prompt_short: Find technical debt in the critical path most likely to cause incidents.
prompt_filtered: Critical path debt risk only. Incident probability estimation.

title: Review vendor concentration and lock-in risk
prompt: Assess how much of the stack depends on a single cloud provider or vendor. Identify critical services with no portable alternative. For each, estimate the migration effort and business impact of switching, and whether the lock-in level is acceptable.
prompt_short: Assess vendor lock-in risk and portability for critical dependencies.
prompt_filtered: Vendor concentration only. Lock-in severity and migration cost.

title: Build a risk register with likelihood-impact scoring
prompt: Create a risk register for this system. For each identified risk, score likelihood (1-5) and impact (1-5). Plot on a 5x5 risk matrix. Focus mitigation investment on risks in the top-right quadrant (high likelihood, high impact). Assign an owner and target mitigation date to each quadrant-4-5 risk.
prompt_short: Build a risk register with likelihood-impact scores and assign owners to top risks.
prompt_filtered: Risk register only. Scoring, matrix placement, and ownership assignment.

title: Assess key-person dependency risk in engineering
prompt: Identify engineering knowledge concentrated in single individuals — code only one person understands, systems only one person can deploy, on-call rotations too small to cover absences. For each key-person dependency, estimate the impact of a sudden 4-week absence and recommend a knowledge transfer plan.
prompt_short: Find key-person dependencies and estimate impact of a 4-week unplanned absence.
prompt_filtered: Key-person risk only. Single-person knowledge concentration and transfer plans.

title: Design a kill switch inventory for emergency feature disablement
prompt: Inventory all production features and classify each as: always-on (no kill switch possible), kill-switch-available (feature flag exists), or planned-kill-switch (flag not yet added). For features in the third category with high risk (payment, auth, data migration), prioritize adding a kill switch.
prompt_short: Inventory features by kill-switch availability and prioritize adding flags to risky ones.
prompt_filtered: Kill switch inventory only. Feature flag coverage and risk-prioritized gaps.

title: Assess operational risk from infrastructure complexity
prompt: Evaluate whether infrastructure complexity has grown beyond the team's ability to operate it reliably. Signs: no engineer understands the full system, runbooks are out of date, new engineers take months to become productive on-call. Identify the highest-complexity components and recommend simplification.
prompt_short: Find infrastructure complexity exceeding the team's operational capacity.
prompt_filtered: Operational complexity only. Components too complex for reliable team operation.

title: Quantify financial exposure from a 24-hour outage
prompt: Estimate the financial exposure from a complete 24-hour service outage: direct revenue loss (transactions not processed), SLA penalty payouts, customer churn from loss of trust, engineering hours for recovery, and reputational cost. Use this to justify reliability investment to business stakeholders.
prompt_short: Estimate total financial exposure from a 24-hour complete service outage.
prompt_filtered: Financial exposure only. Revenue, SLA penalties, churn, and recovery cost.

title: Assess regulatory risk from recent compliance changes
prompt: Identify regulatory changes in the past 12 months (GDPR enforcement updates, new state privacy laws, industry-specific regulations) that apply to this system. For each, assess whether the current compliance posture satisfies the new requirement or creates regulatory risk.
prompt_short: Find recent regulatory changes creating new compliance requirements for this system.
prompt_filtered: Regulatory change risk only. Recent updates and compliance posture gaps.

title: Evaluate data breach probability and detection capability
prompt: Assess the probability of an undetected data breach: how many attack vectors exist, how many are mitigated, whether DLP (data loss prevention) tools are in place, and how long a breach would take to detect with current monitoring. Estimate the expected breach detection time and recommend detection improvements.
prompt_short: Estimate data breach detection time and recommend DLP and monitoring improvements.
prompt_filtered: Breach detection only. Attack vectors, DLP coverage, and detection latency.

title: Assess business continuity risk from critical team dependencies
prompt: Identify business processes that depend on specific engineering teams being available — deployments gated on a single team's approval, critical access controlled by one team. Assess the impact if that team is entirely unavailable for a week (illness, natural disaster). Design fallback approval paths.
prompt_short: Find business processes blocked if a critical team is unavailable for a week.
prompt_filtered: Team dependency risk only. Business-critical approval and access concentration.

title: Design a risk acceptance and exception management process
prompt: Design a formal risk acceptance process for known risks that cannot be immediately mitigated: the risk is documented, the accepting party is named (manager or above), the acceptance expires after a defined period (max 90 days), and a compensating control is required. Identify risks currently accepted informally.
prompt_short: Design a formal risk acceptance process with expiry, owner, and compensating controls.
prompt_filtered: Risk acceptance only. Formal process, expiry, and compensating control requirements.

title: Evaluate incident frequency as a leading risk indicator
prompt: Analyze incident frequency over the past 6 months. A rising trend is a leading indicator of increasing system risk, even if MTTR is improving. Categorize incidents by root cause category and identify any category growing faster than overall incident volume — a sign of an emerging systemic risk.
prompt_short: Find emerging risk categories from rising incident frequency trends.
prompt_filtered: Incident trend analysis only. Root cause categories and growth rate.

title: Assess physical and environmental risk for on-premise infrastructure
prompt: For any on-premise or co-located infrastructure, assess physical and environmental risks: cooling failure, power failure, physical access control, fire suppression, and natural disaster risk for the facility location. Identify single points of failure in the physical environment with no redundancy.
prompt_short: Assess physical and environmental SPOFs for any on-premise infrastructure.
prompt_filtered: Physical risk only. Cooling, power, access, and natural disaster exposure.

title: Evaluate counterparty risk in critical integrations
prompt: Assess the financial and operational stability of the top 5 vendors this system integrates with. Signs of counterparty risk: startup without funding runway, company acquired (integration may change or shut down), service deprecation announced. Identify integrations where counterparty risk is unacceptably high.
prompt_short: Assess financial and operational stability of top 5 vendor integrations.
prompt_filtered: Counterparty risk only. Vendor stability and integration shutdown scenarios.

title: Design a risk review cadence tied to system changes
prompt: Design a risk review cadence: lightweight monthly review of the active risk register (any items expired, any new risks from recent incidents), and a quarterly deep review (re-score all risks, retire resolved items, add risks from the roadmap). Define who owns the review and the escalation path for new critical risks.
prompt_short: Design monthly and quarterly risk review cadence with owner and escalation path.
prompt_filtered: Risk review cadence only. Monthly lightweight vs quarterly deep review process.

title: Assess reputational risk from a security incident
prompt: Model the reputational risk from a security incident: what user data would be exposed, which media publications would likely cover it, the expected user churn from trust loss, and the long-term brand recovery timeline. Use this model to justify security investment to non-technical stakeholders.
prompt_short: Model reputational risk from a security incident for business stakeholder justification.
prompt_filtered: Reputational risk only. Data exposure, media coverage, and trust recovery timeline.

title: Identify risks introduced by upcoming major infrastructure changes
prompt: Review the roadmap for upcoming major changes — cloud migration, database upgrade, framework version bump, new region launch. For each, identify the risks introduced: migration window, data integrity during transition, rollback complexity, and new operational unknowns. Require a risk sign-off before each major change.
prompt_short: Find risks introduced by each upcoming major infrastructure change on the roadmap.
prompt_filtered: Roadmap risk only. Change-specific risks and required sign-off process.

prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

prompt: Recommend Next Steps

prompt: Score for Production Readiness

title: Build Risk Matrix
prompt: Build Risk Matrix

title: Audit Vendor Dependencies
prompt: Audit Vendor Dependencies

title: Score Risks by Likelihood and Impact
prompt: Score Risks by Likelihood and Impact

title: Find Unmitigated High Risks
prompt: Find Unmitigated High Risks

title: Audit Risk Register Completeness
prompt: Audit Risk Register Completeness

title: Review Risk Owner Assignments
prompt: Review Risk Owner Assignments

title: Assess Control Effectiveness
prompt: Assess Control Effectiveness

title: Build a risk register for the top ten operational risks in this system
prompt: Build a risk register for the top ten operational risks in this system. For each risk, assess probability, impact, and current mitigation status. Order by expected loss value — probability times impact. Recommend the risk treatment — accept, mitigate, transfer, or avoid — for each.

title: Identify the three scenarios that represent existential risk to this sys
prompt: Identify the three scenarios that represent existential risk to this system — the failures that would result in unrecoverable data loss, sustained unavailability, or regulatory action. For each scenario, assess the current preparedness and the highest-impact risk reduction action available.

title: Audit third-party dependencies for concentration risk
prompt: Audit third-party dependencies for concentration risk. Find external services where multiple critical functions depend on a single vendor. For each concentration, assess the blast radius of that vendor becoming unavailable. Recommend diversification or fallback strategies for the highest-concentration dependencies.

title: Identify the top five technology risks with no current mitigation
prompt: Identify the top five technology risks with no current mitigation. For each, estimate the probability of occurrence in the next 12 months and the business impact if realized. Calculate the expected value of each risk as probability multiplied by impact cost. Rank by expected value. Recommend the mitigation controls ordered by risk reduction per dollar of implementation cost.

title: Assess the residual risk level after all current controls are applied
prompt: Assess the residual risk level after all current controls are applied. Identify risks where controls reduce likelihood but not impact, where a successful attack or failure is still catastrophic. For each, recommend additional controls that reduce impact through containment, backup, or graceful degradation. Distinguish between risks that should be mitigated, transferred to insurance or vendors, accepted with documented rationale, or avoided entirely.

title: Design a risk early warning system
prompt: Design a risk early warning system. Identify the leading indicators that predict each top risk materializing, including increasing error rate preceding an outage, growing dependency vulnerabilities preceding a security incident, and rising deployment failure rate preceding a reliability crisis. For each leading indicator, define the monitoring threshold that triggers a proactive risk response before the impact reaches users.

title: Review the risk management process for third-party and supply chain risk
prompt: Review the risk management process for third-party and supply chain risks. Identify all third-party dependencies that could introduce risk if they experienced a security incident, service outage, or end-of-life. For each, assess the current exposure and the mitigations available including alternative vendors, self-hosted alternatives, or graceful degradation. Recommend the supply chain risk management improvements.

title: Evaluate the risk communication process from engineering to leadership
prompt: Evaluate the risk communication process from engineering to leadership. Identify how technology risks are currently surfaced to executive stakeholders, whether risks are communicated reactively after incidents or proactively through structured risk reporting. Design a risk dashboard format suitable for board-level reporting showing top five risks, likelihood, impact, trend direction, and mitigation status. Recommend a monthly risk review cadence.

title: Evaluate the risk management posture across the system
prompt: Evaluate the risk management posture across the system. Identify whether a formal risk register exists and is maintained with current risk assessments. Find operational risks — infrastructure failure, supply chain compromise, key person dependency — that have no documented mitigation or contingency plan. Assess whether risk reviews occur at regular intervals or only reactively after incidents. Look for risks that have materialized in the past 12 months and assess whether the post-incident actions were sufficient to reduce recurrence probability. Identify the five highest-priority unmitigated risks based on expected impact times probability. Recommend the risk management process improvements that would give the team systematic visibility into and control over operational risk.

title: Assess technical debt risk specifically
prompt: Assess technical debt risk specifically. Identify areas of the codebase where technical debt creates operational risk — modules that are so complex that changes frequently introduce regressions, dependencies that are no longer maintained, or patterns that conflict with current best practices. Find the specific technical debt items that have already caused production incidents. Assess whether technical debt is tracked and prioritized alongside feature work, or treated as optional cleanup. Identify the single highest-risk technical debt item that poses the greatest threat to system stability if left unaddressed. Recommend a technical debt reduction strategy that prioritizes items by operational risk rather than by developer convenience.

title: Conduct a comprehensive technology risk assessment covering the full spe
prompt: Conduct a comprehensive technology risk assessment covering the full spectrum of risks that could disrupt business operations, compromise data security, or cause regulatory non-compliance. Begin by establishing a risk taxonomy, categorizing risks as operational risks affecting service availability, security risks affecting data confidentiality or integrity, compliance risks affecting regulatory standing, and strategic risks affecting the organization's ability to achieve its technology objectives. For each category, enumerate the specific risks relevant to the current architecture and operating environment, using historical incident data, threat intelligence, and architectural analysis to identify risks that are not yet documented. Assess each risk on two dimensions: the likelihood of occurrence given current controls and threat trends, and the business impact if the risk materializes, expressed as financial loss, reputational damage, regulatory consequence, and customer impact. Identify the risks above the acceptable threshold, those requiring additional mitigation before they can be accepted. Evaluate the effectiveness of current mitigations at actually reducing likelihood or impact, distinguishing between controls that are implemented and effective, controls that are implemented but not validated, and controls that exist only as policy without technical enforcement. Recommend the risk mitigation investments that would most reduce the aggregate risk exposure.

title: Evaluate the risk governance structure and its effectiveness at ensuring
prompt: Evaluate the risk governance structure and its effectiveness at ensuring that technology risks receive appropriate attention at the right organizational level. Assess whether risk ownership is clearly assigned, whether each identified risk has a named owner who is accountable for monitoring the risk, implementing mitigations, and escalating when the risk level changes. Review whether risk owners have the authority and resources to act on their risk management responsibilities, whether they can allocate engineering time to mitigations without competing against feature development for budget. Evaluate the risk review cadence, whether risks are reviewed quarterly and after significant architecture changes, or whether the risk register is a static document that falls out of date between major incidents. Assess whether risk appetite is defined at the organizational level, whether leadership has articulated the level of technology risk that is acceptable given the organization's strategic position, and whether engineering decisions are evaluated against that stated appetite. Review whether technology risk is considered in product and business planning decisions. Recommend the governance improvements that would make risk management an ongoing organizational capability rather than a periodic compliance exercise.

title: Review the effectiveness of the current risk mitigation portfolio in red
prompt: Review the effectiveness of the current risk mitigation portfolio in reducing the most significant risks facing the organization. Identify the mitigation controls implemented in the past 12 months and assess whether they reduced the likelihood or impact of the risks they addressed. Find controls that were implemented but are not functioning as intended, access controls with exceptions that undermine their effectiveness, monitoring systems that generate alerts no one responds to, or backup systems that have never been tested for restore success. Evaluate whether the organization is investing in the right risk mitigations, whether the control portfolio addresses the highest-priority risks or whether it reflects historical decisions that no longer align with the current threat landscape. Assess whether controls are layered, whether multiple independent controls protect against the most critical risks so that a single control failure does not create a complete exposure. Review whether controls are tested and validated, whether the organization has evidence that controls function under actual attack or failure conditions rather than design assumptions. Recommend the control portfolio adjustments that would most improve risk mitigation effectiveness given the current threat landscape and organizational priorities.

title: Assess blast radius before deploying this change
prompt: Analyze the proposed change. Identify every system, service, and user segment that could be affected. Map the risk of the change by blast radius, reversibility, and the confidence level in the test coverage for affected paths.
prompt_short: Assess the blast radius and reversibility of this proposed change.
prompt_filtered: Impact analysis only. Blast radius, reversibility, and test confidence.

title: Create a rollback plan for this deployment
prompt: Design a rollback plan for the proposed change. Define the rollback trigger conditions (error rate threshold, latency spike, specific error patterns), the rollback steps in order, and the verification steps to confirm the rollback succeeded.
prompt_short: Design a rollback plan with trigger conditions and verification steps.
prompt_filtered: Rollback plan only. Trigger criteria and step-by-step rollback procedure.

title: Review change isolation strategy for safe deployment
prompt: Assess whether the proposed change is adequately isolated — behind a feature flag, deployed to a canary cohort, or limited to a non-critical user segment first. Identify changes that go directly to 100% production traffic without any isolation.
prompt_short: Review change isolation strategy and flag all-at-once risky deployments.
prompt_filtered: Isolation strategy only. Feature flags, canary, and staged rollout.

title: Find undocumented breaking changes in this release
prompt: Review the release diff for breaking changes — removed API endpoints, changed response shapes, renamed fields, new required parameters, changed authentication requirements. Verify each breaking change is documented in the release notes and migration guide.
prompt_short: Find breaking changes in this release not documented in release notes.
prompt_filtered: Breaking changes only. API contract and interface changes.

title: Audit change history for recurring problem patterns
prompt: Review recent incidents and their root causes. Identify if specific types of changes consistently cause problems — database migrations, config changes, third-party upgrades, new feature launches. Recommend a targeted change process improvement for the top pattern.
prompt_short: Find recurring incident patterns caused by the same type of changes.
prompt_filtered: Pattern analysis only. Which change types cause incidents most often.

title: Design a canary deployment strategy for high-risk changes
prompt: Design a canary deployment strategy: route 1% of production traffic to the new version, define the automatic promotion criteria (error rate < X%, p99 latency < Y ms for Z minutes), and the automatic rollback trigger. Include the minimum canary duration before full promotion.
prompt_short: Design a canary strategy with automatic promotion criteria and rollback triggers.
prompt_filtered: Canary deployment only. Traffic split, promotion criteria, and rollback automation.

title: Assess change failure rate and classify failure categories
prompt: Measure change failure rate (deployments causing an incident / total deployments) over the past quarter. Classify failures by category: code bug, config mistake, dependency regression, data migration error, capacity issue. Identify the highest-frequency category and design a targeted pre-deployment gate.
prompt_short: Measure change failure rate and classify by category to find the top failure type.
prompt_filtered: Change failure rate only. Failure category breakdown and gate recommendation.

title: Design a change freeze policy for high-risk periods
prompt: Design a change freeze policy for high-risk periods — year-end, peak traffic events, major launches. Define: what constitutes a freeze (code deployments only, or infrastructure changes too), who can grant exceptions, the emergency change process during a freeze, and how to communicate the freeze schedule to all teams.
prompt_short: Design a change freeze policy with exception process for high-risk periods.
prompt_filtered: Change freeze only. Scope, exceptions, emergency process, and communication.

title: Audit database migration safety practices
prompt: Review the database migration process for safety: are migrations backward-compatible (new column nullable before old code is removed), is there a tested rollback for each migration, are migrations run in a separate deployment step from code, and are large table alterations done online without locking. Identify unsafe patterns.
prompt_short: Audit database migrations for backward compatibility, rollback, and online safety.
prompt_filtered: DB migration safety only. Backward compatibility, rollback, and lock-free patterns.

title: Design a feature flag lifecycle and cleanup process
prompt: Design the full feature flag lifecycle: creation (with expiry date), rollout, full enablement, and mandatory cleanup after the flag reaches 100%. Define a stale flag audit (flags older than 90 days at 100%) and a cleanup sprint cadence. Estimate the technical debt cost of uncleaned flags.
prompt_short: Design a feature flag lifecycle with mandatory cleanup and stale flag auditing.
prompt_filtered: Feature flag lifecycle only. Creation, rollout, and cleanup process.

title: Review infrastructure change process for configuration drift prevention
prompt: Assess whether infrastructure changes are made exclusively through IaC (Terraform/CDK) with no manual console changes allowed. Identify drift between IaC state and actual infrastructure. Design a drift detection process and an enforcement policy that blocks manual changes from reaching production.
prompt_short: Find infrastructure drift from IaC and design enforcement against manual changes.
prompt_filtered: Config drift only. IaC vs actual state and manual change prevention.

title: Design a dual-control process for critical production changes
prompt: Identify changes that require dual-control authorization — two independent parties must approve before execution. Candidates: direct production database writes, credential rotation, firewall rule changes, billing configuration. Design the approval workflow and audit trail for each dual-control change type.
prompt_short: Design dual-control approval for changes requiring two independent authorizers.
prompt_filtered: Dual control only. Change types requiring two-person authorization and audit trail.

title: Assess lead time for changes and identify slowest pipeline stages
prompt: Measure lead time for changes — from code commit to production deployment — broken down by pipeline stage: PR review wait, CI build, automated test, manual approval gate, deployment. Identify the slowest stage and recommend a specific improvement to reduce it by 50%.
prompt_short: Break down lead time by pipeline stage and find the slowest for targeted improvement.
prompt_filtered: Lead time only. Pipeline stage breakdown and bottleneck identification.

title: Review communication plan for customer-impacting changes
prompt: Assess the process for communicating customer-impacting planned maintenance and releases: how far in advance (24h? 1 week?), through which channels (email, status page, in-app banner), the format of the notice, and who approves the communication. Identify planned changes deployed without customer notice.
prompt_short: Design advance communication process for customer-impacting planned changes.
prompt_filtered: Change communication only. Notice period, channels, format, and approval.

title: Audit post-deployment verification checklist completeness
prompt: Review the post-deployment verification steps run after each production deployment. Verify the checklist covers: smoke tests on critical paths, error rate vs pre-deploy baseline, latency vs pre-deploy baseline, and key business metric sanity checks. Identify missing verification steps that have allowed degraded deployments to go undetected.
prompt_short: Audit post-deployment verification for error rate, latency, and business metric checks.
prompt_filtered: Post-deploy verification only. Checklist completeness and baseline comparison.

title: Design a progressive rollout strategy by user segment
prompt: Design a progressive rollout strategy for high-risk changes: internal users first, then beta cohort, then 1% production, then 10%, then 50%, then 100%. Define dwell time at each stage, automatic and manual promotion gates, and the rollback procedure at each stage.
prompt_short: Design a staged rollout strategy with dwell time and gates at each traffic level.
prompt_filtered: Staged rollout only. Cohort sequence, dwell time, and gate criteria.

title: Assess the rollback success rate for recent deployments
prompt: Review recent rollback events. For each, measure rollback duration, whether the rollback succeeded completely, and whether any data was permanently affected. Identify deployments where rollback failed or took longer than the MTTR target — and the root cause (non-reversible schema change, stateful migration, corrupted cache).
prompt_short: Measure rollback success rate and identify deployments where rollback failed or was too slow.
prompt_filtered: Rollback success only. Duration, completeness, and failure root causes.

title: Review change advisory board effectiveness for critical system changes
prompt: Assess the Change Advisory Board (CAB) process for critical infrastructure changes. Measure: average time from CAB submission to approval (too slow = bottleneck), percentage of changes approved without modification (CAB rubber-stamp risk), and changes that bypassed CAB and caused incidents (governance gap). Recommend process improvements.
prompt_short: Measure CAB effectiveness for approval speed, quality, and bypass rate.
prompt_filtered: CAB effectiveness only. Approval time, quality, and bypass incident correlation.

title: Audit dependency update process for security patch SLA
prompt: Measure the time from CVE publication to patch deployment for the last 10 security vulnerabilities affecting production dependencies. Identify whether there is a documented SLA (e.g., Critical CVEs patched within 24 hours, High within 7 days) and whether actual patch time meets the SLA. Identify which team is responsible.
prompt_short: Measure patch SLA adherence for the last 10 production CVEs.
prompt_filtered: Security patch SLA only. CVE-to-patch time and SLA target comparison.

title: Design an automated change impact report for stakeholder communication
prompt: Design an automated report generated after each deployment summarizing: what changed (diff summary), deployment duration, error rate comparison to pre-deploy baseline, and any incidents correlated with the change. Distribute to engineering leads and product owners automatically after each release.
prompt_short: Design an automated post-deployment impact report for engineering and product leads.
prompt_filtered: Change impact reporting only. Automated post-deploy summary content and distribution.
