title: Review the change management process for the last ten production deploym
prompt: Review the change management process for the last ten production deployments. Identify which deployments had a rollback plan tested before deployment. Find deployments that went to production without post-deployment validation. Recommend the minimum change checklist that would catch the most common deployment failure modes.

title: Identify all production changes made in the last 30 days that bypassed t
prompt: Identify all production changes made in the last 30 days that bypassed the standard review process. For each, assess what risk was taken on by skipping review. Find the root cause — time pressure, unclear process, or missing tooling — and recommend the process fix that makes the correct path the path of least resistance.

title: Audit change communication across teams
prompt: Audit change communication across teams. Find production changes in the last quarter that affected dependent teams without advance notice. For each, identify the communication gap — no RFC process, no changelog, or no notification channel. Recommend the change communication improvements that would eliminate surprise breakages for downstream consumers.

title: Identify the five change types with the highest historical incident rate
prompt: Identify the five change types with the highest historical incident rate. For each, analyze the common failure pattern including insufficient testing, missing rollback plan, incorrect deployment order, or inadequate monitoring. Design a change-type-specific checklist that addresses the most common failure cause. Estimate the incident reduction from enforcing the checklist for each change type.

title: Assess the deployment pipeline for progressive delivery capability
prompt: Assess the deployment pipeline for progressive delivery capability. Identify services that deploy to 100 percent of traffic simultaneously with no canary phase. Find services where a breaking change would affect all users before the team detects it through monitoring. Design a canary deployment strategy for the three highest-traffic services, specifying the traffic percentage, the monitoring window, the success criteria, and the automatic rollback trigger.

title: Review change coordination across teams for mutual dependency risks
prompt: Review change coordination across teams for mutual dependency risks. Identify changes in the past 90 days that caused incidents because a dependency changed without notifying downstream consumers. Design a change communication process that requires teams to notify downstream consumers before deploying changes to shared APIs or data contracts. Recommend the tooling that would enforce notification without creating friction for simple changes.

title: Evaluate the emergency change process for security patches and critical
prompt: Evaluate the emergency change process for security patches and critical fixes. Identify whether the standard change process has an expedited path for changes that cannot wait for normal review cadence. Assess whether the emergency process maintains minimum safety controls including at least one peer review, a tested rollback procedure, and post-change monitoring. Find cases where emergency changes were executed with no review. Recommend the emergency change process improvements.

title: Design a change risk scoring model for automated gate assignment
prompt: Design a change risk scoring model for automated gate assignment. Define the risk dimensions including scope of change, test coverage, deployment method, service criticality, and time of day. Assign weights to each dimension. Specify the score thresholds that determine which review gate applies: fully automated for low-risk, peer review for medium-risk, and architecture review for high-risk. Validate the model against the last 50 changes to assess calibration.

title: Evaluate the change management process for production deployments
prompt: Evaluate the change management process for production deployments. Identify whether every production change — code deployment, configuration change, infrastructure modification — goes through a defined review and approval process. Find changes that can be made to production without any review — direct database edits, configuration changes through admin UIs, or infrastructure changes applied manually. Assess whether rollback procedures are defined and tested before each significant change, or created reactively when a rollback is needed. Review post-deployment validation — whether changes are verified correct in production before they are considered complete. Identify the three change management process gaps that represent the highest risk of the next self-inflicted production incident.

title: Review the change communication strategy for internal and external stake
prompt: Review the change communication strategy for internal and external stakeholders. Identify whether breaking changes to APIs or interfaces are communicated to consumers with adequate notice. Find the most recent breaking change and trace how downstream consumers were notified and how long they had to adapt. Assess whether a changelog is maintained that stakeholders can subscribe to. Review whether major infrastructure changes trigger notifications to dependent teams. Identify any change in the past six months that caused a downstream team to be surprised by breakage they had no opportunity to prepare for. Recommend the change communication improvements that would eliminate cross-team surprises and reduce the blast radius of uncoordinated changes.

title: Conduct a comprehensive review of the change management process and its
prompt: Conduct a comprehensive review of the change management process and its effectiveness at balancing deployment velocity with production stability. Evaluate the end-to-end change lifecycle from the moment a change is proposed to the moment it is confirmed stable in production. Identify the change categories recognized by the current process, including standard changes that follow a defined procedure, normal changes requiring review and approval, and emergency changes requiring expedited handling, and assess whether the category boundaries are clearly defined and consistently applied by the engineering team. Review the testing requirements associated with each change category, whether automated test coverage is required before deployment, whether integration tests validate the change in an environment resembling production, and whether performance tests are run for changes affecting high-traffic paths. Evaluate the change approval process, whether it provides meaningful risk reduction or creates bureaucratic delay without improving change quality. Assess the rollback capability for each change type, whether rollback procedures are defined before deployment begins, tested to confirm they actually work, and executable within a defined time window. Identify the change management gaps most frequently associated with production incidents in the past 12 months. Recommend the process improvements that would most reduce change-induced incidents.

title: Evaluate the deployment practices across all production services and the
prompt: Evaluate the deployment practices across all production services and their contribution to system reliability. Identify the deployment methods used, including blue-green deployments, rolling updates, canary releases, and feature flags, and assess whether the method is matched to the risk level of the change. Find services that deploy all changes to 100 percent of production traffic simultaneously, with no progressive rollout that would limit the user impact of a failed deployment. Assess whether deployment monitoring is configured to validate key metrics, including error rate, latency, and business conversion, during and immediately after each deployment, with automatic rollback triggered if metrics degrade beyond acceptable thresholds. Review whether feature flags are used to decouple code deployment from feature activation, allowing risky features to be deployed dark and activated only after validation in production with a controlled user segment. Evaluate whether the deployment pipeline includes a pre-production environment that faithfully replicates production configuration, data volume characteristics, and traffic patterns. Recommend the deployment practice improvements that would most reduce the frequency and impact of deployment-induced incidents.

title: Assess the change communication practices across engineering teams and t
prompt: Assess the change communication practices across engineering teams and their adequacy for maintaining a coherent understanding of the production system's state. Identify whether the organization maintains a change calendar, a shared record of planned significant changes including database migrations, major library upgrades, and infrastructure modifications, that gives all teams visibility into upcoming changes that might interact with their own work. Review whether the API and data contract change process enforces backward compatibility requirements, whether consumers are notified before breaking changes are deployed, whether deprecation periods give consumers time to migrate, and whether the breaking change is released only after all consumers have confirmed readiness. Evaluate whether post-deployment communication informs relevant stakeholders that a change has been deployed and provides the key indicators to watch for any user-visible impact. Assess whether the incident response process distinguishes between changes deployed in the past 24 hours as a likely contributing factor, whether the first question in an incident investigation is always what changed recently. Recommend the change communication improvements that would give the entire engineering organization accurate situational awareness about the state of production.

title: Audit data pipeline reliability and failure handling
prompt: Review all data pipelines for failure handling — what happens when a source is unavailable, a schema changes unexpectedly, or a write fails halfway through. Identify pipelines that fail silently or leave data in a partial state.
prompt_short: Find data pipelines with no failure handling or silent partial failures.
prompt_filtered: Pipeline reliability only. Failure modes and partial write safety.

title: Find data quality issues at pipeline ingestion points
prompt: Audit data validation at ingestion. Identify pipelines that accept data without schema validation, type checking, or null handling. Find where bad data enters the system and corrupts downstream analytics or application state.
prompt_short: Find ingestion points that accept bad data without validation.
prompt_filtered: Data quality at ingestion only. Validation gaps and bad data entry.

title: Assess data lineage tracking and provenance documentation
prompt: Evaluate whether the origin and transformation history of data can be traced. Identify datasets where you cannot determine: where the data came from, what transformations were applied, and when it was last refreshed.
prompt_short: Assess data lineage tracking and find datasets with unknown provenance.
prompt_filtered: Data lineage only. Origin and transformation traceability.

title: Identify idempotency gaps in ETL and sync jobs
prompt: Review all ETL and data sync jobs for idempotency. Find jobs that re-run would create duplicate records, double-count metrics, or corrupt aggregates. Recommend idempotency keys and deduplication strategies.
prompt_short: Find ETL jobs that produce duplicates or corruption when re-run.
prompt_filtered: ETL idempotency only. Duplicate creation and re-run safety.

title: Review backfill strategy for historical data corrections
prompt: Assess how historical data corrections are handled. Identify whether pipelines can be safely re-run for past time periods, how backfills are orchestrated without affecting live pipelines, and how downstream consumers are notified of corrections.
prompt_short: Assess the backfill strategy for correcting historical data safely.
prompt_filtered: Backfill strategy only. Historical correction and live pipeline impact.

title: Assess schema evolution strategy for upstream changes
prompt: Evaluate how pipelines handle schema changes in upstream systems. Identify pipelines that break silently when a column is renamed, retyped, or removed. Recommend schema evolution policies — backward compatibility, forward compatibility, or explicit contract versioning.
prompt_short: Find pipelines that break silently when source schemas change.
prompt_filtered: Schema evolution only. Upstream change handling and contract policies.

title: Audit data quality expectations for coverage and actionability
prompt: Review the data quality expectation suite in use. Identify tables with no expectations, expectations that have never fired (potentially misconfigured), and expectations that fire frequently but are ignored. Recommend the minimal expectation set for each critical table.
prompt_short: Audit data quality expectations for coverage and actionability.
prompt_filtered: Expectation coverage only. Missing and stale data quality rules.

title: Find CDC gaps where real-time data changes are not captured
prompt: Identify tables or systems where changes are not captured via Change Data Capture. Find analytical use cases requiring near-real-time freshness that are currently served by batch pipelines. Recommend CDC adoption priority based on freshness SLA breaches.
prompt_short: Find where Change Data Capture is missing for real-time freshness needs.
prompt_filtered: CDC coverage only. Real-time freshness gaps in batch pipelines.

title: Review data partitioning strategy for query performance
prompt: Audit large tables for partitioning strategy. Identify tables queried by date range or entity with no partitioning, causing full scans. Recommend partition keys and pruning strategies per table, prioritized by query frequency and data volume.
prompt_short: Find large tables with no partitioning causing expensive full scans.
prompt_filtered: Partitioning only. Table partitioning keys and query pruning gaps.

title: Evaluate orchestration tool coverage for job dependencies
prompt: Assess whether all data pipeline dependencies are explicitly modeled in the orchestration tool. Find pipelines running on naive time-based schedules where a dependency could have failed silently, causing downstream jobs to run on stale or incomplete data.
prompt_short: Find pipelines with no explicit dependency modeling in the orchestrator.
prompt_filtered: Orchestration dependencies only. Time-based vs dependency-based scheduling.

title: Audit data warehouse for orphaned or unused tables
prompt: Identify tables and views in the warehouse that have had no queries in the past 30 days. Assess whether they are obsolete intermediate tables, deprecated models, or legitimate low-frequency tables. Recommend an archival or cleanup plan to reduce warehouse complexity and cost.
prompt_short: Find unused warehouse tables and recommend archival or cleanup.
prompt_filtered: Orphaned tables only. Zero-query assets in the past 30 days.

title: Review cost allocation for compute-intensive pipeline steps
prompt: Analyze compute cost by pipeline job. Identify the most expensive pipeline steps in terms of compute or warehouse credits. Assess whether cost is proportionate to business value — high-cost pipelines serving low-usage dashboards. Recommend cost reduction via frequency reduction, incremental processing, or caching.
prompt_short: Find the most expensive pipeline steps and assess their business value.
prompt_filtered: Pipeline cost only. Compute spend vs business value analysis.

title: Assess data access control and row-level security gaps
prompt: Review data warehouse access controls. Identify tables containing PII or sensitive business data accessible to all data users. Find missing row-level security policies for multi-tenant data. Recommend the minimum access model that enables analysts without exposing restricted data.
prompt_short: Find data warehouse access gaps exposing sensitive data to all users.
prompt_filtered: Access control only. PII exposure and row-level security gaps.

title: Find monitoring gaps for data pipeline SLA breaches
prompt: Identify data pipelines with defined freshness SLAs that have no alerting when the SLA is breached. Find pipelines that failed silently and were only detected by a downstream consumer noticing stale data. Recommend alerting thresholds and escalation paths.
prompt_short: Find pipelines with no alerting when freshness SLAs are breached.
prompt_filtered: SLA monitoring only. Alert gaps and escalation paths.

title: Evaluate the dbt model DAG for complexity and test coverage
prompt: Review the dbt DAG for model complexity — chains longer than 5 hops, wide fan-outs, or models with no downstream consumers. Audit test coverage: identify source tables and staging models with no schema tests. Recommend test additions prioritized by model business criticality.
prompt_short: Audit dbt DAG complexity and test coverage gaps across all models.
prompt_filtered: dbt quality only. DAG complexity and test coverage by model.

title: Identify duplicate transformation logic across pipelines
prompt: Find cases where the same transformation logic — revenue calculation, user cohort classification, session definition — is implemented independently in multiple pipelines, causing discrepancies when implementations drift. Recommend consolidation into a canonical model or shared library.
prompt_short: Find duplicate transformation logic implemented differently across pipelines.
prompt_filtered: Logic duplication only. Inconsistent implementations of shared concepts.

title: Review streaming pipeline backpressure and lag handling
prompt: For streaming pipelines, audit consumer lag monitoring and backpressure handling. Identify consumers with no lag alerting, unbounded retry loops, or no dead-letter queue for poison messages that would stall processing indefinitely.
prompt_short: Find streaming pipeline consumer lag issues and missing backpressure controls.
prompt_filtered: Streaming only. Consumer lag, backpressure, and dead-letter gaps.

title: Fill in Missing Data Gaps
prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

title: Gain full understanding before completing
prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

title: Find What's Broken
prompt: Find What's Broken

title: Recommend Next Steps
prompt: Recommend Next Steps

title: Score for Production Readiness
prompt: Score for Production Readiness

title: Audit Pipeline Quality
prompt: Audit Pipeline Quality

title: Check Data Lineage
prompt: Check Data Lineage

title: Map Pipeline Freshness Gaps
prompt: Map Pipeline Freshness Gaps

title: Flag Schema Drift Risks
prompt: Flag Schema Drift Risks

title: Benchmark Job Run Times
prompt: Benchmark Job Run Times

title: Find Silent Failure Points
prompt: Find Silent Failure Points

title: Prioritize Backfill Debt
prompt: Prioritize Backfill Debt

title: Audit the data pipeline for reliability and completeness
prompt: Audit the data pipeline for reliability and completeness. Identify all pipelines with no row count validation between source and destination. For each gap, describe the data loss or duplication scenario that would go undetected. Recommend the minimum validation check that catches the most common pipeline failure modes.

title: Find the five data sources with the poorest data quality — highest null
prompt: Find the five data sources with the poorest data quality — highest null rates, most schema drift incidents, or most failed validation checks in the past 30 days. For each, trace the root cause and recommend the upstream fix or compensating quality control that would most improve downstream analysis reliability.

title: Audit data lineage documentation
prompt: Audit data lineage documentation. Identify tables or metrics where the calculation logic is not documented. For each, describe the risk to the business when analysts make decisions based on a metric whose definition is ambiguous or inconsistent across teams.

title: List every pipeline job that runs more than once per hour
prompt: List every pipeline job that runs more than once per hour. For each job, record average duration, peak memory usage, and failure rate over the past thirty days. Rank by total compute cost. Identify the top three jobs where optimization would yield the greatest savings. Output a prioritized recommendation table with estimated effort per optimization.

title: Review all schema change events in the past ninety days
prompt: Review all schema change events in the past ninety days. Classify each change as additive, breaking, or ambiguous. For breaking changes, trace downstream consumers and assess impact severity. Identify tables with more than three breaking changes and flag them as schema instability risks. Produce a schema health report with a risk score per table.

title: Audit every data source for freshness SLA compliance
prompt: Audit every data source for freshness SLA compliance. Define freshness as data arriving within the expected window with no gaps exceeding fifteen minutes. List sources currently violating their SLA. Identify root causes grouped into infrastructure, upstream, and ingestion categories. Recommend alerting improvements for each category.

title: Map the complete data lineage from raw ingestion to every reporting tabl
prompt: Map the complete data lineage from raw ingestion to every reporting table used in executive dashboards. For each hop, document transformation logic, owner, and last validated date. Highlight any undocumented transformations or orphaned intermediate tables. Output a lineage graph description and a list of documentation gaps.

title: Evaluate the on-call runbook for data incidents
prompt: Evaluate the on-call runbook for data incidents. Check that every pipeline has a corresponding runbook entry covering symptoms, triage steps, escalation path, and rollback procedure. List pipelines missing runbook coverage. Score the existing runbooks on completeness using a zero to ten rubric. Recommend three immediate improvements.

title: Review the data engineering pipeline from ingestion to consumption
prompt: Review the data engineering pipeline from ingestion to consumption. Identify every data source, its ingestion method, its update frequency, and the transformation logic applied before data reaches the analytical layer. Find pipelines with no data quality checks — where corrupt, incomplete, or duplicate data would propagate silently to dashboards and reports. Assess whether schema changes in source systems are detected and handled gracefully or cause silent pipeline failures. Look for pipelines with no alerting on failure, where a broken pipeline is discovered only when a downstream analyst notices a data gap. Summarize the five highest-priority data engineering reliability improvements.

title: Evaluate the data infrastructure for scalability and operational maturit
prompt: Evaluate the data infrastructure for scalability and operational maturity. Assess whether processing jobs complete within their scheduled windows at current data volumes. Find jobs that are approaching processing time limits that would be breached at 2x data volume. Review the orchestration tooling — whether job dependencies are explicit, whether failures trigger retries with backoff, and whether partial failures are distinguishable from complete failures. Assess whether data engineers have sufficient visibility into job performance — run time trends, row counts, and error rates — to diagnose issues proactively. Recommend the infrastructure investments that would most improve data pipeline reliability and observability.

title: You are a senior data reliability engineer conducting a quarterly review
prompt: You are a senior data reliability engineer conducting a quarterly review of a production data platform. Your scope covers all batch and streaming pipelines, the ingestion layer, transformation logic, and delivery to downstream consumers. Begin by inventorying every pipeline by type, owner, and SLA tier. Then assess reliability metrics including mean time to detect, mean time to resolve, and SLA breach rate for the past quarter. Examine schema evolution practices to determine whether breaking changes are communicated and versioned properly. Review alerting coverage and identify any pipelines that produce no alerts on failure. Evaluate data quality checks embedded in the pipeline and flag any that have not fired in over sixty days, as this may indicate the check is misconfigured. Assess backfill capability and document the maximum look-back window for each critical pipeline. Review the on-call rotation and incident log to surface any recurring failure patterns. Conclude with a prioritized action plan that distinguishes between quick wins achievable in one sprint and strategic investments requiring roadmap slots. Format your output as an executive briefing followed by a detailed technical appendix.

title: You are a data architecture consultant tasked with evaluating a company'
prompt: You are a data architecture consultant tasked with evaluating a company's pipeline cost efficiency. Your analysis covers compute spend, storage footprint, and data transfer costs across all pipeline stages. Start by identifying the top ten most expensive jobs by cloud billing data and correlate cost with business value delivered. Examine partitioning strategies and determine whether time-based partitioning is applied consistently to all high-volume tables. Review caching and materialization decisions to find views being recomputed unnecessarily on every query. Analyze job scheduling to detect underutilized time windows where batch loads could be deferred to off-peak hours for lower spot pricing. Identify duplicate data stored across environments and estimate the cost of eliminating redundancy through a single source of truth approach. Evaluate whether streaming pipelines are sized appropriately for their actual throughput or are over-provisioned. Review retention policies and confirm that cold storage tiering is applied to data older than ninety days. Summarize findings in a cost optimization report with estimated monthly savings per recommendation and implementation complexity ratings.

title: You are a data governance lead performing an access control audit on a p
prompt: You are a data governance lead performing an access control audit on a production data warehouse. Your goal is to ensure that data access follows the principle of least privilege and that all sensitive data is properly classified and protected. Begin by listing every role with read access to tables containing PII, payment data, or health information. Cross-reference this list against the active employee directory to identify any former employees or contractors with residual access. Review service account permissions and flag accounts with broad SELECT privileges that could be scoped to specific tables. Examine column-level security policies and determine whether masking is applied consistently to high-sensitivity columns in all reporting tools. Audit data sharing agreements with third-party partners and verify that shared datasets are limited to the agreed scope. Review the change data capture configuration to ensure that audit logs capture all INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE events on sensitive tables. Assess whether data masking is applied in non-production environments to prevent PII leakage in development. Produce a compliance gap report mapped to relevant regulatory frameworks and include a remediation timeline.

title: Identify the metrics the team uses to make decisions
prompt: Survey which metrics are regularly used to make product and engineering decisions. Identify metrics that are trusted vs questioned, metrics that drive decisions but have no reliable source, and important business questions with no metric answer.
prompt_short: Find which metrics drive decisions and which important questions have no metric.
prompt_filtered: Decision metrics only. Used metrics vs missing metric gaps.

title: Audit dashboard data freshness and accuracy
prompt: Review all reporting dashboards for data freshness — how old is the data displayed, is the lag acceptable for its use case, and are there any dashboards showing stale data without a freshness indicator.
prompt_short: Audit all dashboards for data freshness and acceptable lag.
prompt_filtered: Data freshness only. Lag measurement and acceptable thresholds.

title: Find metric definitions inconsistent across teams
prompt: Identify metrics that are defined differently across teams or dashboards — different date ranges, different filtering logic, different attribution windows. These create disagreements in meetings and decisions based on incompatible numbers.
prompt_short: Find metrics defined differently across teams causing reporting conflicts.
prompt_filtered: Metric consistency only. Conflicting definitions across dashboards or teams.

title: Assess data warehouse structure for query efficiency
prompt: Review the data warehouse schema. Identify tables that are queried frequently but are not properly structured for analytics use — missing partitioning, no materialized views for slow aggregations, denormalization opportunities for common joins.
prompt_short: Find data warehouse structural issues causing slow analytical queries.
prompt_filtered: Warehouse structure only. Partitioning, materialization, and denormalization.

title: Identify self-serve analytics gaps blocking the team
prompt: Assess how easily non-engineers can answer their own data questions. Identify questions that currently require an engineer to write a query, and evaluate whether the data infrastructure can support self-serve with better tooling or documentation.
prompt_short: Find BI gaps that force non-engineers to ask engineers for basic queries.
prompt_filtered: Self-serve gaps only. Questions requiring engineering for basic analysis.

title: Audit semantic layer definitions for consistency
prompt: Review the semantic layer or metrics layer for consistency. Identify metrics defined in the semantic layer that conflict with ad-hoc query definitions used in dashboards, causing the same metric to return different values depending on the tool used.
prompt_short: Find semantic layer metric definitions that conflict with ad-hoc queries.
prompt_filtered: Semantic layer only. Metric definition conflicts across tools.

title: Find dashboards owned by no one with no review date
prompt: Audit all dashboards for ownership and last review date. Identify dashboards with no assigned owner, dashboards not viewed in 60+ days, and dashboards whose underlying data model was modified without a dashboard review. Recommend an ownership assignment and sunsetting process.
prompt_short: Find ownerless dashboards and those not reviewed after data model changes.
prompt_filtered: Dashboard ownership only. Orphaned and stale dashboards.

title: Identify BI tool sprawl creating single-source-of-truth problems
prompt: Map every BI tool in use across the organization. Identify teams that have independently built reporting for the same metrics in separate tools. Assess the divergence in metric definitions across tools and recommend a consolidation path to a single authoritative BI layer.
prompt_short: Map BI tool sprawl and find metric divergence across tools.
prompt_filtered: BI tool consolidation only. Multi-tool metric drift and duplication.

title: Design a report governance process for executive dashboards
prompt: Propose a governance process for executive-facing dashboards. Include: review cadence, change control for metric definitions, accuracy verification before distribution, stakeholder sign-off for new metrics, and a process for communicating metric redefinitions to existing consumers.
prompt_short: Design a governance process for executive dashboard accuracy and changes.
prompt_filtered: Executive governance only. Accuracy, change control, and sign-off process.

title: Find embedded analytics coverage gaps in the product
prompt: Identify product surfaces where users would benefit from embedded analytics — usage summaries, personalized insights, trend alerts — but currently see raw data with no interpretation. Prioritize by the user decision the embedded insight would support.
prompt_short: Find product surfaces where embedded analytics would add user value.
prompt_filtered: Embedded analytics only. In-product insight gaps.

title: Assess BI query performance impact on production databases
prompt: Identify BI queries running against production databases rather than analytical replicas. Find the slowest recurring BI queries and estimate their impact on production query response times. Recommend replica promotion, query optimization, or result caching per case.
prompt_short: Find BI queries on production databases impacting application performance.
prompt_filtered: BI query impact only. Production DB load from analytical queries.

title: Evaluate report scheduling for off-hours and cache warming
prompt: Review the schedule of heavy dashboard queries and report exports. Identify reports scheduled during peak user traffic hours that compete with application queries. Recommend rescheduling to off-peak windows and pre-caching results for common dashboard views.
prompt_short: Find reports scheduled during peak hours and recommend off-peak scheduling.
prompt_filtered: Report scheduling only. Peak hour conflicts and cache warming.

title: Audit row-count and aggregate sanity checks across dashboards
prompt: Add automated sanity checks to critical dashboards: row count within expected range, totals within expected bounds, zero-value alerts for metrics that should never be zero. Identify dashboards with no automated accuracy check that would catch a broken pipeline serving wrong numbers.
prompt_short: Identify dashboards with no automated sanity checks for data accuracy.
prompt_filtered: Sanity checks only. Expected-range and zero-value alerts for dashboards.

title: Map metric ownership to DRI and escalation path
prompt: Create a metric ownership registry mapping each business-critical metric to a directly responsible individual and an escalation path. Identify metrics with no DRI who are used in board or investor reporting. Recommend a governance model where the DRI signs off on definition changes.
prompt_short: Create a metric DRI registry for business-critical metrics used in reporting.
prompt_filtered: Metric ownership only. DRI assignment for board-level metrics.

title: Identify missing cohort analysis in current BI stack
prompt: Assess whether the BI stack supports cohort analysis — comparing user behavior by signup month, plan tier, or acquisition channel. Identify key business questions that require cohort analysis but are answered with aggregate metrics that obscure cohort-level divergence.
prompt_short: Find business questions needing cohort analysis being answered with aggregates.
prompt_filtered: Cohort analysis only. Missing cohort breakdowns in the BI stack.

title: Review alerting thresholds for key business metrics
prompt: Audit alerts configured on key business metrics. Identify metrics with no alert, alerts with thresholds that have never fired (too loose), and alert fatigue situations where alerts fire so frequently they are ignored. Recommend calibrated thresholds per metric.
prompt_short: Audit metric alerts for missing coverage and miscalibrated thresholds.
prompt_filtered: Metric alerting only. Missing alerts and threshold calibration.

title: Find BI reports that would benefit from drill-through capability
prompt: Identify summary reports where users regularly export data to drill into root causes that the report does not surface. These are candidates for drill-through or linked detail views. Prioritize by frequency of export requests and analyst time spent on manual investigation.
prompt_short: Find summary reports where users export to dig into root causes manually.
prompt_filtered: Drill-through gaps only. Reports requiring manual investigation to interpret.

prompt: Find Reporting Gaps

title: Map Metric Ownership by Team
prompt: Map Metric Ownership by Team

title: Spot Dashboard Redundancy
prompt: Spot Dashboard Redundancy

title: Calibrate Alert Thresholds
prompt: Calibrate Alert Thresholds

title: Identify Self-Serve Blockers
prompt: Identify Self-Serve Blockers

title: Score BI Infrastructure Maturity
prompt: Score BI Infrastructure Maturity

title: Audit all business intelligence dashboards for data freshness
prompt: Audit all business intelligence dashboards for data freshness. Identify dashboards where data is older than the stated update interval. For each staleness issue, trace whether the root cause is a pipeline delay, a query performance problem, or a misconfigured schedule.

title: Find the three most-viewed dashboards and assess whether the metrics the
prompt: Find the three most-viewed dashboards and assess whether the metrics they display have documented definitions. Identify any metric used in executive reporting whose calculation logic is ambiguous or disputed across teams. Recommend the definition documentation process that would eliminate metric disagreements.

title: Review BI access controls
prompt: Review BI access controls. Identify any dashboard or report that contains sensitive business data — revenue, headcount, salary — that is accessible to all authenticated users. Recommend the role-based access policy that limits sensitive report access to the appropriate audience.

title: Inventory every dashboard in the BI platform
prompt: Inventory every dashboard in the BI platform. For each dashboard, record the owner, last viewed date, average daily active viewers, and data source. Flag dashboards with zero views in the past sixty days as candidates for archival. Group remaining dashboards by business function and identify functional areas with overlapping coverage. Produce a consolidation plan that reduces total dashboard count by at least twenty percent.

title: Audit the metric catalog for consistency
prompt: Audit the metric catalog for consistency. List all metrics defined in the BI layer and check for duplicate metric names with different calculation logic. Flag any metric missing a business owner, refresh schedule, or approved definition. For each metric used in executive reporting, verify that its calculation matches the authoritative formula documented in the data dictionary. Output a metric health scorecard.

title: Review the self-serve analytics adoption rate by department
prompt: Review the self-serve analytics adoption rate by department. Identify which departments rely entirely on BI team-built reports versus those actively creating their own. Interview or survey five power users per department to understand blockers to self-serve adoption. Map blockers into categories: access, training, data availability, and tooling. Recommend a targeted enablement plan for the two departments with the lowest self-serve adoption.

title: Assess BI query performance across the ten most-used dashboards
prompt: Assess BI query performance across the ten most-used dashboards. For each dashboard, measure average load time, maximum load time under peak concurrency, and the number of queries exceeding a five-second threshold. Identify queries performing full table scans on tables over one billion rows. Recommend indexing, caching, or pre-aggregation strategies for each identified bottleneck. Estimate load time improvement after each optimization.

title: Evaluate the data refresh schedule across all BI dashboards
prompt: Evaluate the data refresh schedule across all BI dashboards. List every dashboard that claims to show real-time data and verify whether the underlying data source actually refreshes in near real-time or on a batch schedule. Flag any dashboard where the displayed refresh timestamp is misleading. Recommend a transparent refresh labeling standard. Propose a tiered SLA for refresh frequency based on business criticality.

title: Evaluate the business intelligence infrastructure for completeness and r
prompt: Evaluate the business intelligence infrastructure for completeness and reliability. Identify every dashboard and report in use, its intended audience, and how frequently it is accessed. Find reports whose underlying data has not been validated for accuracy — where the numbers displayed could be wrong without any automated detection. Assess whether the BI layer has a single source of truth for each key metric or whether multiple dashboards calculate the same metric differently. Look for reports that are used in executive decision-making but have no documented owner responsible for their accuracy. Summarize the five BI reliability gaps with the highest decision-making risk.

title: Review the BI tooling and query performance
prompt: Review the BI tooling and query performance. Identify dashboard queries with execution times that cause noticeable load times for users. Find queries that run against production databases rather than analytical replicas, adding load to systems that serve user traffic. Assess whether query caching is implemented for expensive BI queries and whether the cache TTL is appropriate for the data refresh frequency. Look for ad-hoc query patterns that could degrade database performance for all users when executed against large tables. Recommend the BI infrastructure improvements that would most improve performance and reduce operational risk from analytical queries.

title: You are a business intelligence director conducting an annual maturity a
prompt: You are a business intelligence director conducting an annual maturity assessment of your organization's analytics capability. Your evaluation covers four dimensions: data foundation quality, analytics tooling, user enablement, and governance. Begin by benchmarking your current state against a five-level maturity model spanning ad hoc reporting, managed reporting, self-serve analytics, predictive analytics, and embedded intelligence. Assess the data foundation by reviewing the completeness and freshness of your core metrics layer. Evaluate tooling by comparing your current BI platform against leading alternatives on dimensions of performance, governance features, and total cost of ownership. Measure user enablement by tracking the ratio of self-serve report creators to total business users and comparing it against industry benchmarks. Review governance by auditing metric definitions for consistency and checking whether a certified metric catalog exists and is actively maintained. Identify the single biggest bottleneck preventing advancement to the next maturity level. Develop a twelve-month roadmap that prioritizes capabilities with the highest business impact per implementation effort. Present your findings in a board-ready maturity report with a current state spider chart and a future state target.

title: You are a BI engineering lead tasked with redesigning the metrics layer
prompt: You are a BI engineering lead tasked with redesigning the metrics layer for a high-growth SaaS company. The current metrics layer consists of ad hoc SQL views with inconsistent logic, no versioning, and no ownership assignments. Your mandate is to design and document a semantic layer that serves as the single source of truth for all company metrics. Start by cataloging every metric currently used in board reports, investor updates, and operational reviews. For each metric, document the business question it answers, the calculation logic, the data sources involved, and the acceptable refresh latency. Identify conflicts where the same metric name returns different values in different dashboards. Design a canonical metric registry with versioning, owner fields, approval workflows, and deprecation procedures. Define naming conventions that encode the metric type, time grain, and business domain in the metric name. Specify the technical implementation using a headless BI tool or dbt metrics layer, including how the semantic layer will be exposed to downstream consumers including BI tools, Slack bots, and APIs. Produce a migration plan that transitions existing dashboards to the new layer with zero disruption to end users.

title: You are a data strategy consultant advising a retail company on building
prompt: You are a data strategy consultant advising a retail company on building a customer-360 analytics capability. The company has transaction data in a legacy ERP, customer interaction data in a CRM, and behavioral data in a web analytics platform, none of which are currently joined at the customer level. Your task is to design the analytical architecture and governance model for a unified customer view. Begin by auditing the three source systems for completeness of customer identifiers, data freshness, and historical depth. Define the identity resolution strategy that will link records across systems using deterministic matching on email and phone, with probabilistic fallback for unresolved records. Design the customer-360 schema including lifetime value, purchase frequency, channel preferences, support history, and product affinity scores. Specify the refresh cadence and pipeline architecture for keeping the unified view current. Define the access model that controls which teams can access which customer attributes based on data sensitivity. Recommend a phased rollout plan starting with the highest-value use case, which is likely churn prediction or next best offer. Identify the three KPIs that will demonstrate ROI from the investment within the first quarter after launch.

title: Define the top 5 KPIs for this product right now
prompt: Based on the product's goals and stage, define the 5 most important KPIs. For each, specify: the exact calculation, the data source, the target value, the measurement frequency, and the owner responsible for improving it.
prompt_short: Define the 5 most important KPIs with calculation, source, and owner.
prompt_filtered: KPI definition only. Calculation, source, target, and ownership.

title: Find vanity metrics masking real performance issues
prompt: Identify metrics currently being tracked that look good but don't correlate with actual business health — total registered users vs active users, total API calls vs successful calls, gross installs vs retained installs. Recommend actionable alternatives.
prompt_short: Identify vanity metrics and recommend actionable replacements.
prompt_filtered: Vanity metrics only. Metrics that look good but hide problems.

title: Audit metric tracking for coverage of user journeys
prompt: Map the key user journeys against the events and metrics tracked. Identify steps in important funnels with no measurement — you can't optimize what you can't measure. Prioritize by commercial and retention impact.
prompt_short: Find user journey steps with no metric tracking for optimization.
prompt_filtered: Journey coverage only. Unmeasured steps in key funnels.

title: Identify leading indicators vs lagging indicators
prompt: Classify the current metric set into leading indicators (predict future performance) and lagging indicators (measure past results). Identify whether the team has enough leading indicators to act before problems become visible in lagging metrics.
prompt_short: Classify metrics as leading vs lagging and find prediction gaps.
prompt_filtered: Indicator classification only. Leading vs lagging metric balance.

title: Create a single source of truth for core business metrics
prompt: Design a metrics registry that defines each core business metric: name, calculation, owner, data source, refresh frequency, and acceptable data lag. This becomes the reference document to resolve disagreements about metric definitions.
prompt_short: Design a metrics registry to define all core metrics and resolve conflicts.
prompt_filtered: Registry design only. Metric definition and ownership documentation.

title: Map the AARRR funnel with current metric coverage
prompt: Apply the AARRR framework — Acquisition, Activation, Retention, Revenue, Referral — to this product. For each stage, identify the primary metric, the current measurement method, and the stages with no reliable metric. Find the funnel stage most underinvested in measurement.
prompt_short: Map the AARRR funnel and find the stages with no reliable metric.
prompt_filtered: AARRR funnel only. Stage-by-stage metric coverage assessment.

title: Define the North Star Metric for this product
prompt: Define the single North Star Metric that best captures the value this product delivers to users. Justify why it predicts long-term revenue better than alternatives. Identify the input metrics — levers the team directly controls — that most reliably move the North Star.
prompt_short: Define the North Star Metric and the input levers that drive it.
prompt_filtered: North Star only. Single core metric and its input drivers.

title: Find metrics gaming risks in the current KPI set
prompt: Identify KPIs that can be improved without creating genuine user value — inflating session counts with auto-refresh, counting trial starts as activations, or increasing DAU by sending empty push notifications. For each gaming risk, recommend a paired metric that makes gaming detectable.
prompt_short: Find KPIs that can be gamed and recommend anti-gaming paired metrics.
prompt_filtered: Gaming risk only. KPIs improvable without genuine user value.

title: Assess whether team KPIs are actionable within a sprint
prompt: Review team-level KPIs for actionability. Identify metrics that are too lagging to influence within a two-week sprint, too aggregated to diagnose, or outside the team's control. Recommend leading indicator substitutes that teams can observe, diagnose, and act on within a sprint cycle.
prompt_short: Find team KPIs too lagging or aggregated to act on within a sprint.
prompt_filtered: Actionability only. Sprint-timescale leading indicator gaps.

title: Create a metric health dashboard for the weekly review
prompt: Design a one-page metric health dashboard for the weekly leadership review. Include: the 5-7 most critical metrics, their week-over-week trend, a traffic-light status (healthy/at-risk/critical), and a single sentence interpretation for each. Exclude metrics that rarely change week-to-week.
prompt_short: Design a one-page weekly metric health dashboard with traffic-light status.
prompt_filtered: Weekly dashboard only. Critical metrics with trend and status.

title: Identify which metrics correlate with revenue over 90 days
prompt: Run a correlation analysis between leading behavioral metrics — DAU, activation rate, feature engagement — and 90-day revenue outcomes. Identify the 2-3 behavioral signals most predictive of revenue at 90 days. These become the primary leading indicators for growth bets.
prompt_short: Find the behavioral metrics most predictive of 90-day revenue outcomes.
prompt_filtered: Predictive metrics only. Leading indicators correlated with revenue.

title: Benchmark KPIs against industry cohorts
prompt: Compare the product's core KPIs — DAU/MAU ratio, 30-day retention, NPS, CAC payback period — against industry benchmarks for products at the same stage and category. Identify metrics where the product is materially below benchmark and where it is a standout performer.
prompt_short: Benchmark product KPIs against industry standards for the same stage.
prompt_filtered: Benchmarking only. Below-benchmark metrics vs standout performers.

title: Review metric instrumentation for measurement accuracy
prompt: Audit the instrumentation underlying key metrics. Identify events that are double-fired, missing on certain client platforms, blocked by ad blockers, or not deduplicated server-side. Find the metrics whose instrumentation accuracy you cannot verify and recommend audit procedures.
prompt_short: Audit event instrumentation accuracy for key metrics.
prompt_filtered: Instrumentation only. Double-firing, missing events, and deduplication.

title: Assess customer health score components and weights
prompt: Evaluate the customer health score model. Identify which signals — login frequency, feature adoption, support ticket volume, NPS — are included and how they are weighted. Find signals with high predictive value for churn that are missing from the current score. Recommend weight recalibration based on churn correlation analysis.
prompt_short: Evaluate the customer health score for missing signals and incorrect weights.
prompt_filtered: Health score only. Signal selection and weight calibration.

title: Find duplicate or redundant metrics confusing the team
prompt: Identify metrics in the current tracking plan that measure the same underlying construct with different event names, calculation windows, or scope filters. These create confusion in reviews when numbers do not match. Recommend canonical definitions and aliases to deprecate.
prompt_short: Find redundant metrics measuring the same thing with different numbers.
prompt_filtered: Metric deduplication only. Redundant definitions causing confusion.

title: Create a KPI review cadence with decision triggers
prompt: Design a KPI review schedule: which metrics are reviewed daily (operational), weekly (growth and quality), monthly (strategic), and quarterly (goal-setting). For each review cadence, define the decision triggers — what change in a metric should trigger an action, investigation, or escalation.
prompt_short: Design a KPI review cadence with specific decision triggers per metric.
prompt_filtered: Review cadence only. Frequency and action triggers per metric tier.

title: Identify missing cohort retention metrics across product tiers
prompt: Assess whether retention is measured separately for users on different plans, acquisition channels, or signup cohorts. Find cases where overall retention looks healthy but a specific cohort is churning at rates hidden by aggregation. Recommend the retention breakdown that would reveal the most actionable insight.
prompt_short: Find retention divergence hidden by aggregated metrics across cohorts.
prompt_filtered: Cohort retention only. Plan, channel, and signup cohort breakdowns.

title: Fill in Missing Data Gaps
prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

title: Gain full understanding before completing
prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

title: Find What's Broken
prompt: Find What's Broken

title: Recommend Next Steps
prompt: Recommend Next Steps

title: Score for Production Readiness
prompt: Score for Production Readiness

title: Audit KPI Definitions
prompt: Audit KPI Definitions

title: Identify Vanity Metrics
prompt: Identify Vanity Metrics

title: Identify Leading vs Lagging Indicators
prompt: Identify Leading vs Lagging Indicators

title: Remove Vanity Metrics
prompt: Remove Vanity Metrics

title: Design Metric Review Cadence
prompt: Design Metric Review Cadence

title: Map Metric to Business Outcome
prompt: Map Metric to Business Outcome

title: Detect Gaming Risk
prompt: Detect Gaming Risk

title: Review the top five KPIs tracked by the team
prompt: Review the top five KPIs tracked by the team. For each, verify there is a documented definition — numerator, denominator, time window, and exclusions. Identify any KPI calculated differently in different tools or by different teams. Recommend a canonical definition and single source of truth for each.

title: Identify vanity metrics currently tracked and reported
prompt: Identify vanity metrics currently tracked and reported. For each, explain why it is a vanity metric — that it can increase without reflecting business value. Recommend the actionable metric it should be replaced with, and describe how the replacement metric would change the team's behavior.

title: Audit the KPI hierarchy
prompt: Audit the KPI hierarchy. Identify whether top-level business KPIs cascade into team-level metrics and individual-level leading indicators. Find any team that tracks metrics with no clear line to business outcomes. Recommend the metric restructuring that would align team behavior with business goals.

title: List every metric currently tracked across all teams
prompt: List every metric currently tracked across all teams. For each metric, record the owner, definition, data source, refresh cadence, and whether it appears in a board or executive report. Flag any metric with no assigned owner or with a definition that has not been reviewed in over twelve months. Group all metrics into strategic, operational, and diagnostic tiers. Output a full metric registry in table format.

title: Evaluate each metric in the executive dashboard for actionability
prompt: Evaluate each metric in the executive dashboard for actionability. A metric is actionable if a team can directly influence it within a single quarter. Mark metrics that are purely lagging indicators with no corresponding leading indicator tracked. For each lagging metric, identify one leading indicator that predicts its movement with at least a two-week lead time. Recommend adding the leading indicators to the operational review cadence.

title: Audit the current OKR-to-metric alignment
prompt: Audit the current OKR-to-metric alignment. List every objective and key result for the current quarter. For each key result, verify that a tracked metric exists and that its target is backed by historical baseline data. Flag key results with no associated metric or with targets set without reference to historical performance. Recommend a structured target-setting workshop for the next planning cycle.

title: Assess metric gaming risk for the five most prominent KPIs
prompt: Assess metric gaming risk for the five most prominent KPIs. For each KPI, describe at least two ways a team could improve the number without improving the underlying business outcome. Rate the gaming risk as low, medium, or high. For high-risk metrics, recommend a complementary counter-metric that makes gaming visible. Produce a metric integrity report with recommendations.

title: Design a metric review rhythm for a cross-functional leadership team
prompt: Design a metric review rhythm for a cross-functional leadership team. Define which metrics should be reviewed daily in operational standups, weekly in leadership syncs, and monthly in strategic reviews. For each cadence, specify the alert threshold that triggers an unscheduled review. Create a template for the weekly metric review meeting including agenda, format, and decision criteria. Estimate time required per review cadence.

title: Audit the metrics and KPI framework used to measure business performance
prompt: Audit the metrics and KPI framework used to measure business performance. Identify every metric tracked at the business, product, and team level. Assess whether each metric has a documented definition — including how it is calculated, what data sources it uses, and what time window it covers. Find metrics that are calculated inconsistently across tools, causing disagreement in review meetings. Identify leading indicators — metrics that predict future performance — versus lagging indicators that only confirm past results. Assess whether the metric set is sufficient to detect problems early or whether issues are only visible after they have already affected business outcomes. Summarize the five metric framework improvements that would most improve decision-making quality.

title: Evaluate whether the current KPI set drives the right team behaviors
prompt: Evaluate whether the current KPI set drives the right team behaviors. Identify KPIs that can be gamed — improved without creating genuine business value. Find any KPI that has been consistently met despite users experiencing poor outcomes. Assess whether the KPIs incentivize short-term results at the expense of long-term quality. Look for important business outcomes that have no corresponding metric — areas where the team is flying blind. Review the cadence of KPI review — whether metrics are reviewed at a frequency appropriate for taking corrective action. Recommend the KPI framework changes that would most closely align measured success with actual user and business value.

title: You are a chief analytics officer helping a growth-stage startup build i
prompt: You are a chief analytics officer helping a growth-stage startup build its first metrics framework from scratch. The company currently tracks thirty-seven metrics across six spreadsheets with no standardized definitions, no ownership, and no connection to strategic priorities. Your mandate is to design a metrics framework that is both rigorous enough to support investor reporting and simple enough for every team lead to use without analyst support. Begin by facilitating a metric prioritization exercise that maps each candidate metric to a strategic pillar and asks whether a team can act on the metric within a sprint. Eliminate metrics that fail both tests. For the surviving metrics, define calculation logic in plain language, identify the data source, assign a business owner, and set a review cadence. Design a three-tier hierarchy with north star metrics at the top, strategic KPIs in the middle, and operational diagnostics at the base. Specify the governance process for proposing a new metric, retiring an obsolete metric, and resolving definition conflicts. Design the weekly metrics review ritual including the standard format, the escalation criteria, and the decision log. Recommend the tooling stack for metric storage, visualization, and alerting that fits a team of under fifty. Deliver your output as a metrics framework document suitable for a company all-hands presentation.

title: You are a product analytics lead designing a metrics system for a consum
prompt: You are a product analytics lead designing a metrics system for a consumer mobile app with five million monthly active users. The app currently measures DAU, MAU, and revenue but lacks any behavioral metrics that explain why retention is declining. Your task is to design a behavioral metrics framework that connects user actions to retention and monetization outcomes. Start by mapping the user journey from first launch through habituated use, identifying the key actions at each stage. Define activation as the milestone after which a new user is twice as likely to return in the next thirty days, and use cohort analysis to identify what that milestone is for your app. Define engagement depth as a composite score of session frequency, session length, and feature breadth, and document the calculation. Design a funnel metric for each stage of the user lifecycle covering acquisition, activation, engagement, retention, and revenue. For each funnel metric, specify the numerator, denominator, time window, and segment dimensions needed to make the metric actionable. Identify the one behavioral metric most strongly correlated with twelve-month LTV based on available data. Design an anomaly detection rule set for each metric that triggers an alert when the metric moves more than two standard deviations from its rolling thirty-day average. Produce a metrics specification document with implementation notes for the engineering team.

title: You are a finance-analytics hybrid consultant helping a B2B SaaS company
prompt: You are a finance-analytics hybrid consultant helping a B2B SaaS company align its go-to-market metrics with its financial model. The company has a sales team, a marketing team, and a customer success team each tracking different metrics with no reconciliation process, causing recurring disagreements in the quarterly business review. Your goal is to design a unified GTM metrics framework that finance, sales, marketing, and customer success can all agree on. Begin by conducting a metrics audit where you collect every metric each team reports and identify the top five areas of definitional conflict, such as whether a trial counts as a lead and how churn is calculated. Facilitate a cross-functional working session to align on canonical definitions and assign a single system of record for each metric. Design the revenue waterfall model that tracks the flow from marketing-qualified lead through opportunity, closed-won, onboarded, retained, and expanded, with a conversion rate metric at each stage. Specify the cohort-based calculation for net revenue retention that accounts for expansion, contraction, and churn separately. Define the payback period calculation that finance and sales both accept and agree to report in board materials. Establish a quarterly metrics reconciliation process where each team signs off on a unified metrics snapshot before the QBR. Deliver a GTM metrics playbook with definitions, owners, data sources, and a sample dashboard layout.

title: Identify the biggest user experience unknowns to research
prompt: Based on the product and current data, identify the top 5 user behavior questions the team is making assumptions about without research. For each, recommend a research method — user interview, usability test, survey, or diary study.
prompt_short: Find the top user experience assumptions that need research to validate.
prompt_filtered: Research gaps only. Assumptions without user validation.

title: Audit existing user research for recency and coverage
prompt: Review all existing user research artifacts — interview transcripts, usability reports, survey results, analytics findings. Identify research older than 12 months that may be stale, and user segments that have never been researched.
prompt_short: Audit user research for stale findings and unresearched user segments.
prompt_filtered: Research inventory only. Recency and coverage assessment.

title: Design a usability test for the core user flow
prompt: Design a usability test protocol for the most critical user flow in the product. Include: the scenario prompt, specific tasks for the participant, success criteria, what to observe, and how many participants are needed for valid findings.
prompt_short: Design a usability test protocol for the most critical user flow.
prompt_filtered: Test design only. Protocol, tasks, and success criteria.

title: Find usability issues in the current onboarding flow
prompt: Analyze the user onboarding flow for usability issues — unclear steps, unnecessary friction, cognitive overload, missing context, confusing UI patterns. Identify the top 5 drop-off risks and recommend specific improvements for each.
prompt_short: Find usability issues in onboarding that cause drop-off or confusion.
prompt_filtered: Onboarding usability only. Friction, clarity, and cognitive load issues.

title: Turn support tickets into product improvement insights
prompt: Analyze recent support tickets and user feedback for patterns. Identify the top 10 recurring issues, categorize them by root cause (UX, bug, missing feature, documentation), and translate them into specific product improvement recommendations.
prompt_short: Extract product improvement insights from support tickets and feedback patterns.
prompt_filtered: Support-to-insight only. Recurring themes from user complaints.

title: Apply Jobs-to-be-Done framework to core user segments
prompt: For each primary user segment, articulate the job they are hiring this product to do — the progress they are trying to make, the context they are in, and the forces pulling them toward or away from this solution. Identify segments where the job-to-be-done is assumed but not validated.
prompt_short: Apply JTBD framework to each user segment and find unvalidated job assumptions.
prompt_filtered: JTBD only. Progress, context, and forces for each user segment.

title: Design a continuous discovery cadence for the product team
prompt: Propose a continuous discovery cadence — weekly user touchpoints that are lightweight enough to maintain without a dedicated researcher. Include: recruiting channels, interview script template, synthesis method, and how findings feed into the product backlog within the same week.
prompt_short: Design a weekly continuous discovery cadence for a team without a researcher.
prompt_filtered: Continuous discovery only. Lightweight weekly interview cadence.

title: Validate current user personas against recent behavioral data
prompt: Compare the documented user personas against current behavioral analytics. Identify assumptions in the persona — technology comfort, use frequency, primary goal — that behavioral data contradicts. Flag personas built on research older than 18 months that have not been revalidated.
prompt_short: Validate user personas against behavioral data and find outdated assumptions.
prompt_filtered: Persona validation only. Behavioral data vs documented persona assumptions.

title: Find the biggest assumption in the product bet and test it cheaply
prompt: Identify the single most critical and least-validated assumption in the next major product bet. Design the cheapest test that would validate or invalidate it — a landing page, a wizard-of-oz prototype, a survey, or a fake door test — before investing in full engineering.
prompt_short: Find the riskiest unvalidated assumption and design the cheapest test for it.
prompt_filtered: Riskiest assumption only. Cheapest validation method for one assumption.

title: Assess the product's NPS driver analysis
prompt: Analyze NPS survey verbatims for the past 6 months. Identify the top 5 themes driving promoters and the top 5 themes driving detractors. Find themes that appear in both promoter and detractor feedback but with different contexts. Recommend the product changes most likely to shift detractors to passives.
prompt_short: Analyze NPS verbatims to find the top promoter and detractor themes.
prompt_filtered: NPS drivers only. Promoter vs detractor theme analysis.

title: Design a guerrilla usability test executable in one day
prompt: Design a guerrilla usability test for the given feature — recruitible in a coffee shop or Slack community, completable in 15 minutes, with 5 participants. Define the task, the key observation points, the success/failure criteria, and how findings are synthesized within 24 hours of testing.
prompt_short: Design a 15-minute guerrilla usability test executable with 5 participants in a day.
prompt_filtered: Guerrilla testing only. Rapid unmoderated protocol for one feature.

title: Map customer journey pain points to product gaps
prompt: Create a pain point map aligned to the end-to-end customer journey. For each stage — discovery, onboarding, core usage, expansion, retention — list the top 3 pain points from research and note whether each has a corresponding product improvement in the roadmap or backlog.
prompt_short: Map customer journey pain points to see which have roadmap coverage.
prompt_filtered: Pain point mapping only. Journey stage vs roadmap gap alignment.

title: Identify which churned users to interview for retention insights
prompt: Design a churned user research protocol. Identify the churn cohort most informative for product improvement — recently churned users who were active before leaving. Segment them by churn reason if available and design a 30-minute exit interview guide focused on the moment they decided to leave.
prompt_short: Design a churned user interview protocol focused on the departure decision moment.
prompt_filtered: Churn interviews only. Cohort selection and exit interview design.

title: Evaluate survey design quality for existing user surveys
prompt: Audit existing user surveys for design quality — leading questions, double-barreled questions, response scale inconsistency, order effects, and lack of a neutral option. For each quality issue found, rewrite the question in a form that produces unbiased, comparable data.
prompt_short: Audit user surveys for leading questions, ambiguity, and scale inconsistency.
prompt_filtered: Survey quality only. Bias sources and rewritten question recommendations.

title: Find the research gap between quantitative and qualitative signals
prompt: Identify metrics or behavioral signals where the quantitative data is clear but the qualitative reason behind the pattern is unknown. These are the highest-value research opportunities — you know something is happening but not why, and research can directly inform the intervention.
prompt_short: Find metrics where you know what is happening but not why users behave that way.
prompt_filtered: Qual-quant gap only. Unexplained behavioral patterns needing research.

title: Design a diary study for extended use-case research
prompt: Design a diary study for a use case that unfolds over time and cannot be captured in a single interview or usability test. Define the participant criteria, daily prompt cadence, response format, and analysis approach for longitudinal patterns.
prompt_short: Design a diary study for a research question requiring longitudinal observation.
prompt_filtered: Diary study only. Prompt design and longitudinal analysis approach.

title: Assess research repository discoverability and team adoption
prompt: Evaluate the research repository — where insights are stored and how they are accessed. Identify whether team members can find relevant research before making product decisions, whether insights are tagged and searchable, and whether the repository has been consulted in the last 10 product decisions.
prompt_short: Assess research repository usability and whether the team actually consults it.
prompt_filtered: Repository only. Discoverability and utilization of past research.
