title: Map the full user acquisition funnel from awareness to activation
prompt: Map the full user acquisition funnel from awareness to activation. Identify the step with the highest drop-off rate. Trace whether the drop-off is a product issue — friction in the signup flow — or a targeting issue — users arriving who are not the right fit. Recommend the single highest-impact intervention to improve funnel conversion.

title: Audit the top three acquisition channels by volume and by quality — meas
prompt: Audit the top three acquisition channels by volume and by quality — measured by downstream retention at 30 days. Identify channels with high volume but low retention, indicating poor fit between acquired users and the product's value proposition. Recommend channel investment reallocation to favor quality over volume.

title: Find the top user cohort by retention rate
prompt: Find the top user cohort by retention rate. Identify what this cohort has in common — acquisition source, onboarding path, or initial feature adoption pattern — that distinguishes them from lower-retention cohorts. Recommend the growth strategy change that would acquire more users who match this high-retention cohort profile.

title: Analyze the acquisition funnel from first touch to paid conversion for t
prompt: Analyze the acquisition funnel from first touch to paid conversion for the past ninety days. Calculate the conversion rate at each stage: impression to click, click to signup, signup to activation, and activation to paid. Identify the stage with the largest absolute drop-off and the stage with the largest relative drop-off. Determine whether each drop-off is a volume problem, a quality problem, or a timing problem. Recommend the single highest-leverage intervention for the worst-performing stage.

title: Evaluate the cost efficiency of each acquisition channel using customer
prompt: Evaluate the cost efficiency of each acquisition channel using customer acquisition cost and first-year lifetime value data. Calculate the LTV-to-CAC ratio for each channel. Identify the channel with the highest ratio and assess whether it is capacity-constrained. Identify the channel with the worst ratio and determine whether the underperformance is due to poor lead quality, a broken funnel stage, or misaligned messaging. Recommend a budget reallocation that improves blended LTV-to-CAC by fifteen percent.

title: Design a referral program for a B2C subscription product
prompt: Design a referral program for a B2C subscription product. Define the referral incentive structure for both the referrer and the new user. Specify the viral coefficient target based on current user base size and growth rate. Design the in-product referral prompt that appears at the moment of highest user satisfaction as identified by behavioral data. Write the email sequence for referral follow-up. Define the fraud prevention rules to prevent referral abuse. Estimate the net subscriber gain from the program in the first quarter.

title: Map the SEO content strategy for the next quarter
prompt: Map the SEO content strategy for the next quarter. Identify the top ten informational queries your target users search before becoming aware of your product. For each query, assess current ranking position, estimated monthly search volume, and keyword difficulty. Prioritize the five queries where you have the greatest opportunity to gain or improve ranking within ninety days. Write a content brief for each prioritized query including the recommended structure, the target word count, and the internal linking strategy.

title: Assess the paid acquisition strategy for efficiency and scalability
prompt: Assess the paid acquisition strategy for efficiency and scalability. Review the creative performance data for the past thirty days and identify the three best-performing and three worst-performing ad creatives by cost per acquisition. Identify the pattern distinguishing high-performing from low-performing creatives in terms of message, format, and audience targeting. Recommend a creative testing roadmap for the next month. Evaluate whether the current bidding strategy is appropriate given campaign maturity and suggest adjustments.

title: Evaluate the growth strategy for user acquisition
prompt: Evaluate the growth strategy for user acquisition. Identify all active acquisition channels and measure each by volume, cost per acquisition, and 30-day retention rate. Find channels with a customer acquisition cost higher than the expected lifetime value of the users they acquire. Assess whether organic acquisition is growing, flat, or declining — and what the drivers are. Look for acquisition leaks — users who start the signup process but abandon before completing it. Identify the onboarding step with the highest abandonment rate and the primary reason users cite for leaving. Summarize the five growth strategy improvements that would most improve acquisition quality and cost efficiency.

title: Assess growth levers beyond top-of-funnel acquisition
prompt: Assess growth levers beyond top-of-funnel acquisition. Identify the current retention rate at 7, 30, and 90 days and benchmark against industry expectations for this product category. Find the features most correlated with high retention — the actions users take in the first week that predict whether they will still be active at 90 days. Assess whether referral or viral growth mechanisms are in place and whether they are performing. Look for the most impactful monetization gap — users who derive significant value from the product but are not on a paid plan. Recommend the growth investment allocation across acquisition, retention, monetization, and referral that would maximize compound growth rate.

title: You are a growth lead at a B2B SaaS company with a freemium model that h
prompt: You are a growth lead at a B2B SaaS company with a freemium model that has successfully acquired thirty thousand free users but is converting them to paid at only one point two percent, well below the industry benchmark of two to four percent for comparable products. The leadership team is investing heavily in new feature development to improve the conversion rate, but you hypothesize that the problem is not product functionality but rather that free users are not experiencing the core value proposition before their trial expires. Your task is to design a growth program that improves free-to-paid conversion to at least two point five percent within two quarters without requiring significant engineering investment. Begin by conducting a cohort analysis comparing the behavior of users who converted to paid versus users who churned from the free tier, identifying the specific in-product actions, sequences, and timing patterns that predict conversion. Define the activation event, the single action or combination of actions that when completed makes a user significantly more likely to convert within thirty days. Map the onboarding flow to identify where most users drop off before reaching the activation event. Design a series of lightweight experiments targeting the three biggest drop-off points in the path to activation, ranging from improved in-app messaging to targeted email sequences to a guided checklist that surfaces key features. Define the experiment framework including success criteria, minimum sample size, and the decision rule for scaling or stopping each experiment. Design the in-app upgrade prompt strategy that appears at the moment users encounter a paywall feature they want to use, using contextual messaging that references the specific feature they are trying to access. Develop the email nurture sequence for free users who have not reached activation within seven days, personalized to their usage pattern. Deliver the growth program plan with a two-quarter experiment calendar and the conversion rate milestones you will hit at thirty, sixty, and ninety days.

title: You are a head of growth building the first structured growth function a
prompt: You are a head of growth building the first structured growth function at a direct-to-consumer subscription brand that has relied entirely on paid social for customer acquisition. The company has achieved five million dollars in annual recurring revenue but is experiencing rising customer acquisition costs as the paid social channel saturates and iOS privacy changes reduce targeting accuracy. The founder wants to diversify acquisition to at least three channels with meaningful contribution within twelve months. Your task is to design the channel diversification strategy and the operational model for testing and scaling new channels. Begin by conducting a thorough audit of the current paid social performance, including CAC trends over the past eighteen months, creative fatigue indicators, audience saturation estimates, and the impact of privacy changes on attribution accuracy. Evaluate four candidate channels for diversification: search engine optimization, influencer and creator partnerships, email acquisition through lead magnets, and a referral program, using a channel scoring rubric that assesses market fit, competitive intensity, time to first meaningful contribution, and capital efficiency. Select the two channels with the highest scores and design the test-and-learn plan for each, specifying the team structure, the budget allocation, the success metric, and the minimum performance threshold that would trigger a scale decision. Design the attribution model for a multi-channel environment where the last-click model currently in use will systematically undervalue upper-funnel channels like content and influencer. Specify the unit economics model that allows you to compare CAC and payback period across channels on an apples-to-apples basis even when channels operate on different conversion timelines. Deliver the channel diversification strategy, the test-and-learn calendar, and the updated attribution framework.

title: You are a growth analytics lead building the measurement infrastructure
prompt: You are a growth analytics lead building the measurement infrastructure for a marketplace that connects freelance professionals with clients. The marketplace currently tracks signups, listings posted, and gross merchandise volume but has no visibility into the engagement behaviors that predict whether a new supply-side or demand-side user will become a high-value repeat participant. The growth team is making channel investment decisions based on signup volume, which is a poor proxy for quality, and the company is spending significant acquisition budget on users who complete one transaction and never return. Your task is to design the measurement framework that allows the growth team to optimize for user quality rather than volume. Begin by defining the engagement quality indicators for both supply-side and demand-side users that predict long-term marketplace participation, using historical cohort data to identify which early behaviors are most strongly correlated with twelve-month GMV contribution. Design the user quality score, a composite metric that the growth team can use to compare the quality of users acquired from different channels within the first thirty days of signup, before twelve-month data is available. Build the channel quality dashboard that shows, for each acquisition channel, the distribution of user quality scores, the median time to first transaction, the repeat transaction rate at ninety days, and the CAC adjusted for user quality. Design the feedback loop that routes user quality data back into paid acquisition targeting, specifying how the quality score is used as a custom audience signal in each advertising platform. Define the experiment framework for testing whether optimizing for quality score in acquisition campaigns reduces GMV per dollar spent or increases it, including the holdout design and the minimum test duration. Deliver the measurement framework specification, the user quality score formula, and the channel quality dashboard design.

title: Design an A/B test for this product hypothesis
prompt: Given the hypothesis to test, design a statistically valid A/B experiment. Define: the control and treatment, the primary metric, minimum detectable effect, required sample size, run duration, and the decision criteria for declaring a winner.
prompt_short: Design a statistically valid A/B test for this product hypothesis.
prompt_filtered: Experiment design only. Hypothesis, metric, sample size, and duration.

title: Audit the experiment infrastructure for validity threats
prompt: Review the experimentation setup for common validity threats — novelty effects, Hawthorne effects, incorrect unit of randomization, sample ratio mismatch, interaction effects between concurrent experiments. Identify threats present in the current setup.
prompt_short: Find validity threats in the current experiment infrastructure.
prompt_filtered: Validity threats only. Bias and confound identification.

title: Identify which product decisions should be tested not shipped
prompt: Review recent and upcoming product decisions. Identify changes significant enough that the outcome is uncertain and worth testing — changes to pricing, onboarding, core UX, viral mechanics. Distinguish from changes so low-risk they should just ship.
prompt_short: Find upcoming changes uncertain enough to require A/B testing first.
prompt_filtered: Test vs ship decisions only. Uncertainty-based prioritization.

title: Analyze an A/B test result for statistical validity
prompt: Review the provided experiment results. Check for: sufficient sample size, correct statistical test, p-value interpretation, practical vs statistical significance, segmentation of results by user cohort, and whether the run duration was sufficient to avoid novelty effects.
prompt_short: Validate this A/B test result for statistical correctness and practical significance.
prompt_filtered: Result validity only. Statistical and practical significance assessment.

title: Create an experiment backlog from the product roadmap
prompt: Review the product roadmap and identify the 10 most uncertain decisions that should be validated with experiments before full build. For each, draft a one-paragraph experiment brief with hypothesis, metric, and success criteria.
prompt_short: Identify the 10 roadmap items that should be experiments before full build.
prompt_filtered: Experiment backlog only. Roadmap items with high uncertainty.

title: Calculate the minimum detectable effect for this experiment
prompt: Calculate the minimum detectable effect for a proposed experiment given: the baseline metric value, the acceptable false positive rate (alpha), the desired statistical power (1-beta), and the expected sample size available. Determine whether the experiment is feasible or whether the detectable effect size exceeds what would be practically significant.
prompt_short: Calculate MDE for this experiment and assess feasibility given available sample.
prompt_filtered: MDE calculation only. Sample size, power, and practical significance assessment.

title: Design a multi-armed bandit alternative to A/B testing
prompt: Propose a multi-armed bandit approach for a case where A/B testing is suboptimal — limited sample, high cost of sending traffic to losing variants, or short decision horizon. Define the exploration-exploitation tradeoff appropriate for the context, the stopping criteria, and the situations where A/B remains preferable.
prompt_short: Design a multi-armed bandit test for a case where A/B testing is suboptimal.
prompt_filtered: Bandit testing only. Exploration-exploitation design and stopping criteria.

title: Audit the experiment holdout strategy for long-term effects
prompt: Assess whether experiments use holdout groups to detect long-term effects that do not appear in short experiment windows. Identify experiments shipped as winners based on 14-day results where 90-day holdout data shows metric decay. Recommend a holdout strategy for experiments with potentially novel effects.
prompt_short: Find experiments shipped as winners whose 90-day holdout data shows metric decay.
prompt_filtered: Holdout strategy only. Long-term effect measurement beyond the experiment window.

title: Find the novelty effect in this experiment result
prompt: Assess whether the experiment result contains a novelty effect — inflated metrics from users engaging with something new rather than genuinely preferring it. Analyze metric trajectory within the experiment window: if the treatment advantage exists on day 1 but fades by day 14, the result is likely a novelty effect. Recommend the minimum run duration to detect post-novelty steady state.
prompt_short: Detect if the experiment win is a novelty effect by analyzing metric trajectory.
prompt_filtered: Novelty effect only. Metric trajectory analysis for fading treatment advantage.

title: Design an experiment for a pricing page change
prompt: Design a statistically valid experiment for a pricing page change. Define: the randomization unit (visitor vs account), the primary metric (trial start rate or immediate conversion), the guard-rail metrics (revenue per visitor, plan mix), the minimum run duration to avoid weekly traffic patterns, and the decision criteria distinguishing statistical from practical significance.
prompt_short: Design a pricing page experiment with randomization, metrics, and decision criteria.
prompt_filtered: Pricing experiment only. Randomization, primary metric, guard-rails, and duration.

title: Assess the sample ratio mismatch in this experiment
prompt: Check the experiment for sample ratio mismatch — whether the actual traffic split between control and treatment matches the configured split. Run a chi-square test on observed vs expected sample sizes. If SRM is detected, the experiment results are invalid. Identify common SRM causes: bot traffic, implementation bugs, or user self-selection into variants.
prompt_short: Check this experiment for sample ratio mismatch and identify the cause if found.
prompt_filtered: SRM check only. Observed vs expected split test and cause identification.

title: Build an experiment learning repository
prompt: Design an experiment learning repository that captures: hypothesis, result (win/loss/inconclusive), effect size, segment breakdowns, and the insight for future experiments. Ensure past negative results are searchable — preventing teams from re-running experiments that already failed. Include a tagging system by product area and hypothesis type.
prompt_short: Design an experiment repository that makes past wins, losses, and insights searchable.
prompt_filtered: Experiment repository only. Learning capture, tagging, and negative result storage.

title: Identify interaction effects between concurrent experiments
prompt: Assess whether multiple concurrent experiments on the same user population interact. Identify experiment pairs where the treatment of one changes user behavior in the measurement surface of the other. Recommend the mutual exclusion or layer partitioning strategy for these conflicts.
prompt_short: Find concurrent experiments that interact and recommend isolation strategy.
prompt_filtered: Experiment interaction only. Concurrent experiment conflicts and partitioning.

title: Design an instrumentation plan for the experiment primary metric
prompt: Before running the experiment, create an instrumentation plan. Define the exact event name, properties, and trigger conditions for the primary metric. Verify the event fires correctly in control and treatment. Add a sanity check that the metric is stable in the pre-experiment period (AA test). Document how the metric will be calculated in the analysis tool.
prompt_short: Create an instrumentation plan and AA test for the experiment primary metric.
prompt_filtered: Instrumentation plan only. Event definition, AA test, and analysis tool setup.

title: Run a retrospective on the last 5 experiment decisions
prompt: Retrospect on the last 5 experiment decisions. For each: was the decision made on statistical significance alone or did it also assess practical significance? Was the decision made before the run duration elapsed (peeking)? Was segment analysis conducted before shipping? Were guard-rail metrics checked? Identify the decision process error most frequently repeated.
prompt_short: Retrospect the last 5 experiment decisions for peeking, practical significance, and guard-rails.
prompt_filtered: Decision quality only. Process errors in the last 5 experiment decisions.

title: Find the highest-value unrun experiment in the product
prompt: Survey the product for high-uncertainty high-impact decisions that have been shipped without testing. Identify the one decision whose outcome is most uncertain, whose impact on the primary business metric is highest, and which could be tested with a low-complexity experiment. This is the highest-value unrun experiment to prioritize now.
prompt_short: Find the highest-value product decision shipped without testing.
prompt_filtered: Priority experiment only. Highest-impact untested decision in the product.

title: Design an experiment governance process for the team
prompt: Design an experiment governance process. Include: a review step before experiment launch (hypothesis clarity, instrumentation check, sample size calculation), a concurrent experiment conflict check, a result review before shipping (practical significance, segment analysis, guard-rail review), and an escalation step for experiments with ambiguous results.
prompt_short: Design an experiment governance process from pre-launch to shipping decision.
prompt_filtered: Experiment governance only. Pre-launch review, conflict check, and result review.

title: Fill in Missing Data Gaps
prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

title: Gain full understanding before completing
prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

title: Find What's Broken
prompt: Find What's Broken

title: Recommend Next Steps
prompt: Recommend Next Steps

title: Score for Production Readiness
prompt: Score for Production Readiness

title: Audit Experiment Validity
prompt: Audit Experiment Validity

title: Design Statistically Valid Test
prompt: Design Statistically Valid Test

title: Write Falsifiable Hypothesis
prompt: Write Falsifiable Hypothesis

title: Calculate Minimum Sample Size
prompt: Calculate Minimum Sample Size

title: Identify Interaction Effects
prompt: Identify Interaction Effects

title: Define Primary Metric
prompt: Define Primary Metric

title: Design Holdout Group
prompt: Design Holdout Group

title: Audit the experimentation program for the past quarter
prompt: Audit the experimentation program for the past quarter. Identify the count of experiments run, the share that produced statistically valid results, and the share that influenced a product decision. Find experiments that ran without sufficient sample size, ran too short to escape novelty effects, or had a sample ratio mismatch indicating randomization broke. Recommend the experimentation infrastructure investment that would most improve result reliability.

title: Review the upcoming roadmap and separate decisions into ship-without-tes
prompt: Review the upcoming roadmap and separate decisions into ship-without-test versus test-first categories. Identify the criteria that should drive each classification — outcome uncertainty, change reversibility, blast radius, and prior evidence. Find roadmap items currently planned for direct ship that carry enough uncertainty to justify experimentation. Recommend the experiment-first prioritization for the next quarter.

title: Examine recent experiment results that were declared winners and shipped
prompt: Examine recent experiment results that were declared winners and shipped. Identify whether each result reflected practical significance — meaningful effect size in business terms — or only statistical significance from a large sample. Find shipped experiments where post-launch metrics did not reflect the experiment's predicted lift. Recommend the result-interpretation discipline that would prevent overshipping marginal wins.

title: Design an A/B test for a proposed change to the pricing page
prompt: Design an A/B test for a proposed change to the pricing page. Write the hypothesis in an if-then-because format. Define the primary metric and two guardrail metrics. Calculate the required sample size based on the current baseline conversion rate, the minimum detectable effect, and the desired statistical power of eighty percent at a five percent significance level. Specify the randomization unit, the traffic allocation, and the minimum test duration accounting for weekly seasonality.

title: Evaluate the statistical validity of the last five experiments run by th
prompt: Evaluate the statistical validity of the last five experiments run by the team. For each experiment, check whether the sample size was calculated before the test began, whether the test ran for a full business cycle, whether novelty effects were accounted for, and whether multiple comparison corrections were applied if more than one metric was tested. Flag experiments with validity concerns. Recommend a pre-registration process that prevents the most common validity issues.

title: Design an experiment prioritization framework for a team running three t
prompt: Design an experiment prioritization framework for a team running three to five experiments simultaneously. Define the scoring rubric that weighs probability of success, impact if successful, implementation effort, and learning value for failed experiments. Apply the rubric to rank the current backlog of ten proposed experiments. Identify the top three experiments to run in the next sprint. Specify how the team will handle experiments that have conflicting effects on the same user population.

title: Write the experiment results report template for a recently concluded A/
prompt: Write the experiment results report template for a recently concluded A/B test. The template should cover the hypothesis, the test design summary, the primary metric result with confidence interval, the guardrail metric results, the segmented results by device type and user cohort, the statistical significance verdict, the decision with rationale, and the next steps. Include guidance on how to communicate a null result as a learning rather than a failure.

title: Assess the experimentation culture maturity of the team
prompt: Assess the experimentation culture maturity of the team. Evaluate five dimensions: the frequency of experiments run per quarter, the quality of hypothesis documentation, the statistical validity of experiment designs, the proportion of experiments that result in a documented learning regardless of outcome, and the speed from idea to experiment launch. Score each dimension from one to five. Identify the two lowest-scoring dimensions and recommend one process improvement for each.

title: Evaluate the experimentation program end to end
prompt: Evaluate the experimentation program end to end. Assess the experiment intake process — whether ideas are prioritized by expected value and uncertainty, or by the loudest stakeholder. Review the experiment design quality — whether hypotheses are written with measurable predictions, whether primary metrics are defined before launch, and whether minimum detectable effects are calculated against historical variance. Find the share of experiments that completed with valid results versus those abandoned mid-flight or invalidated by infrastructure issues. Look for evidence of p-hacking, segment fishing, or selective reporting of post-hoc winning slices. Assess whether experiment results are documented in a searchable archive that prevents teams from re-running settled questions. Summarize the five experimentation program improvements that would most increase decision quality.

title: Audit the experimentation infrastructure for validity threats
prompt: Audit the experimentation infrastructure for validity threats. Identify whether the unit of randomization matches the unit of analysis — user-level randomization for user-level metrics, session-level for session metrics. Find evidence of sample ratio mismatch in recent experiments indicating broken randomization or assignment leakage. Assess whether concurrent experiments interact — whether experiment A's treatment changes the baseline for experiment B running on overlapping users. Look for novelty effects shortening run durations and survivorship bias in segments analyzed only post-launch. Examine whether the platform supports holdout groups, sequential testing with corrected boundaries, and pre-registered analysis plans. Recommend the infrastructure changes that would most reduce false positive rate and increase trust in experiment results.

title: You are a head of experimentation building a structured A/B testing prog
prompt: You are a head of experimentation building a structured A/B testing program at a company that currently runs experiments ad hoc, without a formal hypothesis process, without statistical power calculations, and without a consistent process for acting on results. The team has run forty-three experiments in the past year but has documented learnings from fewer than ten, and several shipped features were based on experiments that were later identified as statistically invalid. Your mandate is to design an experimentation program that produces reliable insights and builds organizational trust in data-driven decision-making within two quarters. Begin by auditing the past year of experiments, re-analyzing the five most consequential ones to determine whether the conclusions were statistically sound, and publishing a transparency report that acknowledges where decisions were made on invalid data. Design the hypothesis documentation standard requiring a structured format covering the observation that motivated the experiment, the proposed change, the expected mechanism of action, the primary metric, the minimum detectable effect the team considers meaningful, and the guardrail metrics that must not be harmed. Build the sample size and duration calculator as a shared tool that automatically accounts for the current baseline conversion rate, the desired statistical power, the significance threshold, and weekly traffic patterns to surface whether a proposed test can reach significance within the planned window. Design the experiment review gate that requires every experiment to pass a statistical design review before launch, and specify who is authorized to approve experiments by risk tier. Create the results communication template that standardizes how experiment results are presented to stakeholders, distinguishing between statistical significance and practical significance and clearly stating the recommended decision. Design the experiment retrospective that runs quarterly to synthesize learnings across all completed experiments and identify the hypotheses that appeared most frequently in winning and losing experiments. Deliver the experimentation program playbook including all templates, the governance model, and a twelve-week launch timeline.

title: You are a data scientist designing an experimentation framework for a ma
prompt: You are a data scientist designing an experimentation framework for a marketplace platform where the unit of randomization creates significant complexity. The platform connects buyers and sellers, and many features affect both sides of the market simultaneously, making simple user-level randomization invalid because of network interference: a seller who is in the treatment group interacts with buyers in the control group, contaminating the control. Your task is to design an experimentation framework that produces reliable causal estimates for features that affect market dynamics. Begin by classifying the feature backlog by interference risk, distinguishing features that affect only one side of the market with no cross-side spillover from features where treatment effects propagate across the marketplace. For low-interference features, document the standard user-level randomization procedure, including the randomization unit selection, the hash function used for assignment, and the logging architecture required for exposure tracking. For high-interference features, design the cluster-based randomization approach using geographic markets or seller categories as the cluster unit, and document the trade-offs in statistical power and minimum detectable effect relative to user-level randomization. Design the switchback experiment methodology for marketplace pricing experiments where the treatment must be applied globally to get clean measurements, using time-based rather than user-based randomization with carryover effect correction. Specify the experiment logging architecture that tracks exposure at the assignment unit level, the conversion event at the user level, and the cross-unit spillover indicators that allow post-hoc contamination analysis. Design the analysis pipeline including the randomization check, the CUPED variance reduction technique, and the delta method for ratio metrics. Deliver the experimentation framework specification as a technical reference document for the data engineering and analytics teams.

title: You are a principal product analyst designing the experimentation govern
prompt: You are a principal product analyst designing the experimentation governance model for a company with fifteen product teams each running their own experiments independently, resulting in uncoordinated tests that frequently target the same user segments simultaneously, creating mutual interference and producing unreliable results. The company runs an average of thirty simultaneous experiments at any given time, and the rate of interaction effects between experiments has made it difficult to trust any individual experiment result. Your task is to design the governance model that maintains team autonomy while preventing experiment collisions from undermining data quality. Begin by mapping the experiment collision landscape by analyzing the past quarter of experiments to identify which user segments were targeted by more than three simultaneous experiments, the correlation between overlap rate and variance in experiment results, and the domains where collision risk is highest due to high experiment frequency and shared user populations. Design the experiment registry that every team must use to register an experiment before launching, including the target segment definition, the primary metric, the expected start and end date, and any known conflicts with other running experiments. Design the collision detection algorithm that automatically flags when a newly registered experiment overlaps with a running experiment on the same user segment and primary metric. Specify the governance process for resolving flagged collisions, including whether teams can negotiate to stagger their launches, whether one experiment takes priority based on business criticality, or whether a mutual exclusion group is required. Design the mutual exclusion group management system that allows teams running related experiments to guarantee non-overlap without requiring manual coordination. Define the experiment scheduling calendar that visualizes all running and upcoming experiments in a timeline view. Establish the experimentation council, a cross-team body that meets bi-weekly to review collision reports, adjudicate priority disputes, and maintain the health of the overall experimentation portfolio. Deliver the governance model specification including the registry design, the collision detection rules, and the council charter.

title: Identify emerging technologies worth a prototype spike
prompt: Assess emerging technologies relevant to this product's domain. Identify 3 technologies worth a time-boxed prototype spike — where the potential uplift is significant and the prototype cost is low. Define the spike scope, success criteria, and go/no-go decision point.
prompt_short: Find 3 emerging technologies worth a time-boxed prototype spike.
prompt_filtered: Technology candidates only. Potential value vs spike cost.

title: Design a research spike for an unresolved technical question
prompt: For the given technical uncertainty, design a time-boxed research spike. Define: what questions need to be answered, what artifacts the spike produces, the maximum time investment, and the decision framework for applying findings vs abandoning the approach.
prompt_short: Design a time-boxed spike to resolve a technical uncertainty.
prompt_filtered: Spike design only. Questions, artifacts, time box, and decision criteria.

title: Assess technical feasibility of this speculative feature
prompt: Evaluate the technical feasibility of the proposed feature. Identify the biggest unknowns, the most likely technical blockers, the approximate implementation complexity, and what would need to change in the existing architecture to support it.
prompt_short: Assess technical feasibility and identify the biggest unknowns for this feature.
prompt_filtered: Feasibility only. Blockers, complexity, and architecture changes needed.

title: Identify where AI could add real value in this product
prompt: Survey the product for high-value AI integration opportunities — not for novelty, but where AI would measurably improve the user experience or reduce manual work. Rank by user value impact vs implementation complexity. Flag opportunities with existing APIs vs those requiring model training.
prompt_short: Find where AI integration would add measurable value in this product.
prompt_filtered: AI opportunities only. Value vs complexity ranking.

title: Review R&D output for production application readiness
prompt: Assess the current R&D work for production readiness. Identify prototypes that are ready to productionize, those that need more validation, and those that should be archived. For production-ready items, list the gaps — tests, error handling, monitoring, documentation — before shipping.
prompt_short: Assess R&D prototypes for production readiness and identify gaps.
prompt_filtered: Production readiness only. Gap list for prototypes ready to ship.

title: Design a discovery sprint for an uncertain product area
prompt: Design a two-week discovery sprint for a product area with high uncertainty. Define: the questions to answer, the methods to use (user interviews, technical spike, competitive teardown, prototype test), the daily schedule, the artifacts produced, and the decision framework — go, no-go, or further research — at the sprint end.
prompt_short: Design a two-week discovery sprint with methods, schedule, and go/no-go criteria.
prompt_filtered: Discovery sprint only. Question framing, methods, and end-of-sprint decision.

title: Build a technical debt triage framework for the engineering backlog
prompt: Design a technical debt triage framework. Classify each debt item by: impact on development velocity (high/medium/low), risk of system failure (critical/moderate/low), and remediation cost (days). Prioritize by the ratio of velocity impact to remediation cost. Find the debt items paying for themselves in velocity improvement within one quarter of resolution.
prompt_short: Triage the technical debt backlog by velocity impact and remediation cost ratio.
prompt_filtered: Debt triage only. Velocity impact, failure risk, and payback period classification.

title: Assess the innovation pipeline for stage-gate distribution
prompt: Review the R&D innovation pipeline. Classify each initiative into: Explore (high uncertainty, early hypothesis), Validate (hypothesis tested, building evidence), Productize (validated, moving to production). Find a pipeline skewed to one stage and recommend the portfolio rebalancing.
prompt_short: Classify the R&D pipeline by stage and find skew toward explore, validate, or productize.
prompt_filtered: Innovation pipeline only. Stage distribution and portfolio rebalancing.

title: Identify research that produced no actionable output
prompt: Audit R&D work from the past 12 months. Identify spikes, prototypes, and research projects that were completed but produced no decision, product change, or documented learning. Find the common patterns — scope too broad, no owner, no defined question — and recommend the spike design standards that prevent unactionable output.
prompt_short: Find R&D projects completed in the past year that produced no actionable output.
prompt_filtered: Output audit only. R&D with no decision, change, or documented learning.

title: Design a 20% time program that produces measurable output
prompt: Design a structured 20% time program for engineers. Include: how time is allocated (one day per week vs a dedicated week per month), what qualifies as R&D use, the output required (demo, written summary, or PR), how discoveries are shared with the team, and how the program is evaluated for its contribution to the innovation pipeline.
prompt_short: Design a structured 20% time program with output requirements and evaluation criteria.
prompt_filtered: 20% time design only. Allocation, output requirements, and pipeline contribution.

title: Evaluate the build vs buy decision framework for new capabilities
prompt: Design a build vs buy decision framework for new technical capabilities. Include: the decision criteria (strategic differentiation, time to value, long-term cost, vendor lock-in, talent availability), a scoring template, and the escalation threshold. Apply the framework to the top 3 current build vs buy decisions.
prompt_short: Design a build vs buy framework and apply it to the top 3 current decisions.
prompt_filtered: Build vs buy only. Decision criteria and scoring for new technical capabilities.

title: Find where AI investment is creating technical debt faster than value
prompt: Audit all AI integrations in the product for net value. Identify integrations where maintenance cost — prompt engineering, model version updates, fallback handling, output validation — is increasing faster than user value. Find AI features with high operational cost and low user adoption. Recommend continue, simplify, or replace with deterministic logic per case.
prompt_short: Audit AI integrations where maintenance cost exceeds user value delivered.
prompt_filtered: AI debt only. High-maintenance AI features with low adoption.

title: Design a research-to-roadmap handoff process
prompt: Design the handoff process from validated R&D prototype to product roadmap inclusion. Include: the evidence required for roadmap consideration, the artifact format (prototype plus documented assumptions plus user validation summary), the review step with the PM, and the timeline expectation from validated prototype to first sprint on the roadmap.
prompt_short: Design the R&D-to-roadmap handoff with required evidence and review process.
prompt_filtered: Handoff process only. Evidence standard and PM review for roadmap inclusion.

title: Assess internal tooling investment against commercial alternative cost
prompt: Audit all internal tooling built by the team. For each tool, estimate the annual maintenance cost in engineer-hours and compare against the cost of a commercial alternative. Identify tools where commercial alternatives would free significant engineering capacity. Recommend whether to migrate, maintain, or open-source each tool.
prompt_short: Audit internal tools against commercial alternatives by maintenance cost comparison.
prompt_filtered: Internal tooling only. Maintenance cost vs commercial alternative comparison.

title: Design a failure-safe prototype evaluation process
prompt: Create a prototype evaluation protocol that treats negative results as equal successes to positive ones. Include: the questions the prototype must answer, the evaluation criteria, the stakeholder review format, the archive procedure for invalidated approaches, and the team recognition for clean kills.
prompt_short: Design a prototype evaluation process that celebrates negative results equally.
prompt_filtered: Prototype evaluation only. Negative result documentation and clean kill recognition.

title: Find the highest-technical-risk item in the current roadmap
prompt: Assess every committed roadmap item for technical risk — novel technology, integration with unreliable external systems, performance requirements at untested scale, or dependencies on architectural changes not yet complete. Identify the highest-risk item and recommend a time-boxed technical spike to de-risk it before the sprint it is committed to begins.
prompt_short: Find the highest technical risk committed roadmap item and design a de-risk spike.
prompt_filtered: Technical risk only. Highest-risk committed item and de-risk spike design.

title: Evaluate the R&D team's knowledge of the domain frontier
prompt: Assess whether the R&D team has current knowledge of the state of the art in the product's technical domain. Identify areas where the team's knowledge is more than 12 months behind the current literature or practitioner community. Recommend the learning investments — paper reading groups, conference attendance, external advisors — that would most close the knowledge gap.
prompt_short: Assess whether the R&D team's domain knowledge is current vs 12 months stale.
prompt_filtered: Domain knowledge only. State-of-art gap and learning investment recommendations.

title: Fill in Missing Data Gaps
prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

title: Gain full understanding before completing
prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

title: Find What's Broken
prompt: Find What's Broken

title: Recommend Next Steps
prompt: Recommend Next Steps

title: Score for Production Readiness
prompt: Score for Production Readiness

title: Find Prototype Spike Opportunities
prompt: Find Prototype Spike Opportunities

title: Assess R&D Production Readiness
prompt: Assess R&D Production Readiness

title: Identify Research Dead Ends Early
prompt: Identify Research Dead Ends Early

title: Map Exploratory to Applied Research
prompt: Map Exploratory to Applied Research

title: Benchmark Against Published State of Art
prompt: Benchmark Against Published State of Art

title: Design Minimum Viable Prototype
prompt: Design Minimum Viable Prototype

title: Assess Productionization Feasibility
prompt: Assess Productionization Feasibility

title: Survey the emerging technologies relevant to this product domain over th
prompt: Survey the emerging technologies relevant to this product domain over the past twelve months. Identify three candidates worth a time-boxed prototype spike, ranked by potential value uplift versus prototype cost. For each, define the spike scope, the success criteria that would justify productionization, and the abandon criteria that would close the spike cleanly. Recommend the spike sequencing for the next quarter.

title: Audit the current R&D portfolio for stalled prototypes, productionizatio
prompt: Audit the current R&D portfolio for stalled prototypes, productionization candidates, and archive candidates. Identify prototypes that have demonstrated their hypothesis but lack a productionization owner. Find prototypes still in flight beyond their original time box without a clear extend or abandon decision. Recommend the portfolio cleanup actions and the productionization handoff process for the validated work.

title: Examine the AI integration opportunities across the product surface area
prompt: Examine the AI integration opportunities across the product surface area. Rank candidate integrations by user value impact and implementation complexity. Distinguish opportunities served by existing inference APIs from those requiring fine-tuning or custom model training. Find the single highest-value AI integration that could be prototyped within a two-week spike and recommend the spike design.

title: Conduct a technology feasibility assessment for a proposed AI feature
prompt: Conduct a technology feasibility assessment for a proposed AI feature. Define the specific capability the feature requires and identify whether the capability is achievable with current state-of-the-art models, requires fine-tuning, or requires novel research. Estimate the computational cost at the target inference volume. Identify the top two technical risks that could prevent the feature from reaching production quality. Recommend the lightest-weight proof of concept that would de-risk both technical risks within four weeks.

title: Review the research portfolio for balance across three horizons: applied
prompt: Review the research portfolio for balance across three horizons: applied research directly feeding the product roadmap within six months, exploratory research with a twelve to twenty-four month horizon, and foundational research with a multi-year horizon. Calculate the percentage of research budget and headcount allocated to each horizon. Benchmark this allocation against the recommended split for a company at your growth stage. Identify the horizon that is most under-invested and propose one initiative to address the gap.

title: Design a knowledge transfer process for moving a research breakthrough f
prompt: Design a knowledge transfer process for moving a research breakthrough from the research team to the engineering team for productionization. Define the artifacts the research team must produce before handoff including a technical specification, a reproducibility package with training code and evaluation benchmarks, a performance characterization across input distributions, and a known failure modes documentation. Specify the joint research-engineering review session agenda. Define the productionization criteria that the feature must meet before it qualifies for the handoff.

title: Evaluate three external research papers relevant to a current R&D initia
prompt: Evaluate three external research papers relevant to a current R&D initiative. For each paper, summarize the key contribution in two sentences, assess the quality of the experimental methodology, identify the limitations the authors acknowledge, and evaluate whether the results are likely to replicate in your domain given differences in data distribution or task formulation. Recommend which paper's approach is most worth implementing as a proof of concept and justify the choice.

title: Design the R&D sprint planning process for a research team working on a
prompt: Design the R&D sprint planning process for a research team working on a six-month applied research project. Define how research sprints differ from engineering sprints in terms of goal definition, deliverable specification, and success criteria. Create a research sprint template that covers the hypothesis being tested, the experimental design, the evaluation metric, the expected completion date, and the decision criteria for proceeding, pivoting, or stopping. Specify the research sprint review format and how findings are documented for future reference.

title: Evaluate the R&D function as a portfolio
prompt: Evaluate the R&D function as a portfolio. Assess whether R&D investment is allocated across exploratory spikes — testing genuinely uncertain hypotheses — versus dressed-up incremental engineering work. Identify the share of R&D output from the past year that influenced product direction, made it to production, or generated reusable knowledge. Find recurring patterns in failed spikes — whether they failed due to legitimate hypothesis invalidation or due to insufficient time, wrong skills, or unclear success criteria. Look for spikes that ran past their time box without a kill decision and absorbed disproportionate engineering capacity. Assess whether R&D findings — both positive and negative — are documented in a way that prevents teams from re-running settled questions. Summarize the five R&D portfolio improvements that would most increase research return on investment.

title: Audit the productionization pipeline from prototype to production
prompt: Audit the productionization pipeline from prototype to production. Identify the prototypes currently in the validated-but-not-shipped state and the gaps preventing each from shipping — test coverage, monitoring, error handling, performance hardening, security review, or documentation. Find the average time from validated prototype to production launch and benchmark against expected throughput. Assess whether productionization is owned by the original prototype team or handed off, and whether handoff fidelity is a quality bottleneck. Look for prototypes that shipped without sufficient hardening and generated post-launch incidents. Examine whether the production-readiness checklist is enforced consistently across teams. Recommend the productionization process changes that would most reduce time-to-production while maintaining reliability standards.

title: You are a research engineering lead designing the applied research progr
prompt: You are a research engineering lead designing the applied research program for an AI company whose models are approaching the performance ceiling achievable with current architecture and training approaches. The product team is requesting capability improvements that the research team believes cannot be achieved through straightforward scaling or prompt engineering improvements. Your task is to design a six-month applied research program that systematically explores the most promising approaches to capability improvement, produces actionable recommendations for the engineering team, and operates within the constraint of six research engineers with access to limited GPU compute. Begin by conducting a capability gap analysis that maps the specific failure modes customers encounter most frequently to the underlying model weaknesses causing those failures, distinguishing between failures due to insufficient knowledge, failures due to reasoning limitations, failures due to instruction following errors, and failures due to factual hallucination. Prioritize the top three capability gaps by the frequency of customer impact and the estimated user experience improvement if resolved. For each prioritized gap, survey the recent literature to identify the top three approaches showing the most promise, and evaluate each approach on the dimensions of implementation complexity, compute requirements, and compatibility with the current training pipeline. Design the experimental protocol for each approach, including the minimum viable evaluation benchmark that would distinguish a meaningful improvement from noise, the compute budget per experiment, and the decision criteria for advancing a promising approach to a full training run. Specify the knowledge transfer artifacts the research team will produce for each successful approach, including a training configuration reference implementation, an evaluation suite, and a productionization checklist. Design the research program governance including the weekly research sync format, the go/no-go criteria for advancing experiments, and the monthly steering committee review. Deliver the six-month program plan with a milestone schedule and a resource allocation model.

title: You are a director of research and development at a biotech software com
prompt: You are a director of research and development at a biotech software company building computational tools for drug discovery. The R&D team has a track record of producing technically impressive research that rarely gets adopted by the product team because the research outputs are not in a form that engineering teams can integrate, and the research priorities are not aligned with the product roadmap. The result is a growing library of research prototypes that demonstrate promising capabilities but generate no business value. Your mandate is to redesign the R&D operating model to produce research that creates product value without constraining the research team to incremental work that lacks scientific novelty. Begin by conducting a retrospective on the past eighteen months of research projects to classify each outcome as productionized, adopted in modified form, adopted as a design influence without implementation, or abandoned, and identify the factors that distinguish the productionized outcomes from the abandoned ones. Design the research partnership model where each research project is paired with a named engineering owner who participates in the research design phase and commits to evaluating the output for productionization before the project closes. Specify the research brief format that requires researchers to articulate the product hypothesis their work is testing, the specific product decision the research outcome will inform, and the minimum research quality bar required for the product team to act on the findings. Design the quarterly research roadmap review that aligns research priorities with the eighteen-month product roadmap horizon, ensuring that at least sixty percent of research capacity is directed at questions that will be relevant to product decisions within two years. Create the research output packaging standard including a technical paper for peer review where appropriate, a practitioner summary for the engineering team, a benchmark suite for evaluating future improvements, and a reference implementation in the production codebase language. Define the research impact metrics including adoption rate of research outputs and the proportion of product features with a documented research foundation. Deliver the redesigned R&D operating model and a transition plan for the current portfolio of active research projects.

title: You are a principal research scientist leading a team tasked with buildi
prompt: You are a principal research scientist leading a team tasked with building an internal foundation model capability at a technology company that currently relies entirely on third-party model APIs. The leadership team has approved an exploratory program to assess whether building proprietary model capabilities would provide a defensible competitive advantage, reduce long-term inference costs, or enable use cases that are not feasible with commercial APIs due to data privacy requirements. Your task is to design the research program that will produce a clear recommendation within nine months on whether to proceed with a full foundation model investment. Begin by defining the three decision criteria that would make a proprietary model capability strategically justified: a capability gap that commercial models cannot close for regulatory or privacy reasons, a performance advantage on company-specific tasks that would translate to a measurable product improvement, or an inference cost reduction that would be material at current or projected usage volumes. Design the benchmarking study that evaluates five state-of-the-art commercial model APIs on the tasks most critical to the company's product, establishing the performance baseline that a proprietary model would need to exceed to justify the investment. Scope the minimum viable proprietary model experiment using a smaller parameter count model fine-tuned on company data to test whether domain adaptation alone yields a meaningful performance improvement over commercial baselines. Define the compute and data requirements for the minimum viable experiment and assess whether they are within the approved research budget. Design the make-versus-buy decision framework that weighs proprietary model performance, development cost, ongoing inference cost, maintenance burden, and strategic flexibility, and specifies the thresholds at which each factor tilts the decision toward build or buy. Specify the research program governance including the quarterly milestone reviews, the criteria for early termination if the evidence clearly supports a buy decision, and the reporting structure for communicating findings to the executive team. Deliver the research program proposal including the decision framework, the experiment design, the budget model, and the nine-month milestone calendar.

title: Design a Chain-of-Thought prompt for complex reasoning tasks
prompt: Write a Chain-of-Thought prompt for the given reasoning task. Include an explicit instruction to think step by step, 2-3 worked examples showing the reasoning trace before the answer, and a final answer format. Test the prompt against edge cases where the naive answer would be wrong without intermediate reasoning.
prompt_short: Write a CoT prompt with worked examples showing the reasoning trace.
prompt_filtered: CoT only. Step-by-step reasoning instruction and few-shot examples.

title: Build a few-shot prompt with representative examples
prompt: Construct a few-shot prompt for the given task. Select 3-5 examples that cover the range of inputs the model will encounter — including at least one edge case and one negative example. Format each example consistently: input, optional scratchpad, and output. Assess example diversity to avoid over-fitting to a narrow distribution.
prompt_short: Build a few-shot prompt with diverse examples covering edge cases.
prompt_filtered: Few-shot only. Example selection and diversity for the task distribution.

title: Design a ReAct prompt for tool-using agent tasks
prompt: Write a ReAct (Reason + Act) prompt for a task that requires using tools or external information. Structure the prompt to alternate between Thought (reasoning about what to do next), Action (tool call), and Observation (tool result) until the final answer. Include the tool definitions and a worked example of the full thought-action-observation loop.
prompt_short: Write a ReAct prompt with Thought/Action/Observation structure for tool use.
prompt_filtered: ReAct only. Thought-action-observation loop with tool definitions.

title: Implement self-consistency sampling to improve answer reliability
prompt: Design a self-consistency prompting strategy for a high-stakes reasoning task. Generate multiple independent reasoning paths (minimum 5) using the same prompt with temperature > 0, then aggregate by majority vote or weighted confidence. Identify the task types where self-consistency adds the most reliability over single-sample inference.
prompt_short: Design a self-consistency strategy: sample 5+ paths, aggregate by majority vote.
prompt_filtered: Self-consistency only. Multi-sample aggregation for high-stakes reasoning.

title: Write a system prompt that enforces output format and constraints
prompt: Write a system prompt that enforces: a specific output format (JSON schema, markdown template, or numbered list), a maximum length, a tone and persona, and constraints on topics the model should refuse or redirect. Test the prompt against jailbreak attempts and prompts designed to elicit format violations.
prompt_short: Write a system prompt enforcing output format, length, tone, and topic constraints.
prompt_filtered: System prompt design only. Format, constraints, and jailbreak resistance.

title: Evaluate a prompt with a regression test suite
prompt: Design a prompt regression test suite for the given prompt. Create a set of test inputs with expected outputs — including happy path, edge cases, adversarial inputs, and known failure modes. Run the suite after every prompt change and track pass rate over time. Define the pass threshold below which a prompt change is rejected.
prompt_short: Design a regression test suite for this prompt with expected outputs per test case.
prompt_filtered: Prompt regression only. Test suite design with pass/fail criteria.

title: Optimize a prompt for latency and token efficiency
prompt: Audit the given prompt for token inefficiency — redundant instructions, verbose examples, repeated context, unnecessarily long system prompts. Rewrite for minimum token count while maintaining output quality. Measure the latency and cost reduction. Identify which prompt elements are load-bearing vs decorative.
prompt_short: Audit this prompt for token waste and rewrite for minimum tokens without quality loss.
prompt_filtered: Token efficiency only. Load-bearing vs decorative prompt elements.

title: Design a meta-prompt that generates task-specific prompts
prompt: Write a meta-prompt that takes a task description as input and outputs a high-quality prompt for that task. Include: instructions for the model to identify the task type (classification, generation, extraction, reasoning), select the appropriate prompting strategy (zero-shot, CoT, few-shot), and generate relevant examples. Test on 5 diverse task types.
prompt_short: Write a meta-prompt that generates task-specific prompts from a task description.
prompt_filtered: Meta-prompt only. Task classification and strategy selection logic.

title: Build a prompt evaluation rubric for output quality
prompt: Design a 5-dimension rubric for evaluating outputs from this prompt: accuracy (factual correctness), format compliance (matches the specified format), completeness (covers all required elements), conciseness (no unnecessary verbosity), and safety (no harmful or off-topic content). Score each dimension 1-5 and define the criteria per score level.
prompt_short: Design a 5-dimension rubric for scoring prompt outputs with per-level criteria.
prompt_filtered: Evaluation rubric only. Accuracy, format, completeness, conciseness, safety dimensions.

title: Design jailbreak defense layers for a production system prompt
prompt: Audit a production system prompt for jailbreak vulnerabilities — prompt injection via user input, role-playing attacks, instruction override attempts, and context window stuffing. Add defense layers: explicit instruction hierarchy, input sanitization guidance, refusal templates for out-of-scope requests, and a canary phrase that alerts the model when the system prompt may have been overridden.
prompt_short: Audit this system prompt for jailbreak vectors and add defense layers.
prompt_filtered: Jailbreak defense only. Prompt injection, role-play attacks, and override attempts.

title: Implement role prompting to improve task-specific performance
prompt: Design a role prompt for this task. Define a specific expert persona — role, background, decision-making style, and the lens through which they evaluate outputs. Test whether role prompting improves output quality versus a generic prompt for this task type. Identify tasks where role prompting helps (judgment-heavy) versus tasks where it does not (mechanical extraction).
prompt_short: Design a role prompt and test whether it improves output quality for this task.
prompt_filtered: Role prompting only. Persona design and quality comparison vs generic prompt.

title: Structure a RAG prompt for retrieved context injection
prompt: Design a prompt template for retrieval-augmented generation. Structure the prompt to: clearly delimit retrieved context from the user question, instruct the model to cite the source document for each claim, specify behavior when the retrieved context does not contain the answer (say so rather than hallucinate), and limit the response to information present in the context.
prompt_short: Design a RAG prompt template that delimits context, requires citations, and prevents hallucination.
prompt_filtered: RAG prompt only. Context delimiting, citation instruction, and hallucination prevention.

title: A/B test two prompt variants with statistical validity
prompt: Design a statistically valid A/B test for two prompt variants. Define: the evaluation metric (human preference, automated score, task completion rate), the sample size required for the desired power (at least 100 outputs per variant for most tasks), the evaluation protocol (blind rating, side-by-side comparison), and the decision criteria for selecting the winning variant.
prompt_short: Design a statistically valid A/B test for two prompt variants with evaluation criteria.
prompt_filtered: Prompt A/B test only. Sample size, evaluation metric, and winner selection.

title: Design a prompt for structured data extraction with schema validation
prompt: Write a prompt that extracts structured data from unstructured text and outputs valid JSON matching a specified schema. Include: the JSON schema definition in the prompt, 2-3 extraction examples, explicit instructions for handling missing fields (use null) and ambiguous values (use the most probable interpretation), and a validation step instruction to check the output before returning it.
prompt_short: Write a structured extraction prompt that outputs valid JSON with schema validation.
prompt_filtered: Extraction prompt only. Schema definition, null handling, and validation step.

title: Implement Tree-of-Thoughts prompting for multi-path exploration
prompt: Design a Tree-of-Thoughts prompting strategy for a task requiring exploration of multiple solution paths. Instruct the model to generate 3 candidate approaches, evaluate each against defined criteria (feasibility, completeness, efficiency), eliminate the weakest, and continue expanding the best candidates until reaching a final answer. Define the evaluation criteria and branching factor for this specific task.
prompt_short: Design a Tree-of-Thoughts prompt with branch generation, evaluation, and pruning.
prompt_filtered: ToT only. Candidate generation, evaluation criteria, and branch pruning.

title: Write a prompt that reduces hallucination for factual tasks
prompt: Design a prompt for a factual task that minimizes hallucination risk. Include: an explicit instruction to say "I don't know" when uncertain rather than confabulate, a citation requirement for verifiable claims, a confidence level request per claim, and an instruction to reason about uncertainty before committing to an answer. Test against questions known to elicit hallucination in naive prompts.
prompt_short: Write an anti-hallucination prompt with uncertainty instruction and citation requirement.
prompt_filtered: Hallucination reduction only. Uncertainty acknowledgment and citation for factual tasks.

title: Design a prompt versioning and changelog process
prompt: Design a prompt versioning system. Each prompt version should include: a unique version identifier, the full prompt text, the date deployed, the model version it was validated on, the regression test pass rate, and the change reason. Define the promotion criteria from staging to production (regression test pass rate threshold) and the rollback trigger (quality score drop in production).
prompt_short: Design a prompt versioning system with test pass rate and rollback criteria.
prompt_filtered: Prompt versioning only. Version metadata, promotion criteria, and rollback triggers.

title: Evaluate prompt sensitivity to phrasing changes
prompt: Run a sensitivity analysis on the given prompt. Make 5 minimal phrasing changes — synonym substitution, instruction reordering, punctuation changes, active vs passive voice, and explicit vs implicit constraints — and measure the output variance. Identify the prompt elements where small changes cause large output changes; these are reliability risks in production.
prompt_short: Run a sensitivity analysis: 5 small prompt changes to find reliability risks.
prompt_filtered: Sensitivity analysis only. Phrasing changes and output variance measurement.

prompt: Improve my prompt

prompt: Write a few-shot example set

prompt: Add chain-of-thought reasoning

prompt: Evaluate this prompt output

prompt: Apply Chain-of-Thought Reasoning

prompt: Write Role-Based System Prompt

prompt: Add Few-Shot Examples
