prompt: Reduce Hallucination Risk

title: Design Structured Output Schema
prompt: Design Structured Output Schema

title: Build Regression Test Suite
prompt: Build Regression Test Suite

title: Calibrate Confidence Instructions
prompt: Calibrate Confidence Instructions

title: Optimize for Token Efficiency
prompt: Optimize for Token Efficiency

title: Audit this prompt for the three most likely failure modes: format violat
prompt: Audit this prompt for the three most likely failure modes: format violations, hallucination on edge cases, and jailbreak vulnerability via adversarial user input. For each failure mode, write the specific input that would trigger it, describe the broken output, and recommend the minimum prompt change that closes the vulnerability without degrading normal-path quality.

title: Design a complete prompt engineering workflow for this task: zero-shot b
prompt: Design a complete prompt engineering workflow for this task: zero-shot baseline, few-shot improvement, chain-of-thought variant, and a regression test suite. For each variant, describe what quality improvement is expected, why the technique applies to this task type, and the evaluation criterion that would confirm the improvement is genuine and not overfitted to the test examples.

title: Compare two prompt strategies for this task — zero-shot with role prompt
prompt: Compare two prompt strategies for this task — zero-shot with role prompting versus few-shot chain-of-thought — across three dimensions: output quality, token cost, and latency. Design the evaluation harness, run 20 test inputs per strategy, and recommend the strategy based on the tradeoff most relevant to the production context.

title: Audit an existing prompt for common failure patterns
prompt: Audit an existing prompt for common failure patterns. Test the prompt on ten adversarial inputs designed to trigger each of the following failures: hallucination of facts not in context, instruction following errors on edge cases, format non-compliance when the output is unusually short or long, and refusal on inputs that should be handled. Classify each failure by type and severity. Rewrite the prompt with targeted mitigations for the two most frequent failure patterns.

title: Design a prompt evaluation suite for a classification task
prompt: Design a prompt evaluation suite for a classification task. Define ten test cases covering the expected distribution of inputs including easy positives, hard positives, easy negatives, and hard negatives. For each test case, specify the input, the expected output, and the evaluation criterion. Run the suite against three prompt variants and measure accuracy, precision, and recall. Select the best-performing variant and document the design decisions that explain its superiority.

title: Write a chain-of-thought prompt for a complex multi-step reasoning task
prompt: Write a chain-of-thought prompt for a complex multi-step reasoning task. Structure the prompt to instruct the model to reason step by step before producing a final answer. Specify the format for the reasoning chain including section headers and the distinction between intermediate reasoning and final conclusions. Test the prompt on five diverse inputs and compare accuracy to a direct-answer prompt without chain-of-thought. Report the accuracy delta and identify the input types where chain-of-thought helps most.

title: Design the system prompt architecture for a production AI application wi
prompt: Design the system prompt architecture for a production AI application with multiple user personas. Define the base system prompt shared across all personas. Write persona-specific overlays that modify the base prompt for each user type without duplicating the base instructions. Specify the injection order when base and overlay instructions conflict on the same dimension. Write the instruction priority hierarchy that tells the model how to resolve conflicts between system prompt instructions and user instructions.

title: Evaluate the token efficiency of a production prompt that is approaching
prompt: Evaluate the token efficiency of a production prompt that is approaching the context window limit. Analyze the prompt section by section and identify the content that contributes the most to task performance versus the content that is redundant, overly verbose, or never referenced in the model outputs. Estimate the tokens that can be removed without degrading task performance. Rewrite the prompt to be forty percent shorter while maintaining a performance delta of less than five percent on the evaluation suite.

title: Evaluate the prompts used in this system for production readiness
prompt: Evaluate the prompts used in this system for production readiness. Audit each prompt for: token efficiency (are there redundant instructions or verbose examples?), output format reliability (does the model consistently produce the expected format or do violations occur?), hallucination risk (does the prompt include safeguards for factual uncertainty?), and jailbreak resistance (can adversarial user inputs override the system prompt's intent?). Find the prompt most likely to cause a production incident — format failure that breaks a downstream parser, hallucination in a user-facing context, or constraint violation from a prompt injection attack. Recommend the minimum changes per prompt to reach production-safe quality.

title: Design a prompt engineering practice for this team
prompt: Design a prompt engineering practice for this team. Define the workflow from task specification to production deployment: how prompts are designed (task analysis, strategy selection, example curation), how they are evaluated (automated regression suite, human preference evaluation, A/B testing), how they are versioned (version identifier, model pinning, changelog), and how regressions are caught before they reach users (staging pass rate threshold, production quality monitoring). Identify the prompt engineering anti-patterns most likely to cause quality regressions — over-fitting examples to a narrow test set, changing prompts without running the regression suite, deploying prompts against a different model version than they were validated on — and the process safeguards that prevent each.

title: You are a prompt engineering lead designing the prompt architecture for
prompt: You are a prompt engineering lead designing the prompt architecture for a production AI customer support system that handles fifty thousand support tickets per day across six product categories. The system currently uses a single monolithic system prompt that has grown to over four thousand tokens through months of incremental additions, and performance has degraded as the prompt has become internally inconsistent, with later additions contradicting earlier instructions. Response quality varies significantly across ticket types, and the team has no systematic way to diagnose which part of the prompt is causing failures for a specific ticket type. Your task is to redesign the prompt architecture from first principles using a modular approach that improves performance, enables targeted debugging, and scales to new product categories without requiring a full prompt rewrite. Begin by conducting a prompt audit that maps every instruction in the current monolithic prompt to the ticket type it is intended to address and identifies instructions that are contradictory, redundant, or never tested against real failure cases. Design the modular prompt architecture with a base system prompt covering universal agent behaviors such as tone, escalation criteria, refusal policy, and output format, and category-specific instruction modules injected based on ticket classification. Specify the instruction hierarchy that resolves conflicts when base and module instructions address the same behavior, with the module instruction taking precedence for category-specific behaviors and the base instruction governing all others. Design the prompt evaluation framework that tests each module independently against a golden dataset of fifty tickets per category, measures accuracy and format compliance, and produces a per-category performance scorecard. Specify the prompt versioning system that tracks which version of each module is deployed in production, enables rollback to a previous version within five minutes, and maintains a changelog with the rationale for each change. Design the regression testing protocol that runs the full evaluation suite before any prompt update is deployed to production and blocks deployment if performance drops more than three percent on any category. Deliver the modular prompt architecture specification, the evaluation framework, and the migration plan for transitioning from the monolithic prompt.

title: You are a principal AI engineer designing the prompt strategy for a retr
prompt: You are a principal AI engineer designing the prompt strategy for a retrieval-augmented generation system that surfaces answers from a corpus of ten thousand technical documents. The system is deployed for enterprise customers who expect citation accuracy, comprehensive coverage of the relevant document set, and clear acknowledgment of uncertainty when the retrieved documents do not contain enough information to answer the question confidently. Current failure modes include citation hallucination where the model cites documents that do not support the claim, answer truncation where the model stops before covering all relevant information from retrieved chunks, and overconfidence where the model answers with full confidence even when retrieved documents are tangentially related rather than directly responsive. Your task is to redesign the prompting strategy to address all three failure modes while maintaining response latency within an acceptable threshold. Begin by designing the retrieval quality signal that the prompt uses to communicate to the model how confident to be in each retrieved chunk, using a relevance score derived from the retrieval step and injected as metadata with each chunk. Design the citation instruction set that requires the model to cite the specific document and section for every factual claim, to use only information explicitly stated in the retrieved documents rather than its parametric knowledge, and to use a structured citation format that the downstream citation verification system can parse reliably. Design the uncertainty instruction set that distinguishes three confidence levels: high confidence when multiple retrieved documents directly address the question, medium confidence when only one document is directly relevant, and low confidence when retrieved documents are only tangentially related, with a different response format and explicit uncertainty language for each level. Specify the completeness instruction that requires the model to cover all distinct claims in the retrieved documents that are relevant to the query without truncating when multiple chunks address different aspects of the question. Design the adversarial test suite covering citation hallucination, overconfidence, and truncation, with fifty test cases per failure mode drawn from real user queries. Deliver the revised prompt specification, the test suite, and the evaluation methodology.

title: You are a prompt engineering researcher developing a benchmark and metho
prompt: You are a prompt engineering researcher developing a benchmark and methodology for evaluating prompt quality across a team of fifteen engineers who write prompts for different AI-powered features. Currently the team has no shared standard for what a good prompt looks like, and prompt quality varies dramatically across features, with some prompts carefully tested and others shipped based on intuition alone. Engineering leaders want to establish a prompt quality standard that can be applied consistently across the team and used in code review to catch low-quality prompts before they reach production. Your task is to design the prompt quality evaluation framework, the benchmark methodology, and the team adoption plan. Begin by defining the dimensions of prompt quality that are most predictive of production performance, drawing on both the academic literature and your team's empirical experience, and narrowing to the five most important dimensions for your specific use cases. Design the prompt quality rubric that engineers can apply in under fifteen minutes to score any prompt on each dimension, with clear descriptions of what constitutes a score of one, three, and five on each dimension and concrete examples drawn from real team prompts with names removed. Design the automated evaluation component that can be run as part of the CI pipeline, covering format compliance checks, instruction contradiction detection, and performance regression testing against a golden dataset. Specify the golden dataset curation process, including how many examples are needed per task type, how they are collected and labeled, and how they are kept current as the underlying task evolves. Design the peer review protocol for prompts, specifying which prompt changes require peer review before deployment, what the reviewer is expected to evaluate, and how disagreements about prompt quality are resolved. Create the team onboarding curriculum for the prompt quality framework, including a two-hour workshop, a prompt quality case study library with annotated examples, and the self-assessment exercise engineers complete before their first peer review. Deliver the complete prompt quality framework as a developer guide, the rubric as a scoring sheet, and the adoption plan with a twelve-week rollout timeline.

title: Audit API surface for undocumented or orphaned endpoints
prompt: Compare the API routes defined in code against the API documentation. Identify endpoints in code with no documentation, deprecated endpoints still receiving traffic, and documented endpoints that no longer exist. Prioritize by traffic volume.
prompt_short: Find all API endpoints not documented or no longer in code.
prompt_filtered: API surface audit only. Code vs documentation vs traffic alignment.

title: Review API versioning strategy and deprecation policy
prompt: Assess the API versioning approach. Identify endpoints with breaking changes deployed without a version bump, endpoints with no deprecation notice before removal, and how long consumers have to migrate when a version is deprecated.
prompt_short: Audit API versioning for breaking changes and deprecation notice gaps.
prompt_filtered: Versioning and deprecation only. Breaking changes and migration windows.

title: Find APIs missing rate limiting or quota enforcement
prompt: Identify public or partner-facing APIs with no rate limiting, no quota enforcement, and no throttling. For each, estimate the blast radius if a single consumer makes unrestricted calls. Recommend appropriate rate limits per consumer tier.
prompt_short: Find public APIs with no rate limiting or quota enforcement.
prompt_filtered: Missing rate limits only. Unprotected public and partner-facing APIs.

title: Assess API contract testing coverage for consumers
prompt: Evaluate whether API contracts are tested from the consumer's perspective, not just from the provider. Identify APIs with no consumer-driven contract tests where a breaking change would go undetected until a consumer is broken in production.
prompt_short: Find APIs with no consumer-driven contract tests for breaking change detection.
prompt_filtered: Contract testing only. Consumer-side coverage gaps.

title: Review API error response consistency and documentation
prompt: Audit all API error responses for consistency — same status codes for the same conditions, same error body format, same error code naming convention. Verify every error code is documented with a description and suggested client-side recovery action.
prompt_short: Audit API error responses for consistency and documentation coverage.
prompt_filtered: Error format consistency only. Status codes, body shape, and documentation.

title: Audit API dependency on third-party services and failure modes
prompt: Map all external service dependencies in the API. For each dependency (payment processors, auth providers, ML services, etc.), identify what happens when that service is slow or down — does the request timeout, fail immediately, degrade gracefully, or hang indefinitely? Test failure scenarios and verify recovery behavior.
prompt_short: Audit external service dependency failures and timeout handling.
prompt_filtered: Third-party service dependencies only. Failure modes and degradation paths.

title: Review API documentation completeness and accuracy against implementation
prompt: Compare the API documentation (OpenAPI, Swagger, or manual docs) against the live implementation. Identify endpoints documented but not implemented, parameters that are documented but ignored, documented error codes that are not actually returned, and missing authentication requirements in docs.
prompt_short: Find API documentation gaps and mismatches with actual implementation.
prompt_filtered: Documentation accuracy only. Discrepancies between docs and live API.

title: Assess API security for injection vulnerabilities and input validation
prompt: Audit all API endpoints for injection vulnerabilities (SQL, command, LDAP, expression language, etc.). For each endpoint, verify input is validated against a schema, length limits are enforced, dangerous characters are escaped or rejected, and parameterized queries are used where applicable. Test with known injection payloads.
prompt_short: Find injection vulnerabilities and weak input validation in API endpoints.
prompt_filtered: Injection and input validation only. Security-relevant input handling.

title: Review API monitoring, logging, and observability for production incidents
prompt: Evaluate API observability: are all requests logged (not PII)? Are error rates and latency monitored? Are slow queries surfaced? Is request tracing enabled end-to-end? Verify that a P1 incident (high error rate, cascading failures) could be diagnosed from logs and metrics within 5 minutes. Identify observability gaps.
prompt_short: Audit API monitoring and logging for incident diagnosis capabilities.
prompt_filtered: Observability and logging only. Error rate and latency monitoring.

title: Assess API compatibility and backward compatibility for client safety
prompt: Review whether the API is safe to evolve without breaking clients. Do you support multiple API versions concurrently? Are deprecated endpoints maintained for a grace period? Can you add optional fields to responses without breaking clients that can't handle extra fields? Identify how many versions back you maintain compatibility.
prompt_short: Audit API versioning for safe client evolution and backward compatibility.
prompt_filtered: Backward compatibility only. Version support windows and breaking change handling.

title: Audit API response caching strategy and cache invalidation correctness
prompt: Evaluate caching at all levels: browser caching headers, CDN/edge caching, server-side caching. For each cache layer, verify: cache headers are correct, Time-To-Live (TTL) values are appropriate, cache invalidation is complete (stale data doesn't surface), and cache hit rates are measured. Identify cache misses or false positives.
prompt_short: Review API response caching and cache invalidation for data freshness.
prompt_filtered: Caching strategy only. Cache headers, TTLs, and invalidation correctness.

title: Evaluate API pagination design for correctness and developer experience
prompt: Assess pagination implementation: does it use offset/limit (unstable under concurrent writes) or cursor-based (stable)? Are page sizes bounded (prevent huge requests)? Is 'hasMore' or 'nextToken' returned? Can pagination break if data is deleted mid-stream? Test edge cases: empty results, single page, last page, concurrent deletes during pagination.
prompt_short: Audit API pagination design for correctness under concurrent writes.
prompt_filtered: Pagination strategy only. Offset vs cursor, stability, and edge cases.

title: Audit API gateway configuration for security and hygiene
prompt: Review the API gateway configuration. Verify TLS 1.2+ is enforced, weak cipher suites are disabled, request size limits are set, and all routes requiring auth have auth enforced at the gateway layer. Identify gaps where backend services trust the gateway but the gateway has exceptions.
prompt_short: Audit API gateway config for TLS, cipher, auth, and request limit gaps.
prompt_filtered: Gateway configuration only. TLS version, auth enforcement, and request limits.

title: Review API deprecation sunset policy and consumer migration tracking
prompt: Assess the end-of-life process for deprecated APIs. Verify each deprecated version has a sunset date, consumers are notified by email and Sunset response header, traffic to deprecated endpoints is tracked, and a migration guide exists. Find deprecated versions still receiving meaningful traffic with no migration plan.
prompt_short: Find deprecated API versions still in use with no consumer migration plan.
prompt_filtered: Sunset policy only. Active traffic on deprecated versions and migration guides.

title: Identify APIs with SLA tiers that are unmonitored or unmeasured
prompt: Cross-reference published SLA commitments (uptime, latency, error rate) with actual monitoring coverage. Identify endpoints with published SLAs but no alerting, no SLO tracking, or no incident response process. For each gap, estimate the breach risk and recommend the minimum monitoring to make the SLA credible.
prompt_short: Find API SLA commitments that lack corresponding monitoring or alerting.
prompt_filtered: SLA coverage only. Unmonitored endpoints with published commitments.

title: Evaluate API key lifecycle management and rotation policy
prompt: Audit API key management. Verify keys have expiration dates, there is a rotation workflow consumers can complete without downtime, leaked keys can be revoked instantly, and key usage is logged per key ID. Find long-lived keys with no rotation history and keys shared across multiple consumers.
prompt_short: Audit API key expiration, rotation workflow, and revocation capability.
prompt_filtered: Key lifecycle only. Expiry, rotation, revocation, and per-key usage logging.

title: Review API request and response logging for debugging completeness
prompt: Evaluate what is logged for each API call — request path, method, status code, latency, consumer ID, and correlation ID. Verify logs are queryable within an acceptable window and that support and engineering can reconstruct a consumer's exact request history for debugging. Identify endpoints with logging gaps.
prompt_short: Audit API logging completeness for effective debugging and support.
prompt_filtered: Logging coverage only. What is captured per request and queryability.

title: Assess API change management and backward compatibility policy
prompt: Review the process for making API changes. Identify the enforcement mechanism preventing breaking changes in minor versions, whether a compatibility test suite runs in CI, and how additive changes (new optional fields, new endpoints) are communicated to consumers. Find recent changes that broke clients without a version bump.
prompt_short: Audit API change management for backward compatibility enforcement.
prompt_filtered: Change management only. Breaking change detection and CI compatibility gates.

title: Evaluate API error code consistency and developer debuggability
prompt: Assess error response consistency. Verify HTTP status codes map correctly to error types, error bodies include a machine-readable error code, a human-readable message, and a correlation ID. Find endpoints returning generic 500 errors for conditions that could return specific 4xx codes. Identify errors with no remediation guidance.
prompt_short: Audit API error codes for consistency and developer debugging utility.
prompt_filtered: Error codes only. Status code correctness and error body structure.

title: Identify internal APIs being used by external consumers without governance
prompt: Find cases where consumers are calling internal, undocumented, or private APIs — routes not in the developer portal, endpoints designed for internal use only, or staging environment endpoints accessed in production. Assess the risk of each and the path to migrating them to governed public endpoints.
prompt_short: Find external consumers calling internal or undocumented APIs without governance.
prompt_filtered: Shadow API usage only. Undocumented endpoints consumed by external parties.

title: Find APIs Without SLA Monitoring
prompt: Find APIs Without SLA Monitoring

title: Audit Error Response Consistency
prompt: Audit Error Response Consistency

title: List APIs With No Consumer Tests
prompt: List APIs With No Consumer Tests

title: Audit the full API surface area against the published documentation
prompt: Audit the full API surface area against the published documentation. Identify endpoints in code with no published documentation, endpoints documented but no longer present in code, and endpoints with traffic indicating active use that lack ownership. Cross-reference with rate limit configuration and authentication requirements per endpoint. Recommend the documentation reconciliation plan and the deprecation schedule for endpoints with no internal owner.

title: Review the API versioning and breaking change history
prompt: Review the API versioning and breaking change history. Identify breaking changes shipped without a version bump, deprecation notices issued without an enforced sunset date, and consumers still pinned to deprecated versions past the migration window. Assess whether the versioning strategy supports backwards-compatible evolution or forces consumers into painful migrations. Recommend the versioning policy revisions that would most reduce consumer-facing breakage.

title: Examine the API error response surface for consistency and consumer ergo
prompt: Examine the API error response surface for consistency and consumer ergonomics. Audit whether the same condition returns the same status code across endpoints, whether error bodies follow a single schema, and whether every documented error code has client-side recovery guidance. Find error codes used in code but missing from documentation, and documented codes that no endpoint actually returns. Recommend the error contract standardization that would most improve client integration quality.

title: Audit authentication and authorization gaps across the API surface
prompt: Audit authentication and authorization gaps across the API surface. Identify endpoints accepting credentials at the wrong scope, endpoints missing authorization checks present on similar routes, and public endpoints that should require authentication. Verify rate limiting is applied at the correct granularity — per IP, per consumer key, per user — and that burst allowances are consistent across related endpoints. Check that auth error responses distinguish unauthenticated from unauthorized without leaking resource existence. Recommend the authentication hardening sequence that would most reduce unauthorized access risk.

title: Assess API observability across all production endpoints
prompt: Assess API observability across all production endpoints. Verify every endpoint emits structured log entries including request method, path, status code, latency, and a correlation ID linkable to the originating client request. Identify endpoints with no latency monitoring, no error rate alerting, and no SLO defined. Find endpoints where a 10x traffic spike would not be detected before cascading failures occurred. Examine whether distributed tracing is enabled end to end so an incident can be correlated from client request through all internal service calls. Recommend the observability investments that would most improve mean time to detection for API-layer incidents.

title: Evaluate the API management discipline end to end
prompt: Evaluate the API management discipline end to end. Assess whether the API surface is governed by a single authoritative specification, whether code generation or runtime validation enforces conformance to that specification, and whether documentation is generated from the same source rather than maintained in parallel. Identify endpoints lacking rate limiting, quota enforcement, or per-consumer throttling that would protect the platform under load. Find authentication and authorization gaps where endpoints accept credentials at the wrong scope or skip authorization checks present on similar endpoints. Examine whether consumer-driven contract tests catch breaking changes before production. Look for API hygiene gaps — undocumented endpoints, endpoints returning inconsistent error shapes, and endpoints with no monitoring on success and latency. Summarize the five API management improvements that would most increase consumer trust and platform stability.

title: Audit the API lifecycle from design to deprecation
prompt: Audit the API lifecycle from design to deprecation. Identify whether new endpoints go through a design review with consumer representation, whether breaking changes require explicit approval, and whether deprecation follows a published timeline with consumer notification. Find endpoints in production for over a year with no recorded review, endpoints with traffic from internal teams that should be migrated to internal RPC, and endpoints with no defined SLA. Assess whether API analytics — request volume, error rate, latency percentiles — are visible to the platform team and to consumers. Look for opportunities to consolidate near-duplicate endpoints, retire endpoints with no active consumers, and elevate internal endpoints that have outgrown their original scope. Recommend the lifecycle process changes that would most improve API quality over time.

title: Audit the API dependency map for hidden coupling and failure propagation
prompt: Audit the API dependency map for hidden coupling and failure propagation risk. Identify every external service the API depends on — databases, caches, downstream microservices, third-party vendors — and for each, assess whether the dependency is on the critical path of the most common request types. Find dependencies with no circuit breaker, no timeout, and no graceful degradation path. Examine whether the API can partially serve responses when non-critical dependencies are unavailable — returning cached data, omitting optional fields, or queuing writes for later reconciliation. Look for cascading failure patterns where a single slow dependency causes all request-handling threads to block, leading to full service unavailability. Assess whether load testing has been performed under realistic traffic with dependencies at reduced capacity. Recommend the resilience architecture changes that would most reduce the blast radius of a single dependency failure on the API layer.

title: Audit webhook delivery reliability and retry strategy
prompt: Review the webhook delivery system. Verify there is retry logic with exponential backoff, a dead-letter queue for permanently failed deliveries, delivery confirmation via response status code, and visibility into delivery success rate per consumer.
prompt_short: Audit webhook delivery reliability, retries, and dead-letter handling.
prompt_filtered: Delivery reliability only. Retry logic and failure tracking.

title: Verify webhook payload signatures for consumer security
prompt: Audit webhook payloads for signature verification. Verify that each webhook includes a HMAC signature in a header, consumers are expected to verify it, and the signing secret is rotatable. Identify webhooks sent without signatures.
prompt_short: Verify all webhooks include HMAC signatures and consumers validate them.
prompt_filtered: Signature security only. Signing and verification implementation.

title: Find webhook consumers not handling duplicate deliveries
prompt: Identify webhook consumers that are not idempotent — processing the same webhook twice would create a duplicate record, double-charge, or double-send. Recommend event ID-based deduplication for each non-idempotent consumer.
prompt_short: Find webhook consumers that would break on duplicate delivery.
prompt_filtered: Idempotency only. Non-idempotent webhook consumer identification.

title: Assess webhook event schema versioning and migration
prompt: Review how webhook event schemas evolve over time. Identify events with no versioning, events where a field was removed without a deprecation period, and consumers that would break if a non-required field was added. Recommend a backwards-compatible schema evolution strategy.
prompt_short: Audit webhook schema versioning and backwards compatibility strategy.
prompt_filtered: Schema versioning only. Breaking changes and consumer migration.

title: Review webhook documentation for consumer integration quality
prompt: Assess the quality of webhook integration documentation from a consumer's perspective. Verify docs include: payload shape with examples, all event types, expected retry behavior, how to verify signatures, how to test locally, and troubleshooting guidance.
prompt_short: Audit webhook documentation quality from an integration consumer's view.
prompt_filtered: Documentation only. Completeness from consumer integration perspective.

title: Audit webhook signature verification implementation for authenticity
prompt: Verify webhook signature verification is correctly implemented. Check: are signatures using HMAC-SHA256 or equivalent? Is the secret key stored securely (not in code)? Is signature verification constant-time (timing-safe) to prevent timing attacks? Are consumers able to verify authenticity? Test with tampered payloads.
prompt_short: Audit webhook signature verification for replay attack and tampering protection.
prompt_filtered: Signature verification only. HMAC implementation and timing-safe comparison.

title: Review webhook retry logic and backoff strategy for consumer reliability
prompt: Evaluate webhook retry behavior: are failures retried with exponential backoff? How many times? For how long? What's the maximum retry window? Are webhooks eventually marked as failed and logged? Do consumers get notified of permanent failures? Test behavior when a consumer is temporarily unavailable.
prompt_short: Audit webhook retry behavior and backoff strategy for reliability.
prompt_filtered: Retry strategy only. Exponential backoff, retry limits, and failure notification.

title: Assess webhook event ordering guarantees and idempotency
prompt: Verify webhook delivery guarantees: are events delivered in order? Can a single event be delivered multiple times (requiring idempotency)? Are events deduplicated? Does each event have a unique ID consumers can track? If ordering is not guaranteed, is that documented? Test concurrent event delivery.
prompt_short: Audit webhook event ordering, deduplication, and idempotency support.
prompt_filtered: Delivery guarantees only. Event ordering, deduplication, and idempotency.

title: Review webhook rate limiting and DDoS protection at receiver endpoint
prompt: Evaluate how webhook receivers are protected: is there rate limiting on incoming webhooks? Can a misbehaving or buggy sender overwhelm a receiver? Are there queue length limits? What happens if the receiver falls behind — are webhooks dropped, delayed, or buffered indefinitely? How would a consumer detect if webhooks are being dropped?
prompt_short: Audit webhook receiver rate limiting and overflow handling.
prompt_filtered: Receiver protection only. Rate limiting, buffering, and overflow behavior.

title: Audit webhook filtering and selective delivery for consumer efficiency
prompt: Evaluate whether consumers can filter webhooks (receive only certain event types). Can they specify which resources or entities trigger webhooks? Are there any performance benefits from filtering or must all webhooks be sent and consumers filter client-side? Document filtering capabilities and limitations.
prompt_short: Audit webhook filtering options and selective delivery capabilities.
prompt_filtered: Filtering and selectivity only. Event type and resource-level filtering.

title: Review webhook testing tooling and developer experience for integration
prompt: Assess webhook developer experience: is there a webhook testing tool or dashboard where developers can replay events, inspect payloads, or simulate event types? Can developers test locally (localhost tunneling or mock server)? Is there example code for common languages? Are error scenarios well documented?
prompt_short: Audit webhook testing tooling and developer experience.
prompt_filtered: Developer tooling only. Testing, replay, and simulation capabilities.

title: Assess webhook security against header injection and man-in-the-middle attacks
prompt: Verify webhook security: are requests only sent over HTTPS (not HTTP)? Is certificate validation performed? Are headers validated against injection (no injected headers from untrusted sources)? Can consumers require mutual TLS (mTLS)? Are there any known vectors for header or body injection?
prompt_short: Audit webhook transport security and header injection defenses.
prompt_filtered: Transport security only. HTTPS enforcement, certificate validation, header injection.

title: Audit webhook replay attack prevention with timestamp validation
prompt: Verify that webhook signatures include a timestamp and that consumers reject payloads older than a tolerance window (e.g. 5 minutes). Without timestamp binding, a captured valid signature can be replayed indefinitely. Check whether the tolerance window is configurable and whether clock skew is accounted for.
prompt_short: Audit webhook signature design for replay attack prevention via timestamps.
prompt_filtered: Replay prevention only. Timestamp binding and tolerance window enforcement.

title: Review webhook ordering guarantees and consumer idempotency design
prompt: Assess delivery ordering: are webhooks guaranteed to arrive in order, best-effort ordered, or unordered? Do consumers have to handle out-of-order delivery? Verify each event type includes a sequence number or event timestamp so consumers can detect and handle reordering. Find consumer implementations that assume ordering but receive unordered events.
prompt_short: Audit webhook ordering guarantees and consumer idempotency requirements.
prompt_filtered: Ordering only. Sequence numbers, delivery guarantees, and consumer idempotency.

title: Evaluate webhook schema versioning for backward compatibility
prompt: Review how the webhook payload schema evolves. Identify whether event types are versioned separately from the API, whether additive changes (new optional fields) are made without a version bump, and whether breaking changes trigger a new event type version. Find consumers that break when new optional fields are added.
prompt_short: Audit webhook payload schema versioning for forward and backward compatibility.
prompt_filtered: Schema versioning only. Event type versioning and additive vs breaking changes.

title: Assess webhook consumer registration and secret rotation workflow
prompt: Review the webhook registration process: can consumers register endpoints via API or only via UI? Is there a way to test delivery before enabling? Can consumers rotate their signing secret without downtime (dual-secret validation window)? Are inactive endpoint registrations cleaned up automatically?
prompt_short: Audit webhook registration, secret rotation, and endpoint cleanup workflow.
prompt_filtered: Registration workflow only. Self-service registration and secret rotation.

title: Find webhook delivery lag outliers impacting time-sensitive consumers
prompt: Analyze webhook delivery latency distribution. Identify event types or consumers with p99 delivery lag exceeding acceptable thresholds. Find the causes of high-latency delivery — queue depth, consumer endpoint slowness, retry backoff accumulation, or infrastructure bottlenecks. Recommend alerting thresholds and queue prioritization changes.
prompt_short: Find webhook delivery lag outliers and their root causes.
prompt_filtered: Delivery latency only. P99 lag by event type and consumer endpoint.

title: Review webhook event type catalog completeness and discoverability
prompt: Assess whether all meaningful state changes in the system emit a webhook event. Identify objects or workflows that change state but produce no event — forcing consumers to poll. Verify the event type catalog is published, searchable, and includes payload examples for each event. Find undocumented event types consumers have discovered through experimentation.
prompt_short: Find state changes with no webhook event and audit event catalog completeness.
prompt_filtered: Event coverage only. Missing events for state transitions and catalog gaps.

title: Audit webhook dead-letter handling and permanent failure notifications
prompt: Review what happens when a webhook exhausts retries. Verify failures are written to a dead-letter log, the consumer is notified (email or dashboard alert), the payload is retained for manual replay, and the endpoint is not silently disabled without warning. Find endpoints that have been disabled without the consumer knowing.
prompt_short: Audit dead-letter handling and consumer notification for permanent webhook failures.
prompt_filtered: Dead-letter only. Failure retention, notification, and endpoint disable behavior.

title: Evaluate webhook observability and per-consumer delivery dashboards
prompt: Assess observability: can consumers view delivery status, retry history, and failure reasons in a self-service dashboard? Can they filter by event type or time range? Is there an API for querying delivery history? Can they trigger manual replay without contacting support? Identify observability gaps that force consumers to raise support tickets to debug delivery issues.
prompt_short: Audit webhook consumer observability and self-service delivery debugging.
prompt_filtered: Observability only. Consumer-facing delivery status and replay capabilities.

prompt: Assess Delivery Latency Outliers

title: Audit the webhook delivery system end to end
prompt: Audit the webhook delivery system end to end. Verify retry logic uses exponential backoff with jitter, that a dead-letter queue captures permanently failed deliveries, and that delivery confirmation tracks consumer response codes. Identify webhook types with delivery success rates below acceptable thresholds and the consumers most affected. Recommend the delivery infrastructure improvements that would most reduce missed events without overwhelming downstream consumers during incident recovery.

title: Review webhook security across all event types
prompt: Review webhook security across all event types. Verify that every webhook includes an HMAC signature in a header, that the signing secret is rotatable per consumer, and that consumers are documented to verify the signature before processing. Identify webhooks delivered without signatures, signatures using weak algorithms, and consumers that have never rotated their signing secret. Recommend the security hardening sequence that would most reduce signature spoofing and replay risk.

title: Examine consumer idempotency assumptions across the webhook surface
prompt: Examine consumer idempotency assumptions across the webhook surface. Identify webhook types where duplicate delivery would create duplicate records, double charges, or duplicate notifications. Find consumers without event-ID-based deduplication and the business impact of a duplicate delivery for each. Recommend the idempotency requirements that should be documented as a consumer contract and the platform tooling that would help consumers implement deduplication correctly.

title: Audit webhook schema evolution for backward and forward compatibility
prompt: Audit webhook schema evolution for backward and forward compatibility. Identify event types where fields were renamed or removed without a deprecation period, consumers that break when new optional fields are added, and event type versions that diverged across producer and consumer without explicit versioning. Verify each event carries an event ID, a schema version, and a timestamp. Recommend the schema evolution strategy that would most reduce consumer-breaking webhook changes.

title: Review the webhook consumer developer experience end to end
prompt: Review the webhook consumer developer experience end to end. Identify gaps in payload documentation — event types with no example payload, fields with ambiguous semantics, and retry behavior not documented in terms consumers can implement against. Find onboarding friction — endpoint registration complexity, signing secret retrieval process, and absence of a local testing tool or sandbox. Assess whether consumers can self-diagnose delivery failures from a dashboard or must raise a support ticket. Recommend the developer experience improvements that would most reduce time to first successful webhook integration.

title: Evaluate the webhook platform as a delivery system and as a consumer-fac
prompt: Evaluate the webhook platform as a delivery system and as a consumer-facing product. Assess delivery reliability under normal load and under recovery from a downstream consumer outage — whether retries respect a backoff schedule, whether the platform protects itself from a consumer that returns errors for hours, and whether consumers can replay missed events from an archive. Identify event schema versioning gaps — events where fields were renamed or removed without a deprecation window. Find consumers receiving events they no longer need and consumers missing events they should be subscribed to. Look for monitoring gaps — webhook types with no per-consumer success metric, no latency tracking from event generation to delivery, and no alerting on sustained delivery failure to high-value consumers. Summarize the five webhook platform improvements that would most increase delivery reliability and consumer trust.

title: Audit the webhook integration developer experience
prompt: Audit the webhook integration developer experience. Assess whether the documentation enables a new consumer to integrate end to end without contacting support — payload examples per event type, signature verification code snippets in major languages, local testing tools or sandbox endpoints, and clear retry semantics. Identify documentation gaps that drive support volume — undocumented event types, ambiguous field semantics, missing example payloads with edge cases. Find onboarding friction in the consumer signup flow — endpoint registration, signing secret retrieval, event subscription management, and replay tooling. Look for opportunities to publish webhook delivery analytics to consumers so they can self-diagnose integration issues. Recommend the developer experience changes that would most reduce time to first successful integration and ongoing support burden.

title: Audit the webhook system for security, reliability, and developer ergono
prompt: Audit the webhook system for security, reliability, and developer ergonomics as a unified consumer-facing product. Assess delivery security — whether every event carries an HMAC signature, whether consumers are documented and supported to verify it, whether the signing secret is rotatable per consumer without downtime, and whether replay attacks are prevented by timestamp binding. Examine delivery reliability under both normal load and during consumer outage recovery — whether retries respect exponential backoff, whether the system protects itself from a consumer returning errors for hours, and whether consumers can replay missed events from an archive. Look for schema governance gaps — events where fields were removed without deprecation, event types documented but not emitted, and consumers that have drifted from the current schema without detection. Assess consumer tooling — whether a self-service dashboard exists for delivery history, whether payload inspection and manual replay are available, and whether local testing is supported without tunneling. Summarize the five webhook system improvements that would most increase delivery reliability and consumer trust.

title: Audit all third-party integrations for active use and health
prompt: Inventory every third-party integration. For each, verify it is actively used, the API keys are still valid, the integration has error monitoring, and there is a documented fallback if the third-party goes down. Identify unused or zombie integrations.
prompt_short: Inventory all integrations and find unused, expired, or unmonitored ones.
prompt_filtered: Integration health only. Active use, credential validity, and monitoring.

title: Find integrations with no error handling or fallback
prompt: Identify third-party integrations where a failure causes a user-visible error with no graceful fallback. For each, assess the frequency of third-party downtime and recommend a fallback behavior — cached response, degraded mode, or queued retry.
prompt_short: Find integrations that fail hard with no fallback when the third-party is down.
prompt_filtered: Missing fallbacks only. Third-party failure user impact.

title: Review integration authentication and secret rotation
prompt: Audit authentication for all integrations. Identify integrations using long-lived credentials with no rotation plan, credentials shared across environments, and integrations with broader permission scopes than they need.
prompt_short: Audit integration credentials for rotation, scoping, and env isolation.
prompt_filtered: Integration auth only. Credential rotation and least-privilege scoping.

title: Assess data flow and privacy for each integration
prompt: For each integration, identify what user data is shared with the third-party — explicitly and implicitly. Verify this is covered by the privacy policy, users have been informed, and the data sharing is proportionate to the integration's purpose.
prompt_short: Audit what user data each integration shares and verify privacy compliance.
prompt_filtered: Data sharing only. What PII flows to each third-party.

title: Find integrations creating vendor lock-in risk
prompt: Identify integrations that would be extremely difficult to replace due to tight coupling — data format dependencies, proprietary APIs with no open equivalent, business logic embedded in the third-party's system. For each, estimate migration effort if the vendor raised prices or shut down.
prompt_short: Find integrations with high lock-in risk and estimate replacement cost.
prompt_filtered: Lock-in risk only. Tight coupling and migration cost estimation.

title: Audit integration error handling and fallback behavior in critical flows
prompt: For each integration, determine: what happens if the integration fails during a critical user flow (checkout, authentication, content publishing)? Does the user's action fail entirely, get queued for retry, degrade gracefully, or have a fallback? Are there timeout limits to prevent hanging? Test failure scenarios and verify the user experience.
prompt_short: Audit integration error handling in critical user flows.
prompt_filtered: Error handling and fallback behavior only. Critical path resilience.

title: Review integration authentication and API key management
prompt: Verify integration credentials are handled securely: are API keys/secrets stored encrypted in secrets management (not in code)? Are they rotated regularly? Can keys be revoked immediately if compromised? Are there separate keys per environment (dev/staging/prod)? Is there audit logging for credential access?
prompt_short: Audit integration credential storage, rotation, and access logging.
prompt_filtered: Authentication and key management only. Secure credential handling.

title: Assess integration data synchronization and consistency guarantees
prompt: Evaluate how data stays in sync between systems: are changes replicated in real-time, eventually, or batch? If data diverges (your system shows X but the integration shows Y), how is it detected and resolved? Are there reconciliation jobs? How long can data be inconsistent before it's noticed?
prompt_short: Audit integration data sync strategy and divergence detection.
prompt_filtered: Data synchronization and consistency only. Replication and reconciliation.

title: Review integration rate limiting and quota management to prevent overage
prompt: Evaluate rate limiting on integrations: does your system respect the third-party's rate limits? Are you implementing backoff when throttled? Is quota usage tracked? Do you alert before hitting limits? What happens when quota is exceeded — graceful degradation or user-facing error?
prompt_short: Audit integration rate limit handling and quota tracking.
prompt_filtered: Rate limiting and quota only. Throttling and overage prevention.

title: Assess integration testing coverage and failure scenario simulation
prompt: Evaluate integration test coverage: are there unit tests that mock the integration? Are there integration tests against a staging environment? Do tests cover happy path and all failure scenarios (timeout, 4xx, 5xx, network down)? Can you test without hitting the live API? Is there a way to simulate third-party service outages?
prompt_short: Audit integration test coverage for failure scenarios.
prompt_filtered: Testing only. Mock coverage, staging tests, and failure simulation.

title: Review integration observability and monitoring for production health
prompt: Verify integration observability: are API calls logged (not sensitive data)? Are latencies tracked? Are failure rates monitored? If the third-party service degrades, would you detect it? Are there alerts for high error rates? Can you trace a user's request through the integration? Identify monitoring gaps.
prompt_short: Audit integration monitoring and alerting for production issues.
prompt_filtered: Observability and monitoring only. Logging, latency tracking, and alerts.

title: Evaluate integration documentation and runbook completeness for incidents
prompt: Review integration documentation: are setup instructions clear for new developers? Is there troubleshooting guidance for common failure modes? Is there a runbook for what to do if the third-party service is down? Are there known limitations documented? Is there escalation guidance (who to contact at the third party)?
prompt_short: Audit integration documentation quality and incident runbook completeness.
prompt_filtered: Documentation and runbooks only. Setup, troubleshooting, and incident response.

title: Assess third-party SLA commitments against your own uptime targets
prompt: For each external integration, compare the vendor's published SLA with your product's uptime target. Identify integrations where the vendor SLA is weaker than your own commitment — creating a ceiling on your reliability. For each gap, assess whether a higher-tier vendor SLA, a fallback path, or graceful degradation can close it.
prompt_short: Find vendor SLAs weaker than your own uptime targets and the gap risk.
prompt_filtered: SLA alignment only. Vendor commitments vs product uptime targets.

title: Identify integrations with no graceful degradation on vendor outage
prompt: For each third-party integration, document what happens to the user experience when the vendor is unavailable. Find integrations where a vendor outage causes a hard failure with no fallback, no cached response, and no user-visible degradation message. Prioritize adding graceful degradation to integrations on the critical user path.
prompt_short: Find integrations that cause hard failures with no fallback during vendor outages.
prompt_filtered: Degradation only. Hard failures vs cached fallbacks vs graceful degradation.

title: Review vendor lock-in exposure and exit strategy readiness
prompt: Assess lock-in risk for each major integration. Identify proprietary APIs with no standard alternative, data stored exclusively in a vendor system, and switching costs (migration complexity, contract penalties). For integrations with high lock-in and no exit plan, recommend the abstraction layer or alternative that would reduce switching cost.
prompt_short: Audit vendor lock-in exposure and the cost of switching each integration.
prompt_filtered: Lock-in risk only. Proprietary APIs, data portability, and switching cost.

title: Audit partner certification and integration quality standards
prompt: Review the standards applied to third-party integrations built by partners. Verify there is a certification process covering security review, API usage correctness, and performance testing. Identify certified integrations that have not been re-tested since a major API change, and uncertified integrations deployed in production.
prompt_short: Audit partner integration certification coverage and re-certification cadence.
prompt_filtered: Certification only. Partner integration quality gates and re-certification gaps.

title: Find integrations sending sensitive data to third parties without review
prompt: Identify integrations that transmit PII, payment data, or confidential business data to third-party services. For each, verify a data processing agreement (DPA) is in place, data minimization principles are applied (sending only the fields required), and the transfer is disclosed in the privacy policy. Flag any undocumented data transfers discovered through code analysis.
prompt_short: Find third-party integrations transmitting PII without a DPA or disclosure.
prompt_filtered: Data transfer only. PII, DPA coverage, and data minimization.

title: Evaluate integration authentication credential management
prompt: Audit how credentials for third-party integrations are stored and rotated. Verify API keys and OAuth tokens are in secrets management, not hardcoded in source. Find credentials that have never been rotated, tokens that have excessive scopes, and shared credentials used across environments. Assess the blast radius if a credential were compromised.
prompt_short: Audit third-party integration credential storage, scopes, and rotation.
prompt_filtered: Credential management only. Storage, rotation, and scope minimization.

title: Assess integration testing coverage in CI for third-party dependencies
prompt: Review how third-party integrations are tested in CI. Identify integrations tested only against live vendor endpoints (creating flaky tests tied to vendor availability), integrations with no contract tests, and integrations whose behavioral changes would not be caught until a production failure. Recommend mock-based contract testing for each critical integration.
prompt_short: Audit CI test coverage for third-party integrations and flaky live-endpoint tests.
prompt_filtered: Integration testing only. Mock coverage and contract tests in CI.

title: Review integration monitoring for anomaly detection and capacity planning
prompt: Evaluate monitoring for each external integration. Verify error rates, latency percentiles, and usage volume are tracked per integration. Identify integrations approaching rate limit thresholds with no alerting, integrations with no baseline metric to detect behavioral regressions, and integrations where a 10x usage spike would cause quota exhaustion.
prompt_short: Audit integration monitoring for rate limit headroom and anomaly detection.
prompt_filtered: Monitoring only. Error rates, latency, and rate limit headroom per integration.

title: Fill in Missing Data Gaps
prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

title: Gain full understanding before completing
prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

title: Find What's Broken
prompt: Find What's Broken

title: Recommend Next Steps
prompt: Recommend Next Steps

title: Score for Production Readiness
prompt: Score for Production Readiness

title: Inventory Active Integrations
prompt: Inventory Active Integrations

title: Find Vendor Lock-In Risk
prompt: Find Vendor Lock-In Risk

title: Audit Zombie Integration Cleanup
prompt: Audit Zombie Integration Cleanup

title: Map Data Privacy by Integration
prompt: Map Data Privacy by Integration

title: Review Credential Rotation Status
prompt: Review Credential Rotation Status

title: Find Hard-Fail Integration Points
prompt: Find Hard-Fail Integration Points

title: Assess Integration SLA Alignment
prompt: Assess Integration SLA Alignment

title: Inventory every third-party integration in production
prompt: Inventory every third-party integration in production. For each, verify active usage by traffic, credential validity by last-rotation date, and monitoring coverage by alert configuration. Identify zombie integrations where credentials are valid but no code path uses them, integrations with expired or near-expired credentials, and integrations with no error monitoring or fallback. Recommend the cleanup and hardening sequence.

title: Audit the resilience posture of the integration portfolio
prompt: Audit the resilience posture of the integration portfolio. Identify integrations that fail closed — propagating third-party downtime to the user — versus those that fail open with cached data, queued retries, or degraded modes. For each fail-closed integration, assess the third-party's historical uptime and the user-visible blast radius of an outage. Recommend the fallback strategy per integration based on criticality and acceptable degradation.

title: Assess the vendor lock-in risk across the integration portfolio
prompt: Assess the vendor lock-in risk across the integration portfolio. Identify integrations using proprietary APIs with no open equivalent, integrations storing business-critical data in vendor-controlled systems, and integrations with deep coupling to vendor-specific data models. For each high-lock-in integration, estimate migration cost if the vendor raised prices, was acquired, or shut down. Recommend the abstraction layer or escape-hatch design that would most reduce lock-in for the highest-risk integrations.

title: Audit the data privacy posture across the integration portfolio
prompt: Audit the data privacy posture across the integration portfolio. For each third-party integration, identify what user data is transmitted — explicitly in request bodies and implicitly via metadata, headers, or URL parameters. Verify a data processing agreement is in place, data minimization principles are applied, and each transfer is disclosed in the privacy policy. Find integrations sending PII to analytics or monitoring vendors without user consent. Recommend the data minimization changes that would most reduce privacy risk without breaking integration functionality.

title: Examine the integration credential lifecycle across all active integrati
prompt: Examine the integration credential lifecycle across all active integrations. Identify credentials that have never been rotated, credentials with scopes broader than the integration requires, and credentials shared across multiple environments without isolation. Assess the blast radius if each credential were compromised — which user data and business operations would be exposed. Verify instant revocation is possible for each credential type. Recommend the rotation schedule and scope-reduction plan that would most reduce credential exposure risk.

title: Evaluate the third-party integration portfolio holistically
prompt: Evaluate the third-party integration portfolio holistically. Inventory every integration with its purpose, data flow, credential ownership, monitoring status, and last security review date. Identify integrations that have outlived their original purpose, integrations duplicating capabilities of other integrations, and integrations with no clear business owner. Find integrations sharing credentials across environments, using long-lived API keys with no rotation plan, or holding broader permission scopes than their use case requires. Look for integrations sending user data to third parties without coverage in the privacy policy and integrations that would create compliance issues during a data subject access request. Examine whether integration health — uptime, latency, error rate — is monitored alongside owned services and whether incidents involving a third party trigger the same response process. Summarize the five integration portfolio improvements that would most reduce risk and operational burden.

title: Audit integration lifecycle management from selection to retirement
prompt: Audit integration lifecycle management from selection to retirement. Identify whether new vendor selection follows a structured evaluation — security review, data processing agreement, exit clause, integration cost estimate — or proceeds case by case. Find integrations introduced by individual teams with no architecture review, integrations with no contract renewal tracking, and integrations whose vendor has been acquired or pivoted away from the original use case. Assess whether vendor performance is reviewed periodically against alternatives — pricing, reliability, feature parity, and support quality. Look for opportunities to consolidate overlapping integrations, replace expensive vendors with open-source equivalents, and migrate vendor-locked data into platform-owned storage. Recommend the lifecycle process changes that would most improve vendor decisions and reduce accumulated integration debt.

title: Audit the integration testing strategy for all third-party dependencies
prompt: Audit the integration testing strategy for all third-party dependencies. Assess whether integrations are tested only against live vendor endpoints — creating test suite fragility tied to vendor availability — or whether contract tests against stable mocks form the primary test layer. Identify integrations with no tests at all, integrations whose error scenarios are never tested, and integrations that would not be caught by CI until a production failure. Find tests that call live payment or communication APIs during CI — incurring cost and side effects. Examine whether integration behavior regressions would be detected before a production release or only discovered by affected users. Look for testing patterns that make integration failures hard to diagnose — generic assertion failures with no request or response context, tests that catch exceptions but do not assert on failure mode behavior. Recommend the integration testing strategy changes that would most improve reliability and reduce vendor-induced CI flakiness.

title: Audit SDK ergonomics from a developer's perspective
prompt: Evaluate the SDK from a first-time developer's perspective. Identify: confusing method naming, missing abstractions requiring low-level API knowledge, verbose code for common operations, inadequate error messages, and missing quickstart examples.
prompt_short: Audit SDK usability from a new developer's perspective.
prompt_filtered: DX ergonomics only. Naming, abstractions, and verbosity.

title: Review SDK versioning and breaking change management
prompt: Audit the SDK release history for breaking changes. Verify that major version bumps accompany breaking changes, deprecation warnings exist before removal, and a migration guide is published with each breaking change. Identify undocumented breaking changes.
prompt_short: Audit SDK versioning for undocumented breaking changes and migration guides.
prompt_filtered: SDK versioning only. Breaking changes and migration documentation.

title: Find SDK methods with insufficient error information
prompt: Identify SDK methods that throw generic errors, return null silently, or provide error messages that don't help developers understand what they did wrong. Recommend specific error types and message improvements for the top 10 confusing errors.
prompt_short: Find SDK methods with unhelpful errors that frustrate developers.
prompt_filtered: Error quality only. Unhelpful SDK error messages and types.

title: Assess SDK test coverage and integration example quality
prompt: Review the SDK test suite and code examples. Identify untested edge cases, examples that demonstrate anti-patterns, and missing examples for the most common integration scenarios. Verify examples are runnable and up-to-date.
prompt_short: Audit SDK tests and examples for coverage and correctness.
prompt_filtered: SDK testing only. Test coverage and example runability.

title: Identify missing SDK capabilities blocking developer adoption
prompt: Survey developer feedback and support issues for the SDK. Identify the most frequently requested missing features, the workarounds developers use, and the capabilities that cause developers to abandon the SDK for a raw API approach.
prompt_short: Find missing SDK capabilities that are blocking developer adoption.
prompt_filtered: Feature gaps only. Most requested missing capabilities.

title: Audit SDK dependency tree for security vulnerabilities and bloat
prompt: Review the SDK's dependency tree: what are the transitive dependencies? Are there any known vulnerabilities? Is the SDK pulling in unnecessary dependencies (is it bloated)? For each dependency, is it actively maintained? Would upgrading break compatibility? Can developers use the SDK in size-constrained environments (mobile, serverless)?
prompt_short: Audit SDK dependencies for vulnerabilities, maintenance status, and bloat.
prompt_filtered: Dependency security and size only. Known vulnerabilities and bloat.

title: Review SDK error messages and error handling for developer experience
prompt: Evaluate SDK error messages: are they descriptive or cryptic? Do they suggest recovery actions? Are errors documented in the SDK docs? Can developers distinguish between network errors, auth errors, validation errors, and server errors? Are there error code constants or enums? Test error scenarios and assess usability.
prompt_short: Audit SDK error messages for clarity and actionability.
prompt_filtered: Error messages and handling only. Clarity and recovery guidance.

title: Assess SDK type safety and IDE autocomplete for developer productivity
prompt: For typed SDKs (TypeScript, Python type hints, Java): are types complete and accurate? Do generated types match the API spec? Is IDE autocomplete functional? Are there any 'any' types? For generated SDKs, is the generator kept in sync with API changes? Test IDE experience with the SDK.
prompt_short: Audit SDK type safety and IDE autocomplete quality.
prompt_filtered: Type safety only. Type completeness and IDE support.

title: Review SDK documentation and example code quality and freshness
prompt: Evaluate SDK documentation: are all public methods documented? Are there runnable examples for common use cases? Are examples kept up-to-date? Is there a migration guide for major version upgrades? Are there troubleshooting sections? Do docs include performance considerations and best practices?
prompt_short: Audit SDK documentation and example code for completeness.
prompt_filtered: Documentation and examples only. Completeness and freshness.

title: Assess SDK backward compatibility and version upgrade friction
prompt: Evaluate SDK versioning: how is semver applied? Are breaking changes documented in migration guides? How long are older major versions supported? Can developers upgrade without rewriting code? Are deprecation warnings provided in advance? How many versions back does the SDK maintain compatibility?
prompt_short: Audit SDK backward compatibility and upgrade friction.
prompt_filtered: Versioning and compatibility only. Breaking changes and migration paths.

title: Review SDK logging and debugging capabilities for developers
prompt: Evaluate debug support: can developers enable debug/verbose logging to see HTTP requests/responses? Is there request tracing? Can developers inspect request/response payloads? Can they set request/response interceptors? Is there a way to log to external systems (Sentry, etc.)? Are there debugging guides in the docs?
prompt_short: Audit SDK logging, request tracing, and debug capabilities.
prompt_filtered: Debugging and logging only. Verbose mode, tracing, and interceptors.

title: Evaluate SDK testing utilities and mocking for developer testing
prompt: Review testing support: does the SDK provide mock clients or test fixtures? Can developers test without hitting the live API? Are there test doubles for async operations? Can developers simulate error conditions in tests? Is there guidance on testing patterns? Are there official integrations with test frameworks?
prompt_short: Audit SDK testing utilities and mocking capabilities.
prompt_filtered: Testing support only. Mock clients, fixtures, and test utilities.

title: Audit SDK versioning strategy and semantic versioning compliance
prompt: Review SDK versioning. Verify semantic versioning is applied — breaking changes bump major, new capabilities bump minor, bug fixes bump patch. Identify releases where breaking changes shipped in a minor version. Assess whether consumers can safely use caret ranges or must pin exact versions.
prompt_short: Audit SDK version history for semantic versioning compliance.
prompt_filtered: Versioning compliance only. Breaking changes in minor releases and range safety.

title: Evaluate SDK code generation pipeline and API contract sync
prompt: Review whether SDK code is manually maintained or generated from the API contract (OpenAPI, Protobuf). Identify manually maintained methods that have drifted from the actual API behavior. Assess whether the codegen pipeline is automated in CI, and whether a contract change automatically triggers an SDK update.
prompt_short: Audit SDK codegen pipeline for API contract drift and automation.
prompt_filtered: Codegen only. Manual drift from API contract and CI pipeline status.

title: Assess language parity across supported SDK languages
prompt: Compare feature coverage across SDK languages. Identify APIs available in the REST API or one language SDK but not others. Find features released in the Python SDK months before the Java SDK. Assess whether each language SDK is maintained by the same team or by community contributors with different quality standards.
prompt_short: Audit feature parity and maintenance quality across all supported SDK languages.
prompt_filtered: Language parity only. Feature gaps and maintenance ownership per language.

title: Review SDK developer onboarding and time to first successful call
prompt: Measure or estimate time for a new developer to install the SDK and make a successful authenticated API call. Identify steps adding friction — prerequisite libraries, auth credential confusion, missing quickstart, or environment setup complexity. Compare against competitors' SDKs for the same task. Recommend the onboarding improvements that would most reduce time to first call.
prompt_short: Audit SDK onboarding friction and time to first successful API call.
prompt_filtered: Onboarding only. Install-to-first-call friction and quickstart quality.

title: Evaluate SDK retry and idempotency handling for transient failures
prompt: Assess whether the SDK handles transient failures automatically. Verify built-in retry with exponential backoff for appropriate status codes (429, 500, 503), idempotency key injection for mutating requests, and configurable retry limits. Find SDK methods that fail immediately on transient errors, forcing consumers to implement retry logic themselves.
prompt_short: Audit SDK built-in retry and idempotency handling for transient API failures.
prompt_filtered: Retry logic only. Built-in backoff, idempotency key, and configurable limits.

title: Audit SDK telemetry and observability instrumentation
prompt: Evaluate what the SDK emits for observability. Verify request/response events are traceable with OpenTelemetry or equivalent, latency per API call is measurable, and error details are structured for log aggregation. Identify SDKs that emit telemetry to the vendor by default without user consent — a privacy and security concern.
prompt_short: Audit SDK telemetry output for observability and privacy compliance.
prompt_filtered: Telemetry only. OpenTelemetry support and default data emission.

title: Review SDK bundle size and tree-shaking support for frontend consumers
prompt: Assess SDK bundle size impact for frontend consumers. Verify the SDK supports tree-shaking (ESM exports), that consumers can import only the methods they use, and that the full bundle size does not bloat the application. Identify SDKs that bundle all dependencies (including server-only ones) in a single file unsuitable for browser use.
prompt_short: Audit SDK bundle size and tree-shaking support for frontend usage.
prompt_filtered: Bundle size only. Tree-shaking, ESM exports, and browser suitability.

title: Assess SDK changelog quality and migration guide completeness
prompt: Review the SDK changelog for major and minor version bumps. Verify each breaking change includes a migration guide with before/after code examples. Identify versions where the changelog says 'breaking changes' but provides no remediation steps. Assess whether an automated migration codemod exists for high-traffic breaking changes.
prompt_short: Audit SDK changelog completeness and migration guide quality for breaking changes.
prompt_filtered: Changelog only. Breaking change documentation and migration codemods.
