title: Audit Bundle Size Impact
prompt: Audit Bundle Size Impact

title: Audit the SDK developer experience from first install to first successfu
prompt: Audit the SDK developer experience from first install to first successful API call. Walk through the quickstart with fresh eyes — install commands, initialization boilerplate, authentication setup, and the first meaningful operation. Identify steps requiring tribal knowledge, error messages that point in the wrong direction, and abstractions that leak underlying API mechanics into user code. Recommend the SDK changes that would most reduce time to first successful call.

title: Review the SDK's breaking change history and version migration support
prompt: Review the SDK's breaking change history and version migration support. Identify versions that introduced breaking changes without a major version bump, deprecation warnings shipped without a removal timeline, and migration guides that omit edge cases consumers actually hit. Find consumers still pinned to deprecated SDK versions past the migration window and the support burden each generates. Recommend the versioning policy that would most reduce consumer-facing breakage.

title: Examine the SDK feature gap against the underlying API surface
prompt: Examine the SDK feature gap against the underlying API surface. Identify API capabilities not exposed in the SDK, SDK methods that wrap the API in ways that lose functionality, and idioms developers consistently re-implement on top of the SDK. Cross-reference with developer feedback and support issues to find the missing capabilities driving the most frustration. Recommend the SDK extension priority list ranked by adoption impact.

title: Audit SDK type safety and IDE integration quality across supported langu
prompt: Audit SDK type safety and IDE integration quality across supported languages. Verify generated types match the current API spec — identify methods returning untyped results, parameters accepting overly broad types, and discriminated unions collapsing to any. Test IDE autocomplete on common operations and identify completions that suggest deprecated or incorrect methods. Find SDK consumers forced to cast results because the type does not match the actual response shape. Recommend the type system improvements that would most increase developer confidence when using the SDK.

title: Assess the SDK long-term maintenance health
prompt: Assess the SDK long-term maintenance health. Identify whether each supported language SDK has a named owner, a defined release cadence, and an open-issue triage process. Find SDK languages with open bugs older than 90 days that block production use cases, SDKs where the last release was over six months ago, and SDKs where the community has forked because official maintenance lapsed. Examine whether SDK releases are automated — triggered by API contract changes — or require manual effort that causes lag. Recommend the maintenance investments that would most reduce SDK abandonment and community fragmentation.

title: Evaluate the SDK as a developer-facing product
prompt: Evaluate the SDK as a developer-facing product. Assess the quality of the public surface — naming conventions, abstraction granularity, error type design, and consistency between methods that perform similar operations. Identify methods returning generic errors, methods accepting overly permissive types, and methods whose behavior changes based on parameters in ways that surprise readers. Find boilerplate the SDK forces on every consumer that could be hidden behind a sensible default. Examine documentation quality from the developer's perspective — whether examples are runnable, whether common use cases are addressed, and whether troubleshooting guidance exists for the top integration failures. Look for quality signals — test coverage on critical paths, integration tests against a real backend, and CI on every supported language version. Summarize the five SDK improvements that would most increase developer satisfaction and reduce support burden.

title: Audit SDK adoption health across the developer community
prompt: Audit SDK adoption health across the developer community. Measure the share of API traffic coming through the SDK versus direct API calls, broken down by language and SDK version. Identify languages with strong API traffic but weak SDK adoption — indicating the SDK is missing capabilities or is harder to use than the raw API. Find versions of the SDK still in production use long after their support window and the consumers running them. Assess whether release cadence matches the underlying API evolution — whether new API features ship with same-day SDK support or lag by quarters. Look for community contribution patterns — whether external developers are filing issues, submitting PRs, or maintaining language-specific forks. Recommend the SDK strategy changes that would most increase adoption and reduce direct-API usage.

title: Audit the SDK distribution, installation, and runtime compatibility stor
prompt: Audit the SDK distribution, installation, and runtime compatibility story. Assess whether the SDK is distributed through all expected package managers for each supported language — PyPI, npm, Maven, NuGet, RubyGems — and whether the package name is intuitive and discoverable. Verify installation instructions are accurate and complete for the minimum required runtime versions. Find runtime compatibility gaps — Node.js version constraints that exclude LTS users, Python version floors that force premature upgrades, or browser compatibility limitations undocumented for frontend consumers. Examine whether the SDK works in constrained runtimes — serverless functions, edge workers, mobile environments — or includes server-only dependencies that prevent browser use. Look for dependency conflicts that surface in real-world consumer projects — transitive version pins that clash with popular frameworks, peer dependencies that error on installation. Assess whether the SDK ships with type definitions bundled or requires a separate types package. Recommend the distribution and compatibility improvements that would most expand the SDK accessible audience.

title: Assess marketplace health and developer activation
prompt: Evaluate the extension or plugin marketplace. Measure developer activation rate (published at least one extension), listing quality, discovery mechanisms, and the conversion from developer sign-up to published listing. Identify the biggest activation bottleneck.
prompt_short: Assess marketplace developer activation rate and the biggest bottleneck.
prompt_filtered: Marketplace activation only. Developer funnel metrics.

title: Identify the highest-value missing ecosystem integrations
prompt: Analyze which integrations customers most frequently request or build themselves. Identify the top 10 missing first-party or certified integrations that would increase product stickiness, expand addressable market, or remove a common sales objection.
prompt_short: Find the highest-value integrations missing from the ecosystem.
prompt_filtered: Integration gaps only. Customer demand-driven priority list.

title: Review ecosystem developer documentation completeness
prompt: Audit the documentation available to ecosystem developers. Identify missing: API reference, webhook documentation, authentication guide, example apps, sandbox environment, rate limit information, and support channels. Score by developer journey impact.
prompt_short: Find gaps in ecosystem documentation that slow developer adoption.
prompt_filtered: Developer docs only. External developer journey documentation gaps.

title: Assess ecosystem governance and quality control
prompt: Review how ecosystem contributions are governed — submission review process, security audit requirements, quality standards, update requirements for breaking platform changes. Identify gaps that allow low-quality or insecure extensions to reach customers.
prompt_short: Review ecosystem governance for quality and security control gaps.
prompt_filtered: Governance only. Submission review and quality standard gaps.

title: Find ecosystem monetization and incentive alignment gaps
prompt: Assess whether ecosystem participants are sufficiently incentivized to build high-quality integrations. Identify gaps in revenue sharing, certification benefits, marketing co-investment, and partner tier structure that reduce the attractiveness of building in the ecosystem.
prompt_short: Find incentive gaps that reduce ecosystem partner motivation to build.
prompt_filtered: Incentive design only. Revenue sharing and partner benefit gaps.

title: Audit ecosystem partner qualification and onboarding process
prompt: Evaluate partner onboarding: are quality bars clear? Can anyone join the ecosystem or is there vetting? How long does onboarding take? Are there API access reviews? Do partners get security training? Are there compliance requirements (SOC 2, GDPR)? Is there an offboarding process for malicious actors?
prompt_short: Audit ecosystem partner qualification and onboarding quality bars.
prompt_filtered: Onboarding and qualification only. Vetting process and requirements.

title: Review ecosystem partner data access controls and API rate limits
prompt: Verify ecosystem partners have appropriate access controls: can partners access only their own data and their customers' data? Are there granular API scopes/permissions? Are rate limits enforced per-partner? Can you revoke access instantly if a partner is compromised? Are access patterns logged and monitored?
prompt_short: Audit ecosystem partner data isolation and API rate limiting.
prompt_filtered: Access control and isolation only. Scopes, rate limits, and revocation.

title: Assess ecosystem support and success resources for partner growth
prompt: Evaluate partner support: is there dedicated technical support for partners? Are there co-marketing opportunities? Is there a partner fund or grant program for development? Are there regular business reviews? Is there public recognition of successful partners? Are there training/certification programs?
prompt_short: Audit ecosystem partner support and success resources.
prompt_filtered: Partner support only. Technical support, co-marketing, and training.

title: Review ecosystem partner compliance and security requirements
prompt: Evaluate security standards: are partners required to maintain SOC 2, ISO 27001, or equivalent certifications? Are they audited? Is there a security incident response protocol? Can customers audit partner security posture? Are there regular security training requirements? How are vulnerabilities disclosed and patched?
prompt_short: Audit ecosystem partner security compliance and audit requirements.
prompt_filtered: Security compliance only. Certifications, audits, and incident response.

title: Assess ecosystem partner feedback loops and roadmap alignment
prompt: Evaluate partner voice: do you have a formal partner advisory board? Are partners involved in roadmap planning? Can partners request features or API changes? Is there a structured feedback process? Are feature requests from partners tracked and prioritized? How are decisions communicated back to partners?
prompt_short: Audit ecosystem partner feedback loops and roadmap alignment.
prompt_filtered: Partner feedback and input only. Advisory boards and roadmap involvement.

title: Review ecosystem marketplace or directory for partner visibility
prompt: Evaluate partner visibility: is there a public marketplace or directory? Can customers easily find and vet partners? Are there partner ratings or reviews? Is there a certification badge system? Are popular partners featured? Does the marketplace help customers match their needs with partner capabilities?
prompt_short: Audit ecosystem marketplace and partner visibility.
prompt_filtered: Marketplace and visibility only. Directory, reviews, and featured partners.

title: Evaluate ecosystem conflict of interest and self-preferencing risks
prompt: Assess conflicts of interest: does your company compete with partners (selling the same service)? Are your own products featured more prominently in the marketplace? Are there fair competition rules? How are conflicts handled? Is there transparency about your company's role in the ecosystem? Are there audit/compliance reviews for fairness?
prompt_short: Audit ecosystem for conflicts of interest and self-preferencing risks.
prompt_filtered: Competition and fairness only. Conflicts of interest and self-preferencing.

title: Assess marketplace discoverability and app ranking algorithm transparency
prompt: Evaluate how apps are discovered in the marketplace. Identify the ranking signals (reviews, installs, recency, revenue share) and whether they are documented for partners. Find ranking changes that dropped partner apps without explanation. Assess whether the algorithm can be gamed and whether organic and paid placements are clearly labeled.
prompt_short: Audit marketplace ranking algorithm transparency and discoverability fairness.
prompt_filtered: Ranking only. Discoverability signals and algorithm transparency for partners.

title: Review partner program tiers, benefits, and progression criteria
prompt: Assess the partner program structure. Verify tier progression criteria are clear and objective (not discretionary), that benefits at each tier (co-marketing, dedicated support, revenue share) are documented, and that partners understand what investment is required to advance. Find tier criteria that are undefined or applied inconsistently.
prompt_short: Audit partner program tier criteria, benefits, and progression transparency.
prompt_filtered: Program structure only. Tier requirements and benefit documentation.

title: Evaluate developer community health and engagement metrics
prompt: Measure developer community health: active community members, forum question response time, response rate, community-contributed resources (tutorials, open-source integrations), and net promoter score for the developer experience. Identify the engagement drop-off points where developers become inactive after initial signup.
prompt_short: Audit developer community engagement metrics and response quality.
prompt_filtered: Community health only. Engagement rates and developer NPS.

title: Identify ecosystem gaps where high-demand integrations are missing
prompt: Analyze the most frequently requested integrations from developer forums, support tickets, and partner conversations. Identify the top 10 missing integrations that would most expand the ecosystem's utility. For each, assess whether to build natively, incentivize a partner, or publish a reference integration for the community to fork.
prompt_short: Find the most-requested missing integrations limiting ecosystem utility.
prompt_filtered: Integration gaps only. High-demand missing capabilities and build vs partner decision.

title: Assess partner technical enablement and certification program quality
prompt: Review the technical enablement resources available to partners. Verify there is a certification program covering API usage, security requirements, and UX standards. Assess whether certification is a one-time gate or requires renewal. Identify the most common technical mistakes made by uncertified partners and whether the certification curriculum addresses them.
prompt_short: Audit partner certification curriculum and technical enablement completeness.
prompt_filtered: Enablement only. Certification curriculum and common partner technical failures.

title: Evaluate ecosystem revenue sharing model fairness and sustainability
prompt: Assess the revenue sharing model. Verify the take rate is competitive with comparable platforms, terms are transparent and stable, changes are communicated with sufficient notice, and there are no hidden fees or deductions. Find cases where revenue share terms changed retroactively, reducing partner earnings without renegotiation.
prompt_short: Audit ecosystem revenue share model for fairness and policy stability.
prompt_filtered: Revenue sharing only. Take rate, transparency, and retroactive change history.

title: Review ecosystem API stability commitments for long-lived partner integrations
prompt: Assess whether the platform provides long-term API stability commitments to partners. Identify the minimum deprecation notice window, whether there are extended support tracks for partners unable to migrate quickly, and whether breaking API changes have caused partner churn. Recommend stability guarantees appropriate for enterprise partner integrations.
prompt_short: Audit API stability commitments for long-lived partner integrations.
prompt_filtered: Partner API stability only. Deprecation notice and extended support tracks.

title: Assess ecosystem security review process for marketplace apps
prompt: Review the security review process for apps published in the marketplace. Verify there is a static or dynamic security review before publication, app permissions are scoped to what they claim to use, user data access is audited, and malicious or compromised apps can be removed quickly. Find apps with excessive permission scopes relative to their stated functionality.
prompt_short: Audit marketplace app security review and permission scope enforcement.
prompt_filtered: App security only. Review gate, permission scopes, and removal process.

title: Fill in Missing Data Gaps
prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

title: Gain full understanding before completing
prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

title: Find What's Broken
prompt: Find What's Broken

title: Recommend Next Steps
prompt: Recommend Next Steps

title: Score for Production Readiness
prompt: Score for Production Readiness

title: Assess Marketplace Health
prompt: Assess Marketplace Health

title: Find Highest-Value Ecosystem Gaps
prompt: Find Highest-Value Ecosystem Gaps

title: Find Partner Churn Signals
prompt: Find Partner Churn Signals

title: Audit API Stability Commitments
prompt: Audit API Stability Commitments

title: Review Revenue Share Fairness
prompt: Review Revenue Share Fairness

title: Map Community Engagement Drop-offs
prompt: Map Community Engagement Drop-offs

title: Assess Security Review Gaps
prompt: Assess Security Review Gaps

title: Audit the ecosystem developer activation funnel from sign-up to publishe
prompt: Audit the ecosystem developer activation funnel from sign-up to published listing. Measure conversion at each step — account creation, sandbox access, first API call, first listing draft, first published listing. Identify the step with the highest drop-off and the root cause — documentation gap, tooling friction, certification overhead, or unclear monetization. Recommend the activation improvement that would most increase published listing volume.

title: Identify the highest-value missing first-party or certified integrations
prompt: Identify the highest-value missing first-party or certified integrations by cross-referencing customer requests, sales objections, and competitor ecosystem gaps. Rank by revenue impact, retention impact, and addressable market expansion. For each top integration, assess build vs partner vs encourage-third-party. Recommend the integration priority sequence for the next four quarters.

title: Review ecosystem governance for quality and security control
prompt: Review ecosystem governance for quality and security control. Audit the listing submission review process — security scan coverage, breaking platform change notification to developers, quality standards enforcement, and removal process for abandoned listings. Find listings with security vulnerabilities, listings broken by recent platform changes, and listings with no developer activity in over a year. Recommend the governance changes that would most improve marketplace quality.

title: Audit the ecosystem security review process for all marketplace apps
prompt: Audit the ecosystem security review process for all marketplace apps. Verify each app submission goes through a security scan covering permission scope validation, data access audit, and malicious pattern detection. Identify apps with excessive permissions relative to their stated functionality, apps that were certified before a major platform API change and have not been re-reviewed, and apps with public security disclosures that remain available in the marketplace. Assess how quickly a compromised or malicious app can be removed and affected users notified. Recommend the security review process changes that would most reduce marketplace security risk.

title: Evaluate ecosystem partner retention and churn risk
prompt: Evaluate ecosystem partner retention and churn risk. Identify partners who were active six months ago and are now inactive — no API calls, no listing updates, no community engagement. For each churned or at-risk partner, assess whether the cause was revenue economics, platform API instability, lack of marketing support, or a competing ecosystem opportunity. Find the partner tier with the highest churn rate and the program investment that would most improve retention. Assess whether partner satisfaction is measured through structured surveys or only inferred through activity signals. Recommend the partner success initiatives that would most reduce churn among high-value ecosystem contributors.

title: Evaluate the ecosystem strategy as a growth engine
prompt: Evaluate the ecosystem strategy as a growth engine. Assess whether the ecosystem expands the product's addressable market by reaching customers the platform team could not, whether it deepens product stickiness through integrations into customer workflows, and whether it generates direct revenue through marketplace economics. Identify the partner tiers and qualification criteria — whether they reward investment in the platform or function as marketing labels with no operational benefit. Find friction in the partner journey from interest to certification — sales coordination, technical onboarding, marketing co-investment, and revenue distribution. Look for partner segments — agencies, ISVs, individual developers, system integrators — that the current program serves well versus segments that drop out at predictable points. Examine whether ecosystem health metrics — partner activation, listing quality, customer-installed integrations per account, partner-driven revenue — are reviewed at the leadership level. Summarize the five ecosystem strategy improvements that would most increase ecosystem-driven growth.

title: Audit the ecosystem incentive design for alignment with platform success
prompt: Audit the ecosystem incentive design for alignment with platform success. Identify whether revenue sharing rewards partners proportional to the value they create, whether certification benefits — co-marketing, technical support, early access — are meaningful enough to justify the investment, and whether the partner tier structure creates a clear progression with concrete benefits at each level. Find incentive gaps that cause high-quality partners to deprioritize the platform — competitor offers paying more, easier paths to customer access on alternative platforms, or platform changes that erode their value. Assess whether the platform's commercial terms — exclusivity, data access, customer ownership — are competitive against alternative ecosystems partners can join. Look for opportunities to differentiate the program with capabilities competitors cannot match — deeper data access, co-selling support, or platform investment in partner success. Recommend the incentive design changes that would most improve partner quality and platform commitment.

title: Audit the ecosystem platform APIs for long-term partner stability and tr
prompt: Audit the ecosystem platform APIs for long-term partner stability and trust. Identify the deprecation and stability guarantees published to partners — minimum notice windows, extended support tracks, breaking change communication process. Find API endpoints used by high-traffic partner integrations that have been deprecated or modified without adequate partner notification. Assess whether partners have a structured channel to report API issues, request features, or flag breaking changes before they reach production. Look for ecosystem API usage patterns that reveal unintended partner workarounds — undocumented endpoints being called, parameters being used outside their intended contract, rate limits being hit as a side effect of poor API design. Examine whether partner API usage is monitored to proactively detect integrations likely to break on upcoming platform changes. Recommend the API stability practices that would most increase partner confidence and reduce ecosystem-driven support volume.

title: Audit notification types for signal vs noise ratio
prompt: Review all notification types the application sends. For each, assess whether users find it valuable or treat it as noise. Identify notifications with high dismiss rates, high unsubscribe rates, and notifications that users report as unexpected or irrelevant.
prompt_short: Find notifications users treat as noise and audit signal vs dismiss rate.
prompt_filtered: Notification value only. Signal-to-noise ratio per notification type.

title: Review notification delivery reliability across channels
prompt: Assess delivery success rates across all notification channels — email, push, SMS, in-app. Identify channels with high failure rates, missing delivery confirmation tracking, and channels with no fallback when the primary channel fails.
prompt_short: Audit notification delivery success rates and find missing fallbacks.
prompt_filtered: Delivery reliability only. Success rates and channel fallbacks.

title: Find notifications lacking user preference controls
prompt: Identify notification types that users cannot opt out of, cannot control frequency for, or cannot redirect to a different channel. For each, assess whether making it mandatory is legally required or simply an oversight. Recommend preference controls per type.
prompt_short: Find notifications users cannot control or opt out of.
prompt_filtered: Preference control only. Opt-out and frequency control gaps.

title: Assess notification timing and frequency optimization
prompt: Review when and how often notifications are sent. Identify users receiving too many notifications in short windows (fatigue risk), notifications sent at inappropriate times (3am local time), and opportunities to batch notifications into digests.
prompt_short: Audit notification timing and frequency for fatigue and appropriate send times.
prompt_filtered: Timing and frequency only. Fatigue risk and send time optimization.

title: Review notification content for personalization gaps
prompt: Assess the personalization of notification content. Identify notifications with generic copy that could be significantly more relevant with user-specific context, and notifications that reference the user by name but contain no other personalization.
prompt_short: Find notifications with generic copy that should be personalized.
prompt_filtered: Content personalization only. Generic notifications with personalization opportunity.

title: Audit notification delivery mechanism and reliability
prompt: Evaluate notification delivery: are notifications delivered in-app, via email, SMS, push, or all channels? Are delivery guarantees at-least-once (duplicates possible) or exactly-once? If delivery fails, is there retry logic with exponential backoff? Are undelivered notifications logged? Can users see failed delivery attempts?
prompt_short: Audit notification delivery mechanisms and reliability guarantees.
prompt_filtered: Delivery mechanism only. Reliability, retries, and failure logging.

title: Review notification timing and frequency for user experience
prompt: Assess notification timing: are notifications sent at appropriate times (respecting quiet hours or user preferences)? Is there frequency capping to prevent notification fatigue? Do batching rules exist (group related notifications)? Are there preference controls for notification cadence? Do users opt-out entirely for specific notification types?
prompt_short: Audit notification timing, frequency, and fatigue prevention.
prompt_filtered: Timing and frequency only. Quiet hours, batching, and preference controls.

title: Assess notification data privacy and PII handling in content
prompt: Review PII in notifications: are sensitive details exposed in push notifications (visible on lock screen)? Are emails archived by providers (Gmail, Outlook)? Is PII encrypted in transit? Can users disable certain notifications to reduce exposure? Are there compliance considerations (GDPR right to erasure of notification history)?
prompt_short: Audit notification privacy and PII exposure in visible content.
prompt_filtered: Privacy and PII only. Sensitive data in visible notifications.

title: Review notification preferences UI and user control over channels
prompt: Evaluate notification preference controls: can users disable specific notification types? Can they choose delivery channels per notification type? Is the preference UI easy to find and understand? Are preferences persisted correctly? Do preference changes apply immediately? Can users bulk-enable or bulk-disable all notifications?
prompt_short: Audit notification preferences UI and channel control options.
prompt_filtered: User preferences only. Granularity of controls and UI clarity.

title: Assess notification analytics and performance metrics tracking
prompt: Evaluate tracking: are you measuring notification open rates, click-through rates, and conversion? Do you track delivery success rates per channel? Are there metrics on user preference changes? Can you identify which notification types drive engagement vs. unsubscribes? Are these insights used to improve future notifications?
prompt_short: Audit notification analytics and performance metrics tracking.
prompt_filtered: Analytics and metrics only. Open rates, click-through, and delivery success.

title: Review notification template management and localization
prompt: Evaluate notification template system: are templates centrally managed? Can non-engineers update templates? Are templates localized (i18n)? Are templates versioned? Is there A/B testing capability? Are templates previewed before sending? Are there guardrails to prevent malformed templates from being deployed?
prompt_short: Audit notification template management and i18n support.
prompt_filtered: Template management only. Central management, localization, and versioning.

title: Evaluate notification unsubscribe flows and one-click opt-out
prompt: Assess unsubscribe: can users one-click unsubscribe from emails (compliant with CAN-SPAM)? Is there a dedicated unsubscribe landing page? Can users unsubscribe from specific types only or all? Is unsubscribe processing instant? Are bounced emails automatically unsubscribed? Is there a way to re-subscribe if they change their mind?
prompt_short: Audit notification unsubscribe flows and compliance with anti-spam laws.
prompt_filtered: Unsubscribe flows only. One-click opt-out and CAN-SPAM compliance.

title: Audit notification digest design for reduced fatigue
prompt: Evaluate whether the notification system supports digest delivery — batching multiple events into a single daily or weekly summary instead of individual real-time notifications. Identify notification types where digest is appropriate (low-urgency activity) vs real-time is required (security, DMs). Assess whether users can choose digest frequency per notification category.
prompt_short: Audit digest notification design for high-volume notification fatigue reduction.
prompt_filtered: Digest design only. Batch frequency options and per-category user control.

title: Review per-user notification preference granularity and UI
prompt: Evaluate notification preference controls. Verify users can configure preferences per notification type, per channel (email, push, in-app, SMS), and per frequency. Find notification types with no opt-out, binary all-or-nothing controls where granular control is appropriate, and preference UIs that are hard to find or understand. Assess whether preference changes take effect immediately.
prompt_short: Audit notification preference controls for granularity and discoverability.
prompt_filtered: Preference UI only. Per-type, per-channel, and per-frequency control options.

title: Assess notification channel routing logic and fallback chains
prompt: Review how the system decides which channel to use for each notification. Verify there is a defined priority order (push > email > SMS), that fallback to the next channel occurs when the primary is unavailable (push token expired), and that over-notification through multiple channels for the same event is prevented. Find notifications firing on all channels simultaneously without routing logic.
prompt_short: Audit notification channel routing and cross-channel fallback logic.
prompt_filtered: Channel routing only. Priority order, fallback chains, and multi-channel deduplication.

title: Evaluate do-not-disturb and quiet hours implementation
prompt: Assess whether the notification system respects user-defined quiet hours or a global do-not-disturb setting. Verify notifications queue during quiet hours and deliver at the next allowed time, that urgent notifications (security alerts, account lockouts) bypass quiet hours with explicit disclosure, and that timezone-aware scheduling is used for digest and scheduled notifications.
prompt_short: Audit quiet hours enforcement and timezone-aware notification scheduling.
prompt_filtered: Quiet hours only. DND enforcement, urgent bypass, and timezone scheduling.

title: Review notification content quality and actionability
prompt: Evaluate the content of each notification type. Verify notifications include enough context to be actionable without opening the app, subject lines are specific (not generic like 'You have an update'), deep links take the user directly to the relevant resource, and notification copy is consistent in tone and length. Find notifications that cannot be acted on without full context.
prompt_short: Audit notification copy quality and deep link actionability.
prompt_filtered: Content quality only. Specificity, deep links, and action-without-app-open.

title: Audit push notification token lifecycle management
prompt: Review push token management. Verify stale or invalid tokens are detected via delivery failure responses and removed from the database, that device registration is refreshed on app update, and that a user uninstalling and reinstalling gets a fresh token associated correctly. Find delivery pipelines sending to millions of stale tokens, inflating costs and delivery latency.
prompt_short: Audit push token lifecycle for stale token detection and cleanup.
prompt_filtered: Token lifecycle only. Stale detection, removal, and re-registration.

title: Assess notification deliverability and email reputation health
prompt: Review email notification deliverability. Verify SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records are configured, bounce and complaint rates are below thresholds (bounce <2%, complaint <0.1%), suppression lists are maintained, and sending reputation is monitored via a dedicated inbox tool. Find notification types with high unsubscribe rates indicating content-preference mismatch.
prompt_short: Audit email notification deliverability, SPF/DKIM/DMARC, and reputation health.
prompt_filtered: Email deliverability only. Bounce rates, complaint rates, and DNS auth records.

title: Evaluate notification analytics and engagement tracking
prompt: Assess notification analytics coverage. Verify delivery rate, open rate, click-through rate, and opt-out rate are tracked per notification type and per channel. Identify notification types with low open rates (content mismatch or fatigue) or high opt-out rates (over-sending). Find notification types with no engagement tracking making it impossible to assess their value.
prompt_short: Audit notification engagement analytics coverage per type and channel.
prompt_filtered: Analytics only. Delivery, open, CTR, and opt-out rates per notification type.

prompt: Review Push Token Staleness

prompt: Map Quiet Hours Enforcement

title: Assess Digest Design Options
prompt: Assess Digest Design Options

title: Audit every notification type for signal-to-noise ratio
prompt: Audit every notification type for signal-to-noise ratio. Measure dismiss rate, unsubscribe rate, and downstream click-through per notification type. Identify notifications with high dismiss or unsubscribe rates that indicate users find them irrelevant or annoying. Cross-reference with user complaints and support tickets mentioning notification fatigue. Recommend the notification types to retire, throttle, or redesign for higher relevance.

title: Review notification delivery reliability across all channels — email, pu
prompt: Review notification delivery reliability across all channels — email, push, SMS, in-app. Measure delivery success rate per channel, identify channels with degrading deliverability, and find notification types missing fallback to a secondary channel when the primary fails. Examine sender reputation health for email and quota usage for SMS. Recommend the delivery infrastructure improvements that would most reduce silent notification loss.

title: Examine notification preference controls from the user perspective
prompt: Examine notification preference controls from the user perspective. Identify notification types users cannot opt out of, cannot change frequency for, cannot redirect to a different channel, and cannot snooze during quiet hours. Cross-reference with regulatory requirements that mandate opt-out for marketing-class notifications. Recommend the preference control additions that would most reduce user-perceived intrusion while preserving operationally critical notifications.

title: Audit notification content quality and actionability across all notifica
prompt: Audit notification content quality and actionability across all notification types. Identify notifications with subject lines so generic that users cannot act without opening the app, notifications that deep-link to a generic home screen instead of the specific resource referenced, and notifications referencing user-generated content without enough context to be understood at a glance. Find notifications that arrive after the actionable window has closed — a reminder for an event that already passed, an alert for a condition that self-resolved. Recommend the content and deep-link improvements that would most increase notification click-through rate.

title: Review notification regulatory compliance across all markets served
prompt: Review notification regulatory compliance across all markets served. Identify notification types classified as commercial communications that require prior opt-in consent under GDPR or CAN-SPAM, and verify consent was captured with the required granularity and stored with a timestamp. Find notifications sent to users in California without CCPA-compliant opt-out paths, and notifications sent to users in the EU without a GDPR-compliant consent record. Assess whether consent records are exportable to satisfy data subject access requests. Recommend the consent architecture changes that would most reduce regulatory risk across notification channels.

title: Evaluate the notification system as a user trust mechanism
prompt: Evaluate the notification system as a user trust mechanism. Assess whether each notification type passes a clear value test — whether the user is better off receiving it than not, whether the timing matches the moment value is delivered, and whether the channel matches the urgency. Identify notification fatigue patterns — users receiving multiple notifications in short windows, daily digests duplicating content already seen in real time, and recurring notifications that ignore prior dismissal. Find notifications sent at inappropriate times for the user's local timezone or stated quiet hours. Examine the personalization layer — whether content references user-specific context that increases relevance or relies on generic templates that read as machine-generated. Look for system gaps — notifications that fire on backend events users don't care about, notifications that fail to fire on user-actionable events, and notifications that arrive after the actionable window has closed. Summarize the five notification system improvements that would most increase user trust.

title: Audit the notification preference and consent architecture
prompt: Audit the notification preference and consent architecture. Identify whether users can express granular preferences — per notification type, per channel, per frequency, per time window — or are limited to a coarse global on-off switch. Find notification types that bypass user preferences for operational reasons and assess whether the bypass is justified or convenient. Examine preference defaults for new users and whether they err toward respect or toward maximum engagement. Look for opt-out paths — whether unsubscribe links work end to end, whether preference changes propagate quickly across all delivery infrastructure, and whether opted-out users still receive notifications through edge-case code paths. Assess regulatory compliance — GDPR, CAN-SPAM, telecommunications consent — and whether the audit trail proves consent was captured. Recommend the preference architecture changes that would most improve user agency and reduce regulatory risk.

title: Audit the notification system for regulatory compliance, consent archite
prompt: Audit the notification system for regulatory compliance, consent architecture, and user rights. Assess whether every notification type that constitutes a commercial communication has verifiable opt-in consent captured at the correct granularity — per notification type, per channel, per jurisdiction. Find notifications sent after a user opted out, notifications for which consent records cannot be produced for a data subject access request, and notifications that bundle marketing content with transactional content in ways that obscure the opt-out path. Examine whether the suppression list is maintained correctly — bounced email addresses permanently suppressed, SMS opt-outs processed in real time, push tokens invalidated on uninstall. Look for compliance gaps introduced by third-party notification services — whether vendor data retention policies align with your own, whether user identifiers sent to vendors are covered by the data processing agreement. Summarize the five compliance improvements that would most reduce regulatory risk and restore user trust in notification practices.

title: Audit WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across the application
prompt: Assess the application against WCAG 2.1 AA success criteria. Check all four principles: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Conformant. Identify failures by criterion number, affected components, and remediation complexity.
prompt_short: Audit WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across all four principles.
prompt_filtered: WCAG compliance only. Criterion failures with component references.

title: Find interactive elements unreachable by keyboard navigation
prompt: Test all interactive elements for keyboard accessibility. Identify elements that cannot be reached via Tab, elements with no visible focus indicator, keyboard traps, and modal dialogs that don't manage focus correctly. Prioritize by user journey impact.
prompt_short: Find all keyboard navigation failures and focus management issues.
prompt_filtered: Keyboard nav only. Tab order, focus indicators, and keyboard traps.

title: Audit color contrast ratios across the UI
prompt: Check color contrast ratios for all text and UI elements. Identify text with contrast below 4.5:1 (normal text) or 3:1 (large text), interactive elements with insufficient focus indicator contrast, and color-only information that fails for color-blind users.
prompt_short: Audit all text and UI elements for sufficient color contrast ratios.
prompt_filtered: Color contrast only. Ratio failures per element type.

title: Review ARIA labels and semantic HTML usage
prompt: Audit ARIA usage and semantic HTML. Identify: interactive elements with no accessible name, ARIA roles misapplied, missing landmark regions, images without alt text, and areas using divs where semantic HTML would be more appropriate. Check for ARIA anti-patterns.
prompt_short: Audit ARIA labels, semantic HTML, and screen reader accessibility.
prompt_filtered: ARIA and semantics only. Accessible names and landmark coverage.

title: Test screen reader experience for the core user flow
prompt: Walk through the core user flow with a screen reader. Identify: navigation confusion, missing announcements for dynamic content changes, form labels not connected to inputs, error messages not announced, and loading states with no accessible progress indication.
prompt_short: Audit screen reader experience for the core user flow end to end.
prompt_filtered: Screen reader only. Dynamic content, form labels, and announcements.

title: Audit form accessibility including label association and error announcement
prompt: Review all forms for accessibility. Verify every input has a programmatically associated label (not just a placeholder), error messages are associated with the field via aria-describedby and announced on submission, required fields are indicated with text (not color or asterisk alone), and autocomplete attributes are set for personal data fields.
prompt_short: Audit form labels, error announcements, and required field indicators for accessibility.
prompt_filtered: Form accessibility only. Label association, error messages, and autocomplete.

title: Review motion and animation against prefers-reduced-motion
prompt: Identify all animations, transitions, auto-playing carousels, and parallax effects. Verify each respects the prefers-reduced-motion media query by disabling or significantly reducing motion when the user has requested it. Find animations that trigger vestibular symptoms — large-scale motion, rapid flashing, or elements moving across the full viewport.
prompt_short: Audit all animations for prefers-reduced-motion compliance.
prompt_filtered: Motion accessibility only. Reduced-motion media query and vestibular triggers.

title: Assess touch target sizes and spacing for mobile accessibility
prompt: Evaluate touch target sizes on mobile. Verify interactive elements meet the WCAG 2.5.5 target size of at least 44x44 CSS pixels, that closely spaced targets have sufficient spacing to prevent mis-taps, and that touch-only interactions have keyboard equivalents. Find icon-only buttons and toolbar items that are too small for users with motor impairments.
prompt_short: Audit touch target sizes and spacing against WCAG 2.5.5 mobile accessibility.
prompt_filtered: Touch targets only. Size, spacing, and motor accessibility on mobile.

title: Review focus order and logical reading sequence
prompt: Verify the tab order follows a logical reading sequence through the page. Identify focus jumps that skip content or move backward unexpectedly, elements that appear visually before others but receive focus after, and CSS-reordered content (flexbox/grid order property) where visual order diverges from DOM order, breaking keyboard navigation.
prompt_short: Audit focus order and reading sequence for logical keyboard navigation.
prompt_filtered: Focus order only. Tab sequence and CSS order vs DOM order divergence.

title: Audit image and icon accessibility including decorative image handling
prompt: Review all images and icons. Verify informative images have descriptive alt text, decorative images have empty alt="" (not role="presentation" alone), icon buttons have an accessible name via aria-label or visually hidden text, and SVG icons include a title or aria-label. Find images whose alt text is the filename, URL, or the word 'image'.
prompt_short: Audit image and icon alt text quality and decorative image handling.
prompt_filtered: Image accessibility only. Alt text quality and icon accessible names.

title: Evaluate data table accessibility with headers and scope
prompt: Review all data tables. Verify column and row headers use <th> with appropriate scope (col, row, colgroup, rowgroup), complex tables use headers and id attributes, caption or aria-label describes the table purpose, and data cells are associated with headers in multi-level header tables. Find tables using divs and CSS grid that screen readers cannot interpret as tabular data.
prompt_short: Audit data table header associations and screen reader accessibility.
prompt_filtered: Table accessibility only. TH scope, header association, and caption.

title: Assess PDF and document accessibility for downloadable content
prompt: Evaluate accessibility of downloadable documents — PDFs, Word files, spreadsheets. Verify PDFs are tagged (document structure, alt text for images, reading order), documents have bookmarks for navigation, and where interactive PDFs are used (forms), form fields are labeled. Identify scanned PDFs with no text layer that are inaccessible to screen readers.
prompt_short: Audit PDF and downloadable document accessibility and tagging.
prompt_filtered: Document accessibility only. PDF tags, reading order, and form fields.

title: Review timeout and session expiration for cognitive accessibility
prompt: Identify all session timeouts, form submission windows, and timed interactions. Verify users are warned before expiration with sufficient time to respond, there is an option to extend the session, and form data is not lost on timeout. Find flows where users with cognitive disabilities or slow reading speed would consistently lose work due to tight timeouts.
prompt_short: Audit session timeouts and timed interactions for cognitive accessibility compliance.
prompt_filtered: Timing only. WCAG 2.2.1 timeout warnings and extension options.

title: Audit error prevention and recovery for complex transactions
prompt: Review complex or irreversible transactions — purchases, account deletion, mass actions. Verify there is a confirmation step with a summary before submission, a way to correct errors before completion, and an undo or reversal path where technically possible. Assess whether error prevention is proportionate to the severity of the action.
prompt_short: Audit error prevention and recovery steps for irreversible transactions.
prompt_filtered: Error prevention only. Confirmation steps and reversibility for complex actions.

title: Evaluate accessible name computation for complex components
prompt: Identify complex interactive components — tab panels, accordions, carousels, date pickers, comboboxes. Verify the accessible name of each interactive element is computed correctly by the browser, that state changes (expanded/collapsed, selected/unselected) are announced via aria-expanded, aria-selected, or aria-checked, and that component roles are appropriate.
prompt_short: Audit complex component ARIA roles and state announcement for screen readers.
prompt_filtered: Component ARIA only. State changes, roles, and accessible name computation.

title: Assess cognitive load and plain language for content accessibility
prompt: Evaluate whether the application's language and content structure supports users with cognitive disabilities. Identify error messages using technical jargon, instructions requiring specialized knowledge, multi-step processes with no progress indicator, and content with reading grade level above WCAG 3.1.5 recommendation (lower secondary education). Recommend plain language improvements.
prompt_short: Audit content plain language and cognitive load for cognitive accessibility.
prompt_filtered: Cognitive accessibility only. Language complexity, instructions, and progress indication.

title: Review color as sole information carrier across the UI
prompt: Find all places where color is the only means of conveying information — red/green status indicators, chart series distinguished by color alone, required field asterisks in red with no text label, and error states communicated only through border color change. Verify each has a non-color reinforcement (icon, pattern, text label, or shape).
prompt_short: Audit UI for information conveyed by color alone with no non-color alternative.
prompt_filtered: Color dependency only. Information conveyed solely through color.

title: Evaluate skip navigation and landmark region implementation
prompt: Verify the page has a visible skip-to-main-content link as the first focusable element, landmark regions (header, main, nav, aside, footer) are used correctly and not duplicated without unique labels, and the navigation landmark contains only navigation. Find pages where keyboard users must tab through 30+ navigation items before reaching content.
prompt_short: Audit skip navigation links and landmark region structure for keyboard users.
prompt_filtered: Navigation landmarks only. Skip links and landmark region labeling.

title: Fill in Missing Data Gaps
prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps

title: Gain full understanding before completing
prompt: Gain full understanding before completing

title: Find What's Broken
prompt: Find What's Broken

title: Recommend Next Steps
prompt: Recommend Next Steps

title: Score for Production Readiness
prompt: Score for Production Readiness

title: Audit WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance
prompt: Audit WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance

title: Find Keyboard Navigation Gaps
prompt: Find Keyboard Navigation Gaps

title: Audit Touch Target Sizes
prompt: Audit Touch Target Sizes

title: Find Color-Only Information
prompt: Find Color-Only Information

title: Review Prefers-Reduced-Motion
prompt: Review Prefers-Reduced-Motion

title: Audit Focus Order Logic
prompt: Audit Focus Order Logic

title: Find PDF Accessibility Gaps
prompt: Find PDF Accessibility Gaps

title: Audit the application against WCAG 2
prompt: Audit the application against WCAG 2.1 AA across all four principles — Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust. Identify failures by criterion number, list affected components or pages, and assess remediation complexity. Cross-reference automated scan output with manual testing results to catch issues automated tools miss. Recommend the remediation sequence prioritized by user journey impact and complexity.

title: Review keyboard accessibility across the application
prompt: Review keyboard accessibility across the application. Test every interactive element for Tab reachability, visible focus indication, expected activation behavior, and absence of keyboard traps. Identify modal dialogs that fail to trap focus, dropdowns with no keyboard equivalent, and skip-link gaps that force keyboard users to traverse repeated navigation. Recommend the keyboard accessibility fixes that would most unblock keyboard-only users on the core user flows.

title: Examine screen reader experience end to end on the core user flow
prompt: Examine screen reader experience end to end on the core user flow. Identify dynamic content changes without ARIA live region announcements, form errors not announced after submission, loading states with no accessible progress indication, and modal dialogs that fail to manage focus on open and close. Cross-reference with semantic HTML usage to find divs hosting interactive behavior that should use semantic elements. Recommend the screen reader fixes that would most improve assistive technology user experience.

title: Assess mobile accessibility across core user flows on touch devices
prompt: Assess mobile accessibility across core user flows on touch devices. Verify all interactive elements meet the 44x44 CSS pixel minimum touch target, that closely spaced controls have adequate spacing to prevent mis-taps, and that touch-only gestures have keyboard equivalents. Test the core flow with iOS VoiceOver and Android TalkBack, identifying announcements that are missing, wrong, or duplicated. Find icon-only action buttons without accessible names and form elements without visible labels on small screens. Recommend the mobile accessibility fixes that would most improve the experience for users relying on assistive technology on mobile.

title: Review the accessibility testing strategy and tooling coverage
prompt: Review the accessibility testing strategy and tooling coverage. Identify whether automated accessibility tests run in CI on every pull request, whether the test suite covers color contrast, ARIA roles, focus management, and keyboard navigation, and whether manual testing with screen readers is performed before significant releases. Find accessibility issues that could not have been detected by automated tools — incorrect reading order, confusing focus management in modals, dynamic content not announced. Assess whether designers receive accessibility training and whether design specs include accessibility requirements. Recommend the accessibility testing investments that would most reduce the rate of accessibility regressions shipped to production.

title: Evaluate the accessibility posture of the application as both a complian
prompt: Evaluate the accessibility posture of the application as both a compliance matter and a usability matter. Assess WCAG 2.1 AA conformance across Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, and Robust principles, identifying systematic failure patterns rather than isolated issues. Find color contrast failures across text, interactive elements, focus indicators, and information conveyed by color alone. Identify keyboard accessibility gaps — elements unreachable by Tab, missing focus indicators, keyboard traps, and modal focus mismanagement. Examine screen reader experience for missing accessible names on icon buttons, ARIA roles misapplied to non-interactive elements, missing landmark regions that hinder navigation, and dynamic content changes that fail to announce. Look for form accessibility gaps — labels not programmatically connected to inputs, error messages presented visually without screen reader announcement, and required field indication that relies on color or asterisk alone. Summarize the five accessibility improvements that would most reduce barriers for users relying on assistive technology.

title: Audit the accessibility practice across the development lifecycle
prompt: Audit the accessibility practice across the development lifecycle. Identify whether accessibility requirements are captured in design specs, whether automated accessibility tests run in CI for every change, and whether manual testing with assistive technology is performed before significant releases. Find component library elements that ship with accessibility issues — unlabeled controls, low-contrast variants, motion that violates reduced-motion preferences. Assess whether developers receive training on common accessibility patterns and whether accessibility code review checks are part of the PR template. Look for accessibility regression patterns — features that shipped accessible and degraded over time as the surrounding code changed. Examine whether accessibility issues reported by users are routed, prioritized, and resolved with the same rigor as functional bugs. Recommend the accessibility practice changes that would most reduce the rate of new accessibility issues entering the codebase.

title: Audit the application accessibility for users with cognitive and motor d
prompt: Audit the application accessibility for users with cognitive and motor disabilities, extending beyond the screen reader and keyboard navigation focus of most audits. Assess whether session timeouts give users sufficient warning and extension options, whether complex forms have progress indicators and the ability to save and resume, and whether error prevention mechanisms — confirmation dialogs, undo options, correction opportunities — are proportionate to the severity of the action. Examine whether the language and content structure supports users with cognitive disabilities — plain language, consistent navigation, predictable interaction patterns, and avoiding cognitive overload through progressive disclosure. Find motor accessibility gaps — drag-and-drop interactions without keyboard alternatives, right-click context menus with no equivalent accessible action, and time-limited interactions that cannot be disabled or extended. Look for design patterns that assume a point-and-click modality without considering stylus, switch control, or voice navigation users. Summarize the five accessibility improvements that would most reduce barriers for users with cognitive and motor disabilities.

title: Audit hardcoded strings that should be internationalized
prompt: Scan the codebase for hardcoded user-facing strings that are not routed through the i18n system. Identify every string that would need to change for a different locale — labels, error messages, dates, currency, plurals.
prompt_short: Find all hardcoded user-facing strings not in the i18n system.
prompt_filtered: Hardcoded strings only. User-facing text outside translation system.

title: Review locale-specific formatting for dates numbers currency
prompt: Identify all places where dates, times, numbers, and currency are formatted. Verify they use locale-aware formatters rather than hardcoded formats. Find uses of en-US formats (MM/DD/YYYY, $1,000.00) not wrapped in locale formatters.
prompt_short: Find hardcoded date, number, and currency formats not using locale formatters.
prompt_filtered: Format localization only. Date, number, and currency formatting.

title: Find UI layouts that break with longer translated strings
prompt: Identify UI components with fixed widths, truncation, or overflow:hidden that would break when German, French, or Japanese translations are significantly longer or shorter than English. Find layouts that assume English word length.
prompt_short: Find UI layouts that will break with longer or shorter translated strings.
prompt_filtered: Layout flexibility only. Fixed-width and truncation issues.

title: Assess translation coverage and missing locale files
prompt: For each supported locale, compare the translation file against the default locale. Identify untranslated strings (showing the key or English fallback), missing locale files, and inconsistent translation key naming across locales.
prompt_short: Find untranslated strings and missing locale files for each supported language.
prompt_filtered: Translation coverage only. Missing keys and untranslated strings per locale.

title: Review right-to-left language support implementation
prompt: Assess whether the application correctly supports RTL languages (Arabic, Hebrew, Farsi). Check for: CSS directional properties using physical (left/right) vs logical (start/end) values, hardcoded text-align: left, icon placement that doesn't flip, and missing dir='rtl' handling.
prompt_short: Audit RTL language support for layout and CSS directional issues.
prompt_filtered: RTL support only. Physical vs logical CSS properties and layout.

title: Audit plural rules implementation for non-English languages
prompt: Identify all plural strings in the application. Verify they use CLDR plural categories (zero, one, two, few, many, other) rather than simple if-else on count. Find strings that only handle singular/plural (breaking Arabic with 6 plural forms, Polish with 4, or Russian with 3). Assess whether the i18n library supports CLDR plural rules for all target locales.
prompt_short: Audit plural rule implementation for CLDR compliance in non-English locales.
prompt_filtered: Plural rules only. CLDR category coverage and library support.

title: Review locale fallback chain configuration and behavior
prompt: Assess the locale fallback chain. Verify that when a translation key is missing in a specific locale, the system falls back to a regional parent (fr-BE falls back to fr, then to the default locale), not directly to the key name. Find missing keys that expose raw key strings to users rather than a readable fallback.
prompt_short: Audit locale fallback chain for graceful degradation without exposing keys.
prompt_filtered: Fallback chain only. Locale hierarchy and missing key rendering.

title: Evaluate translator handoff process and translation context quality
prompt: Review the process for getting new strings to translators. Verify each string is accompanied by a screenshot, a character limit, a description of context (where the string appears, what action it describes), and a glossary of product-specific terms. Find strings submitted for translation with no context, leading to mistranslation of ambiguous terms.
prompt_short: Audit translator handoff process and context quality for translation accuracy.
prompt_filtered: Translator handoff only. Context, screenshots, and character limits per string.

title: Assess locale-aware sorting and collation implementation
prompt: Identify all places where strings are sorted — dropdown options, search results, directory listings. Verify locale-aware collation is used (Intl.Collator or equivalent) rather than byte-order string comparison, which breaks alphabetical sort for accented characters (é, ü, ø). Find sort results that would be incorrect for German, Swedish, or Turkish users.
prompt_short: Audit string sorting for locale-aware collation vs byte-order comparison.
prompt_filtered: Collation only. Locale-aware sort implementation and accented character handling.

title: Review locale detection and user locale preference persistence
prompt: Assess how the application detects the user's locale. Verify it uses Accept-Language header, browser navigator.language, or explicit user preference in that priority order. Find cases where locale defaults to English regardless of browser settings, or where locale selection is available but the preference is not persisted across sessions.
prompt_short: Audit locale detection priority and user locale preference persistence.
prompt_filtered: Locale detection only. Browser signal priority and preference storage.

title: Evaluate email and push notification localization coverage
prompt: Assess whether transactional emails and push notifications are localized. Identify email templates sent in English to non-English users, notifications using the server locale rather than the recipient's locale, and marketing emails with no locale variant. Verify the recipient locale is stored and used at send time, not at template creation time.
prompt_short: Audit email and push notification localization for non-English recipients.
prompt_filtered: Notification localization only. Email templates and push copy per locale.

title: Audit locale-specific legal and compliance text requirements
prompt: Identify user-facing legal text — terms of service, privacy policy, cookie consent, GDPR notices — that must be localized for legal compliance in target markets. Verify consent flows are translated and meet regional requirements (GDPR for EU, LGPD for Brazil, PIPL for China). Find consent dialogs displayed in English to users in jurisdictions requiring native-language disclosure.
prompt_short: Audit localization of legal and compliance text for target market requirements.
prompt_filtered: Legal localization only. Consent flows and jurisdictional language requirements.

title: Assess locale-specific input formats for phone, postal code, and address
prompt: Review forms collecting addresses, phone numbers, and postal codes. Verify fields accept locale-appropriate formats — US ZIP codes differ from UK postcodes differ from Japanese postal codes. Find forms rejecting valid international phone numbers due to a +1 or US-only format assumption, or address fields requiring a state that doesn't exist outside the US.
prompt_short: Audit address and phone input forms for international format acceptance.
prompt_filtered: Input formats only. Address, phone, and postal code locale-specific validation.

title: Review locale-specific image and iconography for cultural sensitivity
prompt: Identify images, icons, and illustrations that carry different meaning across cultures — hand gestures, color symbolism (red as danger vs luck), religious symbols, or animals with cultural significance. Assess whether any imagery would be offensive or confusing to target locale audiences. Recommend locale-specific asset variants where cultural impact is significant.
prompt_short: Audit images and icons for cultural sensitivity across target locales.
prompt_filtered: Cultural imagery only. Icons and illustrations with locale-specific meaning.

title: Evaluate non-Gregorian calendar and alternative date system support
prompt: Identify users in locales using non-Gregorian calendars — Hijri for Arabic, Hebrew calendar, Persian calendar, or Buddhist Era for Thai. Verify date pickers can display and accept dates in the user's calendar system, that date formatting uses the correct era notation, and that date arithmetic accounts for calendar system differences.
prompt_short: Audit date picker and formatting for non-Gregorian calendar system support.
prompt_filtered: Calendar systems only. Hijri, Hebrew, Persian, and Buddhist era date handling.

title: Assess time zone handling for global users and server-side rendering
prompt: Review how the application handles time zones. Verify user-facing timestamps are rendered in the user's local time zone (not UTC or server time zone), time zone preference is stored per user, and server-side rendering of timestamps uses the correct user time zone rather than the server time zone. Find scheduled events or deadlines displayed in server time zone to global users.
prompt_short: Audit time zone rendering for global users and server-side time zone bugs.
prompt_filtered: Time zone handling only. User local time vs server time zone rendering.

title: Review locale-specific number and unit formatting edge cases
prompt: Identify number formatting edge cases by locale — decimal separators (comma vs period), thousands separators, negative number representation (-1 vs 1- for some locales), large number grouping (10,000 vs 1,0000 for East Asian grouping). Verify measurement units convert appropriately (miles vs kilometers, Fahrenheit vs Celsius) for locale.
prompt_short: Audit locale-specific number formatting edge cases and unit conversion.
prompt_filtered: Number formatting only. Decimal separators, grouping, and unit localization.

title: Audit translation key naming conventions and namespace organization
prompt: Review the translation key namespace structure. Verify keys follow a consistent naming pattern, are organized by feature or component (not a flat global namespace), and key names are descriptive enough that translators can infer context without a screenshot. Find key naming anti-patterns — numeric keys, duplicate key synonyms, and keys reused for semantically different strings in different contexts.
prompt_short: Audit translation key naming conventions and namespace organization quality.
prompt_filtered: Key structure only. Namespace organization and naming convention consistency.
