prompt: Audit Pluralization Logic

title: Review RTL Layout Support
prompt: Review RTL Layout Support

title: Map Missing Locale Fallbacks
prompt: Map Missing Locale Fallbacks

title: Find Date Format Hardcoding
prompt: Find Date Format Hardcoding

title: Audit the codebase for hardcoded user-facing strings outside the i18n sy
prompt: Audit the codebase for hardcoded user-facing strings outside the i18n system. Identify every string requiring localization for a different locale — labels, error messages, validation copy, email templates, push notification copy, and metadata. Cross-reference with existing translation keys to find strings that already have keys but are still hardcoded in some component variants. Recommend the extraction priority sequence by user surface visibility.

title: Review locale-specific formatting across dates, times, numbers, currency
prompt: Review locale-specific formatting across dates, times, numbers, currency, and addresses. Find hardcoded format strings using en-US conventions — MM/DD/YYYY dates, dollar signs in number formatters, comma decimal separators applied universally. Identify components rendering values without locale-aware formatters and email templates with date or currency strings rendered server-side using server locale rather than recipient locale. Recommend the formatting standardization that would most reduce locale-specific bugs.

title: Assess UI layout flexibility for translated string variance
prompt: Assess UI layout flexibility for translated string variance. Identify components with fixed widths, single-line truncation, or overflow:hidden that would break when German, Russian, or Japanese translations are significantly longer or shorter than English. Find components using absolute positioning that depend on English text width and forms whose labels must wrap to multiple lines in some locales. Recommend the layout system changes that would most reduce per-locale UI breakage.

title: Audit right-to-left language support across the application
prompt: Audit right-to-left language support across the application. Identify UI components that do not mirror correctly in RTL locales — text truncation on the wrong side, icons with directional meaning that do not flip, progress indicators moving in the wrong direction, and layout padding that is not swapped between start and end sides. Verify CSS uses logical properties rather than left and right hardcoding. Test the application in an Arabic or Hebrew locale and identify all visual regressions. Recommend the RTL support fixes that would most improve experience for RTL-locale users.

title: Assess translation coverage and quality management across all supported
prompt: Assess translation coverage and quality management across all supported locales. Identify locales with translation coverage below 90 percent — strings falling through to the fallback locale. Find locales with translations that are machine-generated without human review, producing grammatically correct but contextually wrong results. Examine the string update process — whether translators are notified when source strings change and whether outdated translations are flagged before release. Assess whether context is provided to translators — screenshots, character limits, string descriptions — to enable quality translations. Recommend the translation pipeline improvements that would most increase quality across the lowest-coverage locales.

title: Evaluate the internationalization and localization architecture
prompt: Evaluate the internationalization and localization architecture. Assess whether all user-facing text routes through a single translation system with clear key naming conventions, whether the translation pipeline catches new keys automatically, and whether missing translations fall back gracefully without exposing keys to users. Identify formatting inconsistencies — date, number, currency, and plural rules applied with mixed conventions across the application. Find pluralization logic using simple count checks rather than CLDR plural rules, breaking for languages with multiple plural forms. Examine the translation workflow — how new strings reach translators, how translation context is provided, and how translated content reaches production. Look for hardcoded strings in email templates, push notifications, error pages, and admin tooling that often escape standard i18n audits. Summarize the five internationalization architecture improvements that would most enable expansion into new locales.

title: Audit the localization coverage and quality across supported locales
prompt: Audit the localization coverage and quality across supported locales. Compare each locale's translation file against the default locale to identify untranslated keys, stale translations from before recent string changes, and locale-specific copy that drifted from the source meaning. Assess whether right-to-left languages render correctly — CSS using logical properties rather than physical left/right values, icon placement that flips appropriately, and bidirectional text handling for mixed-script content. Find locale-specific UI bugs — fixed-width components breaking with longer translations, date pickers rendering incorrectly in non-Gregorian calendars, and form validation messages that don't translate consistently. Examine whether translation quality is reviewed by native speakers before release and whether locale-specific support exists for users encountering localization bugs. Look for opportunities to improve the translation context — screenshots, character limits, glossaries — that translators receive. Recommend the localization quality improvements that would most increase satisfaction in non-default locales.

title: Evaluate the internationalization system for scalability and correctness
prompt: Evaluate the internationalization system for scalability and correctness across complex locale requirements. Assess whether the i18n framework correctly handles plural forms for all supported locales — languages like Russian, Polish, and Arabic have more plural categories than the simple singular and plural distinction handled by most English-first implementations. Find string interpolation patterns that break in languages with different word order, gendered nouns, or noun class agreement. Identify calendar and timezone handling gaps — dates displayed in user-local timezone, week-start day adapted per locale, and fiscal year conventions correct for each market. Examine number formatting for locales using non-Latin digit systems, non-standard decimal separators, or different digit grouping conventions. Look for sorting and collation errors in user-facing lists where locale-specific alphabetical order differs from Unicode code point order. Assess whether locale detection is accurate for edge cases — users in one country using a browser set to a different locale. Recommend the i18n correctness improvements that would most reduce locale-specific bugs for the highest-traffic non-English markets.

title: Identify bus factor risks in the current team structure
prompt: For every critical system, identify which individuals are the only people who understand or can operate it. Assess the risk if any single person left today. Prioritize knowledge transfer for the highest-risk single points of human failure.
prompt_short: Find single points of human failure in system ownership and knowledge.
prompt_filtered: Bus factor only. Single-owner knowledge and system access.

title: Review team structure for clear ownership boundaries
prompt: Assess whether every service and system has a clear team owner. Identify shared ownership that creates diffusion of responsibility, systems owned by multiple teams with no coordination process, and systems with no team owner at all.
prompt_short: Find systems with unclear ownership or multi-team responsibility gaps.
prompt_filtered: Ownership boundaries only. Clear vs shared vs missing ownership.

title: Find cross-team dependencies causing delivery bottlenecks
prompt: Identify work that is frequently blocked waiting on another team. For each blocking dependency, assess whether it should be resolved through: team reorganization, service ownership transfer, improved APIs, better documentation, or SLA agreements.
prompt_short: Find cross-team dependencies that are slowing delivery.
prompt_filtered: Cross-team blockers only. Dependency frequency and resolution options.

title: Assess team cognitive load and context switching
prompt: Evaluate how many different systems, domains, and priorities the team is responsible for. Identify whether the scope is too broad for the team size, causing shallow understanding across many areas rather than deep expertise in fewer. Recommend scope reduction strategies.
prompt_short: Assess if the team is spread too thin across too many systems.
prompt_filtered: Cognitive load only. Team scope vs team size analysis.

title: Design an on-call rotation for sustainable operations
prompt: Assess the current on-call setup. Verify rotation frequency is sustainable (no one on call more than 1 week in 5), that on-call engineers have the context needed to handle incidents, and that alert volume is low enough to allow rest during off-hours on-call.
prompt_short: Design a sustainable on-call rotation with manageable alert volume.
prompt_filtered: On-call design only. Rotation frequency and alert volume sustainability.

title: Analyze Conway's Law alignment between team structure and system architecture
prompt: Compare the current team structure against the system architecture. Identify misalignments where a single team owns components that communicate heavily across team boundaries (high coordination cost), or where multiple teams share ownership of a single service (diffusion of responsibility). Recommend team boundary changes that would reduce coordination overhead.
prompt_short: Find Conway's Law misalignments between team boundaries and service boundaries.
prompt_filtered: Conway's Law only. Team-to-service ownership map and coordination bottlenecks.

title: Assess team topology for stream-aligned vs enabling vs platform teams
prompt: Evaluate the team topology using the Team Topologies framework. Identify which teams are stream-aligned (deliver end-to-end user value), platform (reduce cognitive load for stream teams), enabling (upskill stream teams), and complicated-subsystem (own hard technical domains). Find teams that span multiple topologies — reducing their effectiveness at either.
prompt_short: Map current teams to Team Topologies types and find structural mismatches.
prompt_filtered: Team topology only. Stream-aligned, platform, enabling, and subsystem classification.

title: Review decision-making authority and RACI clarity across teams
prompt: Assess decision-making clarity. Identify decisions that require multiple teams to agree before moving forward, decisions with no clear owner (no one can say yes unilaterally), and decisions that are escalated to senior leadership that could be delegated to the team level. Recommend decision rights changes that would reduce escalation and increase team autonomy.
prompt_short: Find decision-making bottlenecks and ownership gaps across team boundaries.
prompt_filtered: Decision authority only. RACI clarity and escalation reduction opportunities.

title: Identify knowledge silos and cross-functional skill gaps
prompt: Map each team's capabilities against the skills required to deliver their roadmap independently. Identify teams that must depend on another team for security review, design, data engineering, or DevOps — external dependencies that become delivery bottlenecks. Recommend skill distribution changes or shared services that would improve team autonomy.
prompt_short: Find teams that lack skills to deliver their roadmap without external dependencies.
prompt_filtered: Skill gaps only. Cross-functional dependencies blocking team autonomy.

title: Review team cognitive load and service ownership concentration
prompt: Assess cognitive load per team. Identify teams owning more services than they can maintain and improve effectively, teams on-call for systems they did not build and do not understand deeply, and teams whose roadmap is consistently overrun by unplanned operational work. Recommend service redistribution or platform investment to reduce cognitive load.
prompt_short: Find teams with excess cognitive load from too many owned services or on-call burden.
prompt_filtered: Cognitive load only. Service ownership concentration and unplanned work ratio.

title: Evaluate inner-source and cross-team code contribution model
prompt: Assess whether engineers can contribute to services owned by other teams (inner-source). Identify barriers — no contribution guidelines, no PR review SLA for external contributors, no testing documentation, or cultural resistance. Find high-leverage shared libraries with no contribution pathway, forcing duplicate implementations across teams.
prompt_short: Audit inner-source model for cross-team code contribution barriers.
prompt_filtered: Inner-source only. Contribution guidelines, review SLA, and cultural barriers.

title: Assess team OKR alignment and local vs global optimization tension
prompt: Review team OKRs for alignment with company-level goals. Identify OKRs that optimize for local team metrics at the expense of cross-team or customer outcomes, OKRs that create perverse incentives (shipping features regardless of quality), and teams with no shared OKR commitment with the teams they depend on most.
prompt_short: Audit team OKR alignment for local vs global optimization conflicts.
prompt_filtered: OKR alignment only. Local optimization and cross-team goal conflicts.

title: Review postmortem culture and blameless incident learning
prompt: Assess the postmortem process. Verify postmortems are written for all severity-1 incidents, are blameless (focus on system factors not individual error), produce action items with owners and deadlines, and action items are tracked to completion. Find postmortems with blame language, action items never closed, or repeated incidents from the same root cause.
prompt_short: Audit postmortem process for blamelessness and action item follow-through.
prompt_filtered: Postmortem culture only. Blamelessness, action items, and recurring incidents.

title: Identify team structure misalignments causing repeated coordination meetings
prompt: Track coordination overhead by mapping recurring cross-team sync meetings and their purpose. Identify meetings that exist primarily because two teams share ownership of a system or a decision, and whose need would disappear with a different team or ownership boundary. Recommend the structural changes that would eliminate the most coordination meetings.
prompt_short: Find cross-team meetings that indicate structural ownership misalignment.
prompt_filtered: Coordination overhead only. Meetings caused by ownership boundary problems.

title: Evaluate team autonomy and deployment independence
prompt: Assess how independently each team can deploy their services. Identify teams that must coordinate deployments with other teams, teams that cannot deploy without a release train or freeze window, and teams blocked by shared deployment pipelines with no team-level ownership. Recommend the architecture and process changes that would enable independent deployments.
prompt_short: Audit team deployment independence and cross-team release coordination overhead.
prompt_filtered: Deployment autonomy only. Release coordination and pipeline ownership.

title: Review team feedback mechanisms and upward communication channels
prompt: Assess channels for team members to raise concerns — anonymous feedback tools, skip-level 1-on-1s, team retrospectives with management follow-up, and open door culture. Identify whether concerns raised in retrospectives result in visible action from leadership. Find teams with high Glassdoor-type signals (turnover, engagement scores) that have no formal escalation path.
prompt_short: Audit upward feedback mechanisms and leadership responsiveness to team concerns.
prompt_filtered: Feedback channels only. Anonymous options and leadership action rates.

title: Assess technical lead and staff engineer leverage and impact
prompt: Evaluate how technical leads and staff engineers spend their time. Identify leads spending more than 40% on hands-on coding with no strategic leverage — architecture decisions, code review coverage, enabling junior engineers, or cross-team alignment. Find teams without a technical lead, operating without design direction or technical standards enforcement.
prompt_short: Audit technical lead time allocation for strategic leverage vs individual coding.
prompt_filtered: Tech lead leverage only. Time allocation and span of influence.

title: Review team size and two-pizza rule compliance for communication efficiency
prompt: Assess team sizes across the engineering organization. Identify teams larger than 8-10 members where communication overhead is significant (n*(n-1)/2 communication paths), and teams smaller than 3 where bus factor is critical. For oversized teams, recommend split boundaries based on service ownership or user journey alignment.
prompt_short: Audit team sizes for communication efficiency and bus factor risk.
prompt_filtered: Team size only. Oversized and undersized teams and recommended split points.

prompt: Audit Ownership Boundaries

title: Identify Cross-Team Blockers
prompt: Identify Cross-Team Blockers

title: Review Incident Ownership Gaps
prompt: Review Incident Ownership Gaps

title: Find Redundant Team Boundaries
prompt: Find Redundant Team Boundaries

title: Audit On-Call Coverage
prompt: Audit On-Call Coverage

title: Identify bus factor risks across all critical systems
prompt: Identify bus factor risks across all critical systems. For every production service, name the individual or small group with deep operational knowledge and assess the impact of any one of them leaving today. Find systems where only one person can debug production issues, perform deployments, or modify infrastructure. Recommend the knowledge transfer sequence prioritized by system criticality and current single-owner risk.

title: Audit ownership boundaries across the service portfolio
prompt: Audit ownership boundaries across the service portfolio. Identify services with no owning team, services with shared ownership creating diffusion of responsibility, and services with ownership that no longer matches actual work patterns. Find shared infrastructure that everyone depends on but no team owns. Recommend the ownership reassignment that would most clarify responsibility and unblock cross-team work.

title: Examine cross-team dependencies that consistently slow delivery
prompt: Examine cross-team dependencies that consistently slow delivery. Identify the work patterns that block on another team — API changes, infrastructure provisioning, design review, or compliance approval — and assess which dependencies are structural versus addressable through tooling, documentation, or boundary changes. Recommend the dependency reduction sequence that would most increase team autonomy.

title: Assess incident ownership and on-call coverage across all production ser
prompt: Assess incident ownership and on-call coverage across all production services. Identify services with no defined on-call rotation, services where the same individual is on call for multiple unrelated systems creating fatigue risk, and services where the on-call engineer lacks the access or context to diagnose and mitigate an incident independently. Examine incident response process adherence — whether postmortems are completed and action items tracked, and whether repeat incidents indicate systemic fixes are not being prioritized. Recommend the on-call structure changes that would most improve incident response quality and reduce engineer fatigue.

title: Review team communication and coordination overhead for cross-team work
prompt: Review team communication and coordination overhead for cross-team work. Identify workflows that consistently require synchronous coordination across team boundaries — features that touch multiple service owners, infrastructure changes requiring approval from multiple teams, and incidents that involve engineers from more than three teams. Find recurring cross-team dependencies that could be resolved by clarifying ownership, building shared infrastructure, or adjusting team boundaries. Assess whether async-first communication norms reduce context-switching costs or push synchronous discussions into ad-hoc channels. Recommend the coordination improvements that would most reduce planning and delivery friction.

title: Evaluate the team structure as a delivery system
prompt: Evaluate the team structure as a delivery system. Assess whether team boundaries align with the architecture they own — Conway's Law working in the platform's favor rather than against it. Identify teams whose scope is too broad for their size, causing shallow understanding across many systems rather than deep expertise in fewer. Find teams whose scope is too narrow, creating bottlenecks where small teams own systems with disproportionate change volume. Examine cross-team dependencies — the frequency of work blocked on another team, the resolution paths used, and whether dependencies are visible in planning. Look for invisible work — operational toil, security response, compliance overhead — that absorbs team capacity without appearing in planned work. Assess on-call sustainability — rotation frequency, alert volume during off-hours, and whether on-call engineers have the context and tooling to handle incidents without escalating to specialists. Summarize the five team structure improvements that would most increase delivery velocity and team health.

title: Audit the human knowledge architecture across the engineering organizati
prompt: Audit the human knowledge architecture across the engineering organization. Identify systems where critical knowledge lives in individuals rather than documentation, where decisions remembered by people who left are still influencing choices today, and where processes only one person can perform. Find handoff points between teams where knowledge transfer is incomplete — work passed downstream without context, on-call rotations with gaps in coverage knowledge, and incident response that depends on tribal knowledge of which engineer to page. Assess whether documentation, runbooks, and architecture decision records are written, maintained, and actually consulted during incidents. Look for opportunities to reduce knowledge concentration — pair programming on critical systems, recorded walkthroughs of complex flows, and rotation programs that build redundant expertise. Recommend the knowledge architecture changes that would most reduce single-point-of-human-failure risk.

title: Audit the team structure for alignment with Conway's Law — whether the s
prompt: Audit the team structure for alignment with Conway's Law — whether the system architecture reflects the team communication structure, creating friction between teams that should collaborate and independence between teams whose services are tightly coupled. Identify team boundaries that force high-coordination work on every feature — services owned by different teams that change together on every sprint, APIs where the producer team and the primary consumer team have no shared planning cadence, and shared infrastructure where multiple teams make changes without a coordination protocol. Find ownership gaps where a system has multiple nominal owners but no single accountable party for reliability, security, or roadmap. Examine whether team size and composition match the scope of what each team owns — teams owning multiple complex services without headroom for reliability work, and teams with too few members to maintain knowledge redundancy across their domain. Look for signs of accumulated organizational debt — shadow ownership where one team maintains another service informally, deprecated handoff agreements still in practice, and undocumented ownership of systems built by teams that no longer exist. Recommend the structural changes that would most reduce cross-team coordination cost and increase delivery velocity.

title: Find the most common reasons customers churn
prompt: Analyze churned customer data and exit interviews. Identify the top 5 churn reasons. For each, assess whether it's addressable by the product, process, or support team. Find the leading indicator that could have predicted each churn before it happened.
prompt_short: Find the top 5 churn reasons and their leading indicators.
prompt_filtered: Churn reasons only. Root causes and predictive signals.

title: Identify customers at risk of churning in the next 90 days
prompt: Define the product usage patterns and engagement signals that predict churn. Identify customers currently exhibiting those signals. Recommend specific intervention playbooks — outreach timing, message, and success team action for each risk segment.
prompt_short: Define churn prediction signals and find at-risk customer segments now.
prompt_filtered: Churn prediction only. Usage signals and intervention playbooks.

title: Audit customer onboarding completion and drop-off points
prompt: Map the customer onboarding journey and measure completion rates at each step. Identify the steps with the highest drop-off. For each, determine whether the barrier is product friction, unclear expectations, or missing support. Recommend specific improvements.
prompt_short: Find the highest onboarding drop-off points and their root causes.
prompt_filtered: Onboarding drop-off only. Step-by-step completion analysis.

title: Find expansion revenue signals in current customer base
prompt: Identify customers showing signals of being ready for upsell or expansion — heavy feature usage, team growth, approaching plan limits, requesting features on higher tiers. Rank by expansion potential and likelihood of conversion.
prompt_short: Find customers showing expansion revenue signals for upsell targeting.
prompt_filtered: Expansion signals only. Usage patterns indicating upgrade readiness.

title: Create a customer health score for the product
prompt: Design a customer health score using available data — login frequency, feature adoption breadth, support ticket volume, NPS, contract renewal history. Weight each signal by its correlation with retention. Define thresholds for green, yellow, and red health.
prompt_short: Design a customer health score model with green/yellow/red thresholds.
prompt_filtered: Health score design only. Signal weighting and threshold definition.

title: Design the customer onboarding journey for time-to-value optimization
prompt: Map the current onboarding journey from contract signature to the first meaningful value moment. Identify the steps adding friction — IT procurement delays, multi-stakeholder SSO setup, data import complexity — and the steps where customers disengage. Redesign the journey to minimize days-to-value without sacrificing setup completeness.
prompt_short: Redesign customer onboarding to minimize days-to-first-value.
prompt_filtered: Onboarding journey only. Steps to value and friction point elimination.

title: Develop a customer segmentation model for differentiated success motions
prompt: Design a segmentation model for the customer base that determines the success motion — high-touch CSM coverage, low-touch scaled digital, or tech-touch self-serve. Identify the attributes driving segment assignment (ARR, headcount, complexity, industry, champion access) and the specific plays deployed per segment.
prompt_short: Design a customer segmentation model to match success coverage to account value.
prompt_filtered: Segmentation only. High-touch vs scaled vs self-serve assignment criteria.

title: Build an executive business review template for top-tier accounts
prompt: Design an EBR framework for top-tier accounts. Define the cadence (quarterly), agenda (business outcomes, product usage ROI, roadmap alignment, risk discussion), attendee requirements (customer executive sponsor, your champion, CSM, AE), and success criteria for the EBR. Identify accounts that have never had an EBR and are at renewal risk.
prompt_short: Design an EBR framework and identify top accounts with no executive engagement.
prompt_filtered: EBR design only. Agenda, cadence, and attendee requirements.

title: Identify customers with high usage but low feature adoption breadth
prompt: Find customers with heavy usage of one or two features but low adoption of the broader platform. Identify the highest-value features not yet adopted and the recommended adoption sequence per use case. Assess which accounts are one competitive displacement away from churn because they are using a single feature easily replicated by an alternative.
prompt_short: Find customers using few features and at risk from single-feature concentration.
prompt_filtered: Adoption breadth only. Feature usage concentration and expansion opportunity.

title: Design a renewal risk escalation process and playbook
prompt: Define the process for escalating renewal risk. Specify the triggers (health score drop, champion departure, budget freeze, competitor evaluation), the escalation path (CSM escalates to manager, VP, executive sponsor), the intervention plays at each level, and the timeline relative to renewal date. Find at-risk accounts approaching renewal without an active risk mitigation plan.
prompt_short: Design a renewal risk escalation process with triggers and intervention plays.
prompt_filtered: Renewal risk only. Escalation triggers, path, and timeline before renewal.

title: Measure customer success team impact on net revenue retention
prompt: Build a model attributing net revenue retention outcomes to customer success activities. Identify which CSM activities (EBR completion, adoption reviews, QBRs, health check frequency) correlate with higher renewal and expansion rates. Find CSM behaviors with no correlation to retention that can be deprioritized, freeing capacity for high-impact activities.
prompt_short: Model which customer success activities drive net revenue retention outcomes.
prompt_filtered: NRR attribution only. CSM activity correlation with renewal and expansion.

title: Develop a customer advocacy and reference program
prompt: Design a customer advocacy program. Identify the top 20 customers by health score, NPS, and willingness to advocate. Define the ask spectrum — product reviews, case studies, reference calls, speaking opportunities — and the reward structure (executive access, roadmap influence, co-marketing). Find accounts that are strong advocates but have never been asked.
prompt_short: Design a customer advocacy program and identify untapped advocate candidates.
prompt_filtered: Advocacy program only. Reference criteria and advocate identification.

title: Audit customer communication touchpoints for frequency and relevance
prompt: Map every customer-facing communication touchpoint — onboarding emails, in-app messages, CSM check-ins, product newsletters, support follow-ups. Identify touchpoints that are redundant, too frequent (causing opt-outs), or irrelevant to the customer's use case. Assess whether the communication calendar is coordinated across teams or siloed.
prompt_short: Audit all customer-facing communication for frequency, overlap, and relevance.
prompt_filtered: Communication calendar only. Touchpoint coordination and frequency optimization.

title: Design a customer feedback collection cadence and closed-loop process
prompt: Assess the cadence for collecting customer feedback — NPS surveys, CSAT post-support, in-product micro-surveys, and CSM qualitative interviews. Verify there is a closed-loop process: customer feedback is acknowledged, routed to the right team, actioned or declined with rationale, and the outcome is communicated back to the customer. Find feedback collected but never acted on.
prompt_short: Design a customer feedback cadence with closed-loop acknowledgment and routing.
prompt_filtered: Feedback cadence only. Collection frequency and closed-loop communication.

title: Identify customers ready for expansion and design the upsell motion
prompt: Define the signals indicating a customer is ready for expansion — approaching seat or usage limits, requesting features on higher tiers, growing team headcount, positive NPS trend. Build the recommended expansion motion: who reaches out, what the conversation looks like, what the expansion offer is, and what the approval and contract amendment process looks like.
prompt_short: Define expansion readiness signals and design the CSM-led upsell motion.
prompt_filtered: Expansion motion only. Readiness signals and upsell conversation design.

title: Assess champion departure risk and stakeholder depth in top accounts
prompt: Map the stakeholder contacts in your top accounts. Identify accounts where all relationship is concentrated in one champion — where their departure would leave the relationship without an internal advocate. For each single-stakeholder account, recommend the stakeholder expansion strategy: exec sponsor introduction, user community engagement, or product champion cultivation.
prompt_short: Identify single-champion accounts and recommend stakeholder expansion plays.
prompt_filtered: Champion risk only. Single-contact accounts and multi-stakeholder strategies.

title: Evaluate customer success tooling for health signal automation
prompt: Assess the tools available to the customer success team. Verify product usage data flows into the CS platform automatically, health score updates trigger alerts without manual calculation, and CSM task queues are populated from signal changes rather than calendar reminders. Identify health signals currently requiring manual data collection that could be automated.
prompt_short: Audit customer success platform for health signal automation completeness.
prompt_filtered: CS tooling only. Automated signal ingestion and alert triggers.

title: Build a customer outcomes framework tied to business metrics
prompt: Design a customer outcomes framework that maps product adoption to customer business metrics. Define the outcome categories (time saved, revenue generated, risk reduced), the metrics used to measure each, and the data sources. Build the ROI narrative for the top three customer segments. Identify accounts that cannot articulate measurable ROI from the product — churn risk.
prompt_short: Design a customer outcomes framework mapping product adoption to business ROI.
prompt_filtered: Outcomes framework only. ROI metrics per customer segment.

title: Identify Expansion Revenue Gaps
prompt: Identify Expansion Revenue Gaps

title: Analyze churn data from the past four quarters
prompt: Analyze churn data from the past four quarters. Identify the top five churn reasons and the leading indicators that preceded each by 30, 60, and 90 days. Cross-reference reason categories with customer segment, plan tier, and tenure to find segment-specific churn patterns. Recommend the intervention playbooks for each leading indicator pattern.

title: Map the customer onboarding journey from contract signing to the first m
prompt: Map the customer onboarding journey from contract signing to the first meaningful value moment. Measure completion rate at each step and time spent. Identify the steps with the highest drop-off and assess whether the barrier is product friction, unclear expectations, or missing customer success touch. Recommend the onboarding redesign sequence prioritized by retention impact at 90 days.

title: Identify customers showing expansion revenue signals — heavy feature usa
prompt: Identify customers showing expansion revenue signals — heavy feature usage, team growth, approaching plan limits, requests for higher-tier features. Rank by expansion potential and conversion likelihood. Cross-reference with support ticket sentiment and renewal risk to ensure expansion outreach goes to healthy accounts. Recommend the expansion targeting and outreach playbook.

title: Review customer success coverage model across account segments
prompt: Review customer success coverage model across account segments. Identify accounts with no active CSM engagement in the past 90 days, accounts approaching renewal without a success plan in place, and accounts consuming high support volume without corresponding CS attention. Assess whether the coverage model is proportional to account value — whether your highest-revenue accounts receive dedicated attention and whether at-risk small accounts have a scalable digital engagement path. Recommend the coverage model adjustments that would most improve retention in the highest-churn segment.

title: Assess the customer success-to-product feedback loop
prompt: Assess the customer success-to-product feedback loop. Identify whether customer success managers have a structured path to surface recurring customer pain points, feature requests, and usability issues to the product team. Find recurring themes in customer conversations that have never been translated into a product backlog item. Examine whether customers who request a feature and receive it are closed-loop notified — increasing perceived responsiveness. Assess whether customer feedback influences roadmap priority or is gathered but not acted on. Recommend the feedback loop improvements that would most increase customer-perceived responsiveness and product-market fit.

title: Evaluate the customer success function as a retention and expansion engi
prompt: Evaluate the customer success function as a retention and expansion engine. Assess whether customer health is measured continuously through usage signals, support sentiment, and stakeholder engagement, or only at renewal time. Identify the top five churn reasons and whether each is addressable by product changes, process changes, customer education, or pricing changes. Find at-risk customers currently exhibiting churn signals and the intervention playbooks deployed for each pattern. Examine whether onboarding successfully drives the first meaningful value moment within the critical first 30 days, and whether subsequent activation milestones are tracked through the customer lifecycle. Look for systemic gaps — customers in the dark zone between onboarding handoff and renewal motion, customers receiving generic touchpoints regardless of segment or risk, and high-value accounts without an assigned customer success owner. Summarize the five customer success improvements that would most increase net revenue retention.

title: Audit the customer health score architecture and the actions it triggers
prompt: Audit the customer health score architecture and the actions it triggers. Identify the signals currently feeding the health score — login frequency, feature adoption breadth, support ticket volume, NPS, contract renewal history — and assess whether each signal is weighted by its empirical correlation with retention rather than intuition. Find signals missing from the health score that have proven predictive in churn analysis. Examine whether health score changes trigger differentiated playbooks based on root cause — usage decline triggers re-engagement, support sentiment decline triggers escalation, and competitive replacement signals trigger executive engagement. Look for accounts whose health score is misleading — high logins with no value-driving usage, low logins from heavy API consumers, or scores skewed by champion departures. Assess whether the health score is reviewed by leadership at appropriate cadence and whether trend changes drive product or process investment. Recommend the customer health architecture changes that would most improve early intervention quality.

title: Evaluate customer success as a data-driven discipline
prompt: Evaluate customer success as a data-driven discipline. Assess whether the health score model is built on leading indicators — product engagement, feature adoption breadth, support ticket sentiment — rather than lagging indicators like renewal date proximity. Find accounts where the health score indicated green but the account churned, revealing missing signals. Examine whether onboarding milestones are tracked — time to first key action, time to team invite, time to workflow adoption — and whether accounts missing milestones trigger proactive outreach. Look for patterns in expansion revenue — whether upsells correlate with specific usage thresholds, CSM touch patterns, or QBR cadence — and whether the team uses this data to time expansion conversations. Assess whether the CS team has the tooling to see usage data, support history, and billing history in a unified view without switching between systems. Summarize the five customer success investments that would most improve net revenue retention.

title: Audit support ticket categories for product fix opportunities
prompt: Analyze support ticket categories over the past 30 days. Identify the most common issue types. For each, assess whether the root cause is a product bug, a UX confusion, a documentation gap, or a missing feature. Estimate tickets eliminated per fix.
prompt_short: Find the most common support issues and categorize as product vs doc vs UX.
prompt_filtered: Ticket categorization only. Volume by issue type and product vs non-product split.

title: Find support escalation paths with no documented runbook
prompt: Review the support escalation process. Identify ticket types that regularly need engineering escalation because support agents lack a runbook or tool to resolve them independently. For each, create a decision tree or runbook to enable tier-1 resolution.
prompt_short: Find support escalations that could be resolved with a better runbook.
prompt_filtered: Escalation path only. Issues requiring engineering without runbooks.

title: Assess support tooling gaps causing slow resolution time
prompt: Evaluate the tools available to support agents. Identify lookups that require database access, actions that require engineering intervention, or data that isn't surfaced in the support tool. For each, estimate resolution time reduction from building the missing capability.
prompt_short: Find tooling gaps that make support agents slower to resolve tickets.
prompt_filtered: Tooling gaps only. Missing capabilities causing support slowness.

title: Review SLA compliance and first response time trends
prompt: Assess support SLA compliance rates — first response time, resolution time, and customer satisfaction. Identify ticket types or time periods with consistent SLA misses. Determine whether misses are from volume, complexity, tooling, or staffing.
prompt_short: Audit support SLA compliance and find the root cause of misses.
prompt_filtered: SLA compliance only. First response and resolution time analysis.

title: Create a self-service resource for the top 10 support issues
prompt: For the top 10 most common support questions, write a self-service solution — either a FAQ answer, a documentation page, or a UI improvement that eliminates the question. Estimate ticket deflection rate per solution.
prompt_short: Create self-service resources for the top 10 support questions.
prompt_filtered: Self-service content only. FAQ and doc creation for top issues.

title: Audit support ticket routing and triage process
prompt: Review ticket triage: are tickets routed to the right team automatically? Are urgent issues flagged and prioritized? Is there a consistent process for determining priority/severity? Are SLAs set per priority? Do tickets get re-routed if the first team can't resolve? How long in queue before triage?
prompt_short: Audit support ticket routing and triage efficiency.
prompt_filtered: Triage process only. Routing accuracy and priority assignment.

title: Review support response time and first-response quality metrics
prompt: Evaluate support quality: what's the average first response time? What % of issues are resolved in first response (CSAT-weighted)? Are responses personalized or templated? Is product context included in responses? Do supporters escalate prematurely when they could resolve? What's the CSAT trend?
prompt_short: Audit support response time and first-response quality.
prompt_filtered: Response quality only. CSAT, resolution rate, and personalization.

title: Assess support knowledge base and documentation quality
prompt: Review documentation: is it current and accurate? Are there gaps where docs don't exist but support gets questions? Are docs searchable and findable? Are there troubleshooting guides for common issues? Is documentation tested for accuracy? Are screenshots and examples current?
prompt_short: Audit support knowledge base completeness and accuracy.
prompt_filtered: Documentation only. Gaps, accuracy, and searchability.

title: Review support escalation paths and expert routing
prompt: Evaluate expert access: when a support agent can't resolve an issue, how quickly can they escalate to an engineer? Are there clear escalation criteria? Is there a dedicated on-call engineer for urgent escalations? How long for complex issues to reach the right expert? What's the escalation success rate?
prompt_short: Audit escalation paths to engineering and expert availability.
prompt_filtered: Escalation routing only. Expert access and resolution rates.

title: Assess customer support across time zones and languages
prompt: Evaluate global support: what's the coverage for key time zones? Are there language barriers? Is support multilingual? For global customers, what's the response time? Are there translation tools or multilingual support agents? Is there 24/7 support or are there gaps?
prompt_short: Audit support coverage across time zones and language support.
prompt_filtered: Global support only. Time zone coverage and languages supported.

title: Review support feedback loop and product improvement influence
prompt: Assess support-to-product communication: are recurring issues logged and analyzed? Do support insights feed into product roadmap decisions? Can support agents directly report bugs they discover? Is there a process for feature requests from customers via support? What's the feedback frequency to product?
prompt_short: Audit support feedback integration and product influence.
prompt_filtered: Feedback and product influence only. Bug reports and feature requests.

title: Evaluate support team burnout, knowledge gaps, and training
prompt: Assess team health: what's the support agent turnover rate? Are there knowledge gaps in specific product areas? Is there ongoing training? Are agents overwhelmed by ticket volume? Is there mentoring for new support staff? What's the ticket volume per agent (target: 5-10 per day)? What are retention risks?
prompt_short: Audit support team capacity, training, and burnout risks.
prompt_filtered: Team health only. Turnover, training, and ticket load.

title: Design AI-assisted triage and suggested response templates
prompt: Evaluate opportunities to use AI to reduce support overhead. Identify ticket types suitable for AI-suggested responses (factual, repetitive, low-risk), assess whether suggested responses would require agent review before sending, and estimate deflection rate from full AI resolution vs human-reviewed AI drafts. Recommend the AI-assist implementation that improves resolution speed without compromising quality.
prompt_short: Design AI-assisted triage and response suggestion for repetitive ticket types.
prompt_filtered: AI-assist only. Suggested responses and deflection opportunity analysis.

title: Audit sentiment analysis and proactive escalation for negative signals
prompt: Assess whether inbound support tickets are analyzed for customer sentiment. Verify that tickets with negative sentiment (frustration, threat to cancel, repeated issue) are flagged for proactive escalation to a senior agent or CSM before the customer escalates themselves. Find high-sentiment tickets that received a standard SLA response with no escalation.
prompt_short: Audit support sentiment detection and proactive escalation triggers.
prompt_filtered: Sentiment routing only. Negative signal detection and CSM escalation.

title: Evaluate support chatbot coverage and handoff to human agents
prompt: Assess chatbot or virtual agent performance. Identify the containment rate (issues resolved without human agent), the topics where the chatbot fails and escalates to human, the quality of human handoff (context transferred vs user must repeat themselves), and the customer satisfaction differential between chatbot-resolved and human-resolved tickets.
prompt_short: Audit support chatbot containment rate and human handoff quality.
prompt_filtered: Chatbot only. Containment rates and human handoff context preservation.

title: Review support SLA tiering by customer segment and plan
prompt: Assess whether SLA commitments are differentiated by customer segment — enterprise customers with dedicated support having faster SLAs than self-serve. Verify SLA terms are documented in contracts, are operationally enforceable, and that the support tooling can route and prioritize tickets based on customer tier automatically.
prompt_short: Audit support SLA tiering by customer segment and contract enforcement.
prompt_filtered: SLA tiering only. Segment-differentiated SLA and routing enforcement.

title: Design a support deflection strategy through in-product guidance
prompt: Identify the top 10 support questions and assess whether each could be eliminated by in-product improvements — tooltip at the point of confusion, proactive warning before a common error, contextual help panel, or simplified flow. Estimate deflection rate per improvement. Recommend the roadmap items that would most reduce support volume.
prompt_short: Design in-product guidance improvements that would deflect the top support questions.
prompt_filtered: Deflection strategy only. In-product guidance for top ticket types.

title: Audit support ticket aging and stale ticket resolution process
prompt: Review the aging distribution of open tickets. Identify the percentage of tickets open beyond SLA, tickets that have been reopened multiple times, and tickets awaiting customer response with no follow-up reminder. Assess whether stale tickets are auto-closed with a notification or silently aged out. Recommend the aging policy that clears backlogs without abandoning customers.
prompt_short: Audit open ticket aging and stale ticket resolution workflows.
prompt_filtered: Ticket aging only. SLA breach distribution and stale ticket auto-resolution.

title: Evaluate support channel consolidation and omnichannel routing
prompt: Assess the support channels available — email, live chat, phone, community forum, social media DMs. Identify channels where conversations are siloed (agent handling Twitter DMs has no view of the same customer's email history), where response time expectations differ by channel but SLA is applied uniformly, and where low-volume channels absorb disproportionate agent attention.
prompt_short: Audit support channel coverage and omnichannel conversation context sharing.
prompt_filtered: Channel consolidation only. Omnichannel routing and conversation history.

title: Review support quality assurance and agent performance calibration
prompt: Evaluate the QA process for support interactions. Verify there is a random sampling and scoring process, a calibration meeting where agents and managers agree on quality scores, and a coaching process for agents below quality thresholds. Find agents with high volume but low quality scores who are resolving tickets faster by sacrificing resolution quality.
prompt_short: Audit support QA sampling, scoring, and agent coaching cadence.
prompt_filtered: QA process only. Sampling rate, calibration meetings, and coaching feedback.

title: Review CSAT Score Drivers

title: Identify Self-Service Deflection Gaps
prompt: Identify Self-Service Deflection Gaps

title: Analyze support ticket categories from the past 30 days
prompt: Analyze support ticket categories from the past 30 days. Identify the most common issue types and categorize each as product bug, UX confusion, documentation gap, or missing feature. For each category, estimate ticket volume eliminated by the corresponding fix. Recommend the cross-functional fix sequence that would most reduce inbound support volume.

title: Audit the support escalation pipeline for ticket types regularly requiri
prompt: Audit the support escalation pipeline for ticket types regularly requiring engineering involvement. Identify escalations driven by missing tier-1 tooling, missing runbooks, or genuinely complex issues that require deep system knowledge. For each tool-or-runbook gap, design the resolution that would enable tier-1 closure. Recommend the support tooling investment sequence by ticket volume impact.

title: Review SLA compliance trends across first response time, resolution time
prompt: Review SLA compliance trends across first response time, resolution time, and CSAT. Identify ticket types or time windows with consistent SLA misses and the root cause — volume spikes, complex issue accumulation, tool friction, or staffing gaps. Cross-reference with agent capacity and queue depth metrics. Recommend the SLA recovery actions that would most stabilize compliance without burning out the team.

title: Evaluate support knowledge base coverage against ticket volume
prompt: Evaluate support knowledge base coverage against ticket volume. Identify the top 10 ticket categories and check whether each has a self-service article, whether the article is findable before a user submits a ticket, and whether the article resolves the issue or requires a support agent regardless. Find ticket types where users mention searching before contacting support — indicating a documentation gap. Assess whether knowledge base articles are reviewed after product changes, or allowed to drift from the current product behavior. Recommend the knowledge base investments that would most deflect repeat ticket volume.

title: Audit support staffing against ticket volume and SLA commitments
prompt: Audit support staffing against ticket volume and SLA commitments. Identify time windows where ticket queue depth consistently exceeds SLA capacity, ticket types requiring specialist knowledge where no backup agent is trained, and seasons or events that predictably spike volume without corresponding staffing plans. Find SLA commitments that are being met on average but breached for specific customer segments or ticket types. Assess whether support agents have the tooling to diagnose and resolve tickets without routing to engineering. Recommend the staffing, tooling, and routing changes that would most improve SLA compliance.

title: Evaluate the support function as both a customer experience surface and
prompt: Evaluate the support function as both a customer experience surface and a product feedback channel. Assess whether ticket categorization is granular enough to identify product issue clusters versus one-off problems, whether tickets feed into a structured product feedback pipeline, and whether the most common issues drive product or documentation fixes rather than recurring tier-1 toil. Identify the top ten support issues by volume and assess each for root cause — product bug, UX confusion, documentation gap, or missing capability. Find escalation patterns that absorb engineering capacity that could be eliminated with tier-1 tooling, runbooks, or product fixes. Examine support tooling — whether agents have lookup, action, and customer context in a single interface, or rotate between systems for routine resolution. Look for self-service gaps — high-volume questions answerable by FAQ, documentation, or UI improvement that customers cannot find or solve themselves. Summarize the five support function improvements that would most reduce ticket volume and improve resolution quality.

title: Audit the support workflow architecture from intake to resolution to lea
prompt: Audit the support workflow architecture from intake to resolution to learning. Identify whether tickets are routed to the right specialty on first touch or bounce through queues, whether agents have decision authority appropriate to ticket complexity, and whether escalation paths are documented and used consistently. Find ticket lifecycle gaps — tickets sitting in unassigned queues, tickets reopened after premature closure, and tickets resolved without root cause documentation that would help the next agent facing the same issue. Examine the relationship between support and engineering — whether bug reports translate into tracked issues with proper context, whether engineering visibility into support trends is timely, and whether high-impact bugs surface to engineering through escalation rather than waiting for next planning cycle. Look for the feedback loop from resolution to documentation — whether resolved tickets generate knowledge base articles, whether the knowledge base is curated for accuracy, and whether agents trust and use the knowledge base. Recommend the workflow changes that would most increase resolution quality and accelerate institutional learning.

title: Audit the support quality assurance process
prompt: Audit the support quality assurance process. Assess whether resolved tickets are reviewed for quality — whether the solution was correct, communicated clearly, and delivered within SLA — or whether quality is measured only through post-resolution CSAT surveys with low response rates. Find support patterns where agents consistently provide incorrect or incomplete resolutions that reopen, indicating a training or documentation gap. Examine the onboarding and continuous training program for support agents — whether new agents shadow experienced agents before handling tickets independently, whether product updates are communicated to support ahead of launch, and whether common mistake patterns from QA reviews are addressed in training. Look for CSAT score drivers — whether negative scores correlate with specific agents, ticket types, response times, or product areas — and whether this data informs coaching. Assess whether support agents escalate correctly — whether engineering escalation tickets include sufficient reproduction steps and relevant logs, or whether engineering receives escalations they cannot act on without follow-up. Recommend the quality assurance investments that would most improve resolution quality and customer satisfaction.

title: Find missing feedback collection points in the product
prompt: Map the user journey and identify moments where feedback should be collected but isn't — after completing a key task, after a failed interaction, at cancellation, after the first meaningful value moment. Recommend specific feedback prompts and collection mechanisms.
prompt_short: Find user journey moments with no feedback collection.
prompt_filtered: Feedback collection gaps only. Missing touchpoints in the user journey.

title: Audit how user feedback currently reaches the product team
prompt: Trace feedback from collection to action. Identify gaps in the pipeline — feedback collected but never reviewed, feedback reviewed but never prioritized, feedback prioritized but never actioned. Find where feedback is getting lost.
prompt_short: Trace how user feedback flows from collection to product action.
prompt_filtered: Feedback pipeline only. Collection to action gap analysis.

title: Identify patterns in NPS detractor feedback
prompt: Analyze the verbatim feedback from NPS detractors (scores 0-6). Identify the most common themes, the specific features or experiences they reference, and compare to promoter language. Recommend the top 3 improvements most likely to convert detractors.
prompt_short: Analyze NPS detractor feedback for the most common pain patterns.
prompt_filtered: NPS detractor analysis only. Themes in low-score verbatim feedback.

title: Design a structured in-app feedback collection system
prompt: Design an in-app feedback system that collects targeted, actionable input without fatiguing users. Define: trigger conditions, question types, frequency caps, data storage, and how feedback routes to the right team. Optimize for response rate and data quality.
prompt_short: Design an in-app feedback system with triggers, caps, and routing.
prompt_filtered: System design only. Trigger conditions, frequency, and routing.

title: Close the loop with users who provided product feedback
prompt: Review the process for responding to user feedback. Identify whether users who reported issues are notified when fixes ship, whether feature request submitters are notified when their request is released, and whether feedback submission even acknowledges receipt. Recommend a closing-the-loop workflow.
prompt_short: Find gaps in notifying users when their feedback leads to a change.
prompt_filtered: Feedback closure only. Notification and acknowledgment for acted-on feedback.

title: Audit NPS and sentiment analysis to identify systemic pain points
prompt: Analyze NPS scores and open-ended feedback for patterns. Identify recurring pain points, feature gaps, usability issues, and competitor comparisons. Score each pain point by frequency and impact on loyalty. Create a prioritized backlog of improvements.
prompt_short: Identify systemic pain points from NPS and sentiment analysis.
prompt_filtered: Pain points only. NPS patterns and sentiment-based priorities.

title: Review in-app feedback mechanisms and collection quality
prompt: Assess in-app feedback tools: are they easy to find and use? Are questions well-phrased to get actionable feedback? What % of users who encounter an error report it? Are feedback submission abandonment rates tracked? Is there a spam filter? Are users thanked for feedback?
prompt_short: Audit in-app feedback collection mechanisms and response rates.
prompt_filtered: In-app feedback only. Accessibility and completion rates.

title: Evaluate product feedback prioritization framework
prompt: Review how product decisions are made from feedback: is there a voting system or weighted prioritization? Are enterprise customer requests weighted more heavily? Do power users get disproportionate influence? Is there a transparent process for explaining why a feature wasn't built? Is feedback regularly reprioritized?
prompt_short: Audit feedback prioritization framework and weighting rules.
prompt_filtered: Prioritization process only. Weighting rules and transparency.

title: Review beta program and early access for power user feedback
prompt: Assess beta/early access: do you have a power user group for feedback? Can users opt into beta features? Is feedback actively gathered during beta? Are beta participants informed when feedback leads to changes? Is participation incentivized? How many beta users in each cohort?
prompt_short: Audit beta program and power user feedback mechanisms.
prompt_filtered: Beta programs only. User recruitment and feedback gathering.

title: Assess customer interview program and qualitative research rigor
prompt: Evaluate research practices: how many customer interviews per quarter? Are they recorded and transcribed? Is there a systematic process for analyzing interviews? Are learnings shared with the team? Do interviews surface new insights or confirm existing beliefs? Is interview bias addressed?
prompt_short: Audit customer interview program and research methodology.
prompt_filtered: Qualitative research only. Interview frequency and analysis.

title: Review product roadmap transparency and community involvement
prompt: Assess transparency: is the product roadmap public? Can customers view what's being built and when? Can they comment or vote on roadmap items? Are there quarterly updates on progress? Is feedback from the roadmap tracked? Are transparency benefits (trust, advocacy) measured?
prompt_short: Audit product roadmap transparency and public feedback integration.
prompt_filtered: Roadmap transparency only. Public visibility and community input.

title: Evaluate feedback loop metrics and product team accountability
prompt: Review metrics: what % of requests are acted on (even if not implemented)? What's the time from request to decision? Is feedback volume tracked over time? Are product teams accountable for responding to feedback? Is feedback satisfaction measured (did we build what users asked for)? Are metrics public?
prompt_short: Audit feedback-to-decision metrics and team accountability.
prompt_filtered: Metrics and accountability only. Response time and decision tracking.

title: Audit voice of customer synthesis process for recurring theme detection
prompt: Evaluate how qualitative feedback from multiple sources — support tickets, user interviews, NPS verbatim, community posts — is synthesized into recurring themes. Identify whether there is a structured tagging taxonomy, a regular synthesis cadence, and a process for escalating high-frequency themes to product owners. Find recurring themes that appear in multiple channels but have never been consolidated into a product insight.
prompt_short: Audit VoC synthesis process for recurring theme detection across feedback channels.
prompt_filtered: Synthesis only. Cross-channel theme aggregation and escalation to product.

title: Design a structured user interview program for continuous discovery
prompt: Design a continuous discovery interview program. Define the interview cadence (weekly 30-minute sessions with target users), the participant sourcing process, the interview guide structure, and how insights are documented and shared with the product team. Find product teams shipping features without speaking to a user in the past month.
prompt_short: Design a continuous user interview program for ongoing product discovery.
prompt_filtered: User interviews only. Cadence, sourcing, guide structure, and insight documentation.

title: Evaluate NPS and CSAT survey design for actionability
prompt: Review NPS and CSAT survey design. Assess whether follow-up open-text questions are specific enough to produce actionable insights (not just 'tell us more'), whether surveys fire at the right moment (after value delivery, not immediately on first use), and whether response rates are sufficient for statistical significance at the segment level. Find surveys yielding data never referenced in product decisions.
prompt_short: Audit NPS and CSAT survey design for question quality and actionability.
prompt_filtered: Survey design only. Question specificity, timing, and response rate adequacy.

title: Review feedback triage process and routing to product owners
prompt: Assess how inbound feedback is triaged and routed. Verify there is a defined owner for each feedback category, a service-level agreement for initial triage, and a process for routing feature requests to the owning product manager versus bug reports to engineering. Find feedback falling into a category with no owner, accumulating without action.
prompt_short: Audit feedback triage routing and ownership assignment per category.
prompt_filtered: Triage process only. Routing rules and owner assignment coverage.

title: Assess feature request voting and community prioritization mechanics
prompt: If a public feature request board exists, evaluate the voting mechanics. Verify votes are weighted by user segment (enterprise votes weighted differently than free tier), that the board is actively maintained (no requests open for years with no status update), and that status changes are communicated to voters. Find requests with hundreds of votes that were shipped but never marked resolved.
prompt_short: Audit public feature request board voting mechanics and status communication.
prompt_filtered: Feature voting only. Weighting, board hygiene, and status update communication.

title: Design a closing-the-loop communication process for acted-on feedback
prompt: Build a process for notifying users when their feedback is implemented. Define the trigger (feature shipped), the communication channel (in-product announcement, email, community post), the message content (thank you + what was built + how to access it), and the tracking metric (engagement with the closing-the-loop communication). Find users who submitted impactful feedback that shipped with no acknowledgment.
prompt_short: Design a closing-the-loop notification process for implemented user feedback.
prompt_filtered: Closing the loop only. Notification trigger, channel, and engagement tracking.

title: Evaluate beta program design for feedback quality and representativeness
prompt: Assess the beta program structure. Verify beta participants represent the target segment (not just early adopter power users), that feedback is collected systematically (not just ad hoc), that there is a defined end date with a clear ship/no-ship decision gate, and that beta participants are thanked and updated on outcomes. Find betas that ran indefinitely with no decision.
prompt_short: Audit beta program participant representativeness and feedback collection rigor.
prompt_filtered: Beta program only. Participant selection, feedback structure, and decision gates.

title: Audit competitive feedback collection and win/loss interview process
prompt: Evaluate how competitive intelligence is gathered from customer feedback. Assess whether win/loss interviews are conducted systematically (not just when AEs remember), whether competitive reasons for churn are tracked separately, and whether product teams receive a regular digest of competitive feedback themes. Find competitive displacement patterns that engineering teams are unaware of.
prompt_short: Audit competitive feedback collection and win/loss interview systematization.
prompt_filtered: Competitive feedback only. Win/loss interviews and competitive churn tracking.

prompt: Fill in Missing Data Gaps
